reviews.text:
Tucked away down a quiet little laneway, this hidden gem of a café might be easy to miss – there’s no flashy signage, just a cosy, unassuming entrance that leads to something truly special. The moment you step inside, you’re greeted with a warm, homely vibe that makes you feel instantly at ease. The space is compact, with small tables and a snug layout, but what it lacks in size, it more than makes up for in charm and quality.

The coffee here is excellent, but the real standout is their matcha latte – an absolute revelation. It’s rich, smooth, and bursting with genuine green tea flavour that’s better than many you’d find in Japan. You can really taste the depth and quality of the tea leaves, suggesting they use proper, high-grade matcha rather than anything artificial. It’s creamy, comforting, and beautifully balanced – a quiet little luxury in a cup.

This café has that local, word-of-mouth appeal – the kind of place you feel lucky to stumble upon. It’s ideal for a peaceful solo visit or a relaxed chat with a friend, and once you’ve tried their matcha latte, chances are you’ll be thinking about your next visit before you’ve even left. For lovers of matcha and cosy, low-key cafés, this spot is a must.

I stopped by for a morning coffee because the ratings were high, and it’s right across from Bakemono Bakers. Small and charming spot tucked inside Drewery Lane, with just 2 tables that fit around 6–7 people. Simple menu, minimal design, and a cozy atmosphere.

I ordered a hot flat white, which came out in about five minutes with neat latte art. Coffee was decent, not mind-blowing, but nice for a morning stop.

Staff were gentle, friendly, and easy to chat with. It gets busier around 9 AM, mostly office workers grabbing their take-away.

A lovely café in Melbourne offering both coffee and matcha latte selections. I tried the hot matcha latte, and it was rich, smooth, and perfectly balanced. The atmosphere was amazing — warm and lively, with lots of people lining up, but surprisingly, the wait wasn’t long at all. They also sell a wide variety of coffee beans to take home. Definitely worth a visit! 🍵☕

Cool little coffee spot! One of the best matchas I have ever had. I’ve been long term travelling and trying matchas all over the world if that counts for anything! And this matcha was so balanced and creamy, and not overpoweringly sweetened. I was very impressed!! The staff are all cool and very friendly. It gets busy so I recommend take out coffee, unless you get lucky with bagging a seat.

It’s a small space and there’s always a line BUT it’s worth the wait. There’s a reason there’s always a line. It’s really good. The staff is always so nice. I’ve tried the matcha latte and mocha. The mocha uses a chocolate syrup which made it very chocolatey, it was very tasty. Matcha latte was really good too, the foam on top was amazing.

name: Brother Baba Budan
location: 359 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
It’s a unique cafe along little Bourke street (near hardware lane area). Glad to make it here in the morning on a weekend and the cafe was extremely crowded. We queued about 15min to order two latte and their ham & cheese croissant.

We love their flaky croissant. It’s very well baked and on a second thought, I should have bought two croissants instead of one for sharing. Coffee is okay and not bad (not the best I had in Melbourne). One thing great and interesting is the cafe vibe and environment. It’s a very nice ambience to be sitting here, take your morning breakfast and read a book or chill out.

Recommend you all to visit :)

In the heart of Melbourne’s ultra-competitive café scene, this spot stands out for its solid reputation and loyal following. The space inside is compact, but what it lacks in size it makes up for in charm – there’s a cosy, almost intimate feel that draws you in and invites you to slow down and savour the moment.

It’s very much a coffee-first kind of place, and that focus shows. The brew here is well-executed – smooth, balanced, and satisfying, exactly what you’d hope for in a city known for setting high standards in every cup. It’s not trying to reinvent the wheel, but it doesn’t need to – the consistency and quality speak for themselves.

There weren’t any real downsides to mention. While nothing stood out as particularly exceptional beyond the coffee, everything was reliably good – service, ambiance, and overall experience. It’s the kind of café you can count on for a dependable pick-me-up, whether you’re a local or just passing through. In a city filled with standout cafés, this one earns its place with quiet confidence.

📍Brother Baba Budan, 359 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000

@brotherbababudan

A lovely unique coffee spot in Melbourne CBD. They are well known for their golden gate time specialty coffee. Also, you can find chairs hanging from the ceiling!

I have tried their filter coffee before and this time, the golden gate time was also really nice.

Save this for later🍀

We visited a café known for its exceptional coffee and high-quality coffee beans. Upon arrival, we found that all the seating, both indoors and outdoors, was occupied, so we opted for takeout. While we were there, the café became quite busy and crowded, especially for takeout orders. We tried their iced latte, and the flavor of the coffee was impressive, which prompted us to purchase their house-roasted beans as well. I highly recommend this café to anyone seeking quality, tasty coffee.

small, busy Melbourne coffee spot with great vibes ☕️ enjoyed the iced latte!! light, clean soy milk that doesn’t overpower the coffee, so the espresso still shines through. a lil bit of acidity, but super clean and refreshing overall

Had such a good brunch at Krimper Cafe 🤍

The space alone is worth the visit! Its industrial, rustic interiors with high ceilings and warm lighting make it feel cosy but still very “Melbourne CBD cool.”

I ordered the Beef Bulgogi Eggs Benedict and WOW. Such a good twist on a classic. The beef was tender and flavourful, slightly sweet and savoury, and paired so well with the perfectly poached eggs. The hollandaise tied everything together without being too heavy. Generous portion too! I left full and happy.

Coffee was solid (as expected in Melbourne), and the service was friendly and efficient even when it got busy.

Definitely a spot I’d come back to for brunch catch-ups or city coffee dates 👌

Came here for brunch. We chose this cafe as Brick Lane was pretty packed. Service and atmosphere are great. And we had a good time catching up. Noise level is not too bad.

Portion size could be bigger for the price, but taste wise, it’s pretty good. We enjoyed the Chilli Prawn Scramble and Chicken Supreme. Not really a big fan of the Bacon & Waffle.

Overall, a lovely meal at Krimper Cafe! Lovely vibes!

Overall, mid at very best.

I was very excited to take a few of my friends out for a cafe experience, especially some of whom had not been in Melbourne for years.

Firstly, they flatly said the coffee was the worst they'd had in not just Melbourne, but Victoria. Weirdly muddy and gritty.

I tried and actually quite enjoyed the Yuzu Spritz, but a friend was not happy at all with the hot chocolate.

The food took a long time to arrive, probably about half an hour, and while I enjoyed mine, with the classic avo smash, no one else at the table did. A friend got the burger and flatly said they'd rather have gone to Grilled or Royal Stacks.

It was also very loud and seemed to be rather understaffed.

There was also a consistent line for the bathroom.

Overall it's hard to recommend much about this place. At it's best it is fine.

We happened to see someone opening this wooden door on the street, and that's how we found this place! And, what a pleasant surprise! This hidden gem of a cafe boasts an awesome atmosphere, great staff & service, & an amazing menu. We ordered the lamb bake, and the smashed avo: both were amazing. Strongly recommend.

If you want to experience the best coffee, food and atmosphere that Melbourne has to offer - especially the toastiest omg! Look no further than the delightful Krimper Cafe - hidden away from the main streets, down a classically characterful lane, around the corner! The lovely souls that work there are so kind and helpful and they let me watch the Ashes test on my little phone whilst I was waiting for a 3pm check-in ☺️🏏 As the pictures illustrate, the food is an absolute knockout😋 I returned there for coffee every day during my stay in Melbourne because really there is no comparison. Thank you so much for making me feel so welcome.
I am delighted to give this Cafe a 5 star
🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟🙏🏼❤️

name: Higher Ground
location: 650 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Great place. I had the eggs bene with prawns and kimchi on a Chinese donut and it was so tasty and presentation was really good. Only issue I had with the meal was the poached eggs were a little overdone. Atmosphere really good with a friendly vibe. A little warm where we sat. But that’s minor to the whole positive experience.

The place looks edgy and trendy.  I feel like this is more a place to have  drinks then a real cafe brunch place.  I find the food to be mediocal.  The hot cakes were too much.  The cake was too thick.  We had the barramundi was also just ok.  Not much to rave about.  The side salad was very good.  Tasty with the right seasoning.  The place was noisy and  the place leave a bad smell to your clothes when you leave.  Bad ventilation.

You can tell why it’s one of the more iconic and sought after cafes in Melbourne CBD. We went on a Monday and it still got full shortly after 930 am so I highly suggest to book unless you’re wanting to queue for a while.

The entire place is so unique and has an excellent vibe that’s curated through their decor as well as the moody lighting.

Food wise, it’s actually great value, better than you’d expect when you step in the place. The ricotta hotcakes are huge and it’s definitely meant to be shared. The hotcake itself was nice but I found the chocolate layer unnecessary as the sweetness from the syrup was already well balanced with the ricotta and hot cake. The udon noodles was creamy and had a nice umami to it that I’d definitely recommend it if you like pasta and Asian flavours. Chilli scramble was ok and nothing too special but like all the dishes, the volume was surprisingly heavy. Coffee was standard Melbourne level which is good but not too standout.

Overall it’s a great visit and I’d add it to the list of cafes to visit while in Melbourne!

Absolutely LOVED having my hens’ celebration at Higher Ground, Melbourne’s BEST brunch spot! Heartiest thank you to the team (in particular, Tess, Amelia and Sabrina) and to Brad and Dom on the day for making it all happen!! My hens and I absolutely loved the menu (I was superduperly happy to have my favourite Avocado Pretzel), and it was lovely to finally try the hotcakes (wayyyy bigger and fluffier than I imagined) plus the ambience!

Docklands’ true gem and my ultimate weekend go-to. The queue can be a little long, but I’ve always managed to find a comfortable spot , and it’s absolutely worth the wait.

The space itself is impressively large, with a beautiful interior design. Sunlight pours in from every angle, giving the entire venue a warm, vibrant energy that instantly lifts your mood.

When I visit with friends, 1–2 of us can easily sit at the bar area, which is actually one of my favorite spots. Being close to the coffee bar is perfect, especially if you’re like me and enjoy your coffee in rounds: starting with an espresso, followed by a latte.

Breakfast is another highlight. I love being able to mix and match different combinations depending on my mood. That freedom in choosing keeps it exciting and makes this my first choice for brunch every time.

The coffee is consistently delicious, the service is fast, and the staff are always welcoming and friendly.

A place you’ll happily return to , and enjoy every single minute of being there.

Had brunch at Brick Lane and really enjoyed it.
The corn waffle was crispy on the outside, soft inside, with a nice natural sweetness — not heavy at all. The omelette was light, fluffy, and cooked perfectly. Simple menu items but done really well.

What stood out the most was the staff. Super friendly, energetic, and you can tell they work really well together. Even though it was busy, service was fast and everyone stayed cheerful, which made the whole place feel lively and welcoming.

Great vibe, good food, and a solid team. Would happily come back for brunch again💕

A perfect fit into the eclectic Guildford Lane of Melbourne, this cafe offers a yummy menu in an excellent ambience. It is perfect for a team lunch on a work day or a weekend brunch outing. We were lucky to get a table on a Wednesday at 12:45 pm but we could see spaces fill up very quickly, so bookings are recommended if you are a big group. The comedian/actor Dilruk Jayasinghe came to eat at the same place so we know it attracts famous people as well! haha
There's outside seating available as well, for single diners and small groups.
The open kitchen adds to the open vibe of the place. There's choctops available too to fulfil your sweet tooth cravings!
It was the first time for me eating savoury waffles and omg they were so good! They are happy to eliminate the poached eggs from the dish if you want to. My friend got the smashed avo which was great as well!

Great experience at Bricklane. We tried the baba ganoush and the corn waffles with poached eggs, both were fresh, well-balanced, and full of flavour. Busy atmosphere and nicely presented dishes. Would happily return :)

I will usually inhale scrambled eggs. The chilli scramble here was a bit too spicy and oily/cheesy for me. It felt heavy and hard to taste the egg. The bread was interesting, it tasted like sweet brioche.

Crazy busy place! Get there early if you can. The food was incredible! It was a bit tough to hear the staff at our table, but that's what happens when you are in a happening place! We can't recommend this place enough!

Home of the OG mont blanc lived up to the hype! Coffees were literally the perfect ratio and taste, there is also a hot version which was also delish. Fairly limited breakkie menu but sandwiches were tasty (though heads up the pastrami one isn’t toasted, just warmed). Definitely worth a visit in Melbs.

📍Good Measure, 193 Lygon St, Carlton VIC 3053

@goodmeasuremelbourne

When people talk about Mont Blanc, Good Measure is often the one that comes into the conversation. It’s recommended by lots of people.

I had a visit and it was packed  and very busy. In my opinion, this might not be the one that I prefer. I would like to taste a stronger tangy orange flavour. However, it was not overly sweet and the coffee was good.

Save this for later🍀

Splendid wonderful atmosphere cafe here! Great n best of all is opened till late!!
Look at the special Affogato serving. The Matcha Cheese cake is nice n cheesy.
The ice Mont Blanc is a must try.
You will love it!

Spoiled for choice in Carlton, but Good Measure needs to go straight to the top of your list 🔝

Coffee shop by day, wine bar by night. Super diverse crowd and energy. You’ve got people reading books over coffee, couples sharing craft beer, digital nomads tapping away on laptops, early date nights, pre-drinks, bottles of wine being shared. It really is a mix of everything ♥️

As the afternoon rolls on, a DJ kicks in and the whole place just lifts. The service is excellent, the team are relaxed and welcoming, and it’s one of those spots that’s incredibly easy to settle into and stay longer than planned 🤙🏻

One of the best café service experiences I had during my whole Melbourne trip. Good Measure is popular everywhere for their signature Mont Blanc. I went around 4 PM on a weekday — the place was packed inside and outside, but after waiting 5–10 mins, I luckily got a window seat.

I came for Mont Blanc, Vanilla Latte, and Babka, but Babka was sold out… so I got the lemon cheesecake instead.
- Mont Blanc: Pretty sure the base is cold brew mixed with orange juice — super refreshing. The milk cream on top and the orange zest really make the drink. Not too strong, well-balanced, very easy to drink.
- Vanilla Latte (hot): I asked for half sugar but it was still way too sweet :(. I had to add ice to dilute it before I could drink it. The coffee base itself was nice and not burnt.
- Lemon cheesecake: Has tiny salt bits inside that help balance the flavor. Overall decent.

Really enjoyed my afternoon coffee at Good Measure: good vibes, good service, good coffee. Hope I can try the Babka next time, and hopefully find a coffee that fits my taste better.

One of my go to cafe in the area for so many years! Always very consistent with good coffee, and delicious food!! The seating space in there is well designed so you are not too close to the table next to you. It has always been a busy cafe but they are also great with efficiency!! Would always go back for a good coffee!!

The shop name is Seven Seed Coffee Roasters, and I came specifically for coffee. I wasn't disappointed; they have a wide selection of coffee beans. I tried the Tadesseyonka Ethiopia drip coffee (hot), and the barista was very skilled, extracting the aroma and flavor exactly as described on the menu. They also offer brunch, but I didn't try it. Furthermore, the shop has several seating areas and isn't crowded.

Coffee was as nice as we expected. My Waffle Benny came with a load of nicely cooked bacon, the waffle underneath made for easy eating. The sourdough is one of the best we've had in a while, love the side of asian green especailly the Broad beans. Great friendly customer service and happy vibe.

A lofty cafe with a variety of more and less comfy seating arrangements (tables for 2-4, communal tables, counter seating, indoors and outdoors, high and low chairs with and without back support), a contemporary soundtrack and a cool vibe. The food menu is rather compact, but covers all the usual Aussie brunch bases; a notably diverse offering of enticing sweet and savoury pastries; a wide range of coffee offerings that nowadays is a Melbourne coffee culture standard. A bit of an institution, but my experience varies from time to time - sometimes it feels attentive, cheerful and personal, sometimes more of an industrial conveyor line. Also the food has its ups and downs, I’ve just had a pretty waffle benny on a disappointingly dry waffle - but I’ve also had better luck on earlier occasions. Coffee, however, is very reliable.

A great place for good coffee. You really can’t go wrong ordering a coffee here. The staff are super nice, friendly, and really know their coffee. It’s also a great café to hang out either by yourself or with friends.

We came for the Magic coffee, we heard it was good - and the verdict? Yes! We loved it!
We arrived around 9:30 am on a Saturday, plenty of tables available. We chose to sit at the counter against the wall. The cafe filled up within the hour. Service is really good, staff serve customers with smiles. Coffee arrived quickly, food soon after. We were so looking forward to sinking our teeth into the Reuben sandwich, a New York City special. It was much bigger in NYC, so we were kind of deflated that the sandwich here was not as compact. Loved the taste though.
Loved the ambience and had a good time on the whole.
It was a Saturday so if you are going on a weekend, be aware that there will be that surcharge.
Will we come again? Oh yeah!

We tucked ourselves away in Rankins Lane at this "hidden gem" bagel shop. It has such a cool, artistic atmosphere and, more importantly, incredible bagels 🤤 They also serve coffee using ONA beans, which is always a win for coffee lovers🥰

🥓 Breakfast Bagel (AUD 21)
🐮Chipotle Beef (AUD 25)
🍆Grilled Eggplant (AUD 25.5)
☕️Cold Brew (AUD 7)

Every dish we ordered was amazing😚 I was honestly surprised by how generous the fillings were so rich and flavorful😉 I’m already planning my next visit to try the rest of the menu 🤩

Love Manchester Press! This is one of those spots you stumble on and instantly feel glad you did. Down little Rankins Lane, it’s got that classic Melbourne laneway charm — a bit tucked away, a bit quirky, and humming with good energy once you’re inside.

The coffee is excellent and the food is insanely good. The Cuban sandwich deserves a special mention — packed with flavour and big enough to keep you full and happy all afternoon. Pair that with the quirky artwork lining the walls and you’ve got the perfect brunch spot with plenty of character to show off to your interstate friends.

Highly recommend!

One of the best bagel poached eggs with salmon and avocado I have tasted. Coffee was good too. Loved the food, service was good and the place is nice.

We walked in and got a seat immediately. Probably cos it was a Monday, workday.

Super busy, so you might have a bit of a wait. It took 20 mins for a take-out coffee. Cold by the time I got it. Sad experience. From all the hype, I expected better...
Sit on empty milk crates in Rankin Lane. Look at the wall-art.

name: BENCH COFFEE CO. Lt Collins
location: 321 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: One of the best coffees I had in Melbourne. Easily one of my top 5’s. They have a good range of coffee menu. I ordered an iced oat latte once with a friend where we randomly popped in the cafe in St. Kilda and I loved it since then. I also love how are very friendly and accommodating to customers. They go above and beyond with their service and they don’t play with their coffees! Must try to any one visiting Melbourne.

Great coffee, amazing food, and a modern black-and-white space that feels so clean and unique. Staff are super friendly and knowledgeable, explained everything perfectly. Loved the biscuit (not too sweet) and the toast was delicious. Absolutely worth a visit — I’d definitely come back again!

Incredibly good coffee!
•Bold, smooth, and exactly what my morning needed
•comforting on a cold drizzly day
•kind and quick service
•they also give sparkling water to cleanse palate and appreciate the coffee more (which I just learned recently yay!)

We had Batch brew with was pretty nice with dessert, not too sour
Plum Dacquoise: the flavor was lighter than I thought, but still good. Next time I might try black sesame flavor.
Fig pastry was nice and sweet.

Cool vibes and great coffee. Loving the photo exhibition here, and everyone who comes in is dressed so fashionably. Upon ordering, you get a sparkling water while you wait for your coffee too. A good spot for a weekday coffee, where you can sit for a while to feel refreshed before continuing on with your day. Thanks guys 😊

Time after time is the latest patisserie/cafe combo to go pretty viral in Melbourne (marketed as owned by a s1 culinary class wars contestant).

Firstly, I really like the decor and whole stylistic design/concept of the store. Carefully curated colour palettes, shape choices and material selection etc - it’s all very aesthetic. Love the artwork displayed in store.

The food went viral for the charcoal/dark croissants and pastries. However, like many other commentators have echoed, I really didn’t see anything appealing with the pastries. The fig and ricotta croissant was soggy and the pastry itself tasted subpar compared to many other croissant in Melbourne. The toppings were fairly well balanced and subtle. At $13, it was not worth it imo.

The bacon, cheese and egg muffin was also not great. The muffin was dry and dense, and the cheese was not completely melted. I much prefer a McMuffin over this.

The time traveller was also really watery and didn’t have the creamy thick texture that most other places have with their mixed/speciality drinks.

The staff were quite friendly and service speed was ok.

Overall, it’s a nice place to catch up with people and maybe even chill out/get some work done but food wise was disappointing - especially at the current prices. The food has to deliver.

Went to Time After Time (TAT) Café for brunch and was genuinely impressed. the atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming, not flashy.
The matcha croissant was flaky with a rich, earthy matcha flavour, while the fig croissant was lightly sweet and perfectly balanced. The spicy prawn hot dog stood out — juicy prawns with a gentle kick of spice, something a bit different from the usual brunch options. We had the Time Traveler coffee, which was smooth, well-balanced, and paired really well with the food. Overall, it’s a great spot for a slow weekend brunch with quality food and excellent coffee. Would happily come back.

Conclusion first: this shop has officially become my favorite pastry shop in Melbourne!!!!!

I’m absolutely crazy about their matcha croissant🥐crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside (you can literally see the beautiful layers in my photo). The matcha cream topping is milky with just the right amount of sweetness, and the whole croissant is not greasy at all, which I really appreciate. Totally my type😭

We also ordered two toasts, and their sourdough is AMAZING. You must try the warm green one!!! It’s so fresh and incredibly satisfying. Highly, highly recommend!!!

The food is genuinely delicious and creative with no real downsides. The space is chill and perfect for chilling all afternoon. Recommend Mushroom Toast, Fig & Ricotta Croissant; Matcha Croissant, Time Traveller.

How is this even possible? So good.
Looks amazing and tastes even better.
The fig croissant and avocado bread were sooo good.
If you’re a mushroom lover, you have to try this dessert too.
The cafe is quiet and chill even on weekends, perfect for getting some work done.
I’ll definitely be coming back often🫶🏻

name: Two Birds One Stone Cafe South Yarra
location: 12 Claremont St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia

reviews.text:
Best brunch experience I’ve ever had. Portion sizes for The Breakfast were unbelievably generous and the quality was phenomenal. The different ingredients in the Breakfast were super fresh and tasted delicious. First time having thick cut maple bacon and will be going back for more. Ricotta pancakes were fluffy and the berry compote was amazing. Also loved the house blend coffee !! Bought a bag of beans to take home.

Staff were super friendly and welcoming. Outdoor seating brought the vibes and was very chill. Highly recommend Two Birds One Stone. The most amazing brunch experience.

Had a great experience at this spot -the ingredients are fresh, texture and flavours of the food are defs there. Good portion size too. One of the best and favourite brunch experiences so far -will defs be back

Absolutely incredible veggie breakfast with really good portion sizes - this kind of thing seems rare to find here! Everything on the plate had flavour.

Coffee was great too - we had an oat milk latte and an iced mocha (which was very chocolatey).

Staff were lovely and service was quick.

Came here a few weeks ago for the first time. Great little brunch spot with tasty coffee. We booked for 6 and got a lovely booth in the back but they do have outdoor seating for smaller groups.

The menu was typical brunch food but with some twists, it seemed each dish was slightly elevated with something. I got the chilli eggs and added chorizo, super tasty and great portion.

Food came out unbelievably fast and the girls were very attentive. It was quite loud inside from other customers so maybe a table outside would be best if you struggle to hear etc.

Two Birds One Stone delivered such a solid brunch experience beautiful space, great service. The tofu salad was the standout (so fresh + so much texture). Matcha wasn’t their strongest, but the coffee and food absolutely made up for it. A spot we’d happily come back to.

name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
location: The Capitol Arcade (behind Starbucks, SHOP 3/115 Swanston St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
I first came here back in October and absolutely loved it, so stopping by again during my time in Melbourne was a must.
I ordered the Bulgogi sandwich and it was delicious — the fluffy egg, the flavors, everything was just perfect.
On top of that, the staff are incredibly kind and welcoming. Such a lovely place. I’ll definitely be back again! 🤩

So happy I was able to find this hidden gem. Love at first bite. Warmest hello and welcome the moment you walk in. That fluffy egg is pure perfection. A definite must visit. Had the double bacon Korean sando and a matcha, and wow, everything hit. Already obsessed!

☕ Hidden Gem with a Korean Twist
Tucked away in the arcade, this family‑run coffee and sandwich shop is a delightful surprise. The coffee is solid and easily on par with other local favourites, but the real star is the Korean fusion toasted sandwich. The kimchi, beef, and cheese combination is bold, comforting, and absolutely perfect on a cold grey day. Friendly service and a cosy atmosphere make this a spot worth seeking out.

Best K-sandwich in the city! 😍
I had the classic bacon flavor — the softness of the fluffy eggs goes perfectly with everything! 🥓🍳

Not to mention the friendly and welcoming vibe as soon as you step into the store.

If you want to start your morning with happy faces and good energy, this place is highly recommended!

I usually don’t rate on Google as often, but I have to say that here is where you get the best k sandwiches in Melbourne! I’ve been to this place in 2023 and 2024, their k sandwiches taste so good as always. Last week, I ordered the ham (my usual order) but chef offered me the bulgogi, it’s so delicious and I’m definitely coming back for bulgogi (while ham is still my fav haha). Thank you for the great experience!

Great little hidden gem, interesting surroundings. Staff are so friendly and offer suggestions for brunch choices. We had the steak and BRAT sandwiches and they were great. It would be a great place to work away on your laptop and people watch by the window seats. Would return.

This charming, vintage cafe is tucked away upstairs within the workshop. The ambiance is lovely, and the window seat offers a great spot for people watching.

The staff is friendly and welcoming. The coffee is decent, and the canele is crispy on the outside and moist on the inside. It was a great start to my weekend. I love this secret spot, what a gem!

The vibe here is so niche - you can quietly watch the bustling city while sipping a good coffee. Their staff are friendly and great at suggesting food to match your mood 🤭 (mine was their chicken sandwich). I’ve been here several times, and it’s always great experience.
Ordered the Captain Sweet and the Porterhouse steak sandwich both were really good. This place’s got great vibes with classic and vintage interior. Server was really friendly, great service :)

The menu’s a bit limited and mostly has only bread-based dishes, but the food tasted quite good. It was pretty busy when I visited, and the staff kindly let us know they didn’t have time to make scrambled eggs, so they suggested poached eggs instead (They informed in a really polite way)

I’m delighted to have discovered this great laneway coffee shop! The friendly staff and the perfect laneway vibe make it a great place to start your day with a good cup of coffee. I enjoyed my skim flat white :)

And thanks to the previous review, I managed to identify the store by looking for Coffee X 3

Hidden gem in the middle of a city full of outstanding coffee. Stumbled upon this jewel when I met the owner under serendipitous circumstances and when he invited me over for a cuppa to ‘try and see for myself’, I was properly impressed! In a city where every cup of coffee is going to be of a certain level, it’s hard to distinguish yourself but the coffee here was good enough to compel me to take an immediate second sip. Extraction and balance was on point, nutty notes with a medium to light roast made for a pleasant drink on a cool spring afternoon.

A few days ago, I accidentally left my wallet at this café without realizing it. When I came back to check, the staff had already found it and kept it safe.

On top of the amazing service, the coffee here is great and the atmosphere is super cozy, definitely a place I’d love to come back to.

Thank you so much for your honesty and care!

Had the hardest time finding this cafe, was looking for Two Conversations, but the sign says Coffee x3. Don’t be fooled! Look for the Coffee x3 sign.

They didn’t advertise that they offer decaf, if in doubt ask! Seems to be common not to mention decaf on menus in Melbourne.

The decor was amazing, I loved all the figurines spread across the cafe. I wish they had seating, I would love to relax in this cafe!

The decaf piccolo was absolutely amazing. Anyone who says decaf can never taste as good as normal coffee needs to try this. Very clear, a bit fruity flavour. Will be visiting again next time I’m in Melbourne.

Absolutely best coffee in Melbourne. Such a cozy little nook hidden in an alleyway, the coffee is just phenomenal every single time! The service is more than a 10/10, staff and owners are so incredibly lovely and kind. It’s busy for a good reason, and despite this they’re always insanely fast pumping out drink orders. I never see anyone waiting longer than just a couple minutes.

name: Overlay Coffee
location: 320 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Overlay Coffee is a unique café that truly stands out with its creative drink menu. The peanut butter coffee is surprisingly delicious and memorable, blending rich coffee flavours with a smooth, nutty sweetness that works incredibly well together. It’s well balanced, not overpowering, and unlike anything you find in a typical café. The coffee itself is high quality, with a strong aroma and clean finish. The space feels modern and welcoming, making it a great spot to relax or catch up with friends. Friendly staff and innovative drinks make Overlay Coffee worth visiting again.

Checked out Overlay Coffee to try their peanut cream latte. It was really good! You can definitely taste the delicious peanut cream. I wanted to go for the peanut cream cold brew, but I was in a hurry. Looking forward to trying that on my next visit to Melbourne!

Drink so good please come back here often for any sugar fix. Ordered the hojicha latter with peanut cream (they're off menu) but it tasted like reeses peanut butter cup got married with some nutty earthy hojicha. Such a perfect combo. It's creamy thick, not overly sweet either. All the stuff are working super hard and they're really nice too! Don't forget to comeback for their other crafty drinks

Was in Melbourne for a week and was staying at the hotel just across this place. Saw how people queued up and got a lil FOMO moment, and guess what; DID NOT DISAPPOINT!

Came here a couple of times and the peanut butter latte hit every single time. No wonder the queue's there, the coffee's a gem!

Such a unique coffee shop! We came because a friend recommended their Peanut Butter Cream Latte, and as a peanut butter lover, this drink was a total surprise for me. The aroma is rich, the flavor is well-balanced, and it’s not overly sweet. Everything just comes together perfectly. I would absolutely recommend it!

The Matcha Latte is also quite nice, but if you can only order one drink, make sure you try the Peanut Butter Latte first — as long as you don’t have a nut allergy 🤧. It’s definitely the highlight here.

Overall, a very memorable visit and a must-try spot for anyone who enjoys creative coffee drinks. I’d happily come back again.
Love the cute shop! Great coffee on the go! Please add chairs for the fantastic staff so they can get their feet off once in a while❤️❤️❤️

I ducked into the Mitchell House Market Lane on a busy morning and walked out grinning. The little corner space has that clean, art deco feel, all light wood, soft lighting and a bar that lets you watch the shots being pulled. Music sits low, grinders purr in the background and the whole room smells like freshly ground beans. It is the kind of place where you relax as soon as your hands wrap around a warm cup.

The coffee is the headline and it absolutely delivers. My flat white was balanced and silky, with a chocolate and stone fruit thing happening that lingered without turning bitter. A follow up pour over showed off their lighter roast side, bright and sweet with a citrus lift that stayed clean all the way to the last sip. Milk is textured properly so the first sip matches the last, and if you are into filters, the baristas are happy to talk through what is on the bar and point you to something that suits your mood. Pastries in the cabinet are fresh and not an afterthought, perfect if you want a small bite with your cup.

Service is what keeps me coming back. The team is quick, friendly and clearly cares about the details. If there is a line, it moves, and orders land in your hands faster than you expect. Shelves of beans and brewing gear make it easy to take something home, with roast dates and tasting notes that are actually useful. Seating is limited, so plan on takeaway or a quick stand at the bar during the morning rush. Tip: ask for a recommendation, try the batch if you are in a hurry, and grab a bag for later. Consistently excellent coffee, zero fuss and a calm pocket right in the CBD.

Was visiting melbourne and staying just a few doors down from this place!
Best decision I made was coming in as I thought " it's always the smaller places , that have the best coffee" and I was right.

The coffee hit everytime, my friends and I made a habit of going to get a coffee every morning and also bought some of their  cool tote bags.

Staff were so nice! Even in busy times!

I can see why it's a popular chain . I'll definitely come back and try more of their range too.

I had a fantastic latte.  The cafe is stylish, and has a warm atmosphere. The staff were very friendly, and I received excellent service. There were also Japanese staff members, and we had a pleasant conversation in Japanese. It was a lovely way to start my morning, and I felt happy.
Highly recommended.

I’m a regular at this café, and the staff have always been really friendly.
But today was very different.

I came in like I normally do and stood at the counter to order. The male barista kept gesturing for me to step back, which really confused me. I wasn’t in the prep area — I’m very sure I was standing in the same spot I always stand when ordering.

When he handed me my coffee, he turned his head away and looked elsewhere. I said “thank you,” but he didn’t respond at all.

I honestly felt quite offended. I understand that everyone can have a bad day, but this kind of behaviour is unacceptable and felt very disrespectful.

name: Patricia Coffee Brewers
location: Rear of, 493-495 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
This place is absolutely worth the hype! I finally understand why there’s always a line, hands down some of the best coffee I’ve ever had. I walked quite a distance to get here and waited a bit to be served, but every step and every minute was 100% worth it.

The coffee was outstanding, smooth, perfectly balanced, and clearly made with care. What really impressed me though was the service. The staff were friendly and attentive, and they even brought out complimentary sparkling water. Such a small gesture, but it really goes a long way and shows how much they care about their customers.

On top of that, the selection of cakes and pastries is fantastic, so many tempting options to choose from. Everything looked fresh and delicious.

If you’re in Melbourne and love great coffee with equally great service, Patricia Coffee Brewers is a must-visit. I’ll definitely be back!

Melbourne surely has no shortage of great coffee spots, and during our visit we tried to squeeze in as many as possible. This one was by far our favourite. Not only was the coffee EXCELLENT, but we also loved the vibe and the service. There were many baristas on shift, serving the steady crowd quickly and efficiently. Everyone was incredibly friendly and welcoming, and it was a real treat to watch them work, and to watch so many Melburnians start their morning coffee ritual there.

Not sure how I’ve never got around to reviewing this GOAT of a coffee shop. Fast service, standing room only, sun filled high ceilings. Don’t be put off by the long lines and hoard of people out front. They will serve you fast and still find time to ask you how you are doing. Coffee is probably the best in town and strong. Beans are incredible too. Complimentary sparkling water and a taste of filter coffee if lucky. Bowen and Pip have built an institution and it shows with ever visit. A must if in town or just walking by.

My friend and I had coffee here yesterday and it was great! They serve really good coffee. I got my usual Americano using Brazil beans. It had a lovely aroma and rich flavor notes. I was hoping for a croissant, but they were out, so I went for the cinnamon bun instead. It was okay; soft, but a bit more on the bready side. What really stood out was the overall experience— the kindness of the staff and the calm, simple vibe of the café, where people genuinely enjoy their coffee. Definitely coming back!

We had beautifully-made flat whites when we visited this week. It was busy inside but the service was quick and friendly, and sitting outside had a great atmosphere. Both the pain au chocolat and cinnamon bun were amazing too. The server even brought out a sample of their iced filter to try in two beautiful cups which was such a lovely touch! Loved our experience here!

name: Otherside Coffee
location: Shop 3/188 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
A small, charming café on Little Collins Street. The space is tiny: just one table by the window and two along the wal; so most customers grab takeaway. I stopped by on a rainy afternoon for a hot coffee and a toast for lunch. Even sitting inside, the atmosphere felt calm and private. I liked the minimal setup, the quiet vibe, and the friendly but not overbearing service from the two staff.

- Truffle Mushroom Toastie (AUD 15.50): the priciest toast on the menu. It didn’t really carry the truffle oil aroma the name suggests, but it was generous with mushrooms and cheese. The salt seasoning on the crust was nicely balanced, not too salty.
- Hot Flat White: strong coffee flavour; the beans seemed slightly over-roasted but not burnt. Next time I’d customise the milk to suit my taste better. It’s served in a paper cup, so no latte art.

The café also offers a good range of milk options (full cream, skim, oat, almond, soy), and there’s no surcharge for swapping milk, which is a thoughtful offering!

Great coffee just across from our accommodation at The Victoria. Really nice spot to sit in the window and watch the early morning goings-on in the city.

Coffee was very well balanced, and easy drinking. Mine was strong, Tessa's regular, and there was a clear difference between them which is great.

I had a jam doughnut which was yum. They also do toasties which look great, but we didn't have those.

A very nice and simple coffee house. A good place for an early break with interesting food options. Indeed, hot home made toasts are available and they are delicious 🥪
Besides the cookies are very fine, crunchy and smooth 🍪
Eventually the coffee and chocolate were good and quickly served by the smiling staff!
The chicken breast option is convenient for adding extra protein during the day. The almond milk flat white was on point

Great that Otherside Coffee is open weekends. Toastie cravings is not just a Mon-Fri thing.
Coffee ✔️.
Friendly prompt service ✔️.
Toastie variety ✔️
Toasties filling tasty ✔️✔️

A good coffee shop to try in Melbourne. Tried one of their signature, which is the Ice Kyoto Latte, overall it has a nice balance of coffee and matcha flavours, and it is not too sweet. The coffee has a nice balance to the drink, and was expecting the matcha to be more flavoured to balance the drink. The place is quite spacious, great for work or hang out with friends. It is indoors. The staff here are friendly as well. It would also be better if the drinks were served faster. Overall, a great option if you want to try the different cafes in Melbourne.
Visited in the afternoon for a quick coffee and ordered a flat white which was pretty solid without tasting milky. The coffee was served too hot to drink right away though.

I felt as if I was in Palermo, Buenos Aires. I love its style, the matcha was delicious, the pan au chocolat and the cappuccino were very well made as well. Highly recommend, next time I’ll bring my laptop and enjoy the good music and atmosphere while I work

Great coffee in a little side lane.
8.5/10

Shouldn’t have to wait 20mins for a takeaway coffee. Get that you’re busy but standing there with baby in pram as you slowly make your dine in orders. Asked for a refund which took just as long as if you could just quickly make a capuccino. No apology, no awareness, poor service.

name: Little Bang Espresso
location: Level 22/8 Exhibition St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: The coffee shop is located on the 22nd floor with a nice view of the city skyline. As I was taking some photos from where I was sitting, a friendly staff recommended me a better spot for photos which was a nice gesture. The flat white I had was neutral tasting, not quite strong enough but not too milky either. Overall a decent coffee and service was great.
A hidden gem at level22 of an office building. Fantastic view, peaceful vibes, strong long black and a tasty chicken avocado sandwich.

Coming up level 22 to Little Bang is an essential part of my daily routine - I can't start my day without stopping by! Their coffees are consistently excellent and exactly what I need each morning.

Adrian is always there with a warm welcome, and the whole team including Esther and Jody are genuinely friendly and delightful.

The location is fantastic too, with views overlooking the CBD.
Highly recommend to anyone looking for their new favorite coffee spot!

Great coffee, great atmosphere, great views and excellent service and staff!
Highly recommended!

Hiding in the 22F of a office building, not only provide coffee to all the hard worker, but also welcome anyone who's come here for a chill afternoon, quiet, cozy,good for have a meeting or just sit down and relax for a book and enjoy the view !

name: Kumo Desserts Melbourne
location: 198 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
At first, I thought the place was small, but it’s actually quite decent.

They’re open until 10 pm, so it’s a good spot to get dessert after dinner. The prices are reasonable.

We loved their pistachio jam and the Thai tea gelato.

Don’t hesitate to ask if you want other options. My friend wanted to substitute the blueberry with strawberry, and they allowed us to do so.

The food came a bit late, but it was fine as the service was great and the staff were very enthusiastic about serving us.

Kumo Desserts is a must-visit dessert spot right in the heart of Melbourne CBD.
If you have a sweet tooth, this place is absolutely worth stopping by.

The desserts are incredibly delicious, beautifully presented, and packed with flavour. Every bite feels thoughtfully crafted, making it hard to choose a favourite. The quality, taste, and creativity truly stand out, turning dessert into a memorable experience rather than just a treat.

Perfect for a sweet break while exploring the city or a cozy catch-up with friends.
✨ Highly recommended — you definitely have to try it

Visited in Nov 2025

Kumo Desserts is one of the highly rated dessert cafes in MEL and therefore decided to visit it during my recent trip to the city. We tried 3 of their Soufflé pancakes and were super impressed by the quality of the preparation. I think irrespective of the type/flavour of the soufflé pancake you order, it will be a fantastic dessert dish as the underlying pancake is so well made - soft, light, fluffy and almost melts in your mouth.

We also tried some of their mocktails which were fantastic too.

Overall, I was super impressed. I would say this place is a must stop-by if you’re in MEL. I will definitely visit again the next time I am in the city.

Delicious fluffy pancakes in Melbourne! The staff are friendly and service relatively fast, though get here early to avoid wait times.

We got their strawberry, berry brulee, and pistachio papi pancakes and they were all delicious but the berry brulee and pistachio were our favourites. We also tried a lot of their specialty drinks. The ube latte and lychee mocktail were meh but the cloud drinks were absolutely delicious and IMO worth the high price tag

Love this place!!! Idk the difference, even Japan souffle pancakes couldn't compare to this shop! 🥹 We tried the Thai Milk Tea Pancake and it was delicious! Our drinks were also on point and very refreshing ❤️
The whole thing is really good for sharing if already full from a meal. Will definitely come back and has now been added to my approved MLB eats list! 🥰

Dukes Coffee Roasters continues to demonstrate why they are one of Melbourne’s most respected names in specialty coffee.

I started with a piccolo made using their single origin, which was exceptionally clean and expressive. The flavour profile was clear and well-defined, allowing the origin characteristics to shine through without distraction. It reflected careful dialing-in and a deep respect for the coffee itself.

I also had a latte using their seasonal blend. It was balanced, smooth, and structured beautifully for milk — rich without being heavy, and sweet without overpowering the espresso base. It showed thoughtful blending designed specifically for milk applications.

To fully experience their offering, I ordered a pour-over as well. The filter preparation highlighted their commitment to precision.

The cup was clean, transparent, and layered, reinforcing the quality of their organic beans and roasting approach.

Beyond the coffee, the customer service stood out just as much. The team was genuinely welcoming and engaged in meaningful conversation, creating an atmosphere that felt both professional and relaxed. Their passion for coffee was evident, and it elevated the entire experience.

Dukes delivers excellence not only in the cup, but in the culture they create around coffee. Great service, great energy, and great coffee — exactly what defines Melbourne’s specialty scene.

Every cup tells a story.

Visited in Nov 2025

Trying out Dukes Coffee Roasters had been on my list of things to do in MEL for a long time and I finally got the chance to visit this cafe during my recent trip to the city. My friends and I tried 3 of their filter coffees. Every coffee we had was really good. Our unanimous favourite was the Columbia Duberney Cifuentes Gesha and I’d definitely recommend folks to try it. Also tried a couple of food items from their bakery which were quite nice too. I really liked their tea cake.

This place is super popular with crowds streaming in and out, and therefore there is a good chance you may not get a seat inside. Saw most people just do takeaways.

In summary, a great coffee place which you should 100% visit if you’re in MEL CBD area.

Good vibe and nice taste, maybe is the best cafe I've tried in Melbourne. The coffee bean is very balanced. Busy but not crowded, the only flaw is their seats are limited, I waited five minutes to find a seat after I got the coffee. I'd
Love to come here for twice!!

Great coffee with sweet, acidic, and complex notes - easily one of the best in the city. Long black here is delicious.

Nice vibes and a professional setup, making it a comfortable spot to enjoy a cup.

Good selection of pastries to accompany the coffee, rounding out the experience perfectly.

If you want the perfect coffee every single time, this is the place to go. Honestly, if you know anything about coffee, you cannot find fault with the coffee. It’s actually perfect. Full stop. The constant queue out the door reinforces that.
We sat at the coffee and watched the coffee making and it was the most perfectly oiled machine. Every thing has its spot, every staff member knows exactly what they are doing. Your water is refilled without even asking.

I just can’t even tell you one think they can do better. Phenomenal

Wow!! Best breakfast I've ever had in Melbourne, and I've had a few! Its very French, so don't come looking for basic Bacon & Eggs. We had... baked eggs (vegetraian and also a version with chorizo), eggs Benedict that has scallops and asparagus (yes....scollops) and savour plate of the day. 4 differnt cold press juices on offer, all very tasty. The coffee is sensational! Service is quick, friendly and make you feel welcome.

The menu was unique and everything we tried was delicious. I ordered an oat flat white with the Big White Rabbit beans, and the coffee was outstanding. Even though it was a white coffee, the tasting notes were very clear and well balanced. It was honestly one of the most memorable flat whites I have ever had.

Visited in Nov 2025

Hardware Societe is one of the most popular breakfast/brunch places in MEL as per multiple online lists. So decided to try it out during my recent trip to the city. We did not have a reservation and did a walk in, and were able to get a table in 5 mins. If you can, I think it’s best to make a reservation as we saw a 15-20min wait queue forming outside while we were eating.

For food we ordered the Croque Madame, the Fancy Roast Chicken Sandwich and the Benedict of the Week. The Benedict of the Week came with scallops and was my favourite of the 3 items we ordered. For drinks we ordered some iced coffee and cold pressed juice. All the food items we ordered were decent in taste/flavour, and the portion sizes were spot on for brunch/breakfast.

Service was excellent. Kudos to the team here for ensuring good service with the quick rotation of tables, prompt order taking, and low wait times for the food to arrive.

Overall a very decent brunch/breakfast place but not the best I’ve tried in MEL.

This is my absolute favourite place to go, usually for birthdays. The food is always great, service is excellent. It is often very busy but fast. The coffee is market lane coffee and is very good. I often have the French toast and they do change their menu every quarter. Staff has been there for a long time so I would also guess it’s a good place to work. I cannot fault them. Just be prepared to wait for a table if you arrive too late.

Long time lurker ans finally got the chance to try this place out. The place seems to be pretty busy even during lunch hour. Was curious with the price of the $11 hot chocolate so there you go, you have to pour and mix it yourself lol... the taste is worth the price.
$30 each for the mains, we ordered hot smoked salmon and duck confit benedict (both comes with 2 eggs). I saw quite a lot of people order the scallop dish, perhaps I'll try that the next round.

name: ST. ALi Coffee Roasters - South Melbourne
location: 12-18 Yarra Pl, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia

reviews.text:
Long time fan and of this particular OG site. I come here every time I come to Melbourne. I rarely get a dish other than “My Mexican Cousin”, but this time I got the breakfast plate and it was heavenly.

They run a seriously efficient operation. Even when it’s buzzing, service is seamless and the food arrives quickly.

The in-house merch and retail section is a genius addition. Shelves lined with all the good stuff, I can’t help but leave with something new to try each visit.

And last, but certainly not least… the coffee.
☕️🤤

The specialty Gesha I’ve been lucky enough to enjoy a few times is, hands down, one of the best cups of coffee to ever touch my lips. Truly memorable and absolutely worth the price of admission.

Any café using St Ali beans already earns extra cred points in my book, but this place consistently delivers beyond that.

A staple for me. I’ll always keep coming back 😎

Consistency over the years is what keeps us coming back over the years whenever we are in Melbourne. My partner also now orders their coffee beans online for the machine at home!

Love the fit out and staff uniforms at South Melbourne - a bit grungy and eclectic. Staff are friendly and energetic despite the early morning hour.

There is usually space and no waiting on the weekdays for breakfast.

The food is really good - the pancake looks and sounds simple but is delicious, moist and fluffy on the inside. The breakfast hummus is light enough but flavoursome with the pesto, curry leaves, chilli oil and crunchy chickpeas giving texture too.

Parts of the menu changes seasonally so we’ll bound to try something different next time.

Great value kids options is so appreciated! Simple but it’s what they want.

Coffee is next level with choice and passion here. Something for everyone.

We came here as a group of six and asked for a quiet area. We were seated in a beautiful hidden room, which was perfect. The avocado on crumpet with miso butter was absolutely amazing. The service was great, there were plenty of drink options, and everything we ordered was delicious. Our whole group left very happy.

This might be one of the best coffee shops to try in Melbourne! Tried one of their signature drinks and also the batch brew. Forgot which one we ordered (shown in the photos), but that one tastes absolutely phenomenal. The coffee and the cream pairs really well, the cream on top elevates the texture, and overall it is well balanced and it is not too heavy and not too sweet. Definitely recommend trying this one! The batch brew also tastes ok, as it has nice flavours.

As for the service, the hospitality is top notch. The staff are very friendly and explains the menu items well. The place has lots of seatings to choose from. It has indoors and outdoors as well. Overall, this is a great place and highly recommend to visit here when coming to Melbourne!

Recommended by local, apparently they said St Ali has the best coffee in town, so I come and try it out.

They have really lots of variety of beans and brewing, I tried their standard and daily single origin, well, thats really good 😊

Food are very special as well, my favorite is the Barramundi Wings (fish with sweet and spicy source), a bit of thai flavor which is a surprise in view of this is a Coffee place. But i really love it, very fresh fish as well.

Turkish Egg and Fluffy pancakes are good as well.

I stopped by and ordered an iced matcha. It was smooth and creamy with a touch of sweetness, and had a pleasant bitter aftertaste. Honestly, it was so good I finished it in just a few minutes. My only wish is that they offered more than one size.

Very respectful and friendly staff and amazing tasty coffee.
Actually the first coffee shop in Australia which actually knows the difference between cappuccino and flat white :D
Also, the matcha was a 10/10 and strong according to my friend.
Coffee came out fast and staff were friendly and professional. The coffee was nice — we ordered ice long black and oat cappuccino, both were well balanced

Found the best matcha cafe @oko.cafe serving the greatest quality matcha lattes and matcha croissants. And they are a must try for all matcha lovers in melbourne.

Prices are on point, service is good and the owners are very welcoming and friendly folks. A charming vibe as well.

I’ll be back again considering it’s literally located on the next block to my place, and also how perfect t that matcha latte was.
Nice little cafe with friendly staff. Best flat white I’ve had this trip.

I don’t usually post reviews but I felt the need to share my positive experience today at the hatter and the hare. I treated my mum to the high tea experience for her birthday. Fortunately at the last minute my dad was accommodated to join us and order off the regular menu. The decor of the restaurant was beautiful, it was a vibrant but calm atmosphere. The quality, taste and presentation of each individual sweet and savoury was incredible. We were gratefully able to take home the cakes we could not eat. The service was exceptional. The man that looked after us was extremely accomodating and kind. All the staff were so helpful to ensure we had the best experience. Highly recommend dining at THATH and booking the high tea experience!

​This cafe is an absolute delight!
​I love Alice in Wonderland, and the subtle, charming theme is evident in every detail throughout the space.
​The food is fantastic—super yummy with genuinely large portion sizes. If you get an add-on (like avocado or bacon), you get a fair amount, so it's totally worth the price. The coffee is also high quality.
​I'm impressed that the quality here has not dropped over time.
​A special mention: The  Christmas decor is stunning and instantly elevates your Sunday morning brunch to a whole new, festive level! Highly, highly recommend!

We had breakfast and coffee at Hatter and the Hare in Bayswater, and everything was very good. The food was fresh, well-presented, and satisfying, and the coffee was smooth and enjoyable.

What really stood out was the ambiance and beautifully decorated, making it a lovely place to relax and enjoy a meal. It feels warm and inviting, perfect for a casual breakfast or a catch up with friends.

Overall, a great spot with good food, good coffee, and a charming atmosphere.

Stunning cafe / restaurant, with a great menu. The drinks were delicious (although in the pricier side for the size), the food was also super good. The place itself is extremely well decorated, possibly the best I’ve ever seen. You felt like you were in a magical wonderland. Would highly recommend trying this place out.

First time at the Hatter and the Hare and it delivered.

Tried Alice's Daydream, the Viet Lattes and their signature fries - all were fantastic! Excellent flavours and well balanced.

Went for desserts too - Hatter and Cheshire Cat. Both were great, but the Hatter was the standout.

Great coffee, top tier desserts, and a place we'll definitely come back to.

Parking's a bit tight out front, but there's usually parking across the road next to the Bayswater Hotel car park, which makes it manageable.

name: Miyama Melbourne
location: Level 2/300 Lonsdale St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: The garlic brioche bun was outstanding—soft, rich, and packed with garlicky flavor. The ginger pork was savory and well-balanced, and the teriyaki soba was satisfying with a great sauce-to-noodle ratio. Portions were generous; we added extra chicken for $5 and still spent only $65 for all three dishes. We were too full to try dessert, but we’ll definitely be back for sweets and drinks next time.

This place does one of my favourite iced lattes and also recently I tried the cheese brioche with kimchi and now the tuna and cheese brioche! If you haven’t had one of these brioche’s you have no idea what you have been missing out on! It’s so sooooo delicious, the best thing Ive had in many years. 💕

The tuna and corn bread is pretty good. However, the chicken teishoku is a bit of a let down. The chicken is a bit smelly.

Hope they can improve the teishoku menu.

The vibe is good and the staff is friendly.

Went in at 10am today and it was not busy. The staff welcomed me as I entered and we're really friendly. I got a latte and a banoffee tart to takeaway and it was lovely. The tart is just delicious. And the coffee is the perfect hot temperature and tastes great. I'll be back!

Very bright, light and open. Bustling ambiencr but comfortable to sit at. The servers are very proactice and efficient. Service delivery was prompt. QR/online ordering and payment option is also great for introverts who get flustered at options and ordering in person.

It is an asian fusion cafe concept and it was really tasty. I ordered the chilli crab soft shell crab omelette and it tasted really good with a little spice kick to it, would have loved a thick shokupan to absorb all of the goodness instead.

Would definitely recommend to drop by if you are around the area.

We had the beef eggs benedict and corn fritters. The beef is on the sweet side, and the corn fritters have a savoury, slightly sour mix of flavours! Really yummy 🍳🌽 Coffee was really fragrant as well ☕️

It was a chilly morning when we came and waited around 5-10mins for a table. It was a decent wait for a brunch time.

The staff are lovely and really nice!

We ordered the Beef Benedict, French Toast, Prawn Burger, and the Chicken Rice. For the drinks we ordered the Ube Matcha and Flat White.

Beef Benedict - it was on a sweeter side on our taste but it was good.
French toast - a decent french toast!
Prawn burger - 9/10! We really enjoyed it and it was very flavourful.
Chicken Rice - it was just okay. We had a good Hainanese rice in other places so we had high expectations.
Ube Matcha - nice flavoured matcha 👍🏻

Overall, it was a nice experience for us and enjoyed our meal! Will be back to try other dishes ☺️

What can you say?? It’s perfect. Perfect coffee, food, service. Totally packed, sat immediately , perfect cap in 1 min, ordered Thai pork omelette, landed 🛬 5 mins later. Immaculate. Well oiled machine 😜.. Super cool.. 🤙🏼🤙🏼
Solid spot with great food, had the prawn burger and Szechuan spicy baked eggs. Would call their cuisine Asian, all day menus did not disappoint. Filling and decent prices.

name: The Kettle Black
location: Fifty Albert, 50 Albert Rd, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia

reviews.text: The Kettle Black is pretty so it hurts, in a dazzling combination of heritage and contemporaneity. Different rooms and outdoor sections offer diverse seating options, from solo to communal. The food is pretty, too. I had scrambled eggs and was impressed by the freshness and depth of flavour, although the sweet intensity of sambal was a bit hard to reconcile with shrimp and eggs in some more sambal-heavy bites. The coffee was good, the service friendly and efficient. It may not have been anything extraordinary, but the setting made it feel satisfyingly indulgent nevertheless; I left happy. A cool spot!

The Kettle Black never disappoints. The atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming, with excellent service that makes you feel looked after from the moment you arrive. The food is consistently amazing—beautifully presented, fresh, and full of flavour.

Coffee is absolutely on point, and the soulful background music perfectly complements the experience, giving the whole place a laid-back but premium vibe. A standout spot for brunch or coffee in South Melbourne. Highly recommended.

We hosted a bridal shower at the Albert's Table at Kettle Black. It was the perfect intimate space above the cafe. The event staff were very responsive from day one and the staff on the day were very organised and lovely! The set menu was great and they even catered for dietries! We can't thank the staff enough for letting us host the perfect event here! Look forward to visiting again soon!

This restaurant has a section in a beautiful old white house. The interior is very pleasant. We had the breakfast roll and chilli prawn scrambled eggs, plus iced black coffee and latte. Everything tasted good and the service was nice.

Service is good and the atmosphere is great, but the food is average. I’ve been there multiple times as it’s next to my building. We ordered several items, and the last time I ordered the avo toast, pancakes, banana bread, an everything was average.

Had breakfast at The Quarter on Degraves and it was excellent. The food came out fast, cooked well, and tasted fresh. Eggs were done right, the avo was seasoned properly, and the fritters were soft and balanced without feeling heavy. Everything on the table looked great and matched the menu.

The coffee is the standout. Their magic is one of the best I’ve had in Melbourne. Smooth, strong, and consistent.

Great spot for breakfast in the CBD. Easy pick if you want good food and proper coffee.

Breakfast was amazing!! The scrambled eggs were soft, bacon crispy, smashed avocado well seasoned and mushrooms delicious.
Coffee was perfect!! Vintage style interior decoration lending itself to a 40/50’s era.
The service was polite and efficient!

Amazing experience! The food was absolutely excellent, full of flavor and beautifully presented. The staff were incredibly friendly, attentive, and made us feel very welcome from the moment we arrived. The porterhouse was outstanding — perfectly cooked, juicy, and one of the best I’ve had in a long time. Highly recommended, we’ll definitely be back!🤩🤩🤩

The food was excellent, plenty of choices and we didnt have to wait too long.  The pasta was cooked to perfection. The dessert, lemon tart, was huge! The service was excellent also, friendly and attentive. Great atmosphere and we sat outside in the laneway. Highly recommend and we will definately be back!

5 star for "Street Food" eats. (The tables are literally set up on the street.)
Popular place with fabulous vibe. We were here on a busy Sunday (+15% surcharge). Joseph (front of house) and the team provided exceptional attentive and friendly service. Prompt seating and order taking...
Bit of a delay on the delivery, but this gives more time to soak up the atmosphere. Well worth it. Some nice twists on favourite dishes. Seating indoor - or - out on the street.

Awesome coffee and drinks, an array of delicious savoury and sweet pastries, sandwiches, cookies and chia pots. Our choices were heated to perfection and full of flavour. Served by a warm and friendly small team. Lots of spots to sit under cover or inside. Loved our visit, highly recommend!

Whenever I come here, they always welcome me warmly!🥰 Omid and his team’s service is genuinely nice and kind☺️🙏🏻 The coffee and matcha are always delicious. I’ll definitely recommend this place to everyone and come back again and again!🤗🫶🏻

Fresh off the plane from NZ, we hit up this place for coffees and bites.

Coffee was very good. Set high expectations for our trip to Melbourne.

Cabinet food was fresh and tasty. Service was friendly and helpful.

I recently stopped by this cafe in Melbourne CBD during my morning coffee run, and it exceeded my expectations. Despite the bustling crowd, the team showcased remarkable coordination, ensuring a smooth and efficient operation. The result: hot and fresh coffee, paired with a diverse selection of sweet and savory treats.

The menu offered something for every palate, making it a delightful choice for breakfast or a quick pick-me-up. The coffee, crafted to perfection according to my specifications, was a true highlight. It’s evident that the city’s vibrant energy is back post-COVID, and this cafe is contributing to the renewed buzz.

The staff’s dedication to providing excellent service and the welcoming atmosphere make this cafe a standout in Melbourne’s CBD. Whether you’re a local or a visitor, it’s a fantastic spot to experience the lively pulse of the city while enjoying top-notch coffee and treats. Highly recommended!
I was here to have my morning coffee at 7:30. One of the best in terms of morning vibe. The coffee was nice. Nice staff with a big smile on their faces. Well-designed and pet-friendly.

name: Project Zero Coffee Meriton Suites
location: Store 1/140 King St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Stumbled across this little Specialty coffee shop. They roast  their own coffee off site. House blend was nice. It was a Colombian/ Ethiopian blend..but I found that the Ethiopian dominated in flavour (I prefer the Colombian).   8/10
Im curious in trying their strawberry cheesecake coffee. Its meant to be a popular. Verdict...........8.25/10
Very subtle but niice!

First time I came here and I was shocked on how good the coffee was tasting but also how much co-fermentation choice they had which is something you don’t really see every day.
I got a filter and my girlfriend a flat white with the strawberry cheesecake blend which is one of the co-fermented.
Mine was called piña colada and it was literally tasting like pineapple with a very clean acidity and balanced profile.
Will definitely go back to try more🥰

ORDERED: 🍵 Ube matcha ($10.00)

REVIEW:
🌱 If you like grassy matcha flavour, you'd love this!
We had it with full cream milk as well... but maybe oat milk will take away some of the grassiness if that isn't for you.
Colour was great and the ube flavour was nice.

In the end, it wasn't for me.
But grateful to have an ube-matcha option in the city! 🍠

One of my favorite coffees in Melbourne CBD. Tried the strawberry coffee and it’s so flavorful. Their beans are also delicious! Brought back the pina colada one and it’s unique and incredibly fragrant!

Tucked away off kings street is an amazing coffee shop... Friendly staff and delicious treats and even better fresh roasted coffee. Nice sitting area opposite out of the rain or the wind. Highly recommended project zero for a quick coffee or a relaxing long black.. will definitely be back

Wow what a GREAT experience at Zest trying out a flight of pour overs. We chose the set where we could hold constant the producer and region, and try three varietals.
Our immersion guide was Sergii and he was so informative (and fun and funny). It was superb to get to spend time with someone who is so passionate and friendly and welcoming about coffee. We got to have so many of our questions answered about specialty coffees, tasting notes, etc. We can't wait to come back to do another experience so we can keep learning. Sergii, thanks so much for your time and all we learned!

The coffee tasting experience was absolutely wonderful!✨The way the coffee’s characteristics were explained was clear and engaging, and the aroma, flavor, and atmosphere combined to create something truly special.

Google Maps only allows 5 stars, but if I could, I’d give 100 stars🤣. That’s how amazing it was.

The staff were friendly, and being able to chat about things beyond just coffee made the experience even more enjoyable!

I’m still basking in the happiness from the whole day.

We promise to visit again whenever we’re back in Melbourne.

Arigatou😁

Had a wonderful tasting experience with Sergii. He was incredibly friendly and knowledgeable, answering all of our questions The coffee options were diverse and it was a lovely environment. Would recommend

Zest provides a genuinely enjoyable experience each time I visit. The coffee is excellent, and I always learn something new about it. Whether you're new to coffee or an expert, there's something to discover here. The staff's passion for coffee is evident, and they are happy to share their knowledge. In previous visits, I tried CGLE and ninety plus at surprisingly affordable prices. Most recently, I enjoyed APAX LAB’s brewing water , which I believe is unique in Melbourne!!!🥺🥺

Had an awesome coffee class with Aryan at Zest! He’s super knowledgeable and made everything easy to understand. Now I’m even more excited to learn about coffee. The tasting was great too, and the manager was really kind and welcoming. Highly recommend!

Definitely a must visit if you’re a Proud Mary fan like we are. Come by with plenty of time so you can really let your inner coffee nerd out. Highly recommend. Our barista was great and gave us excellent advice on how to brew the beans we were interested in.

Really fun and informative coffee cupping experience in a small group learning about and tasting 6 different coffees from all over the world. Well worth visiting at 10.30am each Saturday for this free session. Stayed on for another delicious pour over coffee of my choice and sat at the bar to watch the barista take such care with the whole process of making it for me. Loved it!

They are really serious about coffee.
Taste good! Bit pricy but worth to try! Recommend!

We stopped by Aunty Peg’s and had delicious V60 pour-overs—beautifully brewed, clean, and full of flavour. The staff were super friendly and clearly passionate about what they do. A must-visit spot for specialty coffee lovers!

Lou expertly advised us about which coffee would be best for us and poured it in front of us showing the skill and knowledge this place is praised for.  We were recommended this place by our travel agent and she definitely was right on the spot. We also visited him in Proud Mary but prefer Aunty Peg's soft vibe and high end coffee atmosphere.

name: Cherry Specialty Coffee
location: 29 King William St, Adelaide SA 5000, Australia

reviews.text: We stopped by here for coffee. We were served a blend called Soar, which was pungent & fruity with a chocolate finish. Actually really enjoyed the brew & it was well presented. The staff & the barista looked like they enjoyed being there & have a passion for their work. Highly recommended

A great place to start your morning. Conveniently located, comfortable and cosy seating, friendly staff, nice range of very good coffee with various brewing methods and delicious croissants. Definitely will be back.

After reading all the rave reviews on Google, I had to try it for myself.

We had a few of their specialty coffees which brought out the inner coffee nerd in me.
Each distinctly different.

Their standard latte is also a big win, perfect balance of flavour and not bitter.

The shop is well thought out, small but mighty, and the staff are extremely passionate about what they do.
Service with a smile!,
The Bircher museli and chia are a great grab and go breakfast.

A nice place for breakfast, it is quite spacious and seating was comfortable. A popular place for take-away coffee. Ordered their special offer of bacon and ham, the wrap and croissant. It was quite nice and flavorful.

This is the best coffee I’ve ever had in Adelaide!!! They definitely make coffee with love!!!
Thanks Team

Had a wonderful breakfast experience here this morning! From the moment we walked in, the service was top-notch – our servers were incredibly friendly and attentive. The food was absolutely delicious! One standout detail was the prompt clearing of plates, which added to the seamless dining experience. Highly recommend!
🥰🍳😋👏😁🎉
We had excellent brunch here plus good coffee. We love it and I personally think this is better than higher ground in the city

I’ve been a fan of this coffee roaster for nearly a decade, and it’s still one of my favourites in 2025.
The Geisha was delicious. It's rare to find coffee with such a lingering aftertaste. Well done!
Amazing coffee and service. Can’t wait for the day Proud Mary adds Café Intégral to their menu. Definitely the most impressive use of the coffee berry I’ve ever seen and tasted.
Great atmosphere! The service was brilliant and knowledgeable of the menu. Food was delicious. Coffee was great.

name: Small Batch Roasting Co.
location: 3-9 Little Howard St, North Melbourne VIC 3051, Australia

reviews.text:
Considering the ubiquity of coffee joints in Melbourne, this is definitely considered a hidden gem. Tucked away in a small alley, you would miss it easily if not for the smell of coffee wafting through the whole alley and the Q of coffee drinkers standing outside eagerly waiting for their dopamine boost.
Menu is simple with coffee, tea and pastries. No brunch menu served here, so the focus is just on good coffee and pastries. There are some shared bench seating outside to enjoy the coffee and maybe meet new coffee friends.
