The coffee brewed is definitely of a high standard than most cafes here, low acidity with only a slight hint of bitterness, but the coffee flavour shines through even through the oat milk. Atmosphere seating by the alley really gives us a hanoi alley cafe vibe but without the occasional bikes riding through. Even the restroom is small dark but cleaner.
Le wife approves.

Not only do they serve excellent coffee, but their pastries are amazing too 🤩 We even pre-ordered two pastries online, so we could just pick them up, super convenient 😙

✨Reuben Corissant (AUD 10.5)
✨Pear and Geraldton Wax Danish (AUD 10)
✨Batch Filter (AUD 5-6)

We picked one savory and one sweet pastry, and both were delightfully crunchy with amazing texture, and the fillings were simply delicious 😋Their daily batch brew changes beans everyday, we tried the Luis Octavio, which had fruity notes, and The Golden Ticket, which combined fruity notes with a chocolatey finish 😉

They also offer a variety of nutty and fruity beans to take home, perfect for coffee lovers 😌

Coffee is gooooood! But I didn't get the blood orange croissant. Even started waiting half hour before they opened the store. I don't understand why they don't make more. I don't quite understand their operating method. I didn't have time to go a second time just for a croissant. I even tried to reserve a blood orange croissant online, but I couldn't get it. Always sold out. A little bit sad and disappointed for this situation.

Delicious coffee. Close to my home so it's amazing to go by. In even early autumn days or autumn days it's good sitting outside and watching the trees or the people coming and going. I tried a croissant and it was a jewel; flaky, but didn't flake so much you get dirty with the croissant chips haha. It's clean to eat, fluffy and buttery. I think it wasn't a messy croissant eat so I remember that the most to be honest which is quite a lot 🤣delicious place and I'd love to try more danishes or pastries!

Small Batch is a lovely bakery tucked away in a back alley. I was mesmerized by the aroma from 100 meters away as I walked towards the cafe.

The famous tiramisu tart was not in sight; I guess one needs to preorder before heading down. I tried their magic coffee, and it was a good and smooth cup of white coffee. I will return for their pastries again

They have some benches and stools outdoor, sitting outdoors with the good sunny weather brings a different kind of serenity.

Came back for the baked goods and the morning bun is 🤌🏻

name: Rosso Coffee Experience
location: 117 Dryburgh St, North Melbourne VIC 3051, Australia

reviews.text:
Rosso is our fave brunch spot in north melbourne. Topnotch food. Award winning coffee made with passion. And a pretty chill brunch place vibe.

Our go to orders would always be the lobster roll (succulent lobster and prawn bits in a brioche roll) with a side of nicely seasoned fries and an addicting aoli dip & chicken and waffles with a unique slaw.

I highly recommend you try their coffee cupping experience as well (i believe it's free during weekdays). Fun for dates or bonding with friends ❤️

Rosso Coffee – A Brunch Experience to Remember

We visited Rosso Coffee over the weekend and were absolutely blown away. The space is buzzing, generous and full of warmth, brunch here feels like a celebration.

I had the Iced Pistachio Cream: cool, nutty, and velvety. The Hot Buttered Lobster and Tiger Prawn Roll was rich and indulgent. For our little one, the Strawberry Marshmallow milkshake and golden ham and cheese toastie were pure joy.

We also tried the Louisiana Fried Chicken & Waffle, almond croissant and took home an apple crumble donut that was unforgettable.

Every dish was crafted with care. Every moment felt magical. Thank you, Rosso Coffee, for turning brunch into a sensory adventure.

A fantastic experience here, first time visitor and I’ll definitely be back. Even on a slightly warm day, the venue is really comfortable and the fit out is crisp!

Absolutely order the Huevos Benny - the brisket is cooked to perfection and the gravy which accompanies the dish is flavour central. Coffee is excellent, the batch brew was absolutely delicious.

A must visit for coffee and food lovers!

If Melbourne does one thing right, it’s coffee. And @rosso_coffee in North Melbourne is proof that passion, precision, and perfect blends still matter.

Step inside and you’ll be greeted by that fresh roast aroma that hits just right — their signature Veneto blend is bold and velvety, while Dolce delivers a creamy, smooth sweetness that keeps you coming back. Every cup feels like a crafted experience, not just a caffeine fix.

Pair it with their brunch favourites — the Huevos Benny with that dreamy yolk pour, the juicy Open Steak Sandwich, or one of their golden, flaky pastries that melt in your mouth.

Add in their warm, friendly service and cozy atmosphere, and it’s easy to see why locals call Rosso their go-to weekend spot. Whether you’re catching up with friends or soaking up that spring sunshine, this place just hits different.

I had such a great time at the coffee cupping session! It was a fun and eye-opening experience — I never realised how many different flavours coffee can have until now. The aroma, texture, and taste testing were all so interesting.
A special mention to Just, who was incredibly knowledgeable and passionate about coffee. He explained everything clearly — from how the beans are sourced and roasted to how to identify floral, fruity, and nutty notes. You can really tell he loves what he does!
Highly recommend this experience to anyone who wants to learn more about coffee or simply enjoy a great time exploring different brews. ☕✨

I had a great experience at Disciple Coffee. The coffee was really good — smooth, well-made, and full of flavour. You can tell they care about quality.

Liam was especially friendly and welcoming, which made the visit even better. Great service and great coffee. I’ll definitely be back!

Small coffee roaster delivering excellent hand ground espresso and pour over coffee. Barista's are super knowledgeable about their coffee and will readily give you recommendations based on your tastes. I bought the Lulu single origin, light, fruity with hints of stone fruit and grapes - and prices here are amazing for the quality of coffee, you would definitely be paying >$40 for similar quality coffee at other roasters like Ona/Toby Estate/Code Black

One of the best gem 💎 in Melbourne for coffee I have every tried! Liam is so expert and friendly about coffee he showed usso many kind of flavour he has got very very nice knowledge! I’m coming back super soon! Thank you to make feel like a home
Probably our favorite coffee spot in Melbs. The beans are all delicious, and we bought a few bags to take home. I especially enjoy the wide range of milk alternatives served—it’s like heaven.
Great coffee, long list of beans, and friendly barista. I saved this café for the last coffee in Australia and didn’t regret anything. Highly recommended.

name: Path Melbourne
location: 362 Victoria St, North Melbourne VIC 3051, Australia

reviews.text: Remarkably many paths in Melbourne lead to good coffee, and this is one of them, for sure. With a nifty guide communicating flavour through relatable references to citrusy, floral, berry and chocolaty, Path offers a nice selection of espressos, white coffees and pour overs at various price points. The focus is clearly on the coffee, but there is usually a pie or two on the counter as well, if you need a bit of a nibble with the brew. The team is very friendly, never forgetting to recite the tasting notes as they come with your cup.

📍Path Melbourne, 362 Victoria St, North Melbourne VIC 3051

@pathmelbourne

Calling all coffee lovers to visit Path Melbourne 🥰
An amazing experience that I had and the baristas are all very friendly. By the look of their menu, you could feel the passion.

Save this for later🍀

Path Brew Bar is a must-visit for caffeine lovers! I thoroughly enjoyed the interior, ambience, and hospitality of the staff. I visited on a Friday morning and had a lovely start to my day with their soy white coffee. The serene vibes and knowledgeable staff made it a great experience.

I’m also excited to learn that they have four batches of single-origin beans each month. I’ll definitely be returning!

Creme de la Creme of coffee! ☕️ was strong, delicate to flavours and brewed with perfection. No need to ask for 2 shots, coffee bean dominant in taste. The crowds were connoisseurs and ambience calm to enjoy the drop. That’s why Japanese coffee shops top the game.  Melbourne cafes pls follow suit, live up to your coffee reputation and stop serving milky and 1 shot coffees! Only milk served is oat. No issue.

We tried the white coffee blends - ‘Seasonal Blend Washed PNG/Natural Brazil’ and ‘Vinhal, Anaerobic Natural, Topazio, Red Catuai, Icatu, Minas Gerais, Brazil’. We could really taste the berry and chocolate notes and thoroughly enjoyed the flavour profiles. Staff took time to explain the flavours, were friendly and attentive. Definitely recommend a visit!

name: Come Back to Earth Coffee Brewers
location: shop 3/601 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: One of the fews places in Melbourne CBD that make coffee well enough to justify the cost, for me at least! Very consistent quality, only one visit out of many did not meet my expectation. No bathroom for customers on site but that's not uncommon for CBD stores. Team is very friendly. Thanks guys!

The guy working today, he charged me .80cents for a teaspoon of sugar in my coffee and on top of that couldn’t even get the order right. All I ordered was two coffees 1 with almond milk, one with normal milk.
After making me pay for sugar, he forgets the sugar and makes them both almond milk. Imagine I had a nut allergy after I told him why did you make both with almond milk? He is seriously lucky I didn’t launch the coffee back at him. Yes I have proof he charged me for the sugar as he made me pay twice one for $12.30 and then for .80cents for the sugar……. Then doesn’t even add it what a spastic quit your job please

I've been here a several times already because the coffee is so good. They roast coffee beans by themselves which is really cool ;) but also coffee bar looks awesome I love their symbols. And the baristas in earth coffee, they are really friendly and professional. They are always care about their quality of coffees! You guys must try here☺️
I just ordered a latte to sit inside and hide from the rain. The coffee was so good, I ended up buying a second one as a today. Really smooth, just delicious. Will definitely be back.
Small selection and the one scroll i had was nothing special. Coffee okay. No smiles… just business. Expectations based on a high rating were not met with reality sadly.

name: MAKER Lt Bourke
location: 387 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
A Masterclass in Coffee & Comfort

This is easily one of the top spots in this very competitive CBD area. I had the Ethiopian single-origin long black - it was a masterclass in roasting. Perfectly medium-bodied, allowing delicate floral notes to really shine.

Despite the busy location, the service is warm and welcoming, and the outdoor alley seating offers a great atmosphere to watch the hustle.

Pro tip: Get the special ham and cheese toastie. It was delicious and the perfect pairing for the coffee. I can’t get enough of this place!

Wr came laye on a weekend afternoon, so there was not much wait. Good coffee.
There are plenty of seats onside and outside of the cafe. There is a pretty awesome cold drip apparatus which isn't just a display but actually making coffee.

📍MAKER Lt Bourke, 387 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000

@makercoffeemelbourne

One of my favourite coffee spots in Melbourne!
I love the cozy white simple aesthetic vibe. Their coffee is nice to enjoy. Great for mornings and afternoons.

Save this for later🍀

⏰Mon to Sun 7-4

Always so busy here i was glad i could get coffee in early morning. Coffee was great sliky smooth and robust. Breakfast fougasse was crunchy and sweet. Some indoor and outdoor steating. Staff were friendly and service fast

The environment in the store is great! It's very atmospheric to shoot casually! The coffee beans used in the store are also very good. Because my friend didn't have lunch that day, I ordered a tiramisu. I didn't expect it to be a Swiss roll type. Unexpectedly, I found that it was delicious. There were a lot of people in the store, but it didn't affect my chatting with my friends. Moreover, this restaurant is not like other stores that will drive away when they are about to close. Customer, the clerk is very friendly.

Amazing brunch spot. Great food, fresh ingredients, friendly staff and a really nice atmosphere. I loved the fact that the waitress told us we had to wait 20-30 minutes for our food - it gave us an option to leave if we wanted to eat quicker/elsewhere. One of the best cafes I’ve tried in Melbourne. Will definitely be back.

My favourite cafe EVER!
amazing food.
great variety of drinks.
and sooooo many games 👏👏👏.
and the staff were gracious, helpful and super efficient.
Thanks for a great Melbourne morning.

I ordered the Tainan style breakfast and a cup of coffee. The coffee was solid, pretty much on par with what you’d get at most good cafés. The breakfast, though, was a bit disappointing in terms of authenticity. I was born in Taiwan, and honestly, the dish wasn’t even 10% close to the real breakfast in Tianan. No wonder no one has posted about this dish before. Having say that, it did still taste good. Perhaps the owner just wanted to highlight the city’s name rather than recreate the traditional flavour. Overall, it’s a nice restaurant and definitely worth giving a try. The only downside is that the location was a bit confusing to find.

The heat rush scrambled egg was sensational and one of the best I’ve had in the city. That hot sauce was beautiful and complex with the added on spicy chorizo it was a dish I will dream about.
Wonderful and friendly staff.
Plenty of board games too
Absolutely delicious food, fantastic coffee, and incredibly friendly staff. Everything was perfect — highly recommended.

Absolutely fabulous.  The staff were friendly and attentive and the food was sensational.  Breakfast was delicious and enjoyed so much, we returned for lunch the next day.  The meals were delicious, the flavours balanced, no request too much trouble.  Our plates went back scraped clean.   We will be back with no hesitation.

What's better than a great breakfast, and Grain Store is a standout in Melbourne. Consistently superb, this place is all class without being pretentious. A foodie's haunt but accessible and welcoming. Grain Store gives you the vibe that these folk love what they do, be it the food, coffee or customer engagement. I'd suggest there's something for everyone's tastes and food requirements. Often there's new menu options.
Grain Store, keep doing what you're doing. You're a credit to those who run the place, those who work there and to epicurean Melbourne.

Nice café with a great atmosphere and friendly service. The space is well designed and comfortable, making it a pleasant place to sit and relax. The food is nicely presented, though the flavours are quite simple and didn’t stand out much. Coffee was smooth and easy to drink. Overall, a good spot for the ambience and service, with room for improvement on the food side.

I ordered the Eggs Benedict and it was very tasty with well-balanced flavours. Proportion was just right for me. I also ordered an iced coffee, which was refreshing and revitalising after being stuck in a very hot stuffy office for my morning! The service was prompt and friendly and our water glasses were refilled without us needing to ask. The toilets are shared with other businesses in the building and located just around the corner accessed from the foyer area.

Great place for brunch. Absolutely delicious options (went for the daily special grilled peach with Prosciutto and burrata on waffle). Great coffee too, can highly recommend. Make a reservation as the place is busy for good reasons.

Omg! Hands down to the “egg tart” or I can say They have the best egg tart in Melbourne!! Its was so crispy on the outside, soft and creamy on the inside. The strawberry shortcake was delicious too. Another item that was also amazing was Dubai chewy cookie. Oh my gosh it was to die for.
Yuzu drink was delicious and refreshing but Dalgona matcha was just okay-ish.
Staff were really nice and friendly.

🤎 I’m a big fan of nutty flavoured drinks.
The grain powder really delivered a sweetness with a warm and toasty flavour profile that I really liked.
There were little bits of texture from the crumble + finer grain pieces which I thought was great to lightly munch on.
I wouldn’t mind more crumble!

So good that I was at The Butter Room at 8am first thing on a Monday morning – their opening time – so I could grab it before starting my work day.

Did someone say “new addiction”?!

I also saw some salt bread offering in the display cabinet which I’m keen to try for breakfast next time!

Staff are friendly.

ORDERED:
☕ Iced Injeolmi Latte: grain powder (soy bean, brown rice, black sesame), oat milk, vanilla cream, crumble ($10.00)
+ card surcharge

I paid $10.15 for this drink!

Ordered the ham and cheese toastie, the bulgogi toastie, the white rabbit coffee, blue matcha, and pandoro. The toasties were great, but the bread got soggy quite quick. I personally didn't feel it was a problem, but my partner was less of a fan of the oily bread. The bulgogi had pieces of jalapeno which gave a nice kick. The coffee was lovely and creamy and smooth, without being overly sweet. The blue matcha (butterfly pea flower and coconut) was actually quite rich. The pandoro we got take away, and really appreciated the little box it came in. The pandoro was lovely even a day later!

Such a lovely space!! The matcha latte was soooo yum. The keyrings were so cute that I wanted to buy them all🥺 There’s also a great variety of desserts, and it’s definitely a place I’d love to visit often 🫶

I’ve always wanted to visit Butter Room and finally had the chance to do so. The café offers visually appealing cakes and pastries that are clearly popular, as they sell out quickly due to strong demand and good quality.

The indoor seating area is limited and becomes very crowded during peak times. Noise levels can be high, making conversation difficult, and the queueing area also feels congested when busy.

One operational challenge observed was seating turnover. Some customers remained seated long after finishing their food, resulting in limited availability for dine-in customers and potential lost revenue during high-traffic periods. Introducing a dine-in time limit during busy hours may help improve seat utilisation and customer flow.

Additionally, for dine-in orders, serving drinks in glassware rather than takeaway cups could enhance the in-house experience and better align with the café’s branding.

Overall, the product is strong and demand is clearly there. With a few operational adjustments, the dine-in experience and efficiency could be further improved.

name: Regretless - Low Carb Pleasure
location: Mid City Centre, Shop 13-14/200 Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: I absolutely love Regretless! Everything on the menu is completely gluten-free, which already makes this place special – but the taste is on another level. It’s honestly some of the best gluten-free food I’ve ever had. Amazing quality, amazing taste – highly recommend to anyone, gluten-free or not!

Asked for extra tomato in my Ham, Cheese, Tomato toastie and received chunks of cold tomato on the side, returned it and asked if it was possible to use smaller slices and put them in the sandwich at toastie temperature and and it was brought back still considerably cold with random pieces of tomato stuffed between two halves. After I then brought this back and requested a full remake I was very satisfied with the final offering and hope i didn’t stress out the lovely staff who did make an effort to fix the error as quickly as possible! I believe that this was an honest communication issue and not something worth lowering the score as I eat here very, often. My advice is to be quite clear with staff if you ask for any amendments to the menu. Again, I appreciate the great staff who I see often and do amazing work here!

Absolutely outstanding experience! As someone who prioritizes both gluten-free options, it can be hard to find a restaurant that truly delivers on both — but this place exceeded every expectation. The menu was thoughtfully crafted, clearly labeled, and full of delicious choices that didn’t feel like “compromises” at all.

Every treat we tried was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The textures were spot-on (which is rare for gluten-free), and the portions were satisfying without being heavy. You can really tell the kitchen understands how to balance nutrition with incredible taste.

The staff were knowledgeable, attentive, and genuinely helpful when answering questions about ingredients and dietary needs, which made the whole experience feel safe and welcoming.

If you’re looking for a restaurant that makes gluten-free and low-carb dining feel indulgent instead of restrictive, this is the place. I’ll definitely be back — highly recommended!

Delicious and definitely guilt free desserts. Lovely yuzu sea salt - burnt basque cheese cake. Shared and enjoyed the atmosphere amongst the hustle and bustle. Worth a trip into CBD for this unique dessert cafe.
Second visit in 2 days - honestly amazing food and wonderful service. Wife is GF I’m not - I’ll be coming back next visit to Melbourne!! Generous serves and great price!

name: LAFAYETTE CAFE
location: Shop 4/290 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: My friend and I were looking for a neurodivergent friendly cafe to have lunch in on Friday afternoon and we decided on this place. My friend had her walking stick and was able to walk in with it. The seats were comfortable, upholstered in blue velvet. The food options were good value. I decided on the salmon and avocado tartine and pistachio iced black coffee. Everything was delightful. It did take maybe 10 minutes or so to get our orders but it was worth the wait and we had air conditioning to make it easier. The only criticism I have is the music, it was very nightclubby and didn’t suit the vibe of the place. I definitely will come back.

Had such a lovely dining experience at Lafayette! The space feels elegant yet cozy and perfect for brunch or a little dinner catch-up. The food was beautifully plated and tasted just as good, especially the prosciutto baguette and the French toast 🤍 The coffee menu has so many fun options too (the Biscoff latte was my fave!).

The staff were super friendly and made the experience feel so warm and welcoming. Definitely a spot I’d come back to and one I’d recommend to anyone looking for a pretty café with great food and vibes!

A beautiful oasis in the heart of Melbourne. Delicious food and coffees. Highly recommended.

(And for anyone who saw the Instagram / website for a cafe with a similar name - Lafayette Cafe is open!)

Firstly, I must say the service was excellent. The staff were attentive, friendly, and professional. My flat white was made beautifully — smooth, well-textured milk with a rich flavour. It was genuinely one of the better coffees I’ve had in the city. The café itself is also very clean, neat, tidy, and well organised. The tables were spotless, and the overall atmosphere was comfortable and welcoming.

The food was delicious and well presented. However — and this is my strong opinion — there is absolutely nothing French about this café apart from the name and perhaps some French-sounding words on the menu. While the menu may appear slightly French at first glance, the dishes are essentially the same offerings you can find in almost any standard Melbourne café.

If you are expecting an authentic French café experience, you may be disappointed. But if you are simply looking for good coffee, tasty food, and excellent service in a clean and organised environment, Lafayette Café well on those fronts.

In summary: great service, excellent coffee, delicious food — but don’t go there expecting France in Melbourne.

Blue velvet, French romance, tucked inside the historic Block Arcade.
This is truly my favourite hidden café I’ve discovered in Melbourne CBD. It’s a place where time seems to slow down, with an atmosphere that feels effortlessly special.
And most importantly—the coffee is really, really good. The baguettes are full of character too, especially the chicken and truffle flavour, which I highly recommend.

On a cold, wet, windy morning we braved the elements to walk to Cafe Court for breakfast. The street-side dining was obviously not available due to the weather so we went inside where it was warm and cozy.

We were seated immediately and ordered coffee while we perused the menu.
The coffee was lovely. Strong and hot.

I ordered the Eggs Benedict with bacon and hubby ordered the “Eggs your Way” Scrambled with bacon &  toast. Both were generous serves and took long enough to be served to know they were freshly cooked. The presentation was beautiful as it was for all dishes we saw coming out of the kitchen.
Best of all, it was delicious. Eggs perfectly cooked. Toast was great and the hollandaise was smooth and creamy.

The staff were all lovely - attentive without hovering but there when you needed them.

The cafe was quite full and we realised it was across from a court building (hence the name). There were lawyers meeting with clients, police grabbing breakfast pre-court and others enjoying a Monday morning coffee an d/or breakfast.

We rarely return to places we have been as we like to discover new places to eat, but I think we might just return to Cafe Court.

Heart-warming food and even greater service! A charming, family run business serving hearty meals.

Had the avocado toast, and omelettes. Multigrain bread is freshly baked and toasted to perfection. Omelettes were well executed and full to bursting with ingredients. They don’t skimp out!

Counter staff were friendly, accommodating, and kind.
The food was really good, both the big brekky and the pancakes. A little small and the QR code ordering is a bit funny (no table numbers) but definitely a good place to stop for a classic breakfast.

I had the Eggs Benedict here and it was awesome!!!The jam is much creamier and milkier than others I’ve tried before.
The portion is generous, and even the eggs are size L.This shop is open until 4 pm, which makes it perfect for late brunchers 🤣
Good food and wonderful service.  The lamb salad was very appetizing and filling. Their famous pancake was unexpectedly delicious. Their hot chocolate was just made to my liking.

name: Tomo’s Little Collins
location: 382 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
We tried their new Christmas specials:

Lobster Yuzu Eggs Benny — Easily the highlight of our visit! The lobster was tender and buttery, the yuzu hollandaise added a fresh citrus kick.

Tomo’s Christmas Big Breakfast — The glazed ham and house-made caramelised bacon were delicious, and the miso mushrooms were a pleasant surprise.

Truffled Eggs with Caramelised Bacon — Soft, fluffy eggs with a subtle truffle flavour that wasn’t overpowering. Paired with the sweet-savoury caramelised bacon, it was comforting and soooo satisfying.

Strawberry Matcha Christmas Parfait — Unique, festive, and incredibly tasty. We haven’t had anything like this before - big kudos to Tomo’s for this one!

The drinks were also amazing, highly recommend the iced earl grey matcha cream!

Service was excellent, with friendly staff and the owner coming over to greet us. The atmosphere was warm and inviting, and we’d definitely come back! 🤩

What a place. Note to self: book a table!!!!!! We just walk up - on reviews of others - and there was a queue. We decided to stand and wait - and we’re not disappointed. Our lovely host - Amira - was the best. She looked out fur us, communicated with us and made sure we enjoyed our breakfast. FOOD: value for money. COFFEE: fabulous.
Highly recommended BUT BOOK THAT TABLE!!

Absolutely lovely staff, the most friendly and inviting. On top of that their food is simply top notch, every few seasons they change their menu and every single time it’s an absolute improvement. Creative ,delicious food, the staff in the kitchen has some absolute magic. Even further their night cafe is simply sublime. Their cakes and pastries, too are absolutely top notch, hats off to their baker.

I came here for a brunch on my birthday, not disappointed !
The staff was so nice, smiling and welcoming !
And the food !! Omg, you gotta try the Benedict eggs 😊
I also had a birthday present from them, with a candle to blow.
10/10, we had such a good time.
Thanks guys !

✨ Finally Meeting My High School Friend — Brunch at Tomo’s Little Collins ✨

I finally met my high school friend! 😍
I did a quick browse to find the best brunch spot for a Sunday morning, and yup — we ended up at Tomo’s Little Collins.

They open at 8am on Sundays. I was worried it might be busy and we wouldn’t get a table because this place is popular. So I made a reservation, and luckily we got a table for two.

I arrived at 8am, right at their opening time, and yes… there were already people inside! Wow, interesting start for a Sunday morning.

We ordered:
•	Eggs Royal — 4.2/5
•	Build Your Own (sourdough, scrambled eggs, smoked salmon)
•	Hot Chocolate
•	Signature Matcha — 4.5/5

This was my first time trying a Western-style brekkie, and honestly, it was worth it! 😍

Food Review — Eggs Royal & Signature Matcha
The Eggs Royal came with toasted bread — the texture was perfect, not too soft and not too hard. It was topped with avocado, smoked salmon, poached eggs, and hollandaise sauce. The flavours were well-balanced and tasted great.

BUT… the smoked salmon was very salty. Too salty for both of us, which made it a bit less enjoyable. Everything else was perfect — just the saltiness of the salmon that didn’t match.

The presentation, though, was BEAUTIFUL. So aesthetic and pretty! They even added a small flower on top, which made it look amazing. 😍✨

For my drink, I ordered the signature matcha — and yes, I liked it! It wasn’t sweet, wasn’t bitter, and the matcha flavour wasn’t too strong. Just nice and easy to drink.

Service
The service was really friendly, especially the guy who welcomed me when I arrived while waiting for my friend. He took our order and even recommended what I should try. I was confused about what to choose, so I followed his recommendation — and it turned out great!

Atmosphere & Place
They have both indoor and outdoor seating. We sat near the cashier, and it was cozy and comfortable for chatting. I think they might have a second floor, but I’m not sure.

Around 9–10am, more people started coming in and the place got busier, so I’m glad we came early.

Overall
My experience here was totally worth it! I enjoyed the food, the service, and the cozy atmosphere. I definitely want to come back and try more dishes next time.

Thank you! ❤️✨😆

name: CHEFS JUNGLE Breakfast Lunch & Private Dinner

reviews.text:
28 Nov 2025

A nice place to have a quiet breakfast.
Not much customer while we are there.
Good music.
Since we're not big eaters, the big breakfast is pretty good, just nice to share for 2.

Our second visit, we accidentally discovered the place last year.

Absolutely the best breakfast I've had in Melbourne city in years.

Very friendly staff too!

**Title: Don’t believe the hype – a total disappointment**

I took the family here for breakfast based on the great reviews on Google Maps, but the reality was entirely different. It was a massive letdown from start to finish.

### The Food & Coffee

I ordered the **pork sandwich**, and it was pathetic. It only contained a couple of small pieces of meat that didn't look appealing at all. We also ordered large cappuccinos because they were advertised as being served "in a mug." Instead, they came in glasses (see attached picture). This was incredibly impractical and uncomfortable because the glass was far too hot to touch. To make matters worse, it wasn't even a real cappuccino; there was zero foam. It was just a poorly made, flat coffee with milk.

### Service & Wait Times

The service was terrible. Upon arrival, the host told us there would be a 30-minute wait, but it ended up taking 45 long minutes for the food to arrive.

Because the kids were starving, my wife eventually walked up to the cashier to ask if we could just buy a couple of the croissants on display to tide them over. The cashier was incredibly rude; she spoke to her very dismissively and told her she had to go back to the table and just keep waiting for the food.

### Final Verdict

All in all: bad service, poor coffee, mediocre food, and unreasonable waiting times. Save your money and go somewhere else in the CBD that actually values its customers.

"CHEFS JUNGLE on Bourke St, Melbourne, serves modern Asian cuisine for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Enjoy dishes like eggs, noodles, and rice bowls in a vibrant atmosphere. Open daily, with takeaway and delivery options available. A popular spot for foodies and locals alike"
Thank you for bringing good service to us. The food is very delicious. Friendly staff and good atmosphere. Highly recommended

name: Soul Soul Cafe
location: shop 1/158 Victoria St, Carlton VIC 3053, Australia

reviews.text:
Had a great brunch at Soul Soul Cafe in Carlton.

The Unagi Dojo was delicious the unagi was tender and perfectly paired with the rice and eggs.
The Thai Salad was fresh and full of flavour.

My favourite was the Tiramisu French Toast soft, rich and not overly sweet.
The Salted Matcha and Strawberry Matcha were also really good and well balanced.

Nice atmosphere and friendly service.
Definitely a spot I’d come back to! 💚

I came on 2 separate occasions, the first time I got the French toast and the 2nd time I got the unagi dojo. The French toast was great and decent in value ($19), but I did wait over half an hour for it. The unagi dojo came much faster, and it's definitely the dish I will be coming back for. A whole piece of eel and scrambled eggs on rice for $35 is not too bad. The iced matcha was ok but was closer to a sweet, grainy taste instead of the that classic, slightly bitter but rich matcha. Not something I'd get again, but open to trying their other drinks. Overall a very spacious and cozy cafe with beautifully plated dishes, definitely will be back for the unagi

Simply amazing.  Came here for brunch.  We ordered three plates, and each came out beautifully presented as works of art.  They were so beautiful we didn’t want to eat them, but we are so glad we did. The taste is just as good as the presentation!

Melbourne CBD can get a little hectic, and sometimes we all need a quiet escape from the chaos 🪴
Soul Soul Café offers a calm, cosy space to slow down and recharge. The café is incredibly inviting, and the food? Absolutely worth the visit.

What we ordered:
🐟 Unagi Don
🍝 Lamb Ragù
🥚 Scrambled eggs with avocado on the side
🍫 Chocolate torsade
☕️ Flat white (extra shot)
🍵 Iced hojicha (sweetness customised)
👶 Babychino
Everything was so delicious and full of flavour. I still haven’t stopped thinking about the Unagi Don! Loved that they can customise the sweetness of the hojicha latte too.

The café has a great atmosphere and a really nice vibe, with friendly and attentive service. Unfortunately, the food didn’t quite live up to expectations. The almond croissant didn’t taste very fresh, and the lamb pasta had an unusual smell and was overly oily. The coffee, however, was decent and enjoyable.but overall the experience fell short for me, and I don’t think I’ll be coming back

The wife and I were in the CBD during the week to watch a midday stage show so we decided to have brunch beforehand and found our way to 16 Oliver Ln off Flinders St where this hidden gem of a café called 'Bowery to Williamsburg' is located.

The cafe looks and feels like an American diner with a cool laid-back vibe with a small outdoor terrace area and sub ground floor area.
Greeting and service by the staff was genuinely impressive who also didn't mind giving us a wrap on how long the cafe has been in operation and the type of food on offer.
It feels great to see the 3x chefs strutting their meal cooking and prep skills and the barista pulling coffees in very close proximity to the indoor seating area

We decided to order 3x different types of toasted sandwiches (refer to attached pics) that were absolutely delicious, and filling with great coffee as well.

Now that we have discovered 'B2W' we will make it our go to brunch spot in the CBD as we do tend to see a lot of shows which are located in the immediate area.
Highly recommended..!

Tucked just off the main road, this hidden gem of a café feels like something straight out of a stylish American side street – effortlessly cool with a touch of industrial charm. The vibe is laid-back yet sharp, with an interior that invites you to linger and a staff that makes you feel instantly welcome.

Service here is genuinely impressive. We were shown to a table right away, and everything flowed smoothly from the moment we walked in. I ordered one of the towering sandwiches I’d seen raved about in reviews, and it still managed to exceed expectations – about 1.5 times larger than I imagined, packed with a generous, juicy portion of meat. It was so hearty that I couldn’t finish it all, but the staff offered a takeaway container without me even having to ask – a small gesture that really stood out.

The coffee? Exactly what you’d hope for in a café like this – bold, smooth, and served just right. It’s the kind of place that would easily become part of your daily routine if you lived nearby, offering not just great food and drinks but that rare feeling of comfort and consistency. A true local favourite waiting to be discovered.

The soy froth on the soy latte was just perfect.
I even checked who the barista was(the Asian guy who made my coffee — you did amazing!)
And the food? Absolutely amazing. Seriously, so good!! Don’t expect much from the service though. If the weather’s nice, I recommend getting takeaway and eating nearby.

Had the eggs and brisket - both delicious and well-prepared.

Nice environment with a convenient location, though it can feel a bit pressured during busy periods.

Not the most relaxing spot, but still a solid choice for a tasty meal.

Service was great, attentive staff. Had high hopes based on the good reviews but unfortunately the food was poor. Ordered chicken & waffles and brisket & eggs. The waffles were chewy and not made to order. Chicken was nice but barely had any maple syrup and was hard to eat with the chewy waffles. Was charged $1.50 when I asked for some more maple syrup. The brisket was very dry and the cornbread had a really strong baking soda taste. Not sure if we came on a busy/bad day but the place was quite empty when we put our order in.

name: Heat Café
location: 101-103 Melbourne St, North Adelaide SA 5006, Australia

reviews.text:
I absolutely love Heat Café! The food is consistently amazing, and their matcha is hands down the best I’ve had. I’m there almost every Tuesday with my laptop, and it’s the perfect spot to get work done in a warm, welcoming atmosphere.

The staff are genuinely lovely. Always up for a great chat and they even remember my order, which we love.

My fav food is the coconut chicken salad, fries and the Biscoff overnight oats!!

Best matcha in town & for all the coffee connoisseurs- the coffee is unreal! Delicious bagels, immaculate vibes & service unmatched.. If you haven’t been to heat cafe in Melbourne street yet, you must add it to your bucket list now!!!

Ham and cheese croissant was just crispy on the outside and melty on the inside. Freezing and rainy outside but it’s surprisingly cosy in this cafe with all the doors open. Sounds from the gym through the cafe adding to the vibe. Service is lovely and efficient.
From a coffee-holic very nice 👌🏼

found my matcha match here!! it’s really good as it is but paired with the strawberry and strawberry foam in their Samantha was freaking delicious 😋 I even ordered 2 at the end of the day! 10/10

I also tried their specials which was some kind of eggs Benedict on bagel paired with fried chicken and arugula(?) 9.5/10 it was really good and big portion wise, i went home happy.

Will definitely be back 💯

Heat is my favourite brunch spot in Adelaide! The matcha tastes exactly like the ones I had in Japan and there are so many fun additions to choose from. The food is also incredible every time, my personal favourite is The Hottie fried chicken sandwich. The girls are always so lovely and friendly and I find the service to be quick too.

name: Late Mornings Cafe & Lumi Dessert Bar

reviews.text: The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming. The matcha was delicious and beautifully presented. We ordered the spicy chicken karaage don and the unagi don—both dishes were absolutely amazing. Honestly, it was some of the best food we’ve had in a long time. Highly recommend! Definitely hidden gem 👍👍👍👍👍

I visited Late Morning Cafe last month because I was craving salmon ochazuke. The cafe is cozy and charming, located in a townhouse style building, which gives it a warm and inviting feel.
We chose to sit outside, as the indoor seating felt a bit warm due to the layout. The outdoor seating was comfortable, and I would recommend it, especially on a nice day.
We ordered the salmon ochazuke, the signature onigiri, the matcha red bean mochi, and two drinks. Overall, the food was good and fresh. The matcha red bean mochi was enjoyable but quite filling, so it may be better to share.
This is a nice place to stop by for a light meal, a short break, or a casual get together with friends.

Beautiful quaint cafe and the most lovely stuff
Exciting Japanese fusion brunch menu.

I was really blown away with the chicken katsu sando and the cheesy beef kimchi sando. Hit, fresh, so incredibly packed with flavour and such tender juicy meat
Looking forward to coming back!

I ordered the Karaage chicken rice and a hojicha latte which should be $31.6 according to the menu but was charged $33.94??
Food was beautifully presented and the yard was lovely. However , the chicken had a very think batter and the meat was also quite dry. My hojicha latte was not too bad.

A lovely way to start the morning! The cafe has a beautiful, relaxed atmosphere and the staff are genuinely friendly. The matcha is smooth and full of flavour, and the udon is delicious. Everything was fresh and perfectly prepared. The place is bright, cosy, and perfect for a quiet start to the day or catching up with a friend. Highly recommend!

name: UR Caffe
location: 117 Melbourne St, North Adelaide SA 5006, Australia

reviews.text: This was a lovely little cafe we stopped in for breakfast and were surprised at the variety of the menu.  We had the PK Benny which was Pork Katsu on Brioche with poached eggs, kale and tater tots topped with hollandaise and fried eggs on toast.  My only criticism was that the PK Benny had chilli on it which wasnt stated on the menu so I couldnt really eat it. The coffee was delicious and the raspberry matcha drink was yummy.

I had a fantastic breakfast right after I landed in Adelaide at UR Caffe! The avocado toast was a highlight—the sourdough was perfectly toasted and provided the best base for the fresh avocado, cherry tomatoes, and feta.
The flat white was also top-tier. Great latte art, smooth texture, and clearly made by someone who knows their coffee. Highly recommend this spot for a quality brunch in Adelaide!
Visiting from Sydney and found this beautiful cafe. The effort put in the coffee art and the amazing food and customer service. I loved this place. Will come again when I’m in Adelaide again

Do yourself a favour and do not hesitate to try UR Cafe- very cosy atmosphere, quirky art, very polite and welcoming staff- all nestled away in North Adelaide.
The special, Prosciutto Focaccia was phenomenal- huge serving (we split this between two), perfect flavour combinations and such fresh ingredients. The coffee was lovely, not too strong but not too milky. Get in early before the rush, although even then it’s not too loud. Such a great experience!

First time visit. Recovering from a long stay in hospital and getting used to walking again after an accident I managed to hobble down to UR Cafe and was not disappointed. The staff seeing my condition were very helpful- I even got table service!  - The food and coffee was great with very generous portions. I highly recommend UR to fellow cafe lovers. Five stars.

name: Golden Hash Cafe
location: 257 Melbourne St, North Adelaide SA 5006, Australia

reviews.text:
This is such a fantastic little spot, with delicious food that is both somehow pleasing to the eye but extremely comforting, just like a homecooked meal. I LOVED my soup.

Their blend of coffee and assortment of treats and pastires are fantastic.

The staff are very friendly and helpful.

Insane amount of sweet & savory selections for a small cozy cafe on Melbourne Street.

Always love a good, strong, flat white, perfect temperature! food is very good too!! I'd highly recommend supporting local.

While sipping my coffee, I smell something cooking in the kitchen. Divine~~~ hmm, must be the crackling pork.

The only drawback is low mobile reception when seated inside. If you need better reception, outside will do the job

Amazing little cafe, delicious food and the owner is very friendly and welcoming. My daughter and I look forward to coming here everytime we travel from Darwin for her medical appointments. We are currently on a family holiday and had to bring my husband, daughter and sister. My daughter and I love the donuts so Jasmine gave me her number and we ordered some to take home with us (not many will make it back, we are already eating them haha) the Bahn Mi and Bao Buns are also worth getting, very tasty! If your passing through it's definitely worth a stop and treat yourself. All the food looks divine and it's always hard to pick what we want. Look forward to our next trip to Adelaide to come back to our fav little cafe!

Vegan donuts! My son said it's the best treat ever.
Quick service and very friendly! Lots of soy free options also.
Great coffee, good atmosphere and nice spot to sit outside!

While everything was still closed after new year it has allowed me to venture into establishments I hadn't considered before. One of these is Johnny Come Lately. I'm so glad I did!
This busy little cafe has a great single minded menu - fantastic coffee and superb toasties. I was very lucky to get my first toast there as they had nearly sold out of all their food! They also serve a very 'Melbourne worthy' Mount Blanc.
The sandwich is quite big and you get a decent amount of filling for the price. The bread is toasted and has a good crunch to it. Definitely worth trying as a quick lunch

Their coffee was smooth with a nice touch of cocoa powder on the top. And I loved their mortadella focaccia! It’s so crispy and delicious.
Ps the lady who made my focaccia was so friendly and heart warming. Her smile made my day :)

I work across the road and quite often pop in. Usually if the man makes it - it will be lovely coffee. However, I have had burnt coffee, sour coffee and generally undrinkable coffee from a couple of the other staff there.

I will be giving these guys a miss from now on - this morning was the kicker. I ordered the smallest sized latte with an extra shot and was charged $7.00. I overheard one of the staff question it and the man who works there (perhaps the owner?) said “don't even worry about it”. So sneaky and dishonest!
A lovely café to visit! Great iced latte, delicious sandwich (greens cheese tomato), and friendly service. The vibe here is so comfortable.

This place was by far the best place I ate in Adelaide and one of the best breakfast spots I've eaten at anywhere. I had 3 different dishes and they were all amazing. Eggs Benedict with mushrooms was my fav but the smashed avo was also on another level with the fried haloumi, sweet chilli and beetroot, and the Turkish eggs were exceptional. I also tried a cold cascara tea made with coffee husks which was delicious with a sweet apricot flavour and stight fizz. Service was great, can't wait to go back next time I'm in Adelaide, thank you

Travelled from Sydney to Adelaide and was excited to try this cafe, it absolutely lived up to the hype. Hands down some of the best coffee I’ve had - the flat white was amazing. The food was also excellent and very reasonably priced. The staff were friendly and welcoming, which made the experience even better. Highly recommend.

Good coffee. Prepare for queue even lot of tables. It was worth it, the brekkie we had was awesome. Mind blown by the duck dish, forget what the name is but taste complex and enjoyable. Mushroom Benedict also nice.

A new favourite breaky spot! Haven’t been to exchange in a couple years but god it was good.

Fast prompt service to get us seated. Only downfall is the continuous nagging to get us to order. It took us about 2 minutes to sit down and for them to ask if we were ready to order. This happened a couple times and also saw a table nearby get visibly annoyed at the same issue. Let us actually read the menu team.

Food was awesome, we both got a serve of eggs and a side of the famous potatoes and dip. Gosh that sauce was mouth watering.

Eggs on both plates were extremely delicious and as I said in a previous breakfast review the bread! God, so simple but easy to give us a good serve of bread with eggs and sides. Everything was perfect and continuous rotation of water was great too.

Thanks team for the great hospitality and food.

lovely morning in Exchange Coffee with almond croissant, breakie burger and flat white mocha hahaha yaya

Exchange Coffee located in Vardon avenue, the buildings around is so classy and i like those red brick buildings here haha, and so comsy sitting insides and enjoy the street view yaya, coffee shop is so packed during our meal loll

for white, you can pick cappuccino, latte, or flat white, we surely go for the one australian claim it’s invention haha even nz does the same lol

flat white having very strong coffee taste, silky and smooth, with some milk flavor, easy to adapt even not a coffee fan haha good for my fam
BEST ESPRESSO MARTINI! I went to a few cocktail bars and Adelaide and this was so good, real coffee ! Not to sweet, so yum. If you love coffee this is the perfect place

Had my 22nd at La Moka and we had a fabulous time. Ben and the team were super accomodating and made the evening run incredibly smoothly. The cocktails and food were top quality. We loved the cheese board and spritz!! Definitely recommend coming here if you’re passing or make an evening out of it. Super cool vibe and supporting local!

Visited this cozy coffee this afternoon and the breakfast burger is to my liking. I think they put in some fresh made jam in besides the traditional tomato sauce and two eggs!! Brilliant 😋 the coffee has many seating available down and upstairs. The partition that they use made it look so lovely and dreamy.

La Moka is a nice spot with good wine and a lovely view of the street. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, making it a great place to relax. Whether you're enjoying a glass of wine or just watching the world go by, La Moka offers a good experience.

this bloke here might just be the kindest fella we've run into. We stumbled in on a rainy Tuesday night with grumbling stomachs. They were about to shut, but he saw our desperation and whipped up one of the yummiest charcuterie boards ever. A must recommend bar. The ambiance is excellent, the customer service is genuine, and the cocktails & mocktails are delicious

name: The League of Honest Coffee
location: 108 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: I went because I came across a TikTok video recommending it, but to be honest, it was just okay. It wasn't really any different from other coffee options.
Amazing flat white, tasty toastie and very friendly staff. If you are nearby, definitely visit this cozy place:)

I really enjoyed this place. I got a large coffee and it was phenomenal very smooth and easy to drink the sandwich. Panini that I got was pretty good. My only critiques would be that the bun perhaps tasted a little too hard. I’m unsure if it wasn’t made the day prior or perhaps it was toasted too long. The atmosphere is nice but very simple. I can recommend this place for the coffee definitely

If you’re in the area, League of Honest Coffee is an absolute must-try. From the moment you walk in, the quick and friendly service sets the tone for a great experience. The team here clearly knows their craft; efficient without losing that warm, personal touch.

The coffee itself is a delight. It carries a pleasant, light aroma that doesn’t overwhelm the senses. The flavour is smooth and well-balanced, with just the right amount of strength — not too bold, not too faint, and it doesn’t linger heavily on the palate, making it an easy, satisfying sip from start to finish.

The vibe of the café adds to the charm. Its quiet, relaxed, and perfect for a calm catch-up or a solo recharge. The standout lime green accents bring a fresh pop of colour without taking away from the understated, peaceful ambiance.

Whether you’re a local or just passing through, this spot deserves a place on your coffee hit list.
Ordered a flat white to have here and it was quite strong with a slight bitter aftertaste; pretty decent coffee.

Cozy place to relax and enjoy some treats.

The latte had lovely art and was really nice.

Great spot for coffee and a chill time with a friend.

Elementary Cafe is Adelaide’s Coffee Gem with a Melbourne Soul!

Tucked just a short stroll from Rundle Mall, Elementary Cafe is the kind of spot that makes you want to linger. The moment you step in, you’re greeted by that unmistakable warehouse conversion charm: high ceilings, exposed brick, plenty of light, and an easygoing buzz that says, “Yeah, we take our coffee seriously here.”

And they really do. I ordered the Magic, a Melbourne-born concoction that’s essentially a double ristretto topped with just the right froth of milk. It's a strong, silky hit of caffeine that means business. No surprise that the barista (and I suspect, the owner) hailed from Melbourne; you can taste that precision in every sip. Even the latte art had flair.

To go with it, I grabbed a focaccia, warm and perfectly seasoned. Simple, but done right. Add in a trendy crowd that seems to have stepped straight out of an indie film, and you’ve got a vibe that’s effortlessly cool without trying too hard.

Elementary Cafe nails it: great coffee, cool space, and that touch of Melbourne coffee culture right in the heart of Adelaide. Consider this my new go-to when I’m in town.

Adelaide has a lot of good food options, no matter what meal you’re seeking. The aroma of coffee pulled me into Elementary. Modelled on a Melbourne style coffee shop, this popular CBD coffee spot is serious about coffee. Their  Young Street blend which combines Brazilian and Colombian beans, throws milk chocolate and something fruity in a cappuccino ($4). The pastry counter offered up beautiful rectangular blueberry danishes ($6/each) topped with firm, plump and acidic fruit. Staff were friendly and not too busy to offer water to those opting to eat inside the simply furnished warehouse-style space despite the long takeaway queue.

Omg I love here mood and coffee ! I’m just a tourist . but I’m so happy to find this cool cafe :) And the cappuccino is super perfect and soft ! ☕️ And also look at this gorgeous latte art ! 🥰 Thank you so much !

Been here for coffee many times and always good. However this morning I tried a bacon and egg roll and it was terrible. Outside burned black, bacon burned into crunchy leather, and drenched in mustard (!).

name: Farmer's Daughters
location: 95 Exhibition Street, 80 Collins Precinct, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Wow. Not much else to say. As soon as we arrived until we left the service was amazing. Our server Nick was funny and friendly and incredible. The food was also sensational. This is not a restaurant with stingy portions, and the quality is incredible. Even the story of the food and where it comes from was great. Both wine matches are delicious as well (Yes they let me and my wife pick the different pairings)

Overall this place is a must visit for anyone visiting Melbourne who wants a taste of delicious Victorian food and wine.

Our party of five enjoyed a relaxed evening at Farmer’s Daughters, a restaurant that impresses not only with its food but with its thoughtful design across three distinct levels. We chose to dine on the rooftop — an inspired decision, given the pleasant weather — and it offered just the right mix of comfort and atmosphere for a leisurely meal.

Opting for the à la carte menu proved to be the right call. Each dish reflected a clear respect for quality produce and seasonal flavours. The lamb shoulder was a standout — slow-cooked to perfection, tender enough to fall from the bone, and paired with a jus that brought depth without overwhelming the meat. The beetroot salad with burrata, resting on a bed of mint sauce, was a delightful surprise — a clever balance of earthy, creamy, and herbal notes. The beef carpaccio was another highlight: delicately sliced, impeccably seasoned, and beautifully presented, showcasing the kitchen’s finesse. The bake scallops were plump and beautifully cooked but I did not really care for the topping of herb and sourdough crumbs. The Marker fish skin on was ok but did not blow me away found it was bland. The Heirloom tomatoes, buffalo mozzarella, strawberries and basil, was lovely and refreshing. I finished off with Cheesecake, berry compote, almong fennel pollen, if was ok but nothing to write home about.  Selection of cocktails were great and would recommend trying the Gippy Sippy, yum!

Farmer’s Daughters staff were accomodating and overall we had a lovely evening.

My wife and I had a fantastic experience her. We went in February 2026 and got the summer set menu which consisted of 5 courses

We paid additional for oysters to start. They were delicious.
Course 1 was a tomato dish which was perfectly balanced. I didn’t think I would love a dish of all tomato, but I did
Course 2 was the trout and soda bread. The soda bread stole the show but the trout was still fantastic.
Course 3 was a salad which was yum although I don’t really count a salad as a course
Course 4 was the venison which I loved. I thought it was cooked perfectly
Course 5 was dessert which was also amazing

The service was some of the best service I’ve ever had. It was the first time I had ever been asked of any dietary preferences (not just requirements like allergies etc)

I would definitely come back again to see what they do in other seasons

We dined at Farmer’s Daughters and chose the Gippsland-inspired menu, which delivered a genuinely memorable dining experience.

What truly sets Farmer’s Daughters apart is its commitment to place. It’s rare to find a fine-dining restaurant where both the food and the wine pairing are drawn from the same region, and that cohesion made the experience feel thoughtful, authentic, and quietly impressive. It’s not just farm-to-table—it’s region-to-glass, executed with confidence.

Every dish was excellent, but the additional beef short rib is an absolute must. If it’s offered, order it—future you will be grateful. The menu balances refinement with generosity, and there’s a warmth to the cooking that stops it from ever feeling overworked or precious.

The service deserves special mention. The team is welcoming, knowledgeable, and genuinely engaging. Questions about the food or wine are met with insight rather than rehearsed lines, and there’s a relaxed professionalism that makes the whole experience feel effortless—no small feat in a fine-dining setting.

In short, Farmer’s Daughters is a standout Melbourne dining destination. Exceptional food, well-considered wine pairings, and service that makes you feel truly looked after. A restaurant well worth seeking out—and revisiting—when you’re in Melbourne.

Overall dining experience was good and appreciated the Gippsland farm ingredients.

Personally loved the trout and the soda bread. But the portions aren’t much for the set menu, I think an average man would walk out still feeling hungry. They do give you an option of adding another main throughout though but wishing they notified about this at the start of the dining too.

The venison was quite chewy and the dessert was a bit disappointing where the chocolate mousse tasted very waxy and the orange flavours were too sweet and overpowering.

Good service and atmosphere, gets very busy on a Saturday night, just happy to try once.

Rightfully a Melbourne institution.   Five plus stars for the menu, service and atmosphere.

We had lunch today which was excellent, but would like to go back in the evening because I feel that the night time feel will be different.   But whenever, this is something really good.

Our choices included the warm sourdough, gnocchi fritto, anchovy toast with peppers, grilled calamari which was really special and something different; and, the centrepiece was the 900g t-bone with potatoes with sauce gribiche, and white cucumber buttermilk and mint, that was really refreshing.

As you would expect, great wines by the glass.

Dessert standout was the dark chocolate, caramel cream with a lovely Jersey milk gelato…very very nice and looked great!   The caramelised brioche  with almond milk and Meyer  lemon ran second but was also worth a look.

Great lunch, no negatives and you get what you pay for, special.

Thank-you.

Great experience all up. Wonderful and dedicated service, my waiter, whose name I missed, was exceptional and attentive. Very appreciative of him proactively advising that sides could be ordered as a half portion - perfect for solo diners who want to try all the things.

The food itself was tasty - not lifechanging but very solid. The anchovy toast and cucumber salad were the highlights for me. Steak was very well cooked and flavorful despite being the tenderloin. I would have loved a mignonette with the oysters, and the chips felt a bit flat.

Drinks were a highlight - the gimlet at Gimlet was very satisfying. I was happy with my glass of chilled beaujolais and then my Sauternes to finish.

The place was bustling for the 8:30 seating and didn’t show signs of slowing down, which is quite impressive for a Sunday night.

Would love to return next time I’m in Melbourne with a larger party to try some more of their signatures including the lobster rice thing.

brought my mother to Gimlet for her birthday lunch and I don’t think we could have imagined it to be any better.

i would like to applaud the wonderful service provided by the gentleman who looked after us. he was polite, attentive and certainly accommodating for such a special occasion. service was seamless, and wow! we felt right at home.

food was spectacularly elegant. chicken was extremely juicy and packed with flavour, scallops were fresh, and the crackers accompanying the oysters were absolutely wonderful. great recommendations on our server’s end!

Gimlet was the experience that everyone had raved about - we are delighted that we paid you a visit while in Melbourne. everything was great, and thank you for making my mother’s birthday an extremely special and memorable one.

such an impressive place, beautiful ambience and the staff were very attentive!! all the food was delicious, but the standouts were definitely the dry-aged duck, t bone steak and the calamari. personally i also loved the mango green tea mocktail (and the fries lol)
definitely lived up to expectations!

Here’s your updated five-star review for Gimlet, now with the booking tip included:

---

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We had the seasonal menu at Gimlet and it was exceptional. Every course was beautifully composed and full of flavour, from the raw tuna and whiting escabeche to the Gippsland beef tenderloin and the dark chocolate dessert. The service was polished but relaxed, and the space itself is stunning.

Every table was taken when we visited, so definitely book well in advance - weeks ahead, if not months. It’s one of those places where everything just works. Easily one of the best dining experiences in Melbourne.

We visited Nomad to celebrate our 2nd anniversary, and we can happily say that we were extremely satisfied with our experience.

For starters, we ordered the Wood Fired Zaatar Bread and the Zucchini Flowers. Both dishes were excellent and full of flavor. As a side, we had the Fried Maris Potatoes, which were perfectly crispy - honestly one of the best versions of fried potatoes we’ve ever had!

For our main courses, we chose the Market Fish and the Charcoal Roast Chicken. Both were beautifully presented and absolutely delicious.

For dessert, we decided to share the Apricot Granita, and we were very pleased with that as well - it was the perfect refreshing finish to our meal.

We would also like to highlight how much the Nomad team cares about allergies and dietary requirements. It was very clear that they paid close attention to the allergy note we included in our reservation, which we truly appreciated.

Overall, we had a wonderful evening and couldn’t be happier with our experience.

An exceptional dining experience from start to finish.

The food was outstanding — refined, balanced, and confidently executed. The menu is thoughtfully structured around Middle Eastern–inspired flavours, with a strong emphasis on share-style plates that allow you to experience a broad cross-section of the kitchen’s capability. Every dish felt intentional, layered, and technically sound.

Service matched the quality of the food. Staff were attentive without being intrusive, knowledgeable about the menu, and genuinely engaged. They were comfortable conversing while maintaining a high level of professionalism and pace — a balance that is often difficult to achieve.

The atmosphere complements the offering: polished but relaxed, with a sense of energy that enhances rather than overwhelms the dining experience.

In short, NOMAD delivers on flavour, execution, and hospitality. A venue that understands its identity and performs accordingly.

I visited NOMAD recently and overall was very impressed, with a 9/10 being a fair rating in my eyes.

The restaurant itself is good vibes, with an open kitchen adding to those in abundance. We were promptly seated and ordered drinks but didn’t get a food menu for 15/20 minutes afterwards. I understand why the restaurant does this, but the wait was too long for me.

Between three we shared the flatbread, burrata and fava bean hummus to start. The burrata was nice but not as good as the bread and hummus.

For mains we got the onion tarte tatin, pork, charcoal chicken with potatoes and asparagus. The pork was the stand out with lovely crackling but super tender. The tarte was great and the goats curd worked well with it, then the chicken was solid. I wasn’t crazy about the sides but they worked well.

Finally we got the olive oil ice cream sandwich, which was delicious but the olive oil flavour is quite subtle and goes missing when eaten with the honey and crumble.

I’d definitely recommend NOMAD and go back myself. It’s very reasonably priced for the quality of produce and cookery (~315 dollars for drink each and the food). A couple minor tweaks and this would be exceptional.

Exceptional lunch at Nomad
Every dish was beautifully balanced and full of flavour, with the zucchini flowers being a standout.

The wood-grilled cuttlefish with creamed corn and charcuterie XO was excellent, as was the market fish with heirloom zucchini ezme and smoked yoghurt.

Service was warm and professional, and the atmosphere relaxed yet refined. Consistently impressive food — well worth a visit.

A sensational place to eat at for food,service and atmosphere. All the servers were super kind, helpful and knowledgeable about the food, although a busy busy night various servers came to help however would’ve liked to see more staff able to tend to such a busy night. At times had to flag them down or long waits in between courses but they were always busy attending to someone else. Great communication and team working together as we were wished a happy anniversary on various occasions.

The food was delicious, some of the best quality foods locally sourced with great portions, amazing attention to detail and a decent price for such an establishment. Highly recommend the tomatoes, house charcuterie, pork(best crackle I’ve ever had) and the fish. Would’ve liked to seen a sharper knife or the meats cut a bit thinner as they were a bit hard to cut for its generous size. Highly recommend and would come back to Melbourne just for this place!

name: Izakaya Domo
location: GPO Postal Lane, Shop 28/350 Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Great place in a great little food alley! Short wait to get a table will book next time. Service was very helpful and friendly. Great table and chair set up ( the seats lift up to put your bags in and there’s a little drawer that pops out from the table with chopsticks etc). Food was quick to arrive, well cooked and tasted delicious. Highly recommend and will go again.

Dining here is like embarking on a nostalgic journey back to the bustling streets of Japan, reminiscent of the vibrant evenings spent between Osaka and Tokyo. As you step inside, the warm ambiance envelops you, transporting you to a time when families and friends gathered to share their favorite dishes and create cherished memories.

Each meal is a carefully crafted masterpiece, showcasing the artistry and dedication of skilled chefs who pour their passion into every plate. From the moment the first dish arrives at your table, it’s an explosion of colors and aromas that beckon you to dig in. The presentation is a feast for the eyes, with vibrant garnishes and artful arrangements that elevate the dining experience.

As you take your first bite, prepare to be captivated by flavors that dance on your palate—each dish uniquely crafted to tell a story of tradition and culinary expertise. The tenderness of perfectly cooked meats, the crunch of fresh vegetables, and the delicate balance of spices create an exhilarating symphony of tastes that is nothing short of mouth-watering.

Whether it’s the hearty embrace of savory ramen, the delicate layers of sushi expertly rolled with freshness, or the rich, umami-laden depth of a steaming bowl of donburi, every morsel invites you to linger, savor, and enjoy. The intimate setting enhances the experience, allowing you to indulge in not just the food, but the heartwarming essence of dining together.

Prepare yourself for a culinary adventure that brings the past to life, where every bite is a celebration of flavors, tradition, and the joy of shared meals. Dining here is not just a meal; it’s an experience that will leave your taste buds singing and your heart full.

So. Good!
It was packed and we were concerned about lack of reservation. But less than 10 mins later we had a table. Food was 👏DELICIOUS👏. Highly (highly) rate the chilli tofu; spicy chicken donburi, chicken skewers, edamame, Gyoza and octopus were all great; only thing we probably wouldn’t reorder is the wagyu ramen. And that’s only because we love  a more flavorful broth. Adding the tofu + chilli sauce to the ramen though, gooood. Drinks were yum too. And couldn’t fault the friendly service

Very busy Japanese restaurant with a good vibe and delicious meals.

The teishoku set portion is huge. I am surprised it has a small bowl ramen included. I thought it was a miso soup only.

The ramen is also very flavourful and the soup is not too thick.

It's a highly recommended Japanese restaurant in the CBD.

Enjoyed our dishes and thought the staff were all very friendly and happy to be there. The ordering system is quite easy (QR code that links you to the menu) and the sets are good value. I really enjoyed my salmon don set (with marinated shitake mushrooms) and would happily have that as an individual dish. My set came with cheesecake, chicken and some salad with yummy Yuzu dressing. The spicy wagyu was delicious (sort of like a black vinegar spicy taste but not very spicy). The atmosphere in GPO is quite trendy with their own toilets inside the GPO building. Lots of seating and popular during peak times. We arrived just as they were opening so we didn't have to wait and the food came very quickly.

I had an unforgettable time at Freyja for our Valentine's dinner. The countertop experience was incredible as we had an opportunity to gaze into the clockwork of the kitchen - even having an opportunity to directly compliment the kitchen staff.

I cannot say enough good things about the food and the care put into the presentation (which we could directly observe). Everything was cooked to perfection, and had such interesting flavour profiles! The white fish was served in this beautiful miso eggplant jus, while the steak was paired with sweet cherry and this bitter witlof. Everything just tasted divine.

Will definitely find more opportunities to return.

Dinner at Freyja and seated in the window looking out onto Collins Street was a particularly nice environment on a cold and blustery Saturday Melbourne evening.

We shared everything and started with the warm sourdough and cultured butter, the latter being very moreish and very delicious.   Next was the tuna which was okay.   The Freyja Signature waffle is something different with smoked sour cream and trout roe is recommended although exxy at $46.

The duck was two pieces of breast.

The scotch fillet was probably the highlight of the night, cooked beautifully with redcurrants and a seafood mustard that altogether made this our pick of the night.

Dessert was titled rhubarb - with other stuff that included Geraldton Wax.   My wife really enjoyed this one.

Our total bill for two pointed towards $380 but that included a cocktail and three glasses of wine.

This is a very nice restaurant that has really good reviews; and, is offering a point of difference which is great but not everything clicked for me last night…often it could come down to my menu choices.   Staff are friendly and helpful to ensure diners enjoy their dining experience.   Many thanks.

We dined here last October. The food was superb, cooked really well, and the waitstaff were very knowledgeable and helpful in advising us on what to order and good food pairings. We had oysters with vinaigrette, both natural and as a starter. We also had the chicken liver, which was a delicious pâté, followed by the goat's cheese, which was well and beautifully presented with fruits. We also tried the caviar bump before our main course arrived. The cocktails here are super delicious and look beautiful. We sat near the window, but inside, you can sit in front of the cooks and watch your food being prepared. The area has two sections. For our main course, we ordered Scotch fillet, roast potatoes (super crispy!), and grilled vegetables. Our steak was cooked to perfection, medium rare 👌, and of course, it's not a lovely dinner without their beautiful dessert. The one recommended for me had pumpkin in it, and it literally melted in my mouth. Not too sweet, but the delightful taste of it just lingered on my taste buds. It's one of the best desserts I've ever had.

Some may say the servings here can be quite small; however, there were three of us, and we ordered everything to try. It was all worth it, and I would definitively come back.

I highly recommend this restaurant if you visit Melbourne! 10 stars 🌟

Shout out to Fiona for being such an amazing waitress… she was attentive, warm, and genuinely lovely. The rest of the staff also made our night so memorable. When we mentioned it was a bit cold, they kindly turned the AC down for us without hesitation. It really speaks volumes about their customer service, truly second to none.
We had the steak the rib eye off the bone 700grams, the beef tartare .

Fantastic restaurant! Just wow! I'll preface by saying that the prices are expensive for very small portions that left me hungry. But the quality? Just wow... The oysters were fresh and the marinade was fantastic. The beef tartare - small, but fresh meat and really well balanced flavours. Even the bread and the butter was delicious! Great quality wines too. Interior is charming. Service: 100%! Only thing to make the place is better is to increase portions, because I was still hungry. But the flavour profile was fantastic!

name: Bodega Underground
location: 55 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Bodega Underground – What a fantastic experience!
From the moment we walked in, the service was absolutely excellent. The staff were warm, friendly, and genuinely attentive without being over the top — the kind of hospitality that makes you feel instantly comfortable and welcome.
The food? Simply superb. Every dish was packed with flavour, beautifully presented, and clearly made with care. You can really taste the quality and authenticity in every bite. It’s one of those places where you end up talking about how good each plate is while you’re still eating it!
The atmosphere was just as impressive — vibrant, relaxed, and full of great energy. Perfect lighting, great music, and a cozy underground vibe that makes it ideal for catching up with friends or enjoying a fun night out.
And honestly, for the quality of food, service, and overall experience, the prices are very reasonable. Great value for money.
Highly recommend Bodega Underground if you’re looking for amazing food, top-notch service, and a memorable night out. Definitely coming back!

Nice ambience with low lighting, intimate tables, music that is loud but not too loud, good service and nice drinks. Food is really not that good - fried cauliflower was oozing uncooked batter which was really off putting. The sauce had burnt bits from the pan which was quite unpleasant. The dinner plates and cups for water that they provided were plastic which is characteristic of a low caliber venue.

Before the visit, I was a little bit worried about the fact that some of the reviews were not very positive here. But I decided to have a bottomless brunch at Bodega and the food was great as well as the cocktails. All the staff were very attentive. Highly recommended especially on Friday since it is not that busy.

Loved this Mexican restaurant. It’s located in a basement and has such a cool vibe.
The tacos were delicious and surprisingly good. I’ll definitely come back next time to try the bottomless brunch!

Good for a date night not so much for a large group. Food is delicious the standout was the corn. We had the feed me menu. The churros was not good, it was a little bit to hard for me, not as a churros should be texture wise. Overall good experience.

name: Goldilocks Rooftop Bar
