location: Denyers, Building Level 5/264 Swanston St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Excellent service. The wait staff really made an effort to ensure we had a nice time. Views were lovely, cute happy plants around helped set an atmosphere. Oh and, my dear. The pickle Tommy’s marg was so respectful of the OG. Tasty fun.
One last note; the rickety elevator makes for a fun ‘back rooms’ vibe if you’re brining anyone from out of town. Id suggest taking the stairs all the way back down. The exit is a heavily graffitied alley that lands you at the entrance of section 88 (excellent bar)

Goldilocks Rooftop Bar is an absolutely wonderful spot in Melbourne! The rooftop offers a truly magnificent view over the city – the perfect backdrop for a relaxing evening.

The cocktails are simply outstanding – creative, beautifully presented, and, most importantly, delicious. You can really tell that the bartenders put thought and skill into every drink.

Service was quick, attentive, and genuinely friendly, which made the whole experience even better. Whether you’re looking for a place to enjoy a sunset drink, celebrate with friends, or just soak in the city atmosphere with a great cocktail in hand, this rooftop bar is a must-visit.

Highly recommended – a hidden gem that captures the best of Melbourne’s nightlife!

Unique Roof Top Bar with cosy Goldilocks 'Just right' atmosphere.
Hard to find on busy Swanston Street, but worth persevering  till you get to the roof. Take lift to 4th floor, and walk up the final flight of stairs to see the cityscape. 🌆
Constantly offering great Asian-Fusion treats, inventive cocktails and drink experiences. We tried the dumplings (Xiaolongbao) and wantons. The Espresso Martini rated high, as did the Maple Old Fashioned. We were tempted to try the 'Boozy Brunch' (all you can eat), however we ran out of time.
They were setting up to promote 'Melbourne Moonshine' this month.  We tried the 'Apple Pie Shine.' It's brilliant!
Frequent events are staged to keep you entertained and returning.
PS:  They can even cater for your function (call Meagan).

First time at Goldilocks. Getting there was slightly confusing (I saw a sign for Goldilocks but I think I was thrown off by the dodgy-looking, secluded lift next to Machi Machi on Swanston St). Once I was there, (lift to Level 4, then up 1-2 flights of stairs) the service from staff was great. A very popular spot, it seems. I think it's sensible that Goldilocks provides table service, as there would not be enough room for everyone to be moving constantly around. The little bear shot was such a cute touch. The baby bear cocktail was delish! I ordered dinner as well ('beef'/tofu, black bean and rice). Really lovely experience. Definitely worth another visit!

I really like the vibe, it’s pretty chill and easy to get to. The cocktails are great too, I ordered a matcha cocktail (can’t remember the name) but it was sweet and easy to drink. I recently went alone on a Tuesday night and didn’t need a reservation since it wasn’t busy. I actually tried going with my friends (3 of us) on a Thursday once, but it was full so we couldn’t get a table. Funny thing is, a couple came in after us and got a table even though they hadn’t booked, and the staff didn’t mention there were only seats left for two. Overall though, it was a really good experience. They also have quite a variety of promotions.

Amazing atmosphere.  Good was good. Our waiter was very patient and accommodating despite we were the only table with kids. Food options was tasty, although if you are bringing kids, there are not many options unless they can take spicy food. We asked the waiter to put the condiments on the sides so the kids ate the dish. They had the fried chicken bao and ordered second round so must be good!.

Great food! The flavours and the way they mix together were really good. We tried a range of the seafood and meat dishes and were very pleased with them. Atmosphere is amazing with the bar and music. It is a bit on the louder side but was still such a great vibe!

delicious - absolutely delicious 🤤

If you want silky and yummy wontons, this place is a must.

Was visiting Melbourne for the AO - got in late around 8:30pm, walked across from the place we were staying around 9:15pm and this place was PACKED - full of people, such a vibe and the food was delicious.

It was so good I came back the next day for some more barramundi and scampi wontons - the chilli and spring onion oil on them, amazing. So so so so good!!!

Highly recommend - wine list is fab too

Happy New Year! 🎊

Our first meal of 2026 was at the much-raved-about Lucy Liu Kitchen & Bar. Don’t be fooled by the dark, mysterious entrance which almost feels like you’re walking into a club. 😅 Inside, the swanky interior is surprisingly inviting, perfect for a cosy meal with the inner circle.

Lucy Liu’s fusion dishes lean towards semi fine-dining, paired with excellent table service from the attentive crew. We like the dumplings, soft shell crab wrap and green veg. The duck is a bit tough and the noodles are braised in the sauce that’s too sweet for our liking.

Overall, it’s a very nice place to visit on New Year’s Day. 🥳

#tiramisoooo #tramisoooojiaks #jiakjiakjiak #lucyliumelbourne #melbourne

Great service and staff. Food is Asian fusion Thai Chinese Viet, try their duck noodles yum! Takes allergies and notes very seriously. If you can get a booth at the back for groups of 6-8 it’s really good

I recently visited Patsys with two friends and we decided to share a selection of different dishes so we could try a bit of everything. The food was nice and well-presented, and it was great to sample a range of options. That said, nothing really stood out as memorable, and we left feeling a little underwhelmed overall - especially given the price.

On a much more positive note, the wine and cocktails were fantastic - definitely the highlight of the visit. The drinks menu is thoughtfully curated and everything we tried was spot on.

While I might not rush back specifically for dinner, I’d happily return for afternoon drinks and a nibble. Patsy's feels perfectly suited to more of a relaxed wine bar vibe.

We had a lovely evening of food, drinks & birthday celebration.
Great location, ambience, music & service.
The stand-out meals were polenta & blue cheese, the stuffed crepes & the creme brulee, but it was all delicious and well prepared.
I love the fact that they grow most of their produce on their farm.
We loved the music selection and the attentiveness of staff.
Thanks for a memorable birthday celebration.

Elegant vegetarian restaurant with warm, rustic‑modern interiors near Queen Victoria Market. I particularly enjoyed the roasted cabbage with hazelnut and mint was delicious and beautifully plated. Textures balanced perfectly: creamy base, crunchy nuts and fresh herbs. Warm service and thoughtful wine pairings made the experience enjoyable.

absolutely stunning meal that was incredibly delicious with a wide variety of tastes and textures. we had the tasting menu for 90AUD. they serve everything in a family style, so they require that everyone at the table has the tasting menu. it was well worth it, we had 4 appetizers, 3 main dishes, a side salad and 2 dessert for the 3 of us.

Best service we have had in a long time. Jonti and his partner (didn’t catch her name!) were both excellent - friendly, professional and warm. No detail was left unturned - waters were full, enthusiastic and genuine  chit chat throughout our sitting. They anticipated our needs and made us feel at home. Would be our go to spot if we actually lived in Melbourne. Cocktails and snacks are delectable.

Tried a few of the main meals which were not bad. Portion was great. Prices are between $20 plus to $40, depending on what mains you ordered. The fries are good, just small portion. The jap whisky is not bad ($9.80 per glass) but not for those who don't prefer it to be sweet. Did not order the sukiyaki and the range for 4 to 6 pay is between $300 plus to $500, depending on which sets you ordered. They have a 90 mins dinning rule and so that can be a rush if you are looking for a place to dine and chat with friends. The staff are all very friendly and helpful. Very crowded if you do not have a reservation and during dining time. I arrived before 5pm so got a table without reservation on a Friday night.

Went to Ikkai for sukiyaki and the wagyu quality really impressed me. Tried both M12 and M9 — the M12 was super rich and melt-in-your-mouth, while the M9 was still very tender but lighter, so it didn’t feel too heavy. The sukiyaki broth was well balanced, not overly sweet. Udon was chewy and comforting, and the fried sides (tempura prawns, karaage chicken, takoyaki) were all freshly made, crispy and not greasy. Overall a really solid Japanese meal with proper wagyu. Would definitely come back. ❤️❤️

Ikkai is a standout spot if you’re craving modern Japanese comfort food with great attention to detail. The menu is creative without being over the top, featuring dishes that are fresh, balanced, and full of flavor. Presentation is clean and thoughtful, and everything tastes like it’s been prepared with real care.

We ordered the sukiyaki set for two to three people with ribeye. We arrived without a reservation and waited around twenty to thirty minutes. Since it was the year end and very busy, we were prepared for the wait. A male staff member kindly thanked us for waiting and even offered two complimentary drinks, which was very thoughtful.

There was a bit of noise at another table, possibly due to a miscommunication, but it did not bother us. The same staff member later apologised to us, saying he was sorry if we were startled. Honestly, he seemed more apologetic than necessary, and we felt for him.

The food took a little while to come out, but the moment I tried the ribeye, my eyes lit up and my heart melted. The Delmonico was also delicious, but the ribeye was on another level and truly incomparable. The vegetables and udon served with the sukiyaki were also excellent and very satisfying.

My boyfriend is Japanese, and he was genuinely impressed, saying the quality was even better than what he usually has in Japan. If we were staying in Melbourne a bit longer, we would absolutely return without hesitation. Highly recommended.

Good place good vibes. Lots of Cantonese speaking people here reminds me of my time in Macau and HK. Chicken bowl is good but compared to the beef bowl it is worse. Beef bowl is excellent and good price. And service is amazing! I love the service people here. I am very picky when it comes to give reviews but this is a solid resturant.

Modern dining at its finest! – Best rooftop view I’ve had so far in Melbourne.

Fable Melbourne delivers an exceptional experience from start to finish. The ambiance is stylish and welcoming, with a warm, contemporary vibe that sets the tone for an unforgettable meal. Service was attentive and professional, making the evening even more enjoyable.

The food is where Fable truly shines:

🥩 Spiced slow-cooked lamb shoulder – paired with pea puree, sunflower & parsnip puree for a rich, balanced flavor profile.

🐟 Pan-seared barramundi – complemented by split caper cream, Jerusalem artichokes & cherry tomatoes for a fresh, vibrant finish.

🧀 Saganaki – perfectly grilled, paired with grapes, pekmez & roquette for a sweet-savory balance.

🍴 Tasting plate – featuring eggplant and zucchini arancini with pesto and nutritional yeast, plus a garlic lamb tenderloin skewer with tzatziki dip. Each bite showcased bold flavors and refined elegance.

Every dish was thoughtfully composed and visually stunning—a true feast for both the eyes and the palate.

Compliments to Chef Chase and Chef Michael for delivering such creativity and precision in every plate. Can’t wait to come back and experience it all over again!

Highly recommended for anyone seeking a memorable dining experience in Melbourne.

-The only small detail that saddened me was the box planter outside the window—the plant seemed to have died and wasn’t cared for. A little greenery, or simply removing it, would make the view even more perfect.

Our recent visit to Fable in the heart of Melbourne’s CBD was nothing short of spectacular. We were seated on the rooftop by the window, and the beautiful view down over the city skyline set the mood perfectly.

We dined as a group and shared a variety of dishes,  cocktails, skewers, risotto, cauliflower, scallops, and bread and dip, and every single item delighted. The cocktails were inventive and well crafted, while the food was flavourful and generous in portion. The risotto was creamy and rich, the scallops perfectly seared, the cauliflower had a brilliant texture and seasoning, and the skewers brought great flavour and presentation.

The ambience at Fable is stylish yet comfortable — you feel elevated (literally on the rooftop!) but not out of reach. The service was attentive without being overbearing, and staff made sure everything flowed smoothly. The setting truly felt special for a night out.

If you’re looking for a spot in Melbourne to relax with friends, enjoy share plates, sip refined cocktails and soak in an amazing view, Fable ticks all the boxes.

My cousin and I celebrated her birthday at Fable and had the chef’s set menu. It was a wonderful experience, each course was beautifully prepared and delicious.

The service was exceptional from start to finish. From the service to the food, everything was lovely. Neil took amazing care of us and went above and beyond by personalising my cousins  dessert with a birthday message.

It was such a thoughtful and memorable surprise. We’ll certainly be visiting again!

Beautiful rooftop terrace setting. We had a lovely spot under the sun on a spring day. The food was delicious, which was part of the bottomless brunch! Endless drinks and the staff were great. We chatted to the manager, who was really friendly. Definitely worth visiting! ☀️🥂🌸

Cute semi open roof top bar with lovely views. Have been here for evening drinks in the past, but did the bottomless brunch recently. AMAZING value, as the food was ample and delicious! Gin cocktails mostly and they allow two at a time, and a little flexible with the 2hr siting which was a pleasant surprise!

The one less star is for two reasons:
1. The venue needs a clean! The chairs had stains and I had never noticed these in the evening. There were drink marks all across the wall, and this at the very least could be easily cleaned.
2. Service was sllllooooow. Whilst they allowed 2 cocktails at a time, once we were done it was difficult to get their attention. The table next to us also complained about it, and were surprised as there werent too much tables occupied either. Regardless of speed, all staff were super lovely and were quickly forgiven.

Will definitely be back, and considering hosting our work function here too!

Outstanding Thai food from start to finish. Every dish we tried was beautifully presented and packed with flavour. The balance across the menu was impressive, rich curries with depth, fresh and vibrant salads, and perfectly cooked proteins. You can see from the plating and taste that a lot of care goes into both technique and ingredients.

The curries were a highlight, silky and aromatic without being heavy, and the fried dishes were crisp and well seasoned without feeling oily. The raw seafood dish was fresh, clean, and delicately dressed, showing confidence in the quality of the produce. Everything on the table worked together and nothing felt like a filler dish.

Service overall was warm and attentive, and the team clearly cares about the experience. The only feedback was when we asked for chillis and then chilli oil. We asked one staff member, then another, and finally the original person again, and it arrived about ten minutes later when we were close to finishing. That was quite annoying, I don’t know how this has been missed and what happens if there are other small requests…

That aside, this was an excellent meal. High quality food, great flavours, and a polished dining experience.

Thanks for a great night once again , our Waitress Efa was a delight, took great care of us all evening. Food was absolutely delicious. Ambience was sooo pretty. I met my husband here 19 yrs ago & we Always Love ❤️🥂🥂 coming here. Thanks again for a wonderful weekend 🥂🥂💘

We visited to celebrate my husband’s birthday and, despite all the great reviews, I personally feel there is room for improvement when it comes to the food.

We chose the banquet menu hoping to try a variety of dishes and enjoy a full meal, but the portions were extremely small for the price. We also found the food a bit greasy, and both my husband and I ended up drinking water throughout the night. It is possible that our food choices just were not the best for our taste.

However, the staff here were absolutely fabulous. They were warm, attentive and genuinely lovely to deal with. The atmosphere was also fantastic and really added to the experience. When we celebrated my husband’s birthday in late October, they even offered a free beer, which was a thoughtful touch. Their cocktails are also great.

Overall, the service and ambience were wonderful, but the food did not quite meet our expectations.

My wife and I normally go to The George if we are going to watch a show at the Regent or Comedy Theatre for an early dinner.
Atmosphere, restaurant / dining layout are great with very friendly assisting staff.
Food is first class, and as we were early (around 4ish) all we could order is their 'bites' selection as the dinner menu doesn't start until 5pm onwards.
We ordered 5x individual 'bite' dishes that were absolutely delicious and tasty....compliments to the Chef with his dish creativity..!
We will be back again soon and we highly recommend The George.

Absolutely mind blowing! By far one of the best restaurants in the city. I was truly impressed by our experience here. The staff are super friendly, kind, helpful and professional. We arrived they during the happy hour and all the drinks were amaizngly delicious and professionally made. Among the appetizers, we really LOVED roasted bone marrow and all the other mains were equally delicious - we had the steak and wayuga noodle. Couldn't point on one matter to improve as everything was perfect and I highly recommend here to everyone else.

name: Khaosan Lane
location: 350 Bourke St Shop G25 Melbourne GPO, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
We ordered all the dishes you see in the photos, and they were all very delicious. Just a heads-up: the food is spicy, so if you don’t like spicy food or have a sensitivity, make sure to let them know in advance.

The food was served very quickly. We also ordered dessert — a coconut-based dessert, which was absolutely amazing. The staff were very nice and friendly.

When we went, there was a long wait, and we had to line up. I would recommend booking a table in advance, as it’s definitely a better option

One of the best Thai restaurants in Melbourne — Khaosan Lane! ✨
I walked in hungry and walked out spiritually transformed.
First of all, their pork bone soup should honestly come with a warning label: “May cause instant happiness.” Rich, punchy, and the kind of flavour that makes you question every soup you’ve had before.
Then there’s the som tam. Oh. My. Goodness.
I ordered it twice… and yes, I finished both BY MYSELF 🤤
No regrets. Only pride.
The pork belly? Crunchy, fatty, and basically the hero we all deserve.
If it were socially acceptable to clap when food arrives, I absolutely would.
Will I come back? Of course. Try and stop me.
Plus, they have daily promos you definitely don’t want to miss, and the whole vibe feels like you’ve been teleported straight into a Thai night market—minus the humidity but with all the flavour.

Such a hidden gem in the city! Tucked away in a laneway, Khaosan Thai really feels like eating street food in Thailand.

We had the seafood salad, beef, pork neck, minced pork rice and the grilled hotpot combo — everything was so good. Super fresh ingredients and the sauces were next level, honestly couldn’t stop eating.

The only downside for me was the fried meat, which was a bit tough (still tasty, but my jaw got a workout 😆).

Great vibes, great food. Would 100% come back!

We are two girls, and we went to the restaurant at 6:50 PM on March 3rd, 2026.
Foods:
- Don Pork Loin (Tuesday $9.9): 9/10 (delicious, well-balanced flavor, slightly spicy, includes soy-marinated egg, good for a creamy texture)
- Big Mama Pork (Hot pot - $29.90): 8/10 (okay at first, but a bit too sour later, meatballs had a slightly gamey smell)
Vibe: 10/10, looks very much like Thai street food
The waiting time for food was a bit long. I know the restaurant is busy, but the wait for the Big Mama Pork was over 30 minutes.

● Dec 2025 ● 4 Stars 🌟🌟🌟🌟

This Thai restaurant in the alley was good. My friend and I were on a hunt to try new Thai restaurant in the city.

Food was good, quite tasty and authentic. I had crispy pork on rice. Gravy was delicious, crispy pork was average but the soup tasted very good too. It was quite busy around lunch time. Price is reasonable. You can get a meal combo with drink for around $20.

I'll definitely come back to try other dishes. Thank you for your service.

I went here to celebrate a big day with my partner, his sister and her husband. We are all in agreement that the food left us feeling disappointed.
We got the signature experience.
The starters were good however the scampi was so fishy, it was definitely not fresh, it has a super strong smell, and I'm a seafood lover myself.
Everything else was great until we got to the mains, we even paid extra to get the grilled Wagyu on the side. However the most disappointing thing was that every dish was super salty, and drowned in sauces.
Some dishes like the chicken wings got away with it, however the main course of the Wagyu steaks, the dry aged duck and the lamb was so drowned in sauces, it was impossible to finish.
It is a disservice to a piece of good meat to soak it in so much dressing that you cannot appreciate the taste of the meat itself.
And I can tell these are good cuts of meat, so it saddens me to see them being treated this way.
As we tried to pick at parts with minimal sauces, but it proves to be a challenge. Maybe it would've been better with sauces left on the side.
Maybe this is one way of selling more drinks but I don't believe this practice is sustainable.

While we had a good time, we left feeling disappointed, since we heard good things about this restaurant being one of the best in Melbourne. Definitely overrated

We celebrated my husband’s birthday at Maha Restaurant and had an absolutely wonderful experience!

From start to finish, the service, food, and drinks were all excellent. We enjoyed the cocktails, which were crafted perfectly, and opted for their Soufra menu. Every dish was a delight: the whipped hummus with heirloom tomatoes and roasted capsicums was incredible, the Lebanese garlic dumplings with smoky eggplant, pine nuts, and red pepper were amazing, and the rice was so flavourful. My husband especially loved the roasted lamb shoulder.

Everything was perfect, and we can’t wait to dine here again soon! Highly recommended.

Absolutely loved our lunch at Maha! We went there to celebrate our anniversary and the staff all went above and beyond to make it a special occasion. It is on the more expensive side but completely worth the price tag. The cocktails and Lebanese wine were fantastic, the food (we had the Soufra menu) was divine and generous portions, and the atmosphere and staff were both lovely. We already want to make a tradition of going there for our anniversary.

Today, I visited a restaurant confidently recommended by an Australian friend. The moment I walked in, I was struck by the lively atmosphere. The restaurant was already packed with people, and the overall vibe was incredibly hip and fantastic. 🤩
​In fact, I didn't even have to choose the menu. My friend took care of everything and ordered the course meal for us. And that choice was absolutely brilliant!

​With every dish that came out, I was so busy eating that I couldn't gather my senses due to the 'incredibly stunning and delicious' visuals and flavors. The delicate plating was a treat for the eyes, and the rich, exotic tastes filling my mouth satisfied my palate completely. Every course flowed in perfect harmony, making me feel like I had been treated to a true gourmet experience.

​All the wines we paired with the food were excellent, but the Raki was a first for me. It had a unique and appealing aroma and taste, and after a few glasses, it truly made me feel tipsy!

Thanks to the pleasant buzz, I enjoyed the meal even more.

​From the ambiance and the food to the new drinking experience! Everything about my time here 'was to my liking.' I completely understood why my Australian friend had recommended it with such confidence.

📍 Maha Bond St
Lunch at Maha Melbourne - Shane Delia is an incredible chef 😍

Bond St is a little land off Flinders St and has you push open the gold doors you enter down gorgeous stairs into a new world away from the hustle & bustle of the city

The staff are wonderfully friendly and explained the menu & wine. There we so many choices for good from à la carte to a couple of set menus. We chose to choose our dishes and we were absolutely blown away by the flavors

Caviar topped:
Charred king prawns, fiery eggplant coriander & mint tarts
Spanner crab sweet yoghurt & saffron and peas
Tuna arawma green tomato zhoug

Slow roasted lamb shoulder fig lemon & parsley with aged rice pilaf & a kale salad

I highly recommend Maha and I would definitely come here again

Came here on the weekend as a group of 6. While the food was pretty good (albeit definitely overpriced for what it is), the service was poor. Every time we needed anything we had to vigorously wave down the wait staff as there were clearly not enough of them on. The atmosphere was lovely and the decor really nice, but the service really let down the whole experience. I hope to return and have a better experience in the future. With saying all that, their signature passionfruit cocktail was amazing — highly recommend that!

Tippy Tay – Melbourne

We dined at Tippy Tay and chose the $70 per person “Feed Me” menu — an excellent decision. Every course was beautifully presented and full of flavour, showcasing the restaurant’s vibrant Italian flair.

The meal began with warm focaccia and creamy stracciatella paired with roasted asparagus and a sunflower seed pesto — absolutely delicious and a perfect start. The salt and pepper calamari fritti followed, crispy and tender, served with a punchy garlic aioli and fresh lemon that lifted the flavours beautifully. The cacio e pepe arancini were rich, cheesy, and perfectly seasoned, complemented by a touch of aioli and Parmesan.

Next came the Amatriciana rigatoni — cooked al dente with guanciale, tomato, basil, black pepper, and Parmesan — a comforting, classic dish done exceptionally well. The Padellino pizza with white anchovy, olive tapenade, and capers was small but packed with big, briny flavours. The pork cotoletta was juicy and crisp, paired with a smooth seeded mustard cream, and the insalata toscana was a refreshing finish — basil, Parmesan, and lemon vinaigrette tying everything together nicely.

The interiors are stunning — colourful, cozy, and full of personality, with that warm Italian energy that makes you want to linger. The service started a bit slow as we were left unattended at first, but once we were assigned a dedicated waitress, the experience turned around completely — she was attentive, friendly, and made sure everything ran smoothly.

Overall, Tippy Tay delivers a wonderful dining experience — delicious food, inviting atmosphere, and a menu that truly celebrates Italian comfort with flair. I’d happily return to try more of the menu (and that stracciatella again — it was perfect).

Food and Drinks are great. But there was one worker just minus all the points of the dining experience. She kept coming every 10 mins in our last 45 minutes of the booking, to tell us we need to leave at 7:30pm, and took our dishes away even there is still food on the dishes.

This is a perfect place to visit with family or friends. It truly didn't disappoint me in terms of food and ambiance. The atmosphere is very beautiful and lively, filled with vibrant colors. The food is excellent, with plenty of options to choose from. The pizza was the best I’ve had, and the tiramisu explained why it’s so highly praised and talked about. I’ll definitely go back just for the tiramisu! One thing to keep in mind is that it tends to get quite busy, so it's recommended to make a reservation in advance.

Was looking forward to visiting Tippy Tay but unfortunately the experience fell way below expectations. I’ll break down my experience below:

* LOCATION: The restaurant is located at the back of Garden State, and to enter, you must line up with the rest of the crowd getting their IDs checked. When I got to the front, I was then directed to a girl with an iPad who checked my booking then just told me to walk to the back where the restaurant is. This process felt like a waste of time. Also, when my sister directly approached the girl with the iPad, a bouncer aggressively asked for her ID despite the obvious that she had a restaurant booking. An unpleasant start to the evening to say the least.

* BOOKING: Upon reaching the restaurant, another waiter checked our booking and told us our table was not ready despite my sister getting a text at 7:40pm saying our table was ready. We then waited 20 mins for our table and we were seated at 8pm for a 7:30pm booking.

* SERVICE: One of the better elements of the night was that our main waitress was nice and attentive. She did accidentally get one of our orders wrong and apologised, although the waitress who brought out the food questioned us repeatedly as if we ordered wrong.

* FOOD: The quality of the food was not up to standard and not worth the expensive price. I have attached photos to back up all my comments. Entree - the focaccia was stale and definitely not fresh with an overly chewy crust. We were served 4 pieces without any accompanying olive oil, balsamic or butter. The olives were overly salty and sour. Mains - the mezze maniche pasta is supposed to be a large tube-shaped pasta but our plate came without a single piece of pasta in-tact. It looks like it was overcooked and all the pieces of pasta were broken into flat pieces. The wood fire pizza had large, burnt areas on the crust which a good wood-fire pizza should not have if it was cooked at the correct temperature. Additionally the bottom of the pizza was completely soggy, making for an unpleasant texture. Dessert - The tiramisu was fine but nothing to rave about.

One of the only positives about this establishment is the decor and buzzy vibe. I do not recommend visiting if you are after high-quality food that’s made with care and pride.

Eating here anytime is a good decision. But Thor's Day of Feasting is a must try. All you can eat mains, just re-order what you like off the set menu. The food here is exceptional and the cocktails are amazing. Been here a few times and it still is an exciting & dramatic experience. Staff are friendly with the Viking spirit on full display. Elegant dining and lovely Viking decor. You can also hold Thor's hammer for a photo op.
Food was great and fun experience overall. Nam was so lovely and provided amazing service for us!  He truly made the night special for me and my fiancé. Would recommend

This place is incredibly true to its concept, and the food is excellent. Drinking out of the horn-shaped cups was such a fun touch, and the fire show during service really added to the atmosphere! The lamb was cooked to perfection, and I highly recommend trying the Riskrem for dessert—it was unique and delicious.
It’s a bit of a bummer that the set menu is only for groups of 8+, but even ordering à la carte was a great experience. The staff were super friendly and helpful. We tried to walk in on our first night but they were fully booked, so they helped us set up a booking for later. I’d definitely recommend making a reservation to ensure you get a table!

The biggest highlight of our Melbourne trip. The atmosphere, food and service were so memorable.

It is truly an amazing experience I would encourage anyone in the CBD to book a table and go.

Loved it so much I ended up buying some horns from the place to take home with me.

Very accomodating for a nut allergy and specially bought a dessert out for my girlfriend as all the desserts on the menu had nuts in it. Service like this is unmatched. 100/10

Without a doubt the best food in Melbourne. Everything came together so beautifully, it was so apparent that all the staff care so much about the expierence they provide. It's clear that the chef is so passionate about his food as it all was very intentional and well crafted that it was hard not to rant and rave over every bite; the floor staff are also so attentive, we were served by Noah who was keen to talk us through the menu and give us plenty of recommendations throughout our evening. Noah also lead us in a toast to the appropriate Norse Gods which was a big boost to the immersion! The complimentary shots were a lovely touch to get us started and we just kept getting blown away from there. We're both devastated that we dont live in Melbourne but we will recommending the restaurant to anyone who will give us an ear and- until you open a venue in Adelaide- we'll be seeing you every time we make the trip again 🥰❤️🩷thankyou again xx

I enjoyed my experience at the supernormal for the most part. 3.5 stars. At first we were seated at the edge of the bar there was a dead bug even though we booked month in advanced. Luckily there was a spare table so we could switch. If that hadn’t had happened I think it would have made my experience quite poor as the seating was so uncomfortable plus the fact that there was a dead bug on the table so the experience would have been 2 stars instead of 3.5. The concept is there overall but there was some poor execution elements. The waiter tried his best even though there were some improvements but I will leave it it wasn’t a big deal. The food however, tasted quite bland and I was really let down by the lobster roll. I had it a few years ago and not sure if my taste buds had changed but it was so mediocre. The other food was also bland, the highlight was the fish and the dumplings but it was nothing to make me go wow I need to come back. Overall I would recommend people to try it once especially if you like asian fusion that is a bit blander side maybe if your an older couple you may enjoy this or don’t enjoy authentic asian but still like flavours. However, I won’t be in a rush to come back.

*An evocatively Supernormal dining experience*

🍽️Supernormal, well located along Flinders Lane, offers superb masterful inventive Asian fusion cuisine ideal for special occasions or a refined lunch or dinner from the start to the finish.

Absolutely a delight from the moment you walk in.  Every experience from the beginning is top-notch. Every dish is a unique discovery. The affable and knowledgeable serving staff is professionally helpful.  The modern and spacious restaurant makes your social or family connections effortlessly warm and cozy.

Let your tastebuds be spolt by its uniquely curated (primarily Chinese/Japanese/Korean focus) menu. Its iconic New England lobster rolls, the best I have eaten, is irresistibly delicious. The flavorful duck bao is another unforgettable standout item. Another classic highlight is the
slow-cooked tender lamb shoulder. It’s richly packed with right balance of flavor. Gippsland striploin well seasoned with sancho pepper served with wild cress and yuzu mustard is incredibly tasteful.

Supernormal offers the unforgettable dining experience that you look forward to the next return trip😋

I had an incredible evening at Supernormal in Melbourne. The vibe is fantastic—stylish, relaxed, and full of energy. The food was top-notch: the saltwater duck with peaches was a 5 out of 5—absolutely exquisite. The raw snapper & roast scallop was also excellent, 4 out of 5 for me ! To top it all off, I had a glass of PYREN Earthscape wine that was recommended to me by the amazing Anna —perfection, another 5 out of 5. And I have to give a huge shoutout to Anna—her service was out of this world. Supernormal is a must-visit for anyone who loves great food and impeccable service. I’ll be back for sure!

Love this place. Great service and food. You can sit at the large bar and watch the kitchen staff, so it's perfect for solo travelers. They also can make most decisions half-portion so you can try more stuff.

I had the brown butter scallop, lobster roll, lamb shoulder + eggplant, and peanut butter parfait. Everything was great but the lobster roll was probably my least favorite. I probably my least favorite. Kinda wish I had gone with a dumpling or bao bun. The lamb shoulder was the star of the show. The meat was tender and juicy and it was swimming in sauce and spices. Get a side of rice to soak up the sauce. Peanut butter parfait was a nice light dessert to finish off. 10/10 recommend this place!

This was my second visit. The first visit was a two years ago. Everything was perfect. Not normal at all. Worth of 5 star. But this time, I felt quite disappointed. It turned from excellent to really supernormal. We were placed in concern seat not sit side by side despite almost empty in bar table. Good oyster and duck dao buns but quite poor ribeye, lobster roll.

Overall a very nice dining experience! We did the 5 share plates deal which gave us a nice taste of lots of things and it was clever how the kitchen portioned it for just the two of us. It was also enough food as we left very satiated.
My favourite was definitely the bao and crying tiger beef. Dishes like the chicken curry, crispy barramundi and the prawn toast needed a little more flavour and spices, felt like they were missing something and just a bit bland for what I'd expect for the price point.

They also forgot to give us rice and we only realised towards the end of our meal when we had to ask for some. Not a huge deal, just would have been disappointing if we hadn't gotten any!

Our server was lovely and explained the menu well to us so we understood. We really appreciated being told what was in the dishes as the staff gave them to us. There's a fun chill vibe to the place overall.

We also really enjoyed the house made sodas, the Cucumber lemon grass was so refreshing and light. The salted peach was good but could also use more spice, it just had a very faint warm hum instead of tasting any actual chili.

Overall it was an easy, pleasant experience, just needs a little more spice and flavour!

Asian fusion restaurant with a great set menu!

Came here to celebrate my birthday and did the banquet menu for $69pp with 6 dishes. The amount of food was so generous and we were stuffed by the end! Drinks and the atmosphere were also nice. It was really loud so a bit hard to talk but the restaurant was very busy so that’s explainable.

Rice Paper Scissors has a great vibe with its modern, casual atmosphere.
The staff are friendly, and the food is fresh, flavorful, and well presented. I really enjoyed the crispy coconut cup.

We were seated quite quickly, due to having a reservation. We ordered food soon after and the food arrived within 20 minutes, even though the restaurant was in its dinner rush. The food tasted absolutely amazing and there were quite a few options. Overall, it was a superb experience.

One of the best bites I’ve had in 2025. The menu is very unique and the atmosphere is fun. Everything about this place is amazing. My wife is gluten free and almost the entire menu suited her. We ordered the 6 plates.

Must try is the Coconut shells. They will knock your socks off.

We had a wonderful dining experience overall. The atmosphere was elegant and very comfortable, perfect for a date or a special occasion.

The portion sizes were actually quite generous for a fine dining menu — by the time the main course arrived we were already very full, which was a pity because we didn’t have room for dessert. The duck was absolutely outstanding, beautifully cooked and full of flavour. I was too full to finish it and had to take the rest away, but it was so good that I didn’t want to waste a single bite.

The foie gras was a real highlight — incredibly smooth, rich and very well-portioned.

The beef tartare was slightly less impressive to me. The prawn cracker didn’t feel like the best match, and there was a bit too much spring onion, which somewhat covered the natural flavour and texture of the beef. I personally prefer a more classic and slightly more raw style, where the quality of the meat can really shine.

Service was warm and professional. I would recommend arriving a bit earlier, as it did get noticeably busier later on and the staff became slightly stretched.

Overall, a very enjoyable experience. I would happily return for the foie gras, the duck and the beautiful setting.

We went to this restaurant a few days ago for my husband’s birthday and it’s truly a beautiful place.
The staff are attentive, polite, and very friendly. The sommelier is extremely knowledgeable and lovely, and she helped us choose the perfect wines for each course.
The food was absolutely amazing! We loved the oyster selection, the crab crumpet and the scallops! honestly so good I could eat a thousand of them😂 and the Wagyu, which was outstanding.
Every dish we tried was excellent.
We also loved the ice cream, where you can add a variety of delicious toppings.

Exceptional! The interiors and vibe is stunning. Food was superb. We had the steak tartare, smoked king ora salmon, duck, 800g rib eye, mash with marrow and asparagus goat cheese. Spent about $500 for the two of us, included a few drinks. Couldn't fault a single dish. Service was friendly and not too pretentious. It's a very fancy place but also felt quite relaxed! Highly recommend!

I can't think of anything I can fault in my years of fine dining. From the food to the atmosphere and the care... It's genuinely unbelievable.

The service in particular was something that I have not experienced in so long. Every staff member was sharp, knew what they were talking about and evidently took a lot of care in what they do.

They did make one mistake in that they brought out one of the wrong side and dispite me saying it was okay, the manager insisted that they get the right one and also didn't charge for either. Stellar care for their diners and just stunning, world class service.

We went to celebrate Lunar NY and you can see the dessert having that on it. One of those little touches that will turn a good restaurant to something that you will never forget.

I am usually very critical and honestly, I can't find a single thing that I can judge here. You may consider it pricey but when you consider everything that you get, I think it was more than reasonable.

Food wise, my highlights were the steak, the dessert, and dear god the bread with the burnt honey butter was something to die for. Oysters were good and the bone marrow pomme puree made me question any mash potatoes that I have ever made. Stunning.

If you want something fancy or are wanting to treat your special someone, let's put it this way, the only place that has impressed me more in Melbourne is Attica and as far as high praise goes, I don't think I can give any higher than that.

Bravo RR. I will absolutely be back and your work was kind, excellent and efficient.

Fine dining restaurant with instagrammable interior. Entrance is a small door in the old ANZ Gothic Bank building next to the ANZ museum on Collins St. Featuring neo-Gothic architecture, stained glass windows and dimly lit lights.

The food:

Beef tartare - very well minced and flavourful, well seasoned. 4.5/5

Scallops - tastes like butter chicken sauce with scallop instead. Very interesting and new take. 4/5

Smoked ora salmon - tasted good but didn’t feel like it was special. Creamy. Tender and well seasoned, the biscuit had lots of roasted spices and was good at mopping up the diced salmon. 3.5/5

Duck- very juicy and tender, perfectly cooked pink in the breast. Honey glaze makes the skin slightly crispy. If you eat the breast with the jus (the chefs recommendation is to eat it alone) it tastes even better, perfect combination of umami, slightly salty and sweet. The little black berry and cabbage garnish is also great to eat a piece of duck with, a splash of sweetness on a background of savoury and umami. 5/5

The pomme puree - mash with bone marrow - extremely smooth mash with extra decadence from the marrow, perfect complement for any protein. 5/5

John Dory - I would’ve liked it slightly softer and flakier. The smoked mussels are a great garnish and the creamy sauce complements the fish well. Would’ve also liked the piece to be slightly larger, since it is fish, it’s not as dense as other meats. 4/5

The service:

Very friendly and attentive water refills, explained the menu well.

Bathrooms also relatively clean as expected for a fine dining restaurant.

name: Palermo
location: 401 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: We hosted our company Christmas party here and had a wonderful experience. The set menu was very good, and sharing the dishes worked surprisingly well. The highlights were definitely the dulce de leche for dessert and the incredibly delicious tuna sashimi. Overall, the atmosphere feels very authentic—you can smell the freshly grilled meat, which adds to the experience. The waitstaff were very attentive and professional throughout the evening. Prices are on the higher side, but the quality and ambiance justify it.

I had a lovely New Year meet-up with a friend at this restaurant, and it was such an enjoyable experience. I ordered my steak medium rare, and it was honestly the first perfectly cooked medium-rare steak I’ve had in Melbourne — I was truly impressed. The fish was also absolutely delicious.

The broccoli salad we ordered with the sauce paired beautifully with the dishes and complemented the flavours really well. On top of the amazing food, the staff were incredibly friendly and attentive, which made our evening even more enjoyable.

We had a wonderful dinner and a great time overall. Highly recommended!

Amazing food. Steak was tender and all the sides were amazing. Great service and good cocktails. Spend under $300 for 7 different items incl a fine cut of steak and 4 drinks between us. Amazing price point for the quality and quantity of food and drinks. Will be back for the Bistecca!

Had an incredible experience at Palermo. The food was absolutely smashing — I ordered the Focaccia and the Entraña, and both were packed with flavour. The steak especially stood out; the texture, seasoning, and overall balance were easily some of the best I’ve had. A genuinely impressive dining experience for anyone who loves quality steakhouse dishes.

The only downside was that service — especially calling in and getting attended to — took a bit longer than expected. Because of that, it’s a solid 4/5 from me.

Still, the food alone makes it worth the visit. I’d definitely recommend checking out Palermo if you’re in the area.

Absolutely loved this place! The food amazing! Literally best Filet I’ve ever had! Everything was mouthwateringly delicious. The sides, service, cocktails and wine! If you want the best steak you’ve ever had in your life come here!

name: Kiss & Tell
location: Level 1/37-41 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Celebrated my birthday here and had an amazing time. The atmosphere was lively and welcoming, and the service was fantastic. Our waitress was incredibly helpful with both food and drink recommendations, which made the experience even better.

The food and drinks were delicious, and the portions were very generous—especially the entrees. We were so full that we didn’t even have room for dessert! The cocktails were made right in front of us and tasted incredible, which added a fun touch to the night.

I’d definitely recommend this place for a special occasion or even a fun dinner date.

So flavorful!  Kiss & Tell was one of the highlights of our recent trip to Australia.  We ordered their tasting menu which includes 2 small dishes, 2 large dishes, and 1 side for a minimum of two people, for A$50 per person.  For the appetizers (small dishes), we ordered the Pork & Prawn Dumplings and the Lobster sliders.  Both were exceptional.  For our entrées, we selected the Vietnamese Braised Pork and the Fried Barramundi.  The braised pork was our favorite dish, so amazingly tender and flavorful.  As a side, the Coconut Rice was an absolute winner.  Only the Chocolate Brownie was average.  Kiss & Tell has been one of the best Asian Fusion experiences that we’ve had in the recent past.  Highly recommended!

I visited this restaurant for Valentine’s Day and chose their special Valentine’s menu. The service was attentive and friendly throughout the evening. Most dishes were delicious and well-prepared, highlighting the chef’s skill and creativity. The barramundi was the only dish that felt a bit underwhelming in flavor compared to the others. The ambiance was cozy and romantic, making it a great spot for special occasions. Overall, it was a memorable dining experience that I would gladly recommend for celebrations or intimate dinners.

Did the Valentine’s Selection and loved everything about it! Food was so so yum, price was appropriate for the amounts you receive, waitress was bubbly and attentive, and the whole vibe of the place was lovely. Thanks for having us and treating us well this Valentine’s!

Another Asian fusion restaurant worth visiting!

We had the tasting selection 2 small, 2 large, and 1 side. I highly recommend the salt and pepper squid it’s caramelised and really delicious, we both loved it! For the large dishes, the Vietnamese braised pork was our favourite well-cooked and tender. The portions were great for two people and not too expensive. Me and my partner always share, so it was just right for us.

As someone with a sweet tooth, I couldn’t resist trying the coconut pudding from their dessert menu and who could say no to that? Overall, it was a great experience. The staff were friendly and attentive, and you can see their teamwork with how quickly and smoothly they work together. 🫶🏻

name: Tipo 00
location: 361 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
This has been on my list for years, so I was excited to finally visit on a beautiful summer day for lunch. Overall, the experience was largely very positive, though the service didn’t quite live up to the expectations I had for a venue of this calibre. Given how popular and well-regarded Tipo 00 is, I was anticipating a warmer, more attentive welcome. Nothing overtly negative happened, but the initial greeting felt a little abrupt — being asked to wait outside rather than welcomed in wasn’t the best first impression. That said, the service did improve as the meal went on, particularly when we were looked after by one of the male staff members, who was far friendlier and more attentive.

Dining as a pair, we decided to treat ourselves. We started with a glass of red wine each and a serve of oysters, followed by complimentary focaccia with ricotta on the side — a lovely touch. For mains, we ordered the spaghetti with heirloom zucchini, lovage and pistachio, the gnocchi di patate with wild boar ragù, saffron and broad beans, and planned to share a third pasta: the tagliolini al nero with squid ink, squid and bottarga.

When we mentioned we were sharing the squid ink dish, the waiter suggested bringing it out as an entrée — and it arrived thoughtfully split into two bowls, which we really appreciated. Of all the pastas, the squid ink tagliolini was the clear standout. Unique, perfectly balanced and executed beautifully, it completely stole the show. I’ll absolutely be returning just for that dish alone.

Despite the slightly underwhelming start, the food is undeniably exceptional and very much lives up to the hype.

Tipo 00 is a very hard restaurant to book, as it always seems to be fully booked, so I was quite curious to finally try it. Overall, the experience was good but didn’t fully live up to the hype for me.

The restaurant itself was nice, though not as fancy as I had expected. On a positive note, the service was very good—friendly, attentive, and professional throughout the meal.

The food was nice, but nothing particularly amazing to justify the level of hype or the price point, in my opinion. My pasta, unfortunately, was not very well presented and arrived not very hot, which was a bit disappointing.

All in all, a good meal with great service, but I was expecting a bit more given its reputation.

I started with the non-alcoholic Miele e zenzero. It was honey forward but well balanced, with a buttery smoothness that paired perfectly with the ginger.

The meal kicked off with the free bread and ricotta. The bread was soft with just enough crust, but that creamy ricotta was the real star. It left me on a cliffhanger for more.

The Grilled Ox Tongue was incredible. I couldn’t get enough of the thin slices with crispy edges and soft middles. The sweet balsamic glaze and pink peppercorns made me want to lick the plate.

For the main, the Tagliolini al nero was to die for. Wowzers. The pasta texture was perfect and the sauce was a buttery umami bomb with a spicy kick. The squid was soft and delicious. This was definitely the highlight for me. It’s also worth noting they have gluten-free spaghetti and gnocchi available.

The Cherry, Almond & Purple Basil Cheesecake was a unique take to close the night. The salt hit me first, then the cherry sweetness and almond, and then the creamy cheese. It was an unforgettable ending.

We had late lunch and the atmosphere was joyful. The zucchini flowers were melt in your mouth delicious. We ordered the scampi paccheri and the braised duck pappardelle and we devoured both dishes. To describe this food is it will take you to Italy and you will feel like you are being served a home cooked meal. Put this on your bucket list to come and enjoy some amazing Melbourne food.

My family and I were in Melbourne for a week and there were no reservations but we were lucky enough to nab a table as a walk in and we were not disappointed. Tipo00 lives up to the hype. We had a great waitress from NZ. The food oh the food. It was faultless. It was delicious. The flavours were incredible. I love this place. The chef's really know what they are doing and that reflects in the dishes. The produce they used was excellent. For such a small kitchen the dishes are superb. This place deserves the excellent reputation it has. Thank you Tipo00. You more than hit the spot x

We loved our experience at Navi. All the food was incredible and the staff welcoming. Able to accommodate a pescatarian diet easily. Great touch the Head Chef bringing over the first and last dishes. A wonderful special event meal.

Navi has been on our list, and we finally did it! We celebrated our anniversary on Saturday night. From the outside, you’d have no idea of the beauty inside — gorgeous, intimate ambience with the perfect lighting and noise level.

Every dish was different and a delight — beautifully crafted and full of variety of flavours. Just when we thought we’d picked a favourite, another dish topped it. The standouts for me were the black garlic and salmon roe macaron (wow!) and the elements of cornfed duck — cooked to perfection and simply delicious. Oh and the venison too👌

Atmosphere and food aren’t complete without great service, and we can’t fault Navi on that. The staff were well-presented, pleasant, and knowledgeable. What stood out was the team approach: we were served by 5-6 people, including chefs — something quite unique compared to other places. It made the experience special and showed the consistency in their service.

Thank you, team Navi, for making our first visit to celebrate our anniversary memorable. We will definitely return!

It was an exceptional dining experience. Exceptional service!! We ordered the set menu, which offered an impressive variety of dishes. Each course was visually stunning, and every dish suited our palate perfectly, innovative yet refined.

Great experience for the past few times we visited in 2025, my husband and I enjoyed their seasonal menu and the beautiful flavors chef has created.

Loved the herbal tea we got last time too. The only fine dinning restaurant we kept and will keep going back!

Absolute gem. The head chef cooks for you right in front of your eyes. They explain every dish, the flavours are amazing, and the house is warm, quiet, and comfy. Pure joy for people who love to eat. Do yourself a favor and go for it.

name: Vue de monde
location: 55 Rialto Towers, 525 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Wow! What an amazing,exceptional dining experience. A perfect way to celebrate our anniversary. Everything was outstanding from the staff- shout out to fellow Kiwi Anika,to the food and views. We loved the attention to detail,from the cutlery resting on small stones or driftwood to the sublime presentation of each course. The tour of the kitchen and helping create part of one course was fantastic! Thank you everyone at Vue de Monde for a truly memorable experience! Vanessa & Tim(NZ)

Vue de Monde offers beautifully crafted food with a strong focus on technique, balance, and presentation. Each course was thoughtfully executed, with clean flavours and a clear sense of refinement. The open kitchen adds to the experience and really highlights the precision behind every dish.

The cheese platter stood out in particular—excellent selection, perfectly matured cheeses, and well-matched accompaniments that elevated the whole course. Service was polished yet warm, and the pacing of the meal felt relaxed and intentional. While the food leans more toward classic fine dining rather than pushing boundaries, the overall experience was elegant, consistent, and very enjoyable. A memorable evening with food that’s confidently done and genuinely delicious.

I wanted to start my 2026 reviews with a bang… and this is it. We went for lunch at Vue de Monde in November 2025, and I genuinely still struggle to articulate just how extraordinary it was. This wasn’t just a long lunch - it was a once-in-a-lifetime experience. The kind that becomes a core memory.

From the moment you arrive, high above Melbourne with those sweeping skyline views, you know you’re somewhere exceptional. The space is dramatic yet refined - luxurious without feeling intimidating. The team were warm, polished, and effortlessly professional, striking that rare balance between world-class precision and genuine hospitality.

The food was phenomenal. Each dish looked beautifully restrained and deceptively simple, but when we spoke to the staff about the construction behind them, it was mind-blowing to hear the level of technique, time, and preparation involved. The precision, balance, and clarity of flavour were just on another level.

What truly elevated the experience were the moments beyond the table. We were given a tour of the kitchen, had the opportunity to create a dish inside the kitchen ourselves, and finished the afternoon with a photo alongside the chefs and front of house team - something I’ll genuinely treasure.

And as if that wasn’t enough, a lovely final touch was receiving a follow-up email detailing everything we ate and drank. It brought the whole experience full circle and allowed us to relive it again afterwards - such a thoughtful gesture.

Vue de Monde isn’t simply a restaurant. It’s theatre, artistry, hospitality and memory-making wrapped into one unforgettable afternoon. This is one experience you absolutely cannot miss. And for me, one I will truly never forget.

So we were celebrating our 25th Anniversary and decided to treat ourselves. Unfortunately we were very disappointed. The Chefs tasting menu whilst was visually pretty the taste for us just was not there, the flavours were over complicated and did not treat the taste buds, and 14 small portions later we left feeling very disappointed in our $1002.00 bill which included only 2 beers and 2 Mocktails.
The flavours were too sickly sweet and contrasted with bland in each dish, there wasn’t one of the servings that we were happy with.
The venue was stifling hot, appreciate that it was a hot day, but you would expect the air conditioning to work in a building 55 floors up in a fine dining restaurant. The view is amazing except for the dirty windows you look through, and it was very loud in the restaurant with every now and then all the chefs yelling “yes chef” throughout the evening.  Not what I would call a fine dining experience. The staff were lovely but we couldn’t hear them when they would serve the food and explain what we were eating because it was so loud in the restaurant.
Perhaps fancy isn’t for us. We would not return due to the experience of the food and the expense for 2 people.
Sorry Vue de monde!

Highly recommend for any special occasions. The experience starts from the moment you enter the foyer at street level where you are greeted and it continues as you take the lift to the 55th floor. The views are spectacular looking out to Melbourne high above the city at sunset.
The wait staff were truly amazing and really did everything to break the mold of robot waiters,they added their own personal touch which was well received. The dining area was spacious and left the experience very intiment.
The food was a absolute culinary masterpiece. The flavors and the textures and unique paintings of some foods left an orgasmic explosion in your mouth. We celebrated our 40th birthdays at Vue we made the most perfect choice of restaurants for this momentous occasion.
The chefs tour of the kitchen added a whole new element to the experience, With Wealth of knowledge and overall experience every section of the kitchen was explaon in great detail by the Sous Chef who was a very funny yet very professional man.
We enjoyed a photo with the chefs and the topped out the experience for us it was beautiful. After this we returned to our table to find beautiful personalized birthday cards signed by the staff. It was truly a night and experience that we will remember forever and pass on the message to as many people as possible. Highly recommended

name: Meatmaiden
location: Basement/195 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: It was in a dimly lit downstairs basement. Very vibrant and alive inside. The waiter Charlie was great, cheerful, polite and knowledgeable. We had the Maidens mood, a seven course set menu. All seven courses were appealing to the eye, and taste buds. Even though I don't normally like fish or seafood, it tasted good. It was not cheap.

My husband and I traveled to Melbourne and booked this fantastic restaurant in advance. It didn't disappoint us in any way.

Because we had trouble choosing, we opted for Maiden's Mood. From the bread to the lobster and cheese macaroni, every dish was delicious. The beef tartare and scallops were especially amazing; the beef was uniquely seasoned and incredibly tender—the most delicious I've ever had. The scallops seemed to be grilled with bacon and a sweet and savory sauce, which was my favorite dish. Of course, the steak didn't disappoint either.

This dinner was definitely worth the price!

Travelling solo for my Birthday and I had booked this table months ago. As soon as I walked in I was greeted. The room was a great temp and the tables were well spread out and not sat on top of each other. Staff were super friendly and happy to answer any questions that I had.

Had the bone marrow to start, was not alot of marrow on it so the crisps could had had bone marrow butter on them but the topping was tasty.

I tried a different cut of meat to what I normaly get and was pleasantly suprised at the flavour, very well cooked and so tasty. It was the Stone Axe Waygu Flank cooked medium as recommended. Paired with the charred greens.

Had a the creme caramel to finsh and it was delish. Cocktails were perfect. Highly recommend.

My server (Charlie) was quick and very helpful and such a lovely person that it made the whole dinner a great vibe. The whole staff that I encountered were happy  and gave great service.

Absolutely delicious… Best steak in Melbourne, no question.
Why did I only discover this place just now..?
Out of all the restaurants I’ve been to, this place has one of the most convincing chef’s selections — literally all the best dishes in one place.
The bread, brisket, and steak were particularly remarkable and highly recommended.
Service was also excellent; our server was genuinely attentive and warm.

This meal was outstanding from start to finish. The steak was one of the best I’ve ever had, perfectly cooked and incredibly tender. The octopus was melt-in-your-mouth tender, and the BBQ burnt ends were pure perfection—sweet, smoky, and rich with an unbelievable texture. Even the bread deserves special mention; it was the best I’ve ever tasted.
The service was just as exceptional as the food. Every detail was handled with care, and the interactions with the chefs and bartenders felt personal and genuine, adding so much to the overall atmosphere. The combination of incredible food, warm hospitality, and an inviting ambiance made this experience truly unforgettable. Overall, absolutely amazing. Would fly from Perth just to eat here again!!!

Chin Chin absolutely lives up to the hype. The food was fantastic, full of flavour and beautifully presented. We went with the chef’s curated menu, which was a great way to experience a variety of dishes without having to decide. Every course felt thoughtfully curated and perfectly paced.

The atmosphere is vibrant and energetic. The music, lighting and buzz of the restaurant create a really fun dining experience that feels very Melbourne. It’s lively without feeling chaotic.

Service was smooth and attentive throughout the evening, and the staff did a great job guiding the flow of the Feed Me menu. Overall, a great spot for a night out with friends or a special dinner. Would happily come back again.

Nice food and great atmosphere. Dinner time is extremely crowded and if you like crowd, and music then this place is for you!

This Asian themed restaurant has a hippy environment and service staff are very enthusiastic and polite. Service was great and upon us providing feedback that the food served were not warm, they immediately bring them back and ensure our meals are served warm.

Oysters: pretty average I feel. Ok to skip

Yellow curry-spiced fried rice: nice when it’s hot and the flavour is just right. Crab meat is added too to enhance this dish

BBQ lamb: awesome and the meat would drop off easily from the bone when you cut it.  It’s well seasoned and we love it very much

We also ordered a few pint of beer and sat at their corner counter seat. The only one feedback is that it was freezing cold at the corner of their bar counter seating. Their big air conditioning fan is positioned in a way to blow at that corner bar counter direction - way too freezing cold. That’s the only downside you need to note.

$89 per head banquet is the best value here and staff are flexible on whats on offer based preference or allergies. For 3 people we were full and couldn't finish the finish dish but if we were still hungry they said they'll bring out more free of charge. The deserts were okay and service was attentive.

Went there for a late lunch as this place comes highly recommended. At 3pm on a Tue, the restaurant was almost empty, with only two tables occupied. I asked for a table for 3, but the manager (who appeared to be the senior staff member) seated us at a tiny table that could barely fit two. When I requested one of the empty window tables, he dismissed it nonchalantly, saying, “Those are for 4,” even though they were all unoccupied. His snobbish attitude really put me off and completely ruined the mood.

As for the food, it didn’t taste authentic Asian—which is fine since it’s meant to be “fusion”—but everything was far too sweet for my liking. The only saving grace was the nice ’80s music they played. To be fair, aside from the manager, the other waiters were pleasant and provided good service.

That said, I won’t be returning. Melbourne has no shortage of genuinely good Asian restaurants to choose from.

Outstanding!
Chin Chin on Flinders Lane was an absolute win. We turned up on a busy Friday night with no booking and honestly didn’t expect much luck, but the guy at the door was brilliant. Super helpful, genuinely empathetic, and went out of his way to sort us out with a table at short notice. That kind of service immediately puts you in a good mood before you’ve even sat down.

Now the food… wow! The caramelised sticky pork was ridiculous in the best possible way. Crunchy on the outside, beautifully tender in the middle, packed with flavour and just the right level of spice. I genuinely couldn’t stop smiling while eating it and didn’t want the plate to end. One of those dishes you keep thinking about after you’ve left. For me personally, the best dish I’ve had in Melbourne.

The crispy barramundi with green apple salad was also excellent. Fresh, light, and perfectly balanced. The spring rolls deserve their own mention because they were some of the best I’ve ever had: crisp, flavourful, and seriously addictive.

Even my daughter cleaned up her prawn egg noodles, which says a lot.

The atmosphere was buzzing without being overwhelming and was loud enough to feel fun, relaxed enough to enjoy the night. Classic Chin Chin energy, and for good reason. We’ll absolutely be back when we visit Melbourne again.

name: Osteria Ilaria
location: 367 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Food is superb, every dish is well put together and we enjoy them all. Even the asparagus vegetable dish can taste so good. Great service with good recommendation and portion for sharing by 4 people. Great ambience but do find the noise level bit much.

A reliable osteria dishing out traditional Italian fare in highly skilled and contemporary execution. It looks great, it tastes better. The drinks and the wine list are on point, the service is excellent, and the vibe is just right. And it’s often possible to get a seat at the bar without a booking even if the place is always bustling.

Really good food. I particularly enjoyed the ravioli with truffle  rich, fragrant, and perfectly balanced without being overpowering. The kingfish was also outstanding, exceptionally fresh with clean flavours and a refined presentation. Overall, a great dining experience and definitely a place I would recommend.

Beautiful tucked away hidden gem in Melbourne city little Bourke St.  Food was delicious I had the spanner crab meal.  Our waitress kept coming up and asking if we were ready to order too much and gave our bill when we didn’t even ask for it- we felt pushed a little.    But overall our meal was 10/10.  Will definitely be back.

We dined at Osteria Ilaria, and it’s easy to see why this restaurant is consistently busy—booking ahead is not optional.

From the moment you arrive, the experience feels well considered. Sitting at the bar for a pre-dinner cocktail before moving to the table is a great way to ease into the evening and sets the tone for what follows. The atmosphere is lively and energetic without being overwhelming, striking that ideal balance of buzz and comfort.

The wine program is another standout. The sommelier is genuinely knowledgeable and approachable, helping guide you to the right wine (or wines) to suit both your meal and your price point. The list is broad and thoughtfully curated, offering plenty of choice without feeling daunting.

Ultimately, though, the food is where Osteria Ilaria truly excels. The dishes are beautifully thought through, confidently executed, and presented with care—but never at the expense of flavour. Everything tastes as good as it looks, with a clear focus on quality ingredients and balanced, satisfying cooking.

Osteria Ilaria is a restaurant that delivers on all fronts: atmosphere, service, wine, and most importantly, exceptional food. A standout Melbourne dining spot and one that’s well worth seeking out when you’re in town.

name: National Gallery of Victoria
location: 180 St Kilda Rd, Melbourne VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text:
National Gallery of Victoria is a fantastic arty spot, especially for those interested in ancient civilizations, contemporary art. It’s air-conditioned, making it a comfortable place to hang out and explore the galleries at your own pace.

There’s a café that spans two floors, perfect for taking a break during your visit.. Currently, you can see *Westwood | Kawakubo* (fashion), *Women Photographers 1900–1975*, and *Dior in the NGV Collection*, offering something for every interest.

Much smaller than other galleries I have been to with smaller condensed collection. Not busy.

Fabulous. I will return once more just for this magnificent gallery, and we all know where Melbourne is in this world;) The variety of art, from old to new, modern to classic, Asian to European, is incredible. In one word, fabulous! Did I mention the entrance is free?

Don’t walk, run to see the latest exhibition: Westwood & Kawakubo. The NGV never disappoints and trust me this was a stand out. The layout flowed beautifully with rooms filled with these fashion icons luxurious garments. The lighting was spectacular coupled with the signage and stories. It takes you to a fantasy world when two amazing women owned the runways and high end fashion business. Truly incredible and well worth the trip to Melbourne City. Get tickets now!

I remember going here when I was a kid and loved it. I went recently with my kids to enjoy the train ride in and experience some culture and gorgeous works of art.  My kids loved every moment. Most of the gallery is free but exhibitions are ticketed, which is ok because you can purchase tickets there as well as online. My kids learned about artists and truly appreciated their creativity. We will be going again.

The National Gallery of Victoria is one of the best places to visit in Melbourne. The gallery has an amazing collection of artworks, from classical paintings to modern exhibitions. I really enjoyed the beautiful architecture, the peaceful atmosphere, and the famous water wall at the entrance.
The exhibitions are well organised and suitable for all ages. There are interactive areas, helpful staff, and plenty of space to explore at your own pace. It is a perfect place to relax, learn about art, and spend quality time with family or friends.
I highly recommend visiting the National Gallery of Victoria if you are in Melbourne. It is free, inspiring, and a must-see cultural destination.

name: World's Largest Indoor Scrap Metartworld-Metal Art Gallery(Guinness World Record Holder) Starry Sky Exhibition/VR Theme Park
location: 155 Boundary St, Port Melbourne VIC 3207, Australia

reviews.text:
If you think this place is only for kids, you’ll be pleasantly surprised—adults can enjoy it just as much. Walking around the venue and seeing the large statues brings back a lot of good memories, and the overall atmosphere feels friendly and welcoming. The electronic games area is fun and adds to the experience, even if you’re just taking your time and not rushing through.

⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5)

The main downside was the 9D movie, which didn’t quite meet expectations. It wasn’t as immersive as advertised and felt a bit underwhelming unless you really forced the excitement. Also, considering how artistic and realistic the statues are, the bases could be much better—plastic pallets take away from the overall presentation. Paying more attention to the walls and adding some visual appeal instead of bare concrete would significantly improve the atmosphere.

Overall, it’s a good experience and worth visiting, with a few improvements that could easily take it to the next level.

Came here with my boyfriend on New Year’s Day for a casual date. At first, I thought it was just metal sculptures and felt a bit unsure. But once we looked closer, we realised all the artworks are made from real scrap metal parts like engines and mechanical pieces — actually pretty cool.

It’s not a traditional art gallery, but it’s fun and different. The mirror maze was way harder than we expected 😂 We tried it twice and still got lost. The 9D cinema line was super long so we skipped it, but we did play a VR game with flipping seats and it was much more exciting than I thought.

The staff were super friendly and even helped us store our bags, which made the experience feel really comfortable. Overall, a really good experience and a fun way to spend the day.

Second time visiting this incredible place. A lot of time and money has gone into creating this. The hundreds of metal art are so beautiful and mind blowing, and the bonus is you can interact with everything and become a part of the sculptures/photo ops. But there’s also more to this place than the art. There is a gorgeous mirror maze with a number of rooms decorated with more mirrors and so many beautiful lights, lots of fun for the kids. Then they have the VR section where you can immerse in the VR world. Highly recommend. I’m sure my son will request to come back here a third time.

We visited MetArt World during a short family trip to Melbourne, and it quickly became one of the highlights of our tour. I was initially a bit sceptical when we stepped inside, but that feeling disappeared almost instantly. The craftsmanship and creativity behind the sculptures crafted entirely from scrap metal were truly mind-blowing. The level of detail in each character was incredibly precise, showcasing exceptional skill and imagination.

Another standout feature was the Starry Sky Exhibition, an immersive space that transports you into a completely different dimension. Surrounded by mirrors and captivating light effects, the room creates a magical, otherworldly atmosphere that both adults and kids will love.

One thing to note is that the exhibition area is not wheelchair or stroller accessible, as it is located on the first floor. Parents with small children will need to carry them to the upper level. The 9D cinema, while a fun addition, was fairly average. The movie we watched, the dinosaur-themed one, lacked strong visual quality, but it still added some entertainment for the kids.

Overall, we spent a delightful two hours exploring MetArt World, and it offered a unique experience that stands out from typical attractions around Melbourne. Parking was stress-free, with free 2P spaces available nearby.

I highly recommend MetArt World to anyone visiting the Melbourne CBD. It is an inspiring and memorable experience for families and art lovers alike.

An awesome exhibition of both scrap metal sculptures, neon painting areas as well as cool themed light rooms and mirrors that allows for lots of good photo taking opportunities.
