There wasn't too many people which was great as it wasn't too crowded to take photos. They have a new section with a bunch of VR games as well as a few game rooms that are free to try out and play.

For a 5 star review they give out a little gift of a DIY painting kit for a little sculpture. Even without the incentive I would still give a 5 stars review.

name: The Ian Potter Centre: NGV Australia
location: Federation Square, Flinders St &, Russell St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Not much of a art person but I loved a some of the historic paintings that look as if it were taken from a camera. The explanation of the paintings was so good.
Free entry.
The cost of running this would be in the multiple millions of dollars per year especially with the amount of security.

We loved the Making Good: Redesigning the Everyday exhibition. It’s filled with a bunch of designers using both innovative and everyday materials to create products that are better for people and the planet. Some of the ideas are genuinely clever, some are beautifully simple, and all of them make you think about how design can solve real problems. If you get the chance, go and take a look — it’s well worth it and it’s free. I could easily go back and spend even more time there.
Highly recommend! So many great works to see! I spent the whole afternoon inside and really enjoyed it. The whole building has 3 floors, be sure to leave at least 3-4 hours when you visit!

This was my one museum stop during our time   In Melbourne and it didn’t disappoint. I loved the collection of Australian art (both indigenous and western) and a surprise giggling for me was the small design centre (tucked behind the gift shop on the ground floor) that had a variety of design sustainability initiatives.

Ian Potter Centre, Melbourne: The world’s-first art gallery dedicated to Australian art – through all genres, from classical to modern art. Housed in the impressive NGV Australia at Fed Square – first marvel at the dapper architecture then move inwards to appreciate the art pieces.  Interspersed with appreciation of the stylish concrete interior design and the of the tall white spire atop The Melbourne Arts Centre. #eatstylishtravelstylish

name: ArtVo
location: Shop NCF 07, Level 1/26 Star Cres, Docklands VIC 3008, Australia

reviews.text: It was a lot of fun. The 3D illusions were impressive. There are suggestions on how to take the photos on the floor and where to stand. But generally hold the camera in a slightly elevated position. Allow at least 1.5 to 2 hours here if you want to take photos at every station and especially if you come with friends. If you come alone you'll need a tripod. Selfies will not work.

Had a great time and lots of fun at ArtVo. We arrived at opening time, so very few people around, and mainly no waiting time to take heaps of photos. The 3D art and illusions are amazing; kudos to the skills and visions of the artists. The staff was very helpful and volunteered to assist taking photos of us in front of the art. It's  not just for kids, and adults will have a good time, too.

It was a lot of fun. The 3D illusions were impressive. There are suggestions on how to take the photos on the floor and where to stand. But generally hold the camera in a slightly elevated position. Allow at least 1.5 to 2 hours here if you want to take photos at every station and especially if you come with friends. If you come alone you'll need a tripod. Selfies will not work.

ArtVo Melbourne – Honest Review (Solo Visitor)

I visited ArtVo solo and overall had a fun and memorable experience. ArtVo is an interactive 3D art gallery where the artwork only really comes alive through photos, so it’s very different from a traditional museum. If you enjoy creative photography and optical illusions, this place is definitely worth a visit.

Even as a solo traveller, I felt completely comfortable. The staff were friendly and very used to helping people who come alone. They happily offered to take photos and even suggested poses and angles, which made a big difference. That really removes the awkwardness people might worry about when visiting solo.

The artworks themselves are colourful, imaginative, and well laid out. Some rooms are more impressive than others, but overall the variety keeps it interesting. I spent about 1 to 1.5 hours inside, which felt like the right amount of time without rushing. If you’re someone who loves taking lots of photos, you could easily spend closer to two hours.

One thing to keep in mind is that ArtVo is very photo-focused. If you’re not interested in taking pictures or posing at all, you may not find it as engaging. It’s more of a fun attraction than a traditional art gallery with detailed explanations.

The Docklands location is convenient, especially if you combine it with a waterfront walk or nearby shopping afterwards. I’d recommend going earlier in the day or on a weekday to avoid crowds and get better photos.

Overall, ArtVo is a fun, light-hearted experience, great for solo travellers, friends, or couples who enjoy interactive attractions and photography. Definitely worth checking out at least once.

Could be a great winter day activity but certainly not on a rainy public holiday with kids. Crowds were obscene. Very touristy. Expensive for what it is.  We lasted 20mins. If they controlled numbers, people were more considerate and the cost was reasonable, I could see the appeal. Better options with kids.

name: Flinders Lane Gallery
location: FLINDERS LANE GALLERY, The Nicholas Building, Level 1/37 Swanston St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Friendly staff, eclectic artworks, frequent exhibitions and central location.

Up the stairs in the Nicholas building near Flinders Lane.

Well worth a look when you are in the CBD!

A beautiful gallery space tucked away in a lovely building in inner Melbourne, have visited several times. Interesting artists from a range of styles and backgrounds, and I love that the gallery supports local and emerging artists. The staff are helpful and friendly, especially gallery owner Claire. Definitely worth a look-in if visiting Melbourne.

Amazing space in an amazing venue! I visited last week yet again and I'm always fascinated by the different artists and styles on display. The staff members are really knowledgeable and helpful. Not trying to sell sell sell. I love visiting and getting immersed in the ever changing exhibits. I really appreciate that there's something for every budget including mine.

A light filled beautiful gallery in the very centre of Melbourne. I’ve been going for years now, always so welcoming and engaging. Staff friendly and inviting. Wonderful collections of art, different every time. Thanks Claire and all the team.

Incredible gallery with a 30 plus year history of supporting Australian artists, both established as well as emerging through their annual Exploration exhibition.
Their new space in the iconic Nicholas Building is fantastic and a welcome retreat from the chaos of Swanston Street.
Highly recommend visiting when in the city and subscribing to their newsletter to find out about upcoming shows.
Terrific that galleries like this have continued through the very difficult lockdown periods.

name: ACMI
location: Federation Square, Flinders St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
I came here because of games & moving images whatever (it was paid)(we went to the free one instead) and I left with a full heart & mayhaps a little tear… it got so beautiful & philosophical while being so so simple, just good old storytelling doing the work.
I’m talking about the hall from mass medias to social media video cut-to-cuts to the palm projection video of memories.

Other than the storytelling philosophical aspect, the other stuff was amazing. The rotating cup animation, the art collecting system that also recommends similar media you can explore later.
It’s such a fresh take on the usual, oftentimes boring & static display of museums. Yet it’s not some empty interactive space, flashy buttons for the kiddies. It’s just so good you shouldn’t miss it!

[also as a graphic designer, I can’t help but notice the wayfinding icons. They’re nice & consistent. It looks like a design studio/someone who knew design worked on it. Turns out it’s their in-house team!]

Australian Centre for the Moving Image (ACMI) is always our go-to place during school holidays. The free exhibition, “The Story of the Moving Image,” is absolutely worth visiting. It suits all ages and takes you right back to the basics of light, image, audio, and video in a fun and engaging way.

It’s very interactive with plenty of hands-on learning, and my kids happily spend a few hours there every time we’re in the city — especially enjoying the introduction to the gaming world.

There’s also a paid gaming experience section, which we haven’t tried yet.

Located at Federation Square, it’s easily accessible by trams and trains.

Overall, a must-visit place for kids in Melbourne.

To be fair the free part of the museum deserves 6 stars. However, upon arrival, we approached the reception/information desk and the person there greeted us cheerfully. I was alone with my child. He asked us whether we would like to explore the Games exhibition since it was absolutely amazing. Yes, of course, we wanted to. Unfortunately, while the exhibition was rather ok, it was definitely not worth the high price. The worst part? 1/3 of the games presented in the paid exhibition were in fact also available for free in the upper floor (G level). Why the reception did not clarify that some of the games (the exact same games with the same names) were in fact available to enjoy in the free part of the museum… This being said, the free part of the museum is by far the best museum we have visited so far in whole Australia (and we have already visited 11 different cities). I would definitely visit this museum again, but would never bother paying for an exhibition there.

ACMI (Australian Centre for the Moving Image) is one of Melbourne’s most interesting and engaging cultural spaces, especially if you’re interested in film, television, games, and digital media. Located at Federation Square, it’s easy to access and fits well into a day of exploring the CBD.

The exhibitions are modern, interactive, and well curated. They cover a wide range of topics, from the history of film and television to contemporary digital storytelling and gaming. The displays are visually engaging and easy to follow, making the experience enjoyable even if you’re not deeply familiar with the subject matter.

One of the biggest strengths of ACMI is how hands-on it is. There are plenty of interactive sections where you can experiment, watch clips, and explore behind-the-scenes aspects of media production. This makes it especially fun for solo visitors, as you can move at your own pace without feeling rushed.

The main exhibition is free, which adds great value, while paid exhibitions are usually well worth it for the depth and quality they offer. The space is clean, well designed, and not overwhelming, even during busier times.

Overall, ACMI is a must-visit if you enjoy creative, digital, or pop culture experiences. It’s educational without feeling heavy and fun without being superficial. Whether you spend 30 minutes or a few hours, it’s a rewarding stop and a great example of Melbourne’s cultural side.

This place is a must see while on holiday. They have free and paid exhibits. What I REALLY loved were these cardboard discs we were given for free at the entrance. You scan the cardboard discs to each informative piece of art within the exhibit and it saves it onto the code embedded into the disc. You can then take this disc home, login on their website and view everything you scanned for free. This reduced the need to photograph what you wanted to remember, and also encouraged our young kids to scan things and be more interactive with the exhibits.

A gorgeous and peaceful art escape close to Melbourne CBD. Loved the exhibits and the whimsical nature of everything on display. The extensive garden and parklands gave this such a uniquely Australian air, like inviting nature into the artistic process. I highly recommend having something at the cafe early as it was closed by about 3pm 🕒

Defs worth paying to see the extra exhibits!

Well presented art invites exploration of the historic homes. New purpose built gallery space is spacious. Sunday Reed’s garden is lovely. Only three toilet cubicles - might be another in restaurant- but really lovely hand soap.

One of the best galleries in Australia and an important part of Melbourne’s creative history. The grounds are worth a walk - there’s a range of interesting, quirky sculptures and multiple buildings that are worth a look.

It’s ticketed - $27 for adults as of September 2025 - and is well worth a visit.
Fantastic gallery to visit. 3 unique exhibitions spaces amongst beautiful parklands, gardens, and outdoor sculptures. This, and the cafe, makes it a perfect day out

I photographed a wedding at Heide MOMA recently and it’s such an interesting, fun venue. The mix of art, architecture, and landscaped gardens creates endless photo opportunities. The team are fantastic and the whole day flowed seamlessly.

name: Buxton Contemporary
location: Corner Southbank Boulevard &, Dodds St, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text: A beautiful contemporary art space Next to the national gallery of Victoria. This place is even free to attend! I've only found out because of the open house Melbourne. Staff here, including Anthea, Maddie and Harriet are all highly passionate and helpful. They have an exhibition change every 3 months once so with checking it out whenever people are coming to visit as well as for your own exploration. Today I saw some of the local artist's work in few different mediums.
Love this gallery. Always something different and a cool place to hang on a hot day. Never too busy too, which is a plus.
Check their website for exhibition programs, as they may close during installation of new shows. Overall, it’s well curated compared to other contemporary art museums in Melbourne.
An excellent free entry art gallery with contemporary art exhibitions. Paid parking nearby or comfortable walking distance from Flinders St Station. Opposite NGV International.

Located inside the Southbank campus of the University of Melbourne, Buxton Contemporary is a hidden gem to explore. It occupies two floors, has a very spacious lift and a small gift store. The staff here are friendly and efficient.

name: Science Gallery Melbourne
location: Science Gallery Melbourne, at/114 Grattan St, Parkville VIC 3052, Australia

reviews.text:
I visited Science Gallery Melbourne in September 2025 for the Distraction exhibition with some friends. I travelled via PTV into the city, and after arriving at Flinders Street Station, I caught the tram up to the museum. There’s a stop right out the front, as it’s directly across from Melbourne University. The gallery was easy to spot with its eye catching Distraction display set on the street corner. When we walked in, the friendly staff explained where the exhibits were. Entry was free, and there was also a cute cafe next to the reception desk.

The upstairs area was really impressive. The building has such a unique design with wide stairs that double as sitting spaces lined with pillows, plus accessible options for everyone. Displays were scattered around the space, each one thought provoking and cleverly tied to digital distractions in life and presence. I loved the interactive nature of it all, everything from an AI Pictionary game to a cosy knitting room draped in fabric with rocking chairs. It was playful, immersive, and really fun to explore.

After finishing upstairs, we headed downstairs to a smaller space where you could trial old school games. Flicking through the retro options gave total Game Boy vibes. It was pretty fun to see the graphics and gameplay compared to today. Toilet facilities were available on both levels, well maintained and clean, which was great. The lower level also has offices, but they’re mostly locked so it’s easy to avoid wandering in.

Back at the entry level, we explored another area of mini exhibits beside the cafe. These were just as interactive and fun, and it was great to be able to swap around and try different things with friends. The cafe itself was really cute too, with a wide range of hot and cold food options and plenty of seating, though I didn’t grab anything this time.

Overall, Science Gallery Melbourne is an awesome spot to check out. Some exhibits are a bit more mature and may not be kid friendly, but most of it is open and enjoyable for all ages. It’s free, super easy to get to from the CBD, and the interactive experiences make it well worth the visit. It wasn’t crowded when we went, so the atmosphere was really chilled for a Saturday afternoon. I had a great time exploring and I’m excited to see what exhibitions come through next.

Came to the Distraction Exhibition here and it was fantastic, the staff are all so friendly and engaging especially with kids. Some really thoughtful art pieces on what the information age is doing to our brains and behaviour. It’s also free so everyone should come visit. I highly recommend.

Interactive gallery that inspires thought. The current exhibit is DISTRACTION. I really enjoyed the variety of exhibited artworks. The staff were friendly and knowledgeable about each piece and were open for a chat. Everything was clean and functioning. An overall excellent experience.

Decided to forgo the actual Museum for the Science Gallery and it was such a good choice. What an awesome collection of incredibly thought-provoking pieces, art, installations and concepts. I really enjoyed it here and the selection of things they had were so gorgeous and it was a really good time.
The distraction exhibition is like the best thing I've done in Melbourne.. so incredibly well thought out, engaging and thought provoking. will be back for a second visit, could spend hours here
Highly recommend the Auto-Photo: Life in Portraits exhibition, on until August 16. An amazing tribute to Alan Adler and his life’s work. Will be back.
The place is funny. You can find Taliban necklace and crown for Putin here. The staff is cool. Store large bags at the reception before entry.
Currently presenting an exposition about a Melbourne Citizen who had photobooths around the city taking pictures of others and himself for 50 years! You can even take your oen photo there! Amazing!!
highly recommend this place, nice exhibition and friendly staffs :)
An amazing exhibition by Molly Morris McGinty and friends. A true young and upcoming artist

Currently two exhibitions. One by First Nations people the other depicting Martha one of the first LGBT+ advocates of USA bu Florence. Incredible spaces. Very charming art advisor at reception. Three months / exhibition more or less with the venue coming available for events on between set ups... great spot for a corporate event or similar.  Ask for Badra

The gallery was a bit smaller than expected but had interesting and enjoyable exhibitions. Friendly staff and gender-neutral restrooms are definitely what makes you smile here. The exhibitions change over time so check the website beforehand to see if you'd be interested or not.

Visit the ACCA for the first time. There were only 2 exhibitions, and both were small but quite interesting. The exhibitions change quite frequently,  so will be checking frequently a gently for future events. Entry is free.

Free entry is great. However content may or may not be of personal interest since exhibition change from time to time. As a tourist, I just enjoy what’s on display. Staffs are very friendly. Love the architectural and the details around the building.

Australia Centre of Contemporary Art, Melbourne: That’s how I feel about the art curation at ACCA. We made a beeline for this from NGV as we were close to closing time and regret sunk deep. Architecture is stunning and layered – the changing geometric lines and angles tease as you approach the compound from different angles but that’s about it. The curation was sub-par and we thought it was an absolute waste of a good space. #eatstylishtravelstylish

A brilliant little collection of quirky and fascinating pieces that will no doubt catch your eye. Whether you are just coming in to browse at the artistry on display or looking to add some unique and modern pieces into your home, you've come to the right place. Love that they have a selection of illustrative books as well if you're wanting something a bit easier to carry home with you

Awesome gallery and wonderful service from Jess. We purchased two prints and she promptly posted them to us interstate. They were so immaculately packaged with such love and care, I was so impressed. Thank you Jess!

My new favourite place to visit in melbourne. A fantastic selection of mixed art and books, there’s something for everyone here and a challenge to leave without making a purchase, everything reasonably priced. Friendly staff. Pictures come framed and bubble wrapped ready to go.
Such a gorgeous quirky little gallery, with a great selection of art.  We have bought art from here twice now. the staff are friendly and are so helpful.

Another vague unprofessional customer service experience from people who looked like they had somewhere better to be. Emailed me to pick up my paintings but they weren’t in the shop. Kept calling me mate and dude neither of which is my name.

Edit : The order was under my wifes name and your staff member definitely did call me mate and dude. You obviously have no idea what is going on in your store.

name: Fed Square
location: Swanston St & Flinders St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
During a recent visit to Federation Square, I had the opportunity to experience firsthand the square’s festive transformation. The Christmas decorations were exquisite; from sparkling lights to the joy of shared public celebration, the atmosphere felt both vibrant and warm, echoing the spirit of the season.

Federation Square, inaugurated on 26 October 2002, occupies a full city‑block site opposite Flinders Street Station and spans some 3.8 hectares. What makes it exceptional is that it was constructed above active rail lines; a structural feat supported by thousands of tonnes of steel beams, vast concrete crash‑walls, and vibration‑absorbing systems; allowing the creation of a public civic and cultural precinct above the bustle of rail traffic.

The design, by LAB Architecture Studio (in partnership with Bates Smart), intentionally embraces a modern, geometric architectural language, reflecting the concept of “federation”; bringing diverse elements together to form a cohesive whole. Since opening, the Square has become not only a landmark, but a living, breathing centre of Melbourne’s cultural life; hosting major galleries and institutions including the Australian Centre for the Moving Image (ACMI), NGV Australia (The Ian Potter Centre), and the Koorie Heritage Trust.

More than just architecture, Federation Square is deeply rooted in place and history: the land was traditionally owned and cared for by Indigenous communities, making the Square; and every gathering there; part of a long continuum of cultural significance.

Walking through the plaza during the festive season, surrounded by lights and joyful chatter, I was struck by how effectively Federation Square weaves together Melbourne’s past, present, and civic spirit. It is a place where architecture, community, celebration, and culture converge; a true reflection of what makes Melbourne unique.

Visiting Federation Square reminded me that remarkable places are those that don’t just house buildings or events, but create moments of connection, belonging, and shared experience.
Big open place in the city. Lots of people and things to see. Can visit art galleries, museums, or sit at cafes. Modern buildings look cool. Good spot to walk around and take photos.

Federation Square is one of those places that really captures the essence of Melbourne — artsy, unpredictable, and always buzzing with energy. Whether you’re here to explore ACMI (Australian Centre for the Moving Image), check out the Ian Potter Centre, or just soak up the city atmosphere, there’s always something going on.

Location-wise, it’s unbeatable. Right across from Flinders Street Station, with trams, walking paths, and the Yarra River just steps away. It’s a perfect starting point for tourists and a casual hangout for locals.

What I loved most:
•	The architecture is bold and unique — a mix of geometric angles, glass, and stone. You either love it or hate it, but you can’t ignore it.
•	It’s a hub for festivals, pop-up events, and cultural happenings — I happened to catch a live art installation and an outdoor screening on my visit.
•	The public space feels open, yet alive, with people lounging on deck chairs, sipping coffee from local cafés, and taking in the view.

A few things to note:
•	The layout can be a little confusing. With its uneven surfaces and unexpected angles, wayfinding isn’t always intuitive.
•	The large open area is mostly paved — which means not a lot of greenery or shaded seating. On a hot day, it can get pretty uncomfortable.
•	While there are some great cultural spots, some parts feel a bit commercial or underused, and a few areas look like they’ve been neglected or need updating.

Overall, I’d still say it’s a must-visit in Melbourne. Just be prepared for a bit of architectural weirdness and bring a hat or umbrella if you’re visiting midday. Federation Square is modern Melbourne in a nutshell: artsy, dynamic, and a little rough around the edges — but that’s part of the charm.
What an iconic Melbourne spot...always full of surprises. Whether it’s fashion, sport, dance, or film, there’s always something exciting happening.

We visited this museum, it was very close to our accommodation.. it’s free to enter however they do have a few exhibits that you have to pay $18 to visit but we did not.. this is a museum that centres on art , some done by indigenous people and their culture .. others done with recycled material, and others from famous people like James Brown + many more .. you can take pictures as well .. there is a gift store and bathrooms on every level .. there are escalators and elevators…

Great space. We used it for a wine trade event for our local wineries from the northeast of Victoria. Perfect size for our producers, and Melbournes hospo professionals came to taste.
Perfect glassware, and the snacks complimented the afternoon.
Staff were seamless, and managed the crowd well.
We’ll be back! Thanks April, Chris, and Stephanie for your efforts 👌🏻🥂

Had a fabulous private dinner here at this art gallery hosted by the Cumulus team - reach out to them for more info. Fit 30 people easily with fantastic food options and lovely venue. If you are looking for a private dinner venue it's also quite affordable compared to other options and something special
Unique space for an intimate wedding or other event. Service and food are top-notch.
4.5. Work christmas party. Loved it :) good food overall. Decent food with good wine pairing. The wine list, though overpriced,  is quite comprehensive!
We held a private event here catered for by Cumulus Inc.  It was fantastic, couldn't have asked for a better location.  Artwork, Food, Drink and Service Were all exceptional.
Very cute new art/event space just behind Lygon st and This is Not A Toy Store. Attached cafe is cute too. Hope to see more things there.
A boutique art gallery with a diverse range. Attended an amazing awareness launch for injustices in Burma.
Awesome little gallery that is in a great location and has very friendly people working there. Great support of local artists too.
An art gallery with a big focus on promoting local artists. Still fairly new, and tucked away in a backstreet it’s got a huge future!
This is more than a gallery, it’s a community and has the most welcoming vibe!!

name: Arts Centre Melbourne
location: 100 St Kilda Rd, Southbank VIC 3004, Australia

reviews.text: Arts Centre Melbourne is one of the most iconic cultural venues in the city. The spire is impossible to miss and the location along St Kilda Road makes it easy to access. The theatres are well maintained and comfortable. Seating offers clear sightlines and strong acoustics. Whether you are watching a play, ballet, or live music performance, the sound quality holds up across the venue. Front of house staff are professional and efficient. Entry lines move quickly and ushers guide you to your seat without confusion. The bars are well staffed, so you are not waiting long during intermission. The program is diverse. You can see local productions, major touring shows, and international acts all in one place. It is a reliable choice if you want a well run night out in Melbourne. If you enjoy performing arts, this venue consistently delivers a polished experience.
Iconic Melbourne institution. Exhibitions, ballet and the arts. Walking between the buildings and the city is a great way to spend an afternoon with many bars, restaurants and sights nearby.

Went to the talented Mr Ripley, omg when I tell you I had fun and was entertained. It was amazing, breathtaking you name it.
Will McDonald, Roman Delo, Claude Scott-Mitchell, Faisal Hamza, Andrew McFarlane and Johnny Nasser were amazing and unmatched.
The stage was so good, matched perfectly were we where during the story.
Overall all you can tell I really enjoyws it.

During the T2 hear my eyes performance on Friday 27/2 the venue chose to keep the bars open through the performance till the end of intermission. This caused a lot of disruption through out the whole performance and made it a very impossible to enjoy the show with patrons constantly leaving their seats to purchase more alcohol. I have seen better behaved crowds at a football game.
Amazing theatre and you can take in a wine! Loved it.
It's such a cute little store filled with Dr Seuss treasures. Standing in the midst of all the beautiful illustrations brought back memories of happy times spent reading the stories to my kids.

After a refreshing coffee at Vespa Caffe, we took a trip downtown (in the mall) to the Dr S, E, U, double S gallery. Such intriguing paintings. Horrible vision of a future, populated by cats and ugly fish 🐠🔮🤡

The lovely Tash attended to us during our visit here. We were obviously very novice in our knowledge of purchasing fine art but she very patiently and enthusiastically walked us through the whole process, making the whole experience very fulfilling and memorable. We are very grateful!
I loved visiting the Gallery. I honestly didn't know one had existed. I saw it at The Block Arcade. I had to go in! Precious! Very interesting!
Love this gallery, some selections of all Dr Seuss artwork from all spheres and stasges of his life.

name: Tolarno Galleries
location: level 5/104 Exhibition St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: An art gallery with an amazingly shiny black floor. Now and then, pretty cool stuff gets reflected in it.

Went to the Team Lab exhibition. The exhibition itself was great. But there lacked cooling and ventilation in the gallery and my friends and I had to leave the room several times to get some air. Stuffy, hot and hard to breathe on a cold rainy day. It was handy having a lift. Climbing 4 flights of stairs would have been dreadful with the big crowds.
The art is so beautiful and very interesting it’s amazing to see art and the stories behind them this is so awesome 🙌 10/10

Went for the team lab exhibit and it wasn’t that great I suppose as compared to the exhibits they brought to singapore. It was only 4 exhibits without any interactive portions and it was really warm inside. Quite a hard place to find but it’s inside some building where you need to take the lift to level 4 or the stairs. Probably good to drop by if you have the time, otherwise, you won’t miss much.

Didn't expect the steep stairs going up. The TeamLab: Reversible Rotation was great but wasn't able to enjoy the experience that much due to having no air conditioning in the gallery and a packed crowd made it worse.

name: Brunswick Street Gallery
location: Level 1 and 2/322 Brunswick St, Fitzroy VIC 3065, Australia

reviews.text:
Artists - Visualization - Live the Life U Love
This is one of most impressive street galleries in Melbourne in one of the most open minded suburbs. A place where you can feel the signatures of talented artists, texture which passionate works and unique vision of different perspectives. It was great experience to been a part of that. Highly recommended for everyone, even if the art is not part of your lives - just visit it and it will become a place to think in a while.
Such a dreamy gallery featuring great local artists! Lots of variety to suit different tastes.
Nice staff, cool space and beautiful artworks. Takes at least 1 h to have a good look.

Delightful to view the Fifty Squared Art Prize 2021. The 50x50cm parameters allowed for artists creativity to flourish, with multiple art forms and subject matter on display. This little tucked away gallery has something for everyone.

The night I went was a big opening launch of some competition they were running. The place was very, very crowded. Hundreds of people, you could barely move. If there had been a fire, people would have been crushed trying to get out in a hurry. It was claustrophobic. I feel they should have managed the crowds better.

The Melbourne Museum is an impressive and thoughtfully designed space, and overall a very enjoyable visit. From the moment you step inside, the building itself sets the tone. It was clean, modern, and wonderfully spacious, with plenty of natural light and well considered layouts that make it easy to explore without feeling rushed or overwhelmed.

The exhibitions are curated with care and clearly aim to balance education with engagement. There’s a strong emphasis on storytelling, context, and visual presentation, which makes complex ideas accessible without being overly simplified. I particularly appreciated how interactive elements are woven throughout, offering moments of discovery that keep both adults and children engaged. It’s a museum that invites curiosity rather than demanding prior knowledge, which is always a win.

That said, while the experience was consistently interesting, it didn’t quite reach the level of fascination I had hoped for. A few exhibits felt more informative than immersive, and I found myself wanting a little more depth or surprise in certain sections. Nothing detracts from the quality of the museum itself, but expectations were perhaps set just a touch higher.

The facilities are excellent and are well maintained, easy to navigate, and clearly designed with visitor comfort in mind. It’s a great place to spend a few hours, especially for families or anyone with a general interest in science, history, and culture.

A solid four stars out of five and I found it polished, enjoyable, and worth visiting, even if it didn’t completely blow my mind.

While many museums across Australia offer free entry, Melbourne Museum truly proves that some experiences are well worth the admission fee.

We spent over five hours exploring the museum and still felt like we could have stayed longer. The very first highlight was joining a guided walk led by Denis, who provided a wonderful and engaging overview of the museum’s collections.

The range of galleries is impressive — from towering dinosaur skeletons to fascinating bug collections and the Gondwana Garden.

My personal favourites were:

*First Peoples– a powerful and moving exploration of Aboriginal history and culture.
*Melbourne Gallery (History of Melbourne)– beautifully curated stories tracing the city’s development.
*Forest Gallery– a serene, living rainforest space right inside the museum. We even had a small picnic outside.

Federation Tapestry collection is also stunning — symbolic, and deeply meaningful. We also joined the eel-feeding session and learned about their habitat.

Very well worth a visit.

This museum was a fantastic experience, largely because it had so many fun and interactive components. It’s a really great place to visit if you enjoy learning about history, seeing different kinds of animals, and exploring nature. I especially liked how engaging everything was, it wasn't just reading displays, but actually getting to participate.

Normally, you don't expect museums to have live animals, but this one was a pleasant surprise because it does! They had a really interesting selection, and it added a unique dimension to the whole experience. Because of all this, I will definitely come back to this museum again in the future. It’s a place worth revisiting!

Recently visited the Melbourne Museum and really enjoyed it. It has great sections on mind, body, science, forest, and nature, plus a unique kids’ area. The science section with dinosaur bones, ancient rocks and extinct animals was fascinating.

I also loved the dedicated space sharing the exhibits on 18th and 19th century Melbourne, including old maps, lifestyle and family albums. The museum was clean, well organised, and the café served excellent coffee. A fun and educational place to visit!

A must visit with kids! What an interactive museum! A whole lot of effort put into a museum. A splendid example how “boring history” really comes to life. They even have a great kids play area, where even the little ones playfully learn things. Excellent museum!

name: Fortress Melbourne
location: Shop 25/23 Caledonian Ln, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Came here for my pre birthday celebration yesterday. Food was delicious but unfortunately we couldn’t finish due to a small quantity of people in our booth, sorry team! Staff regardless was understanding, accommodating and friendly! Offered alternatives regardless. Vibe was great was pretty fun.

Come in somewhat semi-regularly when life allows me to have fun. Went in for a friends birthday this time and they also thoroughly enjoyed it.

Booked a console booth for a few hours and some tacos (Tuesday be taco day).

What really made our night was the new brew a cocktail mini game with the dice. Sufficient to say we did the mini game multiple times ending up with some real magnificient cocktails for a great price. Safe to say will always look out for it when it’s on.

I wish I could give this place more than five stars! This isn’t my first visit, and it definitely won’t be my last. I booked a session for a group of friends to play Switch, and we ordered plenty of drinks along with burgers for everyone. The food was AMAZING the Bacon Smoky is honestly the best one I’ve ever had! The atmosphere is great, and the staff provide outstanding customer service. I’m so grateful every penny we spent here was absolutely worth it.

Went for a birthday lunch and had an amazing time! Had been craving a good proper Poutine since I left Canada and they delivered. 💜 compliments to their chefs! All the staff were amazing and friendly, good board game selection. Overall amazing atmosphere and experience, definitely going to become a regular. :)

Had a Chicken Parma and Carlton draught beer while catching up with friend last night. The environment is nice and special with the gaming set up. Moderate noise around with the music and games all over the place.

Chicken Parma was really good, with fries and salad. Salad is just normal, probably not to my liking.

Overall, recommended if you like a good Parma and beer while hanging out with friends. It would be better if you like to play games too.

The Royal Exhibition Building and Melbourne Museum are excellent places to visit if you enjoy history, culture, and well-designed museum spaces. The Royal Exhibition Building itself is stunning — a grand heritage structure surrounded by Carlton Gardens, and it feels impressive the moment you arrive. Even just walking around the building and gardens is worth the visit.

Inside, the Melbourne Museum is modern, spacious, and very well organised. The exhibitions cover a wide range of topics including Australian history, Indigenous culture, nature, science, and Melbourne’s development. The displays are informative without being overwhelming, and there’s a good mix of visual exhibits, interactive sections, and detailed explanations.

One of the highlights is how open and calm the museum feels. It’s easy to explore at your own pace, making it especially enjoyable for solo visitors. You can spend anywhere from one to three hours depending on your interest level, and there are plenty of benches and quiet corners if you want to slow down.

The surrounding Carlton Gardens add a lot to the experience. They’re perfect for a short walk or a break after the museum, and the setting makes the visit feel relaxed rather than rushed.

Overall, this is a well-maintained, educational, and enjoyable place to visit. Whether you’re interested in architecture, history, or just want a peaceful cultural outing in the city, the Royal Exhibition Building and Melbourne Museum are definitely worth your time.

We went to the Brickvention (Lego exhibition) in the Royal Exhibition Building and what a fun experience! The entry was in a time slot if 20 mins and free to stay as long as you wish till the end. We had to wait for about 20 mins to go in.

Amazed by the magnificent builds and interactions are nice. We loved the oriental express and racing game.

A gorgeous exhibition in a wonderful building!

10/10 must book the promenade, visiting The Royal Exhibition Building, a living, breathing heritage 🤩. The architecture was superb, preservation work still ongoing, applauding initiatives maintaining. The view from the top, watching over Carlton Gardens was exceptional ❤️ A view one must see with their own eyes. Love it, love the guide too. Sparkling! 💎

Enjoyed our tour of the Royal Exhibition Building run by the Melbourne Museum. Ran for about an hour and a half. Guided tour took the group through the grond floor and basement before going up onto the roof. A lift was available for any who needed it we didn't realise there were eight flights of stairs up but made it!

I came here on two occasions.

First time was a guided tour, and the second was the big design market.

It was a joy to learn about the history of this building and how it was built. The tour also allowed me to go up to the top of the building as well as the basement. I loved the building’s murals and I wish I could’ve stayed a little longer - but the tour was only for one hour.

If you are a history buff, this is a must-go!

The second time I was here was for the big design market - everything was Aussie-designed / aussie-made, and there were loads of interesting stalls and foods to try. I had an absolute smashing time here!

name: Melbourne Convention and Exhibition Centre (MCEC)
location: 1 Convention Centre Pl, South Wharf VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text: Lots of restaurants on the river. Plenty of undercover parking. It cost us $16 From 11am to 2.30pm . You can hire boats to cruise up and down the river. There are public toilets. My favorite restaurant is Man Mo's a beautiful fine dinning Chinese restaurant.

BALLOON STORY WAS AMAZING!!!
Family recommended for sure.

Even if you don’t have a family or kids taking your gf out on a date there is also recommended.

I would go there again!!!

It was good to be a child again (being a 22 year old) .

Lots  of photos and memories were made.

My gf loved it and I loved it more so it was a win.

Have a look at some of the photos I’ve attached:) ❤️

Absolutely incredible! My kids loved it - we were there for over two hours - but I was just as in awe. The installations are so creative, well-made, colourful, fun, clever. Everywhere you looked, there was another detail to surprise you. Such talent. Honestly, a core memory for us and I cannot recommend it more highly!! Added bonus is the fact that all the balloons are environmentally friendly! Well done Balloon Story - you absolutely nailed it!

An absolute incredible event . So much imagination and talent. Ball pit was great and colours of balloons were astounding . Would highly recommend.
Food is quite expensive and so are balloon animals.
Had a long 45 minute wait when buying a foil balloon then it deflated and had to return to get another ,so another 10 min wait 🙄

Went to the F1 experience and the convention centre itself is huge, well laid out, great signage. As for the F1 amazing, good overview of the history and aspects of racing cars and a couple of interactive components to it. The audio remotes we great

Excellent space for gigs you can get a good view from the floor. Upstairs affords great views though be early for those Primo spots.(See first pic) Bar Staff really helpful and obliging, security unobtrusive.

The event space is quite spacious (though a bit smaller than the other venue). When I walked in, it didn’t feel cramped at all because of the split-level design. The entrance is a bit narrow, though. There are about four or five toilets in the women’s bathroom (if I remember correctly). I also liked that there’s a notice board showing upcoming events, that’s such a nice touch!
One of the best music venues in Melbourne. Great sounds great bar staff. Lack of mobile reception when you're underground. What's not to love about this place..
I love seeing gigs here. I like the tiered set up and intimate size. The staff are nice and efficient

I came here for a concert almost a year ago, and I still think about how great it was. It is a very intimate venue, which made for a great experience.

There must have been a fire in the adjacent / connected building, or possible a false alarm, which was eventful. Got dealt with very promptly, but that’s not here nor there.

Unfortunately, the ID checking was slow. I understand they have to try to be prompt, but the staff member checking the IDs came off quite rude and demanding. There must have been a miscommunication as different staff members told us different instructions, which made the staff member who checked the IDs more rude.

Despite this, the venue itself is amazing and I had a great experience

name: Merlin Events Melbourne
location: SEA LIFE Melbourne Aquarium Corner of King and, Flinders St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: We used the Sea Life Aquarium for a corporate function during a trade show. The staff were fantastic at accommodating us at the last minute, the food was fantastic, and the unique experience of the venue helped us get more customers to our function. I'd happily recommend using the team at Sea Life Aquarium to anyone.

What an amazing venue for a 'Wow Moment' function.  From our Function Coordinator to all the wait staff on the night, the scrumptious food, the service and set up, not to mention the amazing Fish Bowl vibe with all the gorgeous fish swimming around, Scuba Santa and the Photo Booth, every single part of this event was handled beautifully & loved by everyone who went.  Thank you from all of us at Holmesglen Private Hospital we all had an sensational night.
We booked the upper deck for our EOY event. Overall our staff enjoyed the evening. Thank you to Jaleesha and the team for taking care of us on the night. We really appreciate it.

We could not have found a better place to hold our event. The whole team at   Sea Life made the arrangements so easy and the service on the night outstanding.
Thank you all,
Staff and food were wonderful, great location

What a great music venue. Central location in Melbourne CBD. Great acoustics. While there is not a lot of seating (pay extra for booth seating). If you don’t want to stand in the mosh pit, there is terraced standing with railings to hold drinks or to lean on. The interior is Grecian inspired with beautiful blue domed ceilings. Holds enough people to create a good atmosphere but not too large. Lastly, the bar staff are friendly and l’ve never felt invisible.
Always liked the Forum as a cinema and now as a live venue. Great decorations and grandeur which adds enormously to the experience. The Church, in this instance, were great, the lighting spectacular.
Great venue to see bands. Booth seating with service. Saw Amble here recently and the atmosphere was fantastic. Large venue but still the feel of an intimate show.

Quintessential Melbournian music venue in a 1920s restored theatre styled along ancient Roman themes complete with statuary and a firmament overhead.  The absolutely best place to see a band, not too small, not too big, great sound and atmosphere.  Infinitely better than a tennis court or the StKilda equivalent which has worse acoustics.
stunning venue, the statues at the top were gorgeous ! very impressed would go out of my way to buy tickets for anything held here again

name: Alto Event Space
location: Top Floor, Melbourne GPO Building Cnr Elizabeth + Little Bourke Streets, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
We always enjoy working at Alto Event Space as they help make our job easy : happy guests = great photos.

Clients are always impressed by the high standard of service by the staff at the venue as well as the sensational food and drinks. Perfect for corporate parties and events.

Alto is a hidden gem in the city and looks different every time we visit with just a few tweaks to the styling. Looking forward to our next visit!
reviews.author_name: Man With A Camera Photography Melbourne (Man With A Camera)

reviews.text:
We held our staff Christmas party at ALTO and couldn’t have been happier with the entire experience. The service was impeccable, the food was delicious, and the venue looked absolutely beautiful, the perfect setting for our celebration.

A huge thank you to Jenna and the team for their exceptional preparation and attention to detail. Everything ran seamlessly, and the staff member who took over later in the evening was equally fantastic, with consistently high service right throughout the night.

The feedback from our team has been overwhelmingly positive, with many saying it was one of their favourite Christmas parties we’ve ever held.

Thank you again for looking after us so well. We’ll definitely be back!

We had our wedding ceremony and reception at Alto Events in November 2024 and couldn’t be happier. There’s nothing like a Melbourne city wedding! It really was a dream come true. The team took all the stress away and it was absolutely seamless!!! Thank you so much to all the team at Alto, the day was truly magical!

Hosted our welcome event for our conference at Alto & would highly recommend this venue. They were very accommodating with our requirement & happily answered all my questions. The service & food was fantastic and my guests are still talking about the Parmesan wheel for the pasta! Would have no hesitation in hosting future events at Alto.

The entire team at Alto were incredible. We had a cocktail style wedding early November 2025. Chloe was patient and thorough with all of our planning and questions in the lead up. The staff on the day were attentive, the food and drinks were delicious. The team made planning stress free and the event ran perfectly.

We ahad an amazing experience with Ibrahim Malouf during Melbourne Jaz festival.
Lights sounds bar staff were nice and good.
I’ve now been to two live events at this venue, and it’s been great each time! Lots of room, friendly staff, and an all-round fantastic experience. Thank you, guys! ✨❤️
Dodgy place, but worked well for Jazz Festival performance of Gary Bartz. Great show

Benee concert was great, aircon was blasting on a hot day which I loved and appreciated, shoutout to Rylee who got back to me super fast with answers to my accessibility questions.
A couple other reviews mentioned how dark it was and I agree, the lights need to be on when people are walking in because the steps are too hard to see.
It always sucks when my favs get booked here, like the gig will be fine but it’s pretty crusty and the AV is always a bit shoddy. But charging $15 for a tinny is just straight up un-Australian.

From the very beginning, the experience with Evoqé Events was nothing short of extraordinary.

Our journey started with Roan, who walked me through the site with such vision and confidence that he completely transformed the way I saw the space. The way he painted a picture of what could be achieved — that anything could happen in this venue — genuinely opened my eyes. It was that conversation that gave me the confidence to propose a sit-down luncheon, something ICN had never done before. That moment changed everything.

The handover to Alicia was seamless. She was incredibly accommodating, endlessly helpful, and full of great ideas. She supported us through constant changes to menus and timings with calm professionalism and genuine care — nothing ever felt like too much trouble. Her support made what could have been a stressful process feel easy and collaborative.

Rob’s AV support was simply next level. The audiovisual offering was outstanding — the screens on the pillars completely set the scene and elevated the entire room. The camera-to-screen experience was far beyond what I expected, with close-up views that added real impact and energy to the event. We were so appreciative of the on-site team, who never missed a beat and ensured everything ran flawlessly.

The food was incredible, and the service on the floor was exceptional — attentive, polished, and thoughtful to every guest. The overall experience exceeded our expectations in every way.

We were truly thrilled and over the moon to have delivered such an amazing event at your venue. Evoqé Events played a pivotal role in its success, and we genuinely cannot wait to come back and do it all again.

Recently held a multi-day event at Evoque for approx. 100 guests and to say that the feedback was overwhelmingly positive is an understatement. Not only is the venue beautiful and spacious, but all of the planning in the lead up was seamless thanks to Alicia, Amy, Roan and the team there. We had some "different" requests with what we were trying to achieve, but nothing was too difficult for the team and they accommodated all of our needs.
Special shout out to the new menu (and those preparing the food) as it received very high praise from some usually hard to please critics. Already looking forward to our next event at Evoque!

Evoqe events recently hosted our wedding reception.

From booking and planning our wedding, there was nothing that was too much effort.

The attention to detail setting up the space was impeccable. Serving our bridal party, guests in the manner in which they did, was truely a highlight of the evening.

The service and food were truely standouts and a point of difference from weddings I’ve attended in the past. The kind and friendly wait staff really made my partner and I the centre of the evening.

Alicia, Brad and Liam, thank you from the bottom of our hearts for perfect wedding reception. Your willingness to accomodate the most minute details, made the entire experience seamless.

For those wanting a similar experience on their wedding day, I would recommend Evoqe events without hesitation. If you are considering Evoqe events for your wedding day you truely won’t be disappointed.

Elaine & Peter

As we embark on our journey as husband and wife, we have left behind our fairytale dream wedding. It was nothing short of sensational, luxurious and classy all in one. Wed like to thank Q events and their staff for exceeding our expectations, the staff were absolutely outstanding providing a high level service. All the staff had nothing but a smile, their persona towards the guests was welcoming. From the antipasto to the main course was 10/10. The cocktails were the talk of the night. If your after a luxurious wedding with nothing short of high standards, we recommend Q events.

To the Q events Team. Thank you so much for the Magical Evening. A huge thank you to Carleen, Marijana & Hope. Who worked meticulously throughout the months leading up to our wedding 24.02.24, as well as the staff who delivered raving service. Our guest spoke highly of the friendly faces, food and warm atmosphere. They worked amazingly well with our vendors and were lovely to our family and friends who joined us on the day. The venue it self was transformed and little touches were definitely noticed. Thank you for the memories and a show stopper of a night. R + H

Came here for a night out this past Saturday, and overall had a good experience! We got in at around 10:30 and were let in easily, and greeted with a complimentry champagne upon arrival. The venue felt very lux, the dj played some good tunes so we had a dance! Super impressed with the food platters going around the dance floor featuring mini sliders, bug rolls, chicken skewers and more. We felt this was very generous considering entry was only as much as it is elsewhere! The only put off was the 3 promoters who came up to us while having fun at sperate times on the dance floor expressing their shock at us having paid, and then going to point out how almost everyone else on the dance floor was paid to be there. While we believe they may have had good intentions it made us feel a bit out of place being somewhat the 'only people who paid'. Though overall a good night out! :)

Attended the Club Saturdays launch at Bond Melbourne with a free entry message before 11pm. The door experience was disappointing. One bouncer from South Sudan was selectively enforcing rules — warning some people about hats while others were let through without issues, even though other security staff didn’t seem to care. The way he singled people out felt uncomfortable and borderline racist. Completely killed the vibe. Lucky I didn’t pay for entry.

We were visiting town for a long weekend and were really looking forward to the Olympus event at this club—especially since my partner is Greek and the night featured a Greek artist we both love. We bought tickets in advance and were genuinely excited to be part of the celebration.
Unfortunately, when we arrived, we were turned away at the door for wearing shorts. We had no idea there was a strict dress code, and nothing was mentioned when we purchased the tickets. It was incredibly disappointing, especially after all the anticipation and planning.
Great nightclub with excellent clients and staff

BOND BAR the absolute best place to have/host your hens party or any party for that matter.

Brianna and Michelle were absolutely amazing to deal with making the process so smooth and effortless.

The bartenders and staff are so friendly and accommodating.

Such a fabulous venue with such a great atmosphere. Looking forward to coming back here with the girls again.

My recent experience at the DJ night was deeply disappointing.  The atmosphere was disrupted by an altercation, which escalated unnecessarily.  The lack of visible security personnel was concerning and contributed to the unsettling environment. This oversight created a sense of unease and diminished the overall enjoyment of the event.  I hope organizers will address these safety concerns for future events.

Friendly bouncers, didn't experience any discrimination. It was a night of the after party of the glitch expo (Cos play night thing). So it was full of cos play dressed dweebs. Aside from the people aspect, all of the sound equipment needs a major upgrade, the sound is muffled and not crisp - on all 3 floors. Drinks are a little on the expensive side but comparable to other Melbourne establishments. Not sure if my $30 entry fee was worth it but for an experience, it's ok.

Went to see my sons band play at DV8 reunion gig.
Had a great time.  Good drinks prices, great and helpful staff, especially to us 'oldies' who used to go there 30 plus years ago.
Great atmosphere 👌

I recently had the pleasure of visiting the Inflation entertainment complex located on King Street in Melbourne, and I must say, it was a fantastic experience. One of the standout features of the complex was the impressive bar. It was well-designed and had a great atmosphere, making it a perfect spot to relax and unwind.

The variety of drink options available was also impressive. From an extensive selection of beers to a wide range of whiskies and cocktails, there was something to suit every taste. Whether you're a beer enthusiast, a whisky connoisseur, or a cocktail lover, Inflation had you covered.

The staff at Inflation were truly amazing. They were friendly, attentive, and knowledgeable about the menu. They went above and beyond to ensure that every guest had a memorable experience. Their professionalism and dedication added to the overall positive vibe of the place.

One of the highlights of my visit was the Bollywood night. The mix of Punjabi and Hindi dance songs created an electrifying atmosphere, and everyone seemed to be having a great time. The energy and enthusiasm on the dance floor were contagious, and it made for an unforgettable night of fun and entertainment.

In conclusion, my experience at Inflation entertainment complex was nothing short of fantastic. The great bar, wide drink selection, amazing staff, and the memorable Bollywood night made it a truly enjoyable visit. If you're looking for a place to have a good time in Melbourne, I highly recommend checking out Inflation.

I have been attending Inflation since the 1990s. It was good then, but its brilliant now. The planning of using different levels for different crowds or music types means you can choose and go where you like. They are all different and unique. The types of events vary incredibly and this is a good thing. Ive been to all of them, several times, for years and love the place. I travel from Adelaide almost every month to attend an event and travel back next day ... that's how much I love the place. Drinks are very reasonably priced (despite what some others have said), and the security are very understanding of the kink element and don't get in the way. I always say thank you and goodnight to them. I will continue coming here for years to come. Bar staff are amazing, polite and dont muck around. For those other people that had a less favourable comment, I suggest you check the details of the event first to avoid disappointment or shock. The venue caters for so many types of crowds and needs, and some very extreme needs, and it does all this very well. Thank you.

Watched Saturday Night Fever and it was a hoot. Staff friendly and helpful to find seating. A great view from dress circle. Lineup for ladies toilets upstairs a little lengthy with only 2-3 toilets available but loved the old style. Building and amenities in fine condition. Loved the experience.
Good theatre. Decent pricing so they fill the seats. Good bar too again not crazy prices (eg choc ice only $6)

Always a Joy to attend The Athenaeum Theatre. We seen Saturday Night Fever.  It brought back the music, the moves. The performers were outstanding, and the show was very cleverly presented, It will not dissapoint 👍

The Athenaeum rates 1 star just because it is ancient and some developer hasn't ripped it down yet, but boy it really is a hot mess.

We wanted front row seats but our show sold out so fast that we were way way WAY up in the nose-bleed section.  At the time, I was grateful to get anything.

Beyond shabby and run down, this theatre seems dangerous to me. I saw no lifts and did I really see a step down immediately as you enter the men's room? surely THAT collects a few.  I didn't bother to use the toilets because my husband said the men's room was straight out of the early 20th century.

Our seats were several flights and turns up stairs with varying width hand rails.  The stairs were steep and unforgiving. When we finally made it up to the nosebleed section, we then had to travel down a flight of extremely steep stairs to get to our front row seats. We were admonished by the nice woman to definitely not lean on the rail in front, or apply any pressure to it.
When we sat down, my 6'3 husband was squished low like a kinder kid with his knees crammed toward his torso. There was no room for him to move at all.  FOR 2 hours.  I already have knee issues and my knees were hitting the barrier in front of me.  I also could not move except jiggle my legs up and down for circulation.  My bum was sitting on plywood maybe?  I don't know but the seats were far too low and far too uncomfortable.  More importantly, it appeared to be very dangerous.  Firstly, there was only one fire exit working.  A sign over the other fire exit said "No exit."  That can't be legal.  I felt like I was in a potential tinder trap with no exit -- especially when we saw what looked to be a shiny fire spark in the spot light dust.  It wasn't. But it looked like it.
If we had to move in a hurry to exit the balcony seating in case of fire, we would have been dead. By the time we each had unfolded our numb legs and shook the circulation back into them, we would have been trampled in the panic to try to escape.
Our seats in the front row were obstructed by the thick metal pole that we weren't allowed to apply any kind of weight to.  I was forever looking under the pole or hiking my head over the pole for brief stints.  my husband at 6'3 didn't have that issue.  If someone had tried to go past us, in front of us, they would have had to hang their bums off over the pole.  It did not feel safe at all.  I am thankful no one had to pass by.

Looking from our nosebleed section, there was nothing nice left of this theatre.  You can see that it was once an average theatre with semi-lovely adornments on the walls but it is all so shabby and old that you just wonder if anyone is going to ever fix it up or have you given up.

The people we spoke with were all lovely. No complaints. (I do have a complaint with the way in which these reviews have been responded; bitter snobbery isn't really called for.  Fix the theatre or close it. Or at least close the balcony.

The location was fabulous. I loved being amongst the fine stores and other fine cinemas and hotels.  But, the Athenaeum doesn't measure up and I am glad I didn't get more hurt than I did.  Our 2km walk up hill back to the car was the perfect remedy to being squished in that matchbox for two hours.

Oh, the commentary others have stated about sweltering in the balcony did not hold true for us.  I froze and was happy to get out into the warmth of the evening.  Dusty. dirty. old. yes.  Hot, no.  Shining vibe, yes.

Watching the 9 people sitting in the front row constantly trot back and forth for two hours, getting their beers, was a bit over the top.  They are probably cronies from the theatre taking up space that paying people like me would have loved to enjoy.  Instead they disrupted everyone else's pleasure throughout the show.
This was our second visit.  We will not be back even though our entertainment was excellent.
Great theatre. Staff were friendly. Good sound and views looked good from most seats.

name: Chin Chin Events
location: Level 1/125 Flinders Ln, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
We can’t thank the Chin Chin Events team enough for making our wedding reception absolutely perfect. From the food to the staff to the set-up, everything ran like clockwork and was better than we could have hoped for!

A massive shout-out to Emma for managing everything from start to finish and liaising with all our vendors. On the day, Malachi and the team were incredible—managing the set-up, keeping service smooth, and making sure we (bride and groom!) were always looked after. Communication was spot on all night, and the team made sure everything felt effortless. So grateful—highly, highly recommend this amazing team and venue space!

An absolute favorite place of mine when I’m in Melbourne! I walked in and Funkytown was playing I knew I found my happy place! Sitting at the bar, I witnessed the amazing bartenders creating artwork with the cocktails! Food is Amazing! The Kingfish hamachi is incredible!

Atomsphere, food and service fantastic.. We got the 88 banquet meal and it didn't disappoint, was the perfect feast and every dish was just mouth watering as the next, perfect choices. Although I will mention regarding the wine filler waiter definitely needs more practic5and attention to detail on filling glasses. We found every fill was uneven and quite noticeable as well, so didn't know if one of us was being under served or over served😝. And yes that does make a difference to wine loves when glasses are not even.

You absolutely will never leave unsatisfied if you visit Chin Chin.
It is by far my favourite resturant in all of Melbourne.
The staff are always so lovely and give great food and drink recommendations.
Thanks again Chin Chin team! You are smashing it!
First visit, fantastic tasting  food, great atmosphere, all staff worked at pace and were professional. Will definately recommend, will dine again on my next visit to Melbourne.

Absolutely love this place. Love the food. Love the vibe. Love the service. Love. Love. Love. Been here many times and never bored. There are so many options for whatever you feel like on the day or night. Have taken friends and loved ones here; and they always love it, too. Hofbräuhaus Melbourne certainly captures the spirit of a true German beerhall! 🍻 🇩🇪 🥨

Can't get over how great my whole dining experience tonight was!!

I went to this German restaurant with my mom and my partner. The minute we walked in, a staff greeted us with a big smile. She was lovely and cheery!

We sat down at the table we were escorted to. Then another staff came to our table and took our drinks orders.

The whole ambiance was festive with the fun traditional live music. A lady was asking visiting table to table if it was anybody's birthday.

Later, one of the staff with a microphone again hopped from table to table to invite some guests to the front for some sort of group dance to the live music.

The food was very good too. Schnitzel never goes wrong, was incredible. Sausages and desserts were slightly average though.
Not too complicated but rather simple and yet scrumptious.

They were busy all night but all staff were working happily with positive energy.

I'm gonna go back there again with my friends.

Great experience & beautiful food & drinks. If you have never experienced Germany, this is a huge must, and if you have been to Germany, you'll Want to come here. The staff are friendly too, which is nice in a big city. The Oktoberfest ale was very easy to drink & perfect.

Great atmosphere, can't wait to go back.

Great night out and good time of year to join in on the Christmas celebrations. Service right from the get go was excellent. Atmosphere in the restaurant was awesome. Will eat in the beer hall next time. Our food was delicious and will definitely be back to try more dishes. The wurst platter is great for sharing between two people and the bread dumpling and Kasespatzle were on point. I did find the apfelstrudel a little disappointing as no crispy pastry at all, was just completely soft and moist. Beer of course was on point 🍺

Booked a table for 2 online a d was surprised on how easy it was and was even able to say my guest required gluten free options.  On arriving,  we found it very busy but was met quickly by a staff member within minutes.  We had arrived 20 minutes early but were able to get  table easily.  They had a band playing traditional beer hall music as well as some classic pub tunes.  There was interaction between staff, band and customers which made the setting a joy to be apart of.  We placed our drinks order and 5 minutes later I was enjoying a cold Bavarian stein.  We then placed our main meal order and was happy it took only 15 minutes.  I had the wiener schnitzel and found it similar to what I experienced in Austria.  We then ordered dessert which I had the apple strudel.  Usually I don't eat sweet food but definitely could of eaten more.  I found the whole experience fun and will definitely be back.  I highly recommend 👌.

name: Melbourne Zoo Events
location: Zoo Events, Elliott Ave, Parkville VIC 3052, Australia

reviews.text:
A wedding in the Japanese gardens at my favorite Melbourne place to go on our dates over the years was a dream come true .

We had the most magical day from a heartfelt ceremony in the beautiful and intimate setting of the Japanese gardens , even in a busy zoo day it felt private and perfect ,

We got to ride the carousel all to ourselves , roam the zoo , catered and with drinks and food flowing us along … and omg !!! We got to go behind the scenes with giraffes after closing !!!  Everything Through to having a Delorean drive me inside the zoo !! I just can’t encompass what an amazing day our forever day was I got to marry my best friend and have the kind of day I will still be talking about in years to come to thankyou to the team at Melbourne zoo events for making this dream day come true xx

( a few lovely photos from my day with thanks to Rocco Ancora Photography )

Wedding To Remember!

Huge Thank You for having our wedding at the Werribee Open Range Zoo. Me and my beautiful bride Brittany got married at the Werribee Open Range Zoo on the 8/11/2025. The weather was not the greatest and they had a backup plan for the Wedding and it worked out perfectly! The service was very professional and also very caring and looking after our kids and making sure my guests were happy. Was very awesome to see the staff having fun as well. Huge thank you to Tilly and her Assistant Alyssa. Alyssa did an amazing job taking us around the zoo for photos and making sure we had drinks and got food.

Just had our daughters' 21st party at the rainforest room in the zoo, and the whole experience can be summed up as "perfection". Big thanks to the gorgeous Tori, Hailey, Shannon and the team for our party! From communication, service, food, the function room are all first class! It exceeded our expectation.

We contacted RA - Venues at the Melbourne Zoo to hold a function for my husband’s 70th Birthday.
Victoria is the event Coordinator and we found her to be a warm and friendly person who was able to help us with making decisions on the menus and layout for our function in the Rainforest Room.
She is excellent maintaining the highest standard ensuring she returns your calls and organizing the service so it tailored to fit into our time frames and ideas of how you wish to event to run.
On the day Our Function Supervisor Alisa was fantastic.
She was warm and friendly, quietly spoken lady and nothing was too much trouble for her.
She and her staff did a great job everything ran like clockwork.
We Highly recommend RA Venues at the Melbourne Zoo to anyone who is planning an event and our deepest thanks to Victoria, Alisa and the staff for efforts on the day.
Peter and Domenica.
Great place to visit with plenty of things to do , but the highlights were the walking trails though the trees which made the zoo appear bigger than it is. Very well designed and thought out.

My stay at RACV City Club was part of a large group of 18. The staff were super friendly and helpful throughout the three-night stay.

The room was soooo clean, large (with a bath) and absolutely spotless, as was the co-working space and bistro area.

We did dine in the private dining space one night, and the staff were particularly friendly and helpful, and the meal was stunning!

Thank you for a beautiful few days and a relaxing stay.

This place is great. with clean rooms and easy to navigate areas. the bistro is great and there is a 25m pool for the kids. but it is not the best for kids anyway as this is more of an adult hotel. still
amazing though

Recently came to Melbourne for the MotoGP. Had a lovely stay at RACV. Rooms were lovely. Service was great. When I arrived, my room was not ready. They changed me to another room and gave me extra room credit. Breakfast was great every day. Had a meal in the members dining room which was very nice. Also had a meal in the bistro which was great and quite reasonably priced. Staff were friendly and helpful on all interactions I had. Really couldn't ask for much more. Certainly worth consideration as a place to stay if in Melbourne for business or holiday. Worth noting that the wine bar is not open sunday night and the rooftop bar and green are not open on weekends. Maybe they are aimed at the midweek business crowd but does limit options a little if staying in the hotel

Thank you so much RACV City Club for spoiling me for my birthday,  and upgrade to The Retreat, wonderful.  Lotsa extras, Le Petit Gateaux cake, amazing brekky and delicious dinners in the Bistro.  Staff are very friendly, professional and make sure you are pampered and enjoy your stay to the maximum!!!  We're so happy at the RACV City Club.

I went on a tour of the RACV gym in Bourke St Melbourne. The guide pointed out the mens and women’s changing rooms. Only after asking specific questions did it become clear that men are allowed to use the women’s open plan changing rooms. I was also advised that several men do currently use the women’s change room. Had I not asked the right question I would not have known until too late. The gym does not respect women’s privacy dignity and safety.

A unbearable nights!! Imaging your big day and many important people in your life would be here to giving you blessings…. I cannot thank you enough to both Chris Sheldon and Young and most importantly Rebecca who are helping us to organize in such of last minute change… lets starts with
Service -  if you think the high end restaurant provided better service till you here. Whole night chris is there to make sure all the details and request are to be solved. No one I don’t see them the whole night! All i hear from guest is the service is unbelievable!
Food- i am consider myself a very hard core when it come to food. But i told you one thing, the food is bomb… the plates is warm cook to perfection and normally banquet food nothing to wow… grace by CS … impressive …all i can said is ….. we all walk out happy and feel it so much love from Grace by CS and the guest!!  Thank you again Team!!

We recently had our business event at Grace. I cannot speak highly enough about the food, venue and staff and overall vibe. The team went to great lengths to make sure our event run smoothly. The set up of the venue looked amazing and matched our brief, the staff were all friendly and accommodating, and the food was spectacular. I highly recommend this event for your next event.

Had our 2nd event at Grace in Richmond and it was as delightful and impressive as our first event. Loving the vibe, the staff are so well trained and polished and of course the food is an absolute standout. How can the chef pack so much flavour into each dish ?
I’m a fan and highly recommend.

I have been dreaming about my wedding day since I was young as most girls do. The team at Curtis Stone Events made it better than I could have ever imagined. The hospitality was like nothing I have ever seen before. Everything ran seamlessly. It felt like everyone was apart of our family from the very first meeting.
We had an intimate wedding of 40 people and they have all said that it was something else and they still can’t get over the delicious food and prompt service.
