Every bite of food was so flavoursome with textures that just complemented each other so well. Everyone had an exceptional time and we could not be happier that we chose CSE.
5 stars doesn’t seem enough for what we experienced. If you want quality service, amazing food, great company and people who will go above and beyond for you, then this is the company you need for your next event.

Attended one of the “Wagyu Wednesday”  events at Grace by Curtis Stone Events last night. An unbelievably good experience! Food was amazing and the level of service outstanding. Can’t thank our hosts Rebecca and Chris enough. Would highly recommend attending one of these events which I think they hold once a month.

We celebrated our wedding reception here and everything was perfect from start to finish. The staff were incredibly professional and attentive, and they went above and beyond to make sure the night ran smoothly. The food and night view were exceptional and our guests really loved the whole experience.Thank you Lisa, Tim and the entire team for making our big day so memorable!

We had our wedding reception at Vue and it was spectacular. Vue has exceptional food and their service was great (with the exception of interruption during speeches, which we had asked not to do). The view is so amazing which makes the atmosphere great.

The leading up to the event was not to my liking but that’s based on my personal expectations. I wanted to be able to dictate the timeline for the event and was surprised that my wedding had to revolve around Vue’s food and service instead. If you have no problem with this, then this is the venue for you, but if it isn’t, I suggest you find another place and avoid lots of headaches.

Another thing you’d need to consider is that if you’re hiring a DJ they will need to patch up to their system since there’s a max level of volume. I had no issue with this but I was very disappointed when the speakers were failing right at the start for a few minutes.

Vue has their own chairs and tableware for events but you might not like this aesthetic since it’s very modern d and minimalist, I did not like it personally and had to hire multiple things. I’ll attach photos of what they offer and what I hired instead.

There are very strict guidelines around bump in and bump out and I had to pay a lot more to suppliers to accommodate to these guidelines which really hurt my budget, it is something to consider if you do your event there and have to get external suppliers. There was an issue on the night with one of the pick ups, I am not sure whose responsibility it was but I had to pay a fee for the supplier to go bad and pick up the missing items.

We had our tasting a week before and the food was also amazing, we were very pleased. My only complaint is that they sat us on the bar area, which is not set up for having food, and we were all squeezed up in the little tables and it was very hard to eat on the couches. For the amount of money we paid, I was expecting they would sit us at a table in the restaurant.

Our only feedback for the planning and delivery on the night at Vue, is absolutely glowing reviews! Natalie D and Natalie T both helped to book us in and answer all our questions throughout the planning stages.
On the night, we had such an incredible time at Vue for our wedding reception, from the beautiful location, to the delicious food, and your amazing catering team lead by Marie that ran the event so smoothly! My brother, who was MC, commented on how great and flexible Marie was to run the evening on the night, and a shoutout to the waiter Tim - who multiple guests said was so quick with his service and met everyone's request! Our guests have only said how fantastic all your staff were, and were blown away by the sunset over the city.
So thank you! Would highly recommend this venue to friends and family!

The Vue team delivered an amazing wedding celebration perfectly matching the amazing views of Melbourne. Our guests from Melbourne, interstate and overseas haven't stopped talking about the ceremony (which we held at the venue), the food and the service by entire team. A special thanks to the Lisa who organised our event, the chefs for creating such fabulous food celebrating the best of Aussie produce and Tim who ensured everything was delivered to perfection on the night.

We had our wedding reception at Vue Events and it was simply perfect. Carla was an enormous help for us and worked with all of our other vendors to make sure that we had no stresses on the day. The food was obviously sensational and the wines we selected were divine. We had the best service that I think I have ever had in any situation. The view is just to die for, which you can easily tell from the photos.

Photos by Katie Harmsworth

@stateofgracemelbourne 📍 Every Saturday for $80 per person you can enjoy a delicious Bottomless Brunch and Unlimited cocktails and drinks for a 2 hour sitting (either 12-2 or 3-5)! 🌟

Keen for Bottomless cocktails? 🍸 My favourite was the Pornstar Martini and Espresso Martini cocktails 🍹 apart from cocktails there’s also selected wines and beers available!

For brunch you can expect a sharing menu including capanes, salad, chips and pizza too. 💫

The rooftop venue was nicely tucked away and had beautiful views of Southbank. Worth a visit if you’re nearby!

I loooooved this bar! We had a booking so headed to the downstairs bar which was hidden! A great atmosphere and amazing cocktails!! Hard to order as it was a bit busy. Would benefit from a QR coded system. Will absolutely be back! My new favourite bar!!

Great venue and was perfect for a quick meal before heading to an event at Marvel Stadium.
Didn't have a booking but accommodated us. Staff we had interactions with all very friendly.
Venue has a great character and charm about it.
Will definitely return for a longer experience and to check out the rooftop bat and hidden bar.

Nice and spacious city bar that attracts a mixed crowd. Comfortable space and nice restaurants setting up the back. Great little rooftop, and friendly staff. Even let us move some of the table around to suit our group size.

Unfortunately, today the burgersas I ordered arrived at 30 minutes and their presentation was very bad. The potatoes without their basket, hard and burnt, were stuck, the hambuers were unarmed and warm 2 out of 10. I feel like I lost my money from this dinner.

I could eating my chips.

name: The Irish Times Pub
location: 427 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
We came for a meal. Ordering online after scanning the QR code on the table was easy. Drinks came fast and the food was delivered in a reasonable length of time. There were a couple familiar irish brands used on the menu. The food was ok. I had sausage and gravy and it was pretty good.
The place had a steady pace of people entering but they were also leaving just as fast. It was quite noisy at times. The lighting was dim and I suspect it was hiding a lot of cleanliness issues... The stairs up to the second level were very steep and look like a magnet for accidents later in the night. On a plus my Irish wife said the Guinness was better than most in here in Oz.
The food here is fantastic, the lamb chops were perfect, the Wagyu was amazing, the ribs were fantastic. Staff are friendly and the atmosphere is great. Look forward to my next visit

Óró, sé do bheatha abhaile! Dia duit!

Reminds me of a pub near Crogh Park, sadly time and memory have parted company for me to recall its name. Had a Bloomsday breakfast there.

This pub is legit. Would not be surprised if The Citizen was hid up in a snug holding forth.

Guy serving was a trooper, suffering my stupidity for having insufficient funds on my card and I had to load it up. Cheers, mate.

Had the best night here. The staff were all so genuinely friendly, the food (I forgot to take a picture of the mains but they were amazing too) is so good! Such good comfort food. It’s very impressive the effort they put in yet the prices are so reasonable.

The atmosphere of the place is so good too. Very cozy. I recommend.
Nice cosy Irish pub in CBD. good service, good drinks

Came here around 5pm on Tuesday without a booking for their Steak Frites night, but thankfully they still had a high table available for us.

The steak and fries were good! We ordered it with the béarnaise sauce which was so tasty; creamy and herby.

For the drink, I tried the Gaytime Milk Punch and I love it. To me, it tasted like a sweet cough syrup mixed with a light milkshake 😋 if you like sweet cocktails, please give it a shot!

We had the desserts too and they were sooo yummsss. We ordered the Ricotta Cake with cumquat jam, mascarpone cream, and pistachio; a safe choice that you can never go wrong with. We also ordered the Raspberry semifreddo with crispy filo pastry and white chocolate chantilly. If you prefer something flavourful and refreshing, you should try this one instead!!

We also had the workers check in on us once in a while to make sure we’re good with everything. They’re all so friendly and welcoming.

I would definitely come back and maybe check out the different floors as well (especially the music room 😣😣😣)!!!

Came here for their $25 steak night and we absolutely LOVED it.

Great serving size for the price and the meat quality was good. Also tried their bread and oysters which were okay, definitely enjoyed the steak more! The atmosphere is beautiful but it is really loud as it gets very busy so it can be hard to talk to especially if you have bigger groups. Their rooftop bar was also quite nice but minimal space. Great for a girls night or a date night ❤️

We spent a memorable Christmas Day lunch afternoon here. I want to take this opportunity to thank all the kitchen and front of house team who made our day so special. Warm friendly and professional service, fresh, delicious food, awesome wines and cocktails. Thank you especially to Chef Nada who was very understanding of my dietary requirements and to the staff serving my dishes who ensured I didn’t miss out on anything, I thoroughly enjoyed my lunch. An awesome venue with a great vibe and the very best hospo team!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Her Bar: Where the Steak is $25, and the Service is Priceless (and So is Claudia)
I wasn't looking for Her Bar last night, but let's just say destiny (and perhaps a slight case of the munchies) guided me right to its beautifully lit doors. And oh my god, am I glad it did.
If you're looking for a sign to go, consider this it: The $25 Steak Night is practically a public service. I'm talking perfectly cooked steak with a fresh salad that felt less like an afterthought and more like a fabulous companion. It was the kind of dinner that makes you think, "Wait, is this flirting with me?"
And speaking of fabulous companions... I need to talk about the service, specifically a goddess named Claudia and mostly everyone lol. Seriously, the service was so exceptional it felt less like being waited on and more like being expertly charmed. Claudia was a sheer delight—attentive, witty, and basically the human embodiment of that lovely Italian Prosecco I was sipping. The dessert sampler (hello, Semi-Freddo!) was... okay, but honestly, with service and steak that good, they could have served me a bowl of crunchy leaves and I'd still be singing their praises.
The Vibe? It’s the kind of place that makes you feel instantly cooler just by walking in. It’s got a sultry, magnetic energy that makes a casual dinner feel like a date night with yourself.
I stumbled in, but I'll be sprinting back. Five stars—for the perfect steak, the bubbly Prosecco, the gorgeous atmosphere, and especially for Claudia. See you soon, Her Bar!

After reserving well ahead for the Tuesday steak night, we went in here this week. It didn’t disappoint.

The steak was a great quality cut, cooked precisely as requested, with a superb sauce and a great wine selection too (also reasonably priced).

Will happily be back for another round!

name: Hopscotch Melbourne
location: Ground Floor/4 Riverside Quay, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text:
The place is cool, but there’s no service at all, other than the food/drinks runner that deliver your QR code order to your table.
Ordered chips with guacamole (along with other food) and got served cheap packaged chips, very different from the fresh fried ones displayed on the picture on the menu, definitely not worth 18$.
Rats running over the place (don’t believe me? I’ve uploaded a video too), told a staff member who laughed at it, insinuating it was quite common and did nothing about it. Overall very disappointing (and gross).

The ultimate spot for a beer by the Yarra! Hopscotch consistently delivers an amazing experience. They offer an incredible variety of tap options—a true paradise for beer drinkers. The location on Southbank is unbeatable, and the energy is always high; it's always buzzing and filled with people. The happening atmosphere makes it the perfect place to meet friends. Highly recommend this for anyone looking for a lively, top-tier beer garden experience in Melbourne.

Hopscotch Melbourne is an absolute gem on the Southbank! From the moment you step in, you’re welcomed by a vibrant and inviting atmosphere with stunning riverside views. The ambience is lively yet relaxed, making it a perfect spot for a meal with friends, family, or colleagues. The menu offers a fantastic variety of dishes that are fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. We especially enjoyed burgers and each bite was full of taste and cooked to perfection. Portion sizes are generous and ideal for sharing, which makes dining here even more enjoyable.

The service deserves a special mention. The staff were attentive, warm, and professional throughout our visit. They checked in without being intrusive and made us feel genuinely welcome. Combined with the upbeat setting, delicious food, and efficient service, Hopscotch creates an unforgettable dining experience. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy great food in a vibrant Melbourne location!
Amazing cocktails, energetic vibes, and very friendly staff. The Yarra River view at the end makes it even more special! A must-visit spot in Melbourne! ❤️🥰

Have been here countless times over the course of 9 years. The food is always impeccable having never had a disappointing meal. The beer selection and drinks are vast and delicious often with unique specials and options. On the weekends we love the live music and the massive TV makes it great to catch a game for sports from footy to gridiron. Finally we have been impressed with the staff who actually seem to provide service above the bare minimum.

name: The Drunken Poet
location: 65 Peel St, West Melbourne VIC 3003, Australia

reviews.text:
Warm and pleasent welcome when we arrived followed by a quick pour of that long awaited guinness. They say guinness doesn't travel well and I would say thats true based on this pour. A little watery or maybe just thinner than the many pints I have consummed in Dublin. Still it went down easily. I think that the only food item was a toasted sarnie.    They went well with the guinness and were ready in good time. Lots of Irish memorabilia and I guess darkish wood surrounds. There was a fiddleydee group of players and plenty of punters to create the atmosphere. Apparently there was only a multi-gender toilet which was suffering a bit.
Overall I don't know how it got rated in the top 10 Irish pubs in the world outside of Ireland. Its not really traditional Irish from my experience living in Ireland for the last 6 years however it is a good pub and worth a pint or two there. The bar staff were superb.
Siobhan is the most amazing human. The drunken poet will deliver the best damn pint of Guinness (I will fight ppl on this fact) the vibe is amazing 😍 and if you haven't been you really should ❤️✌️

This is a gem of a place! Friendly staff and well poured pints of Guinness. If you're into poetry and literature then you'll enjoy it immensely. A cozy place to chill. They even have a jukebox! Came in for a cheeky pint while walking through the city, and was glad I did! Excellent! *****

Excellent Gunniess.  $14.50.   3.30 pm Tuesday
No doubt busy at night. Girl was making so many tomato and cheese sandwiches (toasted $7.50) it must get busy.

Holy cow - this place is tiny!!!  The heat and people inside was stifling. (Combined with a 5 person musical performance, literally blocking the door) Unfortunately we had to stand outside (no seats) and drink. Staff were friendly, but this wasn't a good visit sadly.

name: Lord Melbourne Hotel
location: 63 Melbourne St, North Adelaide SA 5006, Australia

reviews.text:
My Gawd if you LOVE Lamb Shanks, this is the sit down place for you. What can I say, the atmosphere was good but tbh I was so busy devouring my meal everything else was a blur. Meals came out faster than ever perceived.
Hubby had chicken schnitzel but yeah it’s all about the lamb shanks baby🤩😂
TRY THE LAMB SHANKS - YOU WONT REGRET IT
Thank you to all the super efficient staff that were on Thursday 24 July

Staying in North Adelaide walked to pub. Great service, very friendly and up for a laugh. Food was delicious. Loved the fresh vegetables don't find that very often these days. Great wine range.
Going back tonight for another meal. Hmmmm what to have ❤️❤️❤️

We had a staff end of year celebratory dinner there. We reserved one of their function rooms, so we enjoyed the private vibes and the fact that we were able to play our own music. The staff were really friendly and service was pretty quick. Everyone enjoyed the food and drinks. It was a good night!

Celebrated my wife’s 50th birthday yesterday , can’t thank Steph and her team enough for making our evening special. Steph is fantastic to work with and a lovely lady who went above and beyond to make my wife’s birthday special, the platters were amazing, can’t recommend them enough, thanks again Steph

The BEST food, service and coffee to die for.
Our favourite place in SA by far.

What an absolute gem of a pub. Had a quick dinner here, starting with Scotch Eggs, which were authentic and oh so tasty. (Gooey egg inside and VERY tasty tomato sauce. 5 stars just for that!) The Cumberland sausages and mash were delicious with just the right amount of mushy peas. Crumbed pork with mushroom gravy as a choice was huge and satisfied a hungry traveller. Felt like I was back in Old Blighty, and DEFINITELY worth seeking out if you want some great comfort food. Staff also excellent and friendly. Loved it!!

The Mog Champ sends his regards.

I love myself a pub feed and Sherlock Holmes does your typical pub food in an excellent way. We were a large group and they served us in a timely manner as well. I shared the pork ribs and the baker street roast which was excellent.

Cannot comment on their drinks as most of us did not order them but I did not recognise most of the brands! Loved the juicy and fine ribs the most, thanks for the food Sherlock Holmes!

Mogliest Regards,
Your Mog Champ

As a Brit in Australia, good pub grub can be hard to come by in Oz but this place has it. The food packs flavour and tastes just like home and really good value in today's climate. Recommended for everyone, especially the Roast of the day, scotch eggs and the sausage and mash
Found an amazing place to have our dinner tonight. It was fantastic, great foods, best British beer, great service and fabulous environment. Highly recommended. 👍

Fantastic pub and eatery. Very accomodating and friendly, made our family feel very welcome and relaxed. Food was excellent, beer variety was great, could have stayed all day whilst the weather outside was wide. Would highly recommend, and we’ll be back.

Good job I didn’t know about this place when I lived in Melbourne back in 2012 for 5 years, would have been my local for sure

Such an amazing space! The atmosphere is so calm. Service & food was incredible - we particularly loved the pizza! Great place for lunch with friends, or even with kids! The natural vibes & layout with all the plants definitely gives it a unique touch.

While the place is quite new and it was buzzing with crowds and live music, the food were very average.
Lamb shoulder - meat was too dry.
Sides coleslaw - feels like packaged one from supermarket
Pork chop - way too dry (overdone)

Service - pretty much non existent. Not sure if its because weekend after NYE. Or it’s usually that busy hence staff were just way too busy to serve. We asked for tap water and glasses, was told to go to the bar ourselves. The staff at the bar was not helpful when we asked where we can get bottle of water and glasses.
Our table was still wet and sticky from previous guests. We had to wait over half hour until we can get staff to re-wipe table.

Ordering and payment all done via QR code.

Very loud music, so quite difficult for chat/talking.

Great vibes, cool glass roof and great food esp the parm. The service though feels very self-service when it’s not supposed to be, and the waitstaffs weren’t the friendliest too. Will come back here for the vibes though - amazing for both lunch and dinner or just some drinks with friends.
I went to have lunch at Garden State Hotel, and I was served by Ariadna, who was very attentive and helpful, always with a smile. The staff’s service was excellent, and the food was good too.

Beautiful hotel & super popular. Aria is waitress extraordinaire with a beautiful smile.

A man hit a woman here on Friday night. I helped one of the bar staff escort the man out. In the confusion the bouncers kicked me out. I simply wanted to finish my beer but they wouldn't let me in.

I returned today, on Wednesday, and they made amends by offering me a lovely class of Shiraz. I'm a simple man and that is fine for me.

I've just notched them down to four stars on atmosphere as I don't think the security work as a team. The head bouncer may be the problem.

Anyway it's a beautiful place, and you won't be disappointed if you stay here, go for meal, have some drinks or hit the dance floor.

Dropped into the Duke of Wellington on a Sunday arvo for a drink in the main bar before heading up to the rooftop for a meal.

Despite the drizzle and bursts of sunshine, the rooftop felt welcoming — plenty of space, greenery, and a relaxed vibe. We did have to wipe down our own table, but once settled, it was a great spot to unwind.

The roast of the day was perfect, with rich, flavoursome gravy, and the chicken parmy hit the mark too. The salad was a bit soggy, but honestly, who comes here for the salad? Great Sunday atmosphere overall.

New Year's Eve at the Duke of Wellington.  This was a last minute arrangement and I am so glad we booked here. Meal, drinks and dancing. Plus we got to see the fireworks 🎆.  Arrived just before 8pm booking, had a drink downstairs.  Listened to a local artist in the bar section before heading to the restaurant upstairs.  Me and my family were seated at a beautiful table and a lovely chap named Sam served us throughout our meal. The food and wine was delicious 😋 then we headed to the rooftop bar were we continue our New Year's Eve festivities. Managed to get home by 3am 🫣😆
Great night had by all 🎉🥂🍾🥳

Buy new crockery, you wouldn’t serve guests chipped plates and glasses at home, why then in your restaurant? Perhaps arrange your seating so that a table of two is not stuck beside a large noisy group. The food was just okay, nothing to rave about. Thankfully the staff were friendly.

We had a great meal on the rooftop at the Duke of Wellington! The service was fast and friendly and the staff made us feel really welcome. The venue is spacious with a great atmosphere, and the meals were both tasty and generous. Perfect spot for a relaxed lunch or dinner with friends. Highly recommend!

It was ok. Staff were attentive, keen to take your order but the food took a while to appear, but was pretty good. I had the chicken tikka marsala, and enjoyed it a lot. Others had the gnocchi, apparently quite rich, cheese burger which looked ordinary, parmi pretty good. Chips were ok, side salad small, drinks reasonably priced

name: Left Bank Melbourne Restaurant & Cocktail Bar
location: Riverside of, 1 Southbank Blvd, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text:
There's a reason why this place is always busy when we walk past and there's more to it than the location.

Although we didn't quite get the table we requested on the night, it was Valentines days so they were excused. The service and food more than made up for it. The waiters were all friendly and very attentive.

The food was far better than we expected with almost every dish being of high quality and the steak was perfectly cooked as requested.

Did the bottomless brunch. Super fun and the cocktail just were really nice. We also got to try each of the mocktails and they were pretty good too! The food is also super yummy. My only criticism (which is really a preference) would be that i would have liked small shared meals (like entres or smth) to pick at throughout the experience rather than just 1 meal at the start. Again, a preference but I think it would have made it more fun to share both the drinks and picky bits with friends. But the food they did have was really good.

Left Bank Melbourne delivered on great food, excellent service, and a fantastic atmosphere. It’s a great spot for a night out with friends, a romantic dinner, or just to enjoy good food and drinks in a vibrant Southbank setting. Highly recommended!

Have been here a few times for lunch, bottomless brunches….  First Christmas without Dad… Mum, Sis & Myself had the most spectacular Christmas Lunch.  Service, Food, Atmosphere all on point.  AMAZING!!! Merry Christmas 🎁🎄 🎅

We had a fantastic lunch experience.
The food was refined with balanced flavours.
It was a lovely dining atmosphere and the serving staff were highly accomodating.

name: The Lion Hotel
location: 161 Melbourne St, North Adelaide SA 5006, Australia

reviews.text:
Great specialised wine dinner location.
Special winery showcasing events are a regular feature here. Priced around the $130 to $150 pp for 4 courses and a paired wine tasting - the experience is excellent.
Casual restaurant is good too.

We ordered the oysters naturals. They came with quite a few lime wedges and the oysters were huge and delicious. Probably the best natural oysters in value that we’ve had.
I had the pork belly and my wife had the lamb Ragu and we would both say that they were excellent and generous in servings.
The food here is fantastic and really good value which is quite strange considering the ambience of the restaurant feels very up market and it should actually be more expensive than what it is.
Except for one thing, the staff here are not as professional as the menu, but are still quite friendly.
It feels like a five star establishment.
The food is 5 star but the pricing is very good value.

In my experience has decreased since last visit.  Never thought a schnitzel would be an option in the restaurant.  Male server who worked in Italy 🇮🇹 was fantastic. Sommelier recommended wine was great.  Female younger generation had no humour or empathy to their customers.  Not eager to return.

The Lion Hotel is an Adelaide institution that never fails to deliver, and on this night it didn’t disappoint. The scotch fillet steak was cooked to perfection, served over a smooth bed of potato mash that complemented it beautifully. The chicken schnitzel was also spot on crispy, tender, and full of flavour. Beyond the food, the atmosphere was exactly what you’d expect from this much-loved pub: lively, welcoming, and full of character. A true Adelaide favourite that continues to impress.

First time here and dropped in on our way to the hills woth our puppers, very early in the morning, all muddy and totally not in the appropriate attair, ordered a half strength latte and they nailed it!
Fantastic place wish I lived locally to enjoy a few more early mornings here!
Thanks for the great coffee
And the water bowls

Adelaide Klee Kai Spitz

name: The Albion Rooftop
location: 172 York St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia

reviews.text: Just phenomenal fun on a Friday night after the salsa dance classes. Great crowd, great music, great service. We even ordered dirty martini’s from the bar and the girl hadn’t made them before but did a fantastic job. They were delicious!
Great atmosphere! Perfect for events. Friendly staff who went above and beyond to make our night out comfortable
One of the best Rooftops in Melbourne. Loved the vibes! Food was amazing. My boyfriend said the Parma was one of the best he’s ever had. Staff super lovely. We’ll definitely be back soon!!!

I love the vibe, the music, the people but most of all the food. The chicken popcorn is out of this world, the chips remind me of the chips in London. The empanadas are awesome as well.
Love it
I recommend this place so much
It is a pleasant three-level space where you can enjoy unforgettable nights. It's incredible—I found a wide variety of Latin and Australian music. It's amazing how many people gather there.

I reall like this pub, great location, atmosphere and certain seats have what would probably be the most iconic view in Melbourne (Flinders Station). Probably my favourite pub in the city. I went there before and AFL game at the MCG and it was great, six month later went back and it was much less busy but still had a charming atmosphere

Stepping back in time here in Melbourne to a very famous pub, but unfortunately, it was the last stop for a lot of young Australians, who sacrifice our lives in World War II. It’s an amazing place and a tribute to those men and women..

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Young & Jackson – Historic Melbourne Icon

Young & Jackson is a true Melbourne classic. Full of history, character, and great atmosphere, it’s one of those pubs that everyone should visit at least once. Right across from Flinders Street Station, it’s the perfect spot for a drink and a dose of old Melbourne charm.

The famous “haunted” stories add to the fun – you can definitely feel the heritage and age in the building, with creaky floors and that slightly eerie vibe in certain corners. Whether you’re here for the history, the legends, or just a good pub experience, it never disappoints.

A must-visit for locals and visitors alike! 🍻👻

Well …. “DONT JUDGE A BOOK  BY ITS COVER!” .. What a magnificent heritage hotel , four floors of an era gone by , and extremely popular with the locals … drinks. Meals , including some excellent deserts …. And location.  Straight opposite Flinders Station 👍👍👍

T bone steak was tremendous. Very well prepared. Only minor hiccup in my opinion was the lack of sides. The fish chips were great as well, juicy and fresh. Oh, no chili sauce in a steakhouse which served fries seems kinda silly. Well if anyone of the management read this, they know what to do. Service by the staff was tremendous. Would certainly come again.

We had a fantastic time at Captain Melville after our walking tour of Melbourne!

The atmosphere is super cool, and the food is great as well.

A great stop with a bit of Melbourne history as well. Highly recommend it for groups of all sizes,

In my opinion this is a tourist trap. Booked a party of six for lunch. Waited at the inside front door to be seated. Passed by wait staff approximately five times who didn’t acknowledge us standing there. When I asked who we should speak to about being seated waiter pointed to a person behind the bar pouring beer. He appeared disinterested however did arrange for someone to seat us. Burger ($28) was small and tasted like a frozen burger microwaved and then had lettuce added. Small thin beef patty. No egg. No tomato. Little taste. Small amount of fries for the price.  Had to ask staff to wipe table after previous guests had left and we were seated. Most staff would be more suited to the undertaking vocation rather than the service industry, especially bar staff. There are plenty of better establishments in Melbourne you could treat yourself to in my opinion.

A lovely quirky old pub in downtown Melbourne. It was a great find on Boxing Day 2025 serving lunch when not much else was open! We thoroughly enjoyed our mash and snags, topped off with a huge apple crumble! Delicious and reasonably priced.  Apparently there will be some upcoming renovations, but as it is, we’d return!

Captain Melville one of the best pub in the CBD. Selection of beers on Taps is good. They even have Guinness on taps, which is rare in Melbourne.
The food was wonderful, simple and delicious.
The service was great, the bar tender is ever ready to recommend the beer. They even give you a taste before you order, to make sure you like the beer you are drinking.
Great food and friendly service by all staff - the sirloin was delicious. The Irish cream tiramisu and rhubarb crumble were to die for - generous servings.

We were visiting Melbourne for a celebration of our 40th Wedding Anniversary and found this awesome place near to our hotel.
Staff were polite and friendly and the process for getting a meal was really efficient. A great choice of beers and food on the menu.
With the lovely warm evening eating outside was a real pleasure.
Highly recommended for the quality of food and drinks available.

Gorgeous classic English pub in the heart of the CBD.

Pros: The food is reliably good and they have a good range of drinks. Great atmosphere inside and lovely outdoor seating (weather providing). Super friendly staff.

Cons: The food, while good, isn't exceptional and it is definitely not a cheap venue. Food service can also take quite a long time (upwards of 30 minute wait).

We had a beautiful Christmas lunch today, three courses, Entree of King Prawns and Shrimp, one of us had Soup served with Sour dough on the side. Mains were Beef short rib which fell off the bone, one of us had Ham with baked vegetables and gravy. Desserts were Plum Pudding, Cheesecake and Trifle. The whole meal was delicious. It was our second Christmas lunch in Melbourne and both were at The Elephant and Wheelbarrow and both were excellent which is why we returned. We are from Sydney and have come down for The Ashes. Great food and wonderful staff. Highly recommended.

This was an incredible english pub, with an atmosphere that is hard to find in the cities over. We came in to try a ruby ale and were blown away by the service Amelia provided, which is the reason we actually decided to write this review. After discussing a beer and the flavours and whether we would enjoy it or not she offered us a taste of both and while neither worked, and I ended up with an asahi,  it was the hospitality and service that I'd come to expect from a 5 star facility - not your local pub. The service made the difference.
Would recommend. 10/10.

Very disappointing experience at The Elephant & Wheelbarrow.

We waited ONE HOUR for our food — three Chicken Parmas — and when they finally arrived, they were cold. To make things worse, our last pizza didn’t even come out with the rest of the meals and arrived about 15 minutes later, so we couldn’t even eat together.

When we raised the issue with the manager, Liam, his response was even more frustrating. He confidently told us that this was “normal” and refused to offer any compensation or attempt to resolve the situation. There was no apology, no effort to make things right — just complete dismissal.

Waiting that long for cold food and then being told it’s normal is something I have never experienced in Melbourne.

We had a very nice pub meal here. The dining room upstairs is a welcoming space with an open kitchen. Good acoustics made for a pleasant meal, even though the restaurant was busy. I recommend booking if possible as it is popular. Service was efficient and the staff seemed happy to be there. The menu is a good mix of pub favourites done well, catering to vegans, vegetarians, and meat-eaters alike.

Went for steak night with a group of 10. Service was quick and friendly. Food was amazing, great vegetarian options (zucchini salad was incredible). Everything was reasonably priced. Great atmosphere too.

Lots of stairs that made my knees creak, great beers on tap and a raspberry sour that was mwaah! We ate tasty cakes, paw paw salad, and rigatoni - all wonderful. The very hip crowd were friendly and forgiving of my old suburban self. A very pleasant afternoon.
Had a very tasty Christmas Eve lunch. Great food with nice service. The chips were amazeballs. We will be back.

Came here for the first time after hearing great things, and it absolutely lived up to the hype. The food was outstanding. I had the chicken schnitzel. It was perfectly crunchy on the outside with juicy, tender chicken inside. The coleslaw was also excellent, none of that sickly heavy mayo, more like a fresh elevated take on sauerkraut.

For dessert, I had the sorbet, which was surprisingly creamy and delicious. It genuinely exceeded my expectations given how hit-or-miss sorbet can be. I also tried a non-alcoholic wine which hit the spot. It was not overly sweet or heavy, just really well balanced and had a sparkling pomegranate flavour.

The atmosphere was great too. It’s a very old pub with almost Victorian-era vibes with the candles and antique paintings. Service was excellent. Our waitress was friendly, prompt, and really knowledgeable about the menu. I’ll definitely be back to try more dishes.

A great surprise to discover an underground pub I’ve walked past dozens of times without noticing. Our group of 14 was served quickly, and everyone loved their meals — from steak and parma to calamari and fries, sandwiches, and burgers. The atmosphere was lively but not too loud for a team lunch, though it may vary at other times. Really impressed and would highly recommend!

Great choice of British beers (including Hobgoblin which is one of my favourites). Also nice food menu with many British classics and cozy atmosphere. Prices are reasonable too. But it gets busy on most evenings so I'd recommend booking a table in advance for dinner specially o  weekends.
Good pub. Hidden away in the Block Arcade. Food was decent. We had a chicken curry and mushroom pate. Food was nice and filling. Good value.

You could write a novel about the food
Our table had a mixture of dishes and all of them were exceptional !
Fish was cooked beautifully, hamburgers had lots of taste, lamb kofta delicious and the roast with Yorkshire pudding was to die for
Staff, we’re friendly with drinks and meals coming out quickly and prices are reasonable
There is a lift available if you can’t navigate stairs, this venue is definitely worth a drop in
Delightful little English styles pub . The atmosphere was lovely and the pub was well organised and had great ambience. Had the pie and peas which was very tasty and it was a great experience.

name: The Joint Bar
location: Level 1/35 Elizabeth St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Great place for the vibes but food could use an uplift. Happy hour is awesome with great drinks.

We got the big pork bao which was nice, way too much mayo though. Some smaller baos too.

They sold out of most starters which was disappointing but we did get the Japanese fried and the fried tofu. One of the sold out dishes was substituted for dumpling. They were nice enough.

Overall a good experience and looks like a fun pace in the evenings.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Incredible vibe and an all-round great night!
The Joint Bar has such a fun, welcoming atmosphere — the staff were super friendly and attentive, and the drinks were top-notch. Perfect spot for catching up with friends or starting a night out. Music, lighting, and overall energy were spot on. Definitely one of my favourite bars around — can’t wait to come back! 🍸🔥

Your weekend plans just got a bao upgrade for $45pp for 90 mins of bottomless baos + drinks and a round of sides @thejointbar_melb on Elizabeth Street… trust me, it’s a vibe

The baos? Next level delicious. The crispy fried chicken bao was juicy with the perfect crunch, the char siu pork belly was melt-in-your-mouth good, the Korean brisket packed so much flavour, and the tandoori pumpkin was a surprise standout — smoky, soft & full of spice

The drinks hit just right too — the spicy margaritas had the perfect kick, the Aperol Spritz was super refreshing, and even the virgin mojito was so fresh and zesty

The service was on point — fast, friendly, and super welcoming. Add a live DJ spinning bangers, fun games inside, and a cool, buzzing weekend crowd… this is exactly where you want to be with friends

📍Elizabeth St, Melbourne
💰$45pp | 90 mins bottomless baos + drinks
🎶 Weekend DJ sets | 🕺 Games & good vibes only

🍹JOINT BAR x BAO PROJECT🍹

Got a change to attend joint bar and bao projects collab along with a drag bingo at the end of the night! Food was super tasty, loved how everything was decently portioned. Flavours were on point, loved the fish and chips and ribs! 😋

Drinks were also tasty, loved that it wasn’t just filled with ice! 🍹Drag bingo looked so fun as well, great vibes and great food! ☺️

Featured:
- dorilocos
- coconut prawn toasted baos w/ green curry sour cream
- crispy zucchini chips w/ dashi yoghurt
- schiucin calamari w/ tartare ranch
- apricot fried chicken
- westholme sirloin
- la galbi
- Japanese beer battered fish & chips
- katsu eggplant parmagiana
- Hawaiian Huli huli chicken bbq

Thank you Joint Bar & The Bao project for organising the invite! 💌

I came to give The Joint Bar a second chance and needless to say, it was not too bad. The bottmlsss baos are $45pp and it is 90mins of all kinds of mini baos.

We’re not big eaters so we opted for some à la carte instead & this is what we ordered:
Mini Bao ($18.5)
- cheese burger
- Nashville chicken
- Korean pulled brisket
Homies Tater Tots ($13.5)
Katsu chicken curry ($27.5)

The food was pretty much the same as when we had it the first time. It was neither amazing nor was it horrible. We did like the Nashville chicken and the Korean pulled brisket mini baos. We found those to be the most flavourful. The cheeseburger mini baos were a little bit disappointing, I think because it had BBQ sauce It just didn’t work with the chargrillef beef. The chicken Katsu curry was okay, I think the rice was a tad bit dry.
Overall, the vibe was really good, but I think the food can definitely be worked on.

Wow wow wow what a spot! Great food and amazing service! They had a medical emergency and still managed to provide such high service! Loved our server Harvey! Wish him well!
Also can’t forget the rooftop and toilet views!! Stunning! Thank you so much! What a beautiful experience!!

We spent the afternoon at this beautiful rooftop bar, and it turned into such a special experience. With Melbourne’s Eureka Tower gleaming in the sun, the Yarra River flowing below, and the buzz of Southbank all around, the view was simply stunning. The vibe was relaxed and welcoming, the food was delicious, and everything felt effortlessly elegant. It wasn’t just a lovely afternoon. It felt like a royal escape high above the city.

Such a great rooftop bar for a fun time out with your friends. Fast and friendly service.

Our foods were also amazing as well.

Highly recommend to check out their creative cocktails!!

Amazing experience at Fleet. Stunning views of the Yarra River and the South Bank. Comfortable table seating and beautifully decorated. Staff was fantastic and understands hospitality. Food was superb! I loved the fried chicken, non greasy with a tasty dipping sauce. The buratta with roasted pumpkin and raisins was soooo delicious and the charred octopus was YUM. I’ll be frequenting this bar 😁

Staying at the Vibe Hotel for work so dropped into Fleet Rooftop Bar at the end of my day. Nice interior, friendly professional staff, tasty food and great views. I was there for happy hour from 3pm to 6pm.

name: Rooftop Bar
location: Curtin House, level 7/252 Swanston St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Rooftop Bar — Curtin House 🍻

Extremely busy — lucky if you can find a spot. Went around 5–6pm.

Drinks are reasonable with good options.
Fries are really nice (thin style).
They have burger options too, but didn’t try.

Most people sit on the stair-style seating.
Great views and a fun vibe overall.

A local friend brought me here. The bar is hidden on the rooftop of Curtin House, so you wouldn’t spot it without knowing.

The space is open and airy, with different seating areas that feel almost like a little playground for all personalities. Sitting here, I got to people-watch so many styles — fashion, haircuts, and nationalities. If you enjoy people-watching, you’ll love this spot.

The menu has a wide range: cocktails, mocktails, beers, and bar snacks like burgers, fries, mac and cheese, etc. Quality matches the price. I ordered the One Way Ticket (AUD 22) — totally my style: refreshing pandan notes, a touch of lime, and a creamy coconut finish.

The view faces the surrounding CBD buildings, slightly blocking the sky, but on warm sunny days, you can probably still catch the sky changing colors at sunset.

What a vibe.

Came here for a beer or for some food, whatever they had. When we got upstairs (level 7) on the balcony, there was a nice bar and some food offerings as well. There was a special for $22 for a Cheeseburger/Mushroom Burger, some frieds and a can of Balter. We got two and it was outstanding.

The dude who served us was super chill and it was a great experience. I would highly recommend dropping in during Mon-Fri working hours, as I imagine it would be packed otherwise.

Thank you to everyone and I look forward to visiting again soon! 😊

The first rooftop bar I visited in Melbourne and it was such a great vibe on Friday evening when everyone was done with their works and ready to get drunk.

The place is a bar on the seventh floor of Curtin House and it’s not the fancy one, which makes it one of the casual spot for a drink with a full view of the city skyline. They have happy hours as well.

I can tell it definitely fills up fast when the weather is nice.
This place is quirky and fun - great blueberry spritz! Gets hugely busy but worth it. Worth waiting for the lift as the seven flights of stairs are a lot!

name: The Valiant Cocktail Bar
location: Level 1/412 Little Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Great service from Anna, Tina and Clement! They were friendly, attentive, and made our night enjoyable. The cocktails were beautifully made and tasted amazing. Thank you for the excellent experience.
Special thanks for clement who was awesome the all night ! We’ll definitely come back! 🍸

Bottomless brunch was amazing, drinks was coming in and didn’t have to wait. Drinks after drinks. If you’re looking forward for bottomless alcoholic beverages. Staff is amazing as well and very social.

We love Anna!!! She was an incredible hostess! The music was great if you love old school RnB
I feel the drinks were too sugary and watered down. It really depends who you’re with, because I had a great experience with my group of friends.

I was here tonight to celebrate my new job and my bestie's graduation and I was livid by poor quality of food and drinks.
Throughout our stay not a single RNB Songs were played and we were seated in the corner behind the main bar and we couldn't hear anything which was the highlight of this brunch and why we paid 70$ each for. Like watered down cocktails jar especially the lemon cello was terrible and 2 burrata cheese and 2 SLICES of margarita pizza with 2 tiny sausage rolls??
Common do better. Absolutely devastating to have chosen this place to celebrate a special night for us.

Great pace to unwind.
Great place to spend your after work hours.
Anna,exceptional customer service,makes you feel at home.very accomodating makes you feel you feel a royalty.

Would recomend this place for every person in melb to have a good time or just unwind.
Happy hour is 7:30 pm to 9:30pm.after that,Every hour is happy hour at Valiant Bar.

I chose the spot to celebrate two of our best friends' birthdays and it's the best decision. The four of us enjoyed every bit of it. Will go back again in here. It's an exclusive place and booking in advance can guarantee your spot on busy days. Loved the theatrical presentation of cocktails.♥️♥️

The moment you step inside, it feels like entering another dimension—warm, enchanting with an old-fashioned charm.
There’s a wide selection of drinks, catering to different tastes and preferences.
The staff are attentive and friendly, offering great service without ever making you feel uncomfortable.

What a hidden gem in the heart of Melbourne! I was lucky to be told about it as I was only in the city for several days so there were only as many places I could visit and this one was definitely in the top. The whole idea of having the door in the middle of nowhere, great selection of drinks, amazing ambience and service - I believe Sharon( too many cocktails to remember:)))) was very lovely and helpful and all the other staff members were amazing. Loved the cocktails and whiskey and cheese tasting and their home made baguette was so yummy. Definitely one place not to miss in Melbourne.

My wife and I had an awesome time. A gentleman with a moustache served us the quintessential package and had excellent presentation skills and later recommended a fantastic Whiskey (it was Scottish aged in smoked barrels I wish I remembered the name..)We’ve visited a few times over the years it a great establishment.

Haven't been down to Eau De Vie for awhile. Me and my partner went there on Saturday night (13/09), atmosphere was buzzing with lovely people.
Staff are amazing as always, wiped down the tables twice as we sat down.

We didn't order food as we had dinner beforehand. We ordered 3 drinks.

Don Giovanni - citrus floral champagne style fizz - Delicious!!

Tomorrow's Party - Watermelon, lime, banana with henny VS cognac - bit strong but punchy and fruity. Great notes together.

Doc Mason's Dragon - Pineapple, lime, Dragon fruit, coconut water with tequila - And what a small performance beforehand. Fire torch dusted with cinnamon (I think) to create a fire breathing dragon) - Would definitely order again!

Overall, such a great night, lovely staff, and ambience.

Must Try and visit. Many drinks on the menu, you can't go wrong!!

name: Santana Rooftop Bar
location: Level 3/169 Melbourne Pl, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Awesome rooftop cigar bar. Service is great, negroni's tasty, they sell cigars and you can BYO cigars. Olive served cold with no oil. Could have topped up with warm olives in oil.

2nd time in the venue with my love. Nothing more special than a cigar with the elegant and relaxing atmosphere at the Santana. Only overcomes by the amazing service of miles. Every single moment worth of any cigar spent with great people all around

The service and drinks are OK

Aperol Spritz excellent.

The location and atmosphere is a bit disappointing compared to other rooftop bars. Not much of a view.

We were so taken care of here. The guys behind the bar really put in the effort.

Especially when it came to discussing the different textures and flavours of the whisky.

The rooftop itself was great. West facing, unspoilt, plenty of sunshine.

I do wish there was more beer options, which I understand is silly at a whisky bar, but just to really cover all wants and tastes.

Overall though, we had a ripper time

This rooftop bar is an absolute gem! The atmosphere is unbeatable, with a perfect blend of chic elegance and vibrant energy. The music sets the mood just right, and let's talk about that city view - breathtaking! The drinks are crafted with precision and taste divine. Can't recommend this place enough for a memorable night out!
If you like hot weather you can go there. Mostly outdoor. During my visit is 33 degrees. I sit indoor which is half indoor. The cocktails I can said is not bad. Just go try.
Great little spot with excellent tunes and cocktails. Very friendly staff made us feel welcome.

We came here for drinks after our Christmas lunch. The service and atmosphere were great. It was an extremely hot day and we were seated upstairs, it would have been nice to have some sort of outdoor cooling system in place for a little bit of breeze. Overall experience was great. Thank you for hosting!
Not sure how much we spend as the bill was paid by the company and I know we definitely had a reservation.

Love the atmosphere here, especially as the sun went down and the lights became more pronounced. Cocktails were all well-balanced, kinda fruity and all very pretty.
Truffle toasted sandwiches were awesome!

Tucked away in a cozy laneway, Union Electric is such a gem in Melbourne! The rooftop bar has a super chill vibe with string lights, colorful tropical decor, and a casual open-air setup — perfect for warm nights and good conversations.

We ordered a bottle of Jublé Prosecco to start, which was crisp, vibrant, and very easy to enjoy. Their tiki-style cocktails were fun, refreshing, and beautifully presented — love the attention to detail with the paper umbrellas and fresh garnishes!

Service was friendly and laid-back, and the overall atmosphere made us feel right at home. Whether you’re after great cocktails, a lively rooftop setting, or just somewhere to hang with friends, this place is well worth a visit.

Would definitely come back for the drinks and the vibes. 🍸✨

name: Nick and Nora's Melbourne
location: 80 Collins Street via, 11 Benson Wk, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: We stopped by Nick and Nora’s for a casual afternoon drink and ended up loving it so much we booked a table to come back later that night for dinner and cocktails with friends. The team are incredibly knowledgeable, genuinely attentive, and super friendly. The menu is bold and creative, and almost every cocktail arrives with a little bit of theatre, making the whole experience feel special, playful, and memorable

One of my favourite bars in Melbourne — it’s always lively, so it’s best to make a reservation in advance.
The drink menu is quite unique. I like the cheese and meat board, though the baby octopus was just average.
I also ordered cocktails named “Kyoto” and “Asa Akira,” served in bamboo and panda-shaped glasses. I do wish they wouldn’t label them with Japanese names, as these elements are representations of Chinese culture.
Other than that, everything was great.

An inconspicuous door in an otherwise ordinary lane leads you up some stairs into Nick & Nora's.
The art deco speak-easy designed with a bar backed by an extensive botte range of whiskeys. It's not  limited to the whiskey finatics but also an assortment of creative cocktails, beers & wine.
The $5 happy hour bubbles started our night off. I then went for an adventure in their whiskey app to find my dram. Meanwhile my companion ventured into the cocktails.
The service from Andrew, Charlotte & Connor was friendly and enthusiastic. They were knowledgable for what they served. I even challenged them to find me a dram within some criterion, to which their selection was delightful and delicious.
Highly recommended if you're in Melbourne and want to chill with a beverage and easy relaxation.

The venue itself is lovely…. Ambience nice…. Greeting from young lady was excellent ! We were seated at the bar with menus and I requested a wine/champagne menu…. Never arrived. We waited and waited and waited and finally we were approached asking if we had been served !
We ordered drinks a couple of small plates to share…… wait time was LONG !
Yes it was a Friday night and it was extremely busy…… but service and a smile are FREE !

Drinks eventually arrived with one dish……. Had to ask for the 2nd dish, which they clearly forgot !

Drinks were nice…. Would have ordered another BUT didn’t really have the chance to
Order one given not one staff member approached us again !

Requested the bill which arrived in seconds ! If only the service was as prompt as the bill request !

Disappointed and probably won’t recommend or revisit

You have such wonderful, attentive staff. Lucas truly made my birthday feel special. I ordered Passion Project and when the bubble burst not long after it was served, he so kindly remade it twice so I could capture that moment for my birthday, which meant a lot to me.

He even came back with a surprise tasting for my partner and me, which was completely unexpected and incredibly thoughtful. I’ve worked in VIP services in luxury hotels before and that level of genuine care really stands out. Thank you, Lucas, for making my day feel so special. You’re an absolute legend!

I pre-booked a table at Loop Roof and went with all my girlfriends — the staff were so lovely from the moment we walked in! The vibes were vibing, the food was amazing, and even when the kitchen was closing, they still made sure we had some hot chips to snack on.

One thing I loved is that they don’t use QR codes — you actually get to talk to the staff, get recommendations, and it just makes the whole experience feel so much more personal. None of us drink alcohol, but the staff went above and beyond with mocktails. There wasn’t even a set menu for it, and they were happy to make anything we requested or completely surprise us, and every single drink was delicious. Such a fun and thoughtful touch!

The food itself was also really good, and the rooftop on Level 4 is absolutely stunning. The staff even helped take photos of us and let us vibe out upstairs with our own little mini photoshoot.

We had such an incredible night and felt so well looked after. Would 100% recommend Loop Roof for a chill evening or a night out with mates. Will definitely be coming back!!
The atmosphere was great and the food was amazing. I’ll definitely come back again! 😊
The food was absolutely delicious, the atmosphere was great, and the chef was really cute. The service was excellent too!! 😊

Was not a fan of the upper rooftops music at all. Has the potential to be a cool spot, but playing Katy Perry Firework and Turn down for what - sent me straight out the door. Venues need to assess music that people today actually enjoy. Not 2012 radio music.  Other than that it was a cute spot.

The best restaurant when I stayed in Melbourne. Everything’s delicious, I’ve here for 3 times. The people here are really nice to me❤️ I definitely will choose this restaurant as my first destination when I back to Melbourne next time.

name: Good Heavens Rooftop Bar
location: level 2/79 Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Great atmosphere, lots of sheltered areas and umbrellas in the event of Melbourne weather. Food was prompt and cocktails all well made. Staff were very friendly.

Beautiful place, especially the rooftop. I’d highly recommend it if you’re planning a gathering with friends or a bar-style night out. We went on a Sunday afternoon and it was very busy. We wanted to order drinks with some light snacks, but on that part of rooftop you can only order drinks no snacks at all, not even chips. Because of that, we didn’t stay long and ended up leaving.

I love this rooftop bar, it has a good view of city buildings, it's close distance to various theatres which is how i discovered it in the first place. It has a great vibe, lovely cocktails and a cool pre-show spot to hang out with your friends before heading off to your theatre performance. One of my fave city go to's. My only 1 complaint is that our mojito was overloased with ice and not much liquid at all and at $20+ dollars, i did expect better.

2025: One of the many good choices for a rooftop bar in the city, gets very busy.
Tables can be booked, but can get hectic ordering drinks at the bar.
Another note, is that they don't serve energy drinks/mixers.
***

Great bar, nice selection of drinks to choose from. kids can be there until 9pm. which is not bad idea . beside that the foods was nice but maybe have a bit more variety beside that is a good place for drinks

name: Blossom Rooftop Bar
location: Pullman Melbourne City Centre, Level 14/265 Little Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3004, Australia

reviews.text: Pizza was great. Got served two warm ciders and even ask they said the weather was too hot. Got there with a booking and someone was at our assigned seats with our name and timing on it but the person didn’t care. Took about 15mins for staff to even notice us and gave us another seat. Staff there are great but I fear their system sucks. What’s the point of booking and everyone just sits where they like anyways. The names and times on the table didn’t mattered and there’s no one there to great you when you get up there. A slightly better system would make this place a hit for sure! Especially after we sat down, watching 1 lady make all the pizza was incredible!

I had an awesome night at Blossom Rooftop Bar, it’s absolutely one of my top spots for a fun dinner with friends.

We shared a few pizzas and they were excellent. The wood-fired pizzas had a lovely crust and the toppings felt fresh and delicious, perfect for sharing.

The mulled wine warmed us up and set a cozy, festive mood,  just right for a night out on a rooftop under the stars.

The view from the rooftop is stunning. Blossom sits high above the city and the sweeping 270-degree city skyline made the whole evening feel a little special.

There’s a relaxed but lively vibe: the rooftop layout, ambient lighting, and mix of indoor/outdoor seating makes it ideal whether you’re planning a big group night or a chill catch-up.

The staff were friendly and service was smooth. Even with a busy crowd, our food and drinks came out quickly and the night had a great flow.

Blossom Rooftop Bar sits so high above Melbourne, the pizzas should come with a boarding pass. Located on top of the Pullman, this place delivers views that are basically a 270-degree panorama of 'Why haven't I been here sooner?' The drinks are expertly crafted, and the fact that you're eating incredible food while looking down on all the CBD traffic is the ultimate flex. If you need an escape, forget meditation—just take the elevator to Level 14, grab a slice, and let the skyline be your therapist. 10/10, would get high (on elevation and pizza) again!

Really nice outdoor patio with views of the whole city from very high up. Powerful heaters all over keep it a comfortable temperature and the decorations were beautiful.

Food was good, served at a good temperature and quickly. Drinks were slow to be served but understandable for a busy evening. I recommend the cheeseburger, it was on the smaller side but still very nice.

We did have an issue with seating around 10 people. The location we were given seated 6 around one table with our other table quite far away. It made it difficult to converse as a group and the staff were not able to accommodate. Not a dealbreaker but good to know for large bookings.

Hayul, Orla, Filly and Benson (Boone)- in fact the whole team at Blossom, were some of the best servers I have ever come across. Everyone was so accomodating to me and my family. Definetly going to bring the girls here for a bottomless brunch (no kids this time 😉)

name: Lui Bar
location: Entry via, Rialto Piazza, Level 55/525 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
A very nice bar, atop one of the taller buildings in Melbourne. 55 floors up even!

The bar is very modern, more of a lounge style, with low tables and comfy chairs.

The huge windows give great view over the city but you can always indulge in stepping outside onto the open air balcony.

The cocktails were great and the service exceptional too.

The only thing to note is the male toilet situation is quite different.

This is one of those places we keep going back to, mostly because we’re completely in love with their cocktails. There’s something about the atmosphere that just feels special. It has a soft, romantic vibe that makes it perfect for a date night, the kind of place where you can sit close, talk for hours, and lose track of time.

The location is also a big plus. Being right in the city center makes it incredibly convenient, whether you’re planning ahead or deciding last minute to go out. And the view? It adds such a beautiful touch to the whole experience, especially in the evening when the city lights create a cozy and slightly magical feeling.

I’m not sure if it’s the best spot for large groups, since the space and ambiance feel more intimate. I’d say it’s ideal for up to five people. With a smaller group, you can really enjoy the setting without it getting too loud or crowded.

Overall, it’s a charming, romantic, and genuinely enjoyable spot. If you’re looking for a relaxed evening with great drinks and a lovely atmosphere, this place won’t disappoint.

Perched high above the city skyline, Lui Bar in Melbourne delivers an experience that’s as refined as it is memorable. From the moment you step inside, you’re greeted not just by the panoramic views of the city lights but by an atmosphere that balances sophistication with genuine warmth. The highlight of my visit, however, was the impeccable service — particularly from Victorio, who proved to be an exceptional host.

Victorio’s passion for mixology and hospitality was evident from the very first interaction. He took the time to understand our preferences, asking thoughtful questions about flavors, spirits, and moods, before crafting drinks that felt entirely personal. This isn’t the kind of bar where you simply order from a menu — at Lui Bar, the experience is tailored. Their cocktails are unique, inventive, and ever-changing, reflecting both the creativity of the bartenders and the evolving seasonal ingredients they work with.

What stood out most was the willingness of the team to listen and create. Rather than relying solely on their established list, they thrive on collaboration, turning your ideas into a one-of-a-kind cocktail you won’t find anywhere else. It’s this blend of artistry and attentiveness that makes Lui Bar such a standout in Melbourne’s competitive bar scene.

Whether you’re a cocktail aficionado or someone seeking a special night out, Lui Bar offers an elevated experience that feels both luxurious and personal. Thanks to Victorio and the team, my evening there was nothing short of exceptional — and I’ll definitely be returning for whatever they dream up next.
Great roof top Bar in CBD serves a selection of alcoholic/ non alcoholic drinks and light snacks. Great vibe, fantastic view, reasonable price and friendly services

I absolutely loved Lui Bar! The place has such a beautiful atmosphere and the view is just stunning. The food and cocktails were amazing, but what made the experience truly special was the staff. They were so kind, friendly, and made us feel really welcome. It’s definitely a spot I’d love to come back to 🩷

What a stellar setup and setting. Excellent service, food and drinks. Both indoor and outdoor bars, great views and ample seating. Whether it’s for a private function or just a casual drink, this place is ideal. Highly recommended.

Cannot fault it. Went on Thursday night after work for drinks. Busy at first then quiet by 7pm ish. Beautiful setting, view is amazing, easy to get a small table, plenty bar staff, chilled and relaxed vibe. Stayed for food inside and was also very good quality and fast service. Perfect night out after work to unwind. Will go back. Highly recommend.
Great venue. Can get quite busy so book ahead where possible. Young crowd the night we went but enjoyed our booth which wasn’t too loud, and service and drinks were bloody excellent - recommend.
I really enjoyed the vibes. Good place to chill and relax. The atmosphere was nice as well as the service! Worth to come and try!✨🥰
Great atmosphere and attentive staff. Loved all the drinks.

I loved this bar and can't understand the handful of recent negative reviews. It looks rough around the edges at first, but that's just part of the charm of the place. The staff and everyone else inside were super friendly and it really felt like a place where anyone would be welcome.

The drinks were great and they have tons of specials and events pretty much every day of the week. Super cheap drinks by Melbourne standards and the pizza is good too. One of the best bars I found in town.

Have been before but the last time I went they were very rude at the front for no reason. The way they spoke to us was as if they were trying to setup a good story for their friends; they had a small smerk of their faces. We ended up going to another venue and had a great night.

The staff were super friendly and welcoming here. Very cool, fun vibe and even at 5.30 it was quite lovely. I could absolutely see this becoming a regular haunt if I lived in Melbourne. They don’t mix any cocktails on site but I had one of their pre made ones and it was good.

Quite possibly the best bartenders in the southern hemisphere.

Absolutely great vibes, I’d recommend going at 5 -7 when they have their great happy hour prices and free pool.

Have had many a great nights here, everyone is up for a chat and the music is always rocking.

Pizza is banging too.
Heartbreaker will always be my favourite bar best vibes, has a pool table, small dance floor and the best music

I almost don’t want to share this because it feels like such a secret, but Yarra Falls is honestly a hidden gem. It’s small, cosy and intimate, with most of the chairs lined up along the bar so you feel part of the experience rather than tucked away at a table. The records spinning in the background give it such a warm, relaxed energy.
There was an Irish bartender working last night who was brilliant. He took the time to explain where the alcohol had been sourced from along the Yarra and you could tell there was real intention behind every bottle. It is not just a drinks list, it is storytelling.
What I love most is the deeper concept. The bar pays homage to the original Yarra Falls, which was once an important meeting place for the Woiwurrung and Boonwurrung peoples of the Kulin nation. You can feel that connection to place in the way they use local ingredients and native botanicals, and even in the waterfall feature behind the bar.
If you love thoughtful spaces, beautiful local drinks and a cosy atmosphere that feels a little bit special, this is your spot.

A unique and high quality bar that is easy to slide into. Good vibes all round with an approachable menu and the best hot potato bread you will find at any bar in Australia. They have a house smoked Irish whisky- an interesting synergy of Aussie and Irish. And if you aren't sure what to have- just ask for a recommendations from the tasteful bar tenders. A place to return to, and genuinely open till 1am.

My partner and I had a few pre-wedding cocktails at Yarra Falls and it was such a special experience. The cocktails are truly spectacular - each one comes with its own story just like the bar itself. The owner was incredibly warm and welcoming, even surprising us with a round of cocktails on the house to celebrate the occasion.

You can really feel the passion behind every detail - it’s such a unique spot in the heart of the city, with character and heart in equal measure. Can’t recommend it enough for anyone who loves great cocktails and great storytelling ♥️

Dropped into Yarra Falls and had an outstanding experience. Brendan, the owner, was running the entire bar on his own, yet he still managed to stay welcoming, attentive, and genuinely committed to giving every customer a great time. His effort and professionalism really stood out.

The drinks were excellent — a custom Aviation Gin, Tipsy Red Centre, Strawberry Sangria, and a Japanese Slipper 2.0. Each cocktail was crafted with care and had its own unique flair.

For a bar with great vibes, impressive cocktails, and an owner who goes above and beyond, Yarra Falls is absolutely worth a visit. Highly recommend.

Gorgeous little bar on Flinders Lane that immediately caught our eye. Beautiful space, great service and wonderful atmosphere. Clearly a place that was created out of a love and appreciation of the area and excellent use of local ingredients to create unique and delicious drinks. We'll be back to try the food 😍

name: Q Rooftop Bar
location: Level 28/509 Flinders Ln, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Drinks were lovely and the vibe was spot on. Unfortunately our chips took 40 mins to arrive and were pretty cold. Not too sure on the reason as wasn’t fully booked. However, would potentially give another go for drinks and atmosphere.

Had a fantastic experience at the Q Rooftop Bar! The views were stunning, the atmosphere was vibrant, and the cocktails were on point — but what truly made our visit exceptional was the service from Javier. He was warm, attentive, and made us feel genuinely welcomed. His recommendations were spot on, and he added a personal touch that elevated the whole evening. Thank you, Javier — we’ll definitely be back!

Despite being packed on Saturday evening, we had a very attentive waitress who took care of us throughout happy hour.
Amazing views up here. Cocktails were good.
Food comes up from Salted Egg downstairs & is consistently sensational.

Q Rooftop Bar is an absolute gem in Melbourne! The panoramic city views are breathtaking, especially at sunset, creating the perfect backdrop for a night out. The ambiance is chic and vibrant, with stylish decor and a great mix of music that sets the mood just right.

The cocktails are top-notch—expertly crafted and full of flavor. Highly recommend trying their signature drinks! The staff is friendly, attentive, and really know their menu, making the experience even better.

Whether you’re looking for a relaxed evening with friends or a lively night out, Q Rooftop Bar is a must-visit. Can’t wait to come back!

The Q roof top bar was amazing with great cocktails and impeccable customer service especially Javier? Not sure how to spell his name. He made us feel at home and offered us great recommendations for drinks and food.
Couldn’t recommend Javier as a server more.

Emporium Mall Melbourne is a really great shopping destination. The mall has a wide variety of branded stores, from fashion to lifestyle, making it easy to find everything in one place.

The mall is very clean, well maintained, and comfortable to walk around. One thing that really stood out was how clean the toilets are — definitely among the cleanest I’ve seen in a shopping mall.

Overall, a pleasant and enjoyable shopping experience. Highly recommended if you’re visiting Melbourne.

It is a big seven-storey shopping centre. It has lots of shops with clothes, shoes, and more. There are also cafes and restaurants. The place is clean and easy to walk around. I enjoyed shopping here.

Emporium Melbourne is a beautiful  heritage building the epicentre of Melbourne CBD. All your luxe retail needs in City Centre located in the heart of Melbourne. Opened one of the largest Australian designer precincts, more than 160 international and specialty stores, an E- venue, restaurants and more✨
With all the happy vibes and a variety of shops featuring different styles, plenty of places, and small coffee kiosks, this mall is really lovely. ❤️
Beautiful shopping mall.. get everything here u want, from body, bath to cars to food...

name: Bourke Street Mall
location: Bourke Street Mall, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Went to visit part of our walking tour, architecture is very nice .. lots of high end shops and restaurants.. bring your wallet, it’s not inexpensive… treat it as an adventure.. apparently there are tunnels underneath but you can’t visit .. they are closed..

Well!!! Well!!!! So much to see, so little time. Truely, cosmopolitan city from highly desirables expensive shopping experiences to discovering mysterious alley ways. Most times what you find by taking the alleys invokes surprise and pleasure.
A truely must do. 😉🫡

Bourke Street Mall – A Vibrant Night in Melbourne

I recently had an unforgettable night exploring Bourke Street Mall, and if you’re planning to visit Melbourne, this spot is a must. My friend and I kicked off our adventure in Chinatown, then made our way to Bourke Street Mall around 9:13 PM on a Saturday. The atmosphere was buzzing – the trams were packed, and the streets were filled with people. It felt like we were at the heart of the city.

The best way to experience Bourke Street Mall is on foot. The wide pedestrian walkways make it easy to explore and take in the sights. The energy here is unmatched – from the street performers to the late-night dancers, there’s always something happening. We also grabbed a bite at Hungry Jack’s along the way, enjoying the city’s 24-hour vibe.

We visited several clubs that night, including Whitehart Club and Carlton Club, hopping from one to the next. It was a fun way to experience Melbourne’s nightlife. Despite the crowd, the city felt safe and smooth, with people just enjoying their weekend. Yes, we saw a few fights and some people smoking in corners, but it didn’t distract from the overall experience.

One thing that really stood out was how Melbourne never sleeps. People were out cycling, walking, and just enjoying the night – the energy was incredible. If you’re visiting, definitely take the time to walk around Bourke Street Mall and check out the nearby Chinatown. You’ll get a real feel for the city’s pulse, and it’s an experience you won’t forget.

Bourke Street Mall is the heart of Melbourne and my personal favorite spot in the city. The vibes here are unmatched, with stunning city views and constant energy. Easily accessible by trams, it’s well-connected, though parking can be tricky. There’s always something happening—live musicians, street performers, and festive events keep the place lively. During Christmas, the Myer windows add a magical charm, making it even more special. Watching the sunset through the streets is an unforgettable experience. Whether you’re shopping, strolling, or just soaking in the atmosphere, this is the best spot in Melbourne!

Always something to see or do.
Free entertainment too!
Bluey on show Today.
EDIT 2025: Buskers galore
EDIT 2026: Myer Windows are a hit

name: Melbourne Central
location: Cnr LaTrobe and Swanston Streets, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: I love pop up store! After spending $88 you can get these cute plushies as yours. Grab your favorite plushies now and hurry! While stock last😂

Melbourne Central is a great place to shop in Melbourne. It is very convenient because it is directly connected to a train station, making it easy to access. There are many food options and restaurants to choose from, as well as plenty of shops for shopping. The place is clean, modern, and always lively. Highly recommended to visit.

Love visiting Melbourne Central when I go into the cbd. So much to see and do. Every level brings something new. I just recently rediscovered the lower level by the ststation. Food, shopping and sight seeing. Gets pretty chaotic at times. If you want a more quiet food court try QV.

Visited Melbourne Central and loved the energy of the place. It’s more than just a shopping centre — it feels like the heart of the city, filled with cafés, restaurants, fashion stores, and small boutiques all under one roof. The mix of modern design and the historic Coop’s Shot Tower inside gives it a unique Melbourne character.
