The food court has great options, from quick bites to quality coffee, and the layout makes it easy to explore. It’s also well-connected, sitting right above the train station and close to trams, so it’s super convenient for locals and visitors alike.

There’s always something happening — live music, pop-up shops, or art installations — which makes every visit feel a little different. A perfect spot to shop, eat, or just experience Melbourne’s vibrant city life.

It is a big shopping centre in the city. There are many shops, cafes, and restaurants. The glass dome with the old shot tower inside is nice to see. You can get there by train or tram, but it can be busy. It is a good place to shop, eat, and spend time.

name: DFO South Wharf
location: 20 Convention Centre Pl, South Wharf VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text:
DFO South Wharf is a great shopping destination, especially during the Christmas season and Boxing Day sales, but it’s important to go in with the right expectations. The range of outlet stores is excellent, with popular brands across clothing, sportswear, shoes, and accessories, making it a strong choice if you’re hunting for discounts and end-of-year deals.

During Boxing Day and the Christmas holiday period, the discounts are genuinely good. Many stores offer significant markdowns, and if you’re patient and willing to browse, you can find some real bargains. It’s particularly good for sports brands and casual wear. That said, not every store has deep discounts, so it’s worth checking multiple shops rather than buying quickly.

Crowds are the biggest factor to consider. During peak times—especially late morning to afternoon on Boxing Day—the place gets extremely busy. Walkways can feel crowded, fitting rooms have long queues, and popular stores can feel chaotic. If you don’t enjoy crowds, this can be overwhelming.

The best time to visit during the sales period is early in the morning, ideally right when it opens, or later in the evening if stores are still operating. Weekdays after Boxing Day are also slightly calmer compared to the actual holiday dates.

Facilities are good, with plenty of food options nearby at South Wharf and along the Yarra River, which is perfect for taking a break after shopping. The location is convenient, with easy tram access and a pleasant waterfront area to relax once you’re done.

Overall, DFO South Wharf is worth visiting during Christmas and Boxing Day sales if you’re focused on bargains and can handle large crowds. Go early, plan ahead, and be prepared for a busy but rewarding shopping experience.
It is a big outlet mall with lots of brand shops and good discounts. It’s nice and easy to walk around. Parking is a bit expensive but you can get discount if you shop. Good place to find deals.
DFO is always fun for a bargain hunt! The Adidas outlet was a standout with some seriously good discounts, scored some great gear for way less than usual. Worth popping in if you’re nearby.

Do not be fooled or lured by the density of this mall on Google maps. The quality of the clothing is not great. Because it is largely outlets, some of the stuff is slightly cheaper, but a lot of it is quite dated or is cheaper knock off stuff that companies make just to sell outlets additionally, the parking is extremely expensive. You should buy one piece of Lindt chocolate for $1.65 so that you can get half off of your parking. That is the wisest decision. You have been warned; you have been educated.

It's always a great place to shop and to visit when in Melbourne. It's a plus that it's close to the city center and around 15 minutes away (including a 5 minute walk) via the city tram. Uber and cab accessible. Lots of dining options inside the mall and even outside along the the wharf.

A beautiful arcade  - we stopped at the tea house for tea and cake. A short wait to get in and very busy. But a nice experience in Melbourne. Shame they did not have roobos tea / strange for a tea house
Very European architecture. As expected the prices of the many cafes ans restaurants were exaggerated. Nice walk-through shopping center.

A shopping area in Melbourne that also has stunning architecture. There are plenty of branded stores to explore, and the building itself is stunning, making the shopping experience feel different! You can also come here to look at the amazing architecture!

Visited The Block Arcade early in the morning, quiet and easy to explore. Beautiful heritage building with ornate mosaic floors, glass roof, and boutique shops. Fun fact: it was opened in 1893 and is one of Melbourne’s most iconic historic shopping arcades.

Nice building. The decoration is colourful. We had high tea at The Tea Rooms 1892.
Will come Back to have a more thorough look at the building again, if have chance.

A lovely place to visit with a classic, old‑world charm. The historic clock sits right above your head, and it’s worth getting up close to watch it properly.

When the clock reaches the hour, 12, 1, 2, 3 and so on, the little toy figure comes out and starts striking the bell. It’s a simple but charming moment that really adds to the atmosphere.

A decent spot to stop by, soak in some history, and enjoy the unique character of the arcade.
Love this arcade every time I’ve visited from when was a small child through to an adult and then taking my children there are seeing them stare at Gog and Magog just as I did and still do!

Royal Arcade, Melbourne’s oldest surviving arcade, was built in 1869. The elegant mosaic floors, wrought-iron balconies, and glass roof create a striking historic atmosphere amid the modern city. A highlight is Gaunt’s Clock with its figures, Gog and Magog, which mark the hours, a subtle reminder of the arcade’s rich heritage.

My partner & I love this place! So many coffee options, little shops to buy gifts and items for home. A must visit place for all that come to the city, Melbourne residents or visitors, it’s a must for everyone!

Really charming historic arcade with lots of character. It’s smaller but full of cute shops and cosy cafés, and the whole place has a great vibe. A lovely spot to wander through while exploring Melbourne.

Ample parking covered, non covered
♿🚺🚹🚻🚼🏧
As I walked around and realized what a huge variety of your favourite brands at this mall with a lot of other brands I have never seen before .
Feeling hungry a lot of choice eateries to choose from you won't be disappointed.
A clean and well kept Mall for those of us with certain disabilities there are mechanicals aids around or check with the information centre

Highpoint is one of the best shopping centres in Melbourne’s west — it really has everything you need in one place. There’s a huge range of stores, from big-name brands and department stores to smaller boutiques and specialty shops. The centre is always clean, modern, and easy to get around, with plenty of parking and good public transport access. There are also loads of food options, from quick bites to nice sit-down meals, plus a great cinema and entertainment area. The atmosphere is lively but comfortable, and the staff around the centre are always helpful. A fantastic place for shopping, dining, or just spending the day out.

This mall is massive, with ample parking and a fantastic range of international brands like Chanel, Dior, Guess, Sephora, and Mecca. The food court offers a diverse selection, and the atmosphere is lively and vibrant. The Christmas decorations were stunning, and the Santa photo booth was a highlight! We visited on a Sunday, and it was quite crowded, but the overall experience was still enjoyable.
The only drawback was the unclean ladies’ restroom, which kept me from giving it a full five‑star rating.”

I visited Highpoint Shopping Centre most recently in early August 2025 on a busy Sunday afternoon. I’ve been there a few times over the years, but this visit was for the Australia vs the World Survivor premiere pop up event. Getting there was easy enough via PTV, though it’s worth noting that Highpoint is one of the few large shopping centres in Melbourne without a nearby train station. I caught a bus from Ascot Vale, which dropped me right near the entrance. There’s also plenty of parking around the centre for those driving. Highpoint is huge, spread across multiple levels with an incredible range of shops. You’ll find everything from high end fashion boutiques to affordable everyday stores, plus plenty of food options throughout. There are multiple food courts and a good mix of dine in spots and quick bite cafes. It was buzzing when I went, full of weekend shoppers, families, and people out enjoying their Sunday.

I met up with friends at a nearby coffee shop before heading to the pop up, which was set up in a large open area decked out with Survivor themed challenges and decorations. It was immersive and a real treat for fans. We lined up for about ten minutes to start, beginning with a quick vote at the voting booth before moving on to the wall challenge, where we had to guide a ball up a board full of holes using string. It was such a fun challenge, and we had a laugh as we tried to get the ball to the top. Next was the bag holding challenge, where we each had to hold up weighted bags while standing on small pedestals for two minutes to win an Australia vs the World buff. We each walked away with a buff, a mini slide puzzle, and a poster. Finally we did a large slide puzzle which took a bit of thinking to complete, but we got there in the end! I really appreciated the effort Highpoint and the Survivor team put into the event. It brought people together and created a fun, community experience.

Overall, Highpoint is a great shopping centre with so much variety, solid food options, and regular events that make it more than just a retail space. It can get a little hectic, especially on weekends or around Christmas, but that’s part of the charm of such a popular spot.

This centre has undergone extensive renovations over the past few years.
Stores are huge, such as Myer,David Jones, Zara, Victoria Secret along with multiple jewellery stores. Plenty of parking available at no charge.
You can spend hours at this location as the shops will have a broad appeal to all ages.

name: QV Melbourne
location: Cnr Lonsdale Street and, Swanston St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Nice place to go around the city. Complete stuffs provided. Big supermarket available in this place. Lot of restaurants will serve you with delicious foods. And after your gym session upstairs, you can go to those restaurants. I love QV

Amazing spots for napping, eating, shopping, and enjoying clean toilets! Perfect for tourists!
We came here for 2 days in a low!

Weekend parking flat rate has almost doubled over the past 3 years. It was $12 and then increased to $17, and now it is $20. I am not convinced the recent recoloured painting of the level marker poles justifies the increase.
I do not recommend this carpark for weekend rates anymore, however the weekday flat rate (buy at least $1 in a shop) is still competitive even with the recent increase to $23.
today came to melbourne qv for lunch been here many times over many years very nice place specially centre of qv  alot of restaurant and cafe to choose from
Plenty of cafe's and restaurants, Woolworths is on the lower level. Lots and lots of shops. The fantastic Chinese restaurant, Old Beijing is there, great for yum cha and al la carte.

is a good place for shopping. There are many stores with different things like clothes, shoes, and home items. It’s easy to walk around and find what you need. The location is great, right next to Southern Cross Station. Good place to find deals and spend some time.

A good place for the families to shop.

There is an area where all the dads and the sons sit together while the mums and the daughters shop and spend the dad's money.

The stores offer high discount so take your time and go to the massage chairs in the middle section before you continue your journey!
Good variety of shops and easy to spend an hour or 2 here. Food court is alright though I need some better coffee outside

Lots of shops, lots of variety, outlets stores so lots of discounted prices, plentiful food court, right next to Southern Cross station. Perfect place to kill time while you wait for train, v-line bus and train, skybus, or even just before a check in at the hotels near-by

They have their christmas gear up down the back! Perfect for pics

A variety of outlet for clothes, shoes, handbags and a food court with variety of fast food and beverages stores.

This outlet does not have any premium luxury stores. Is great for average households to grab a bargain at the outlet.

However beware of the parking ticketing system, the outlet centre has tried to overcharge us for parking 1.5 hours. When we put the ticket in the paying station, the machine bill us a rate that is equivalent to 3 - 4 hours. We end up calling the operator to correct the charge at the paying station.

Please check your parking time and the relevant rate before you made any payment at the parking station for the outlet centre.

Southgate is a great "shopping centre" that has a lot of eateries and restaurants.
I say shopping centre in quotations because the majority of "shops" are restaurants and eateries, as well as a few other shops.
Still great though.
Will visit again.
This is a really nice area of Melbourne that shouldn't be missed. Nice bars and restaurants as well as a nice walk along the river and park.
So many changes and all for good it was finally lively full of funny buskers, balloon artists plenty of resteraunts full of people, bars, and floating bars wow I loved it!!
Beautiful memorial to the brave, noble and fallen that have given their all to Australia.

An old gem. Incredibly well laid out. Very clean and lot's  of parking. Plenty of cuisines to choose from in the Foodcourt with a smattering of special shops to please everyone.  Food fashion and fun. So yes..

We have seen an interacted with Maria in the food court many times during our visits
She is an excellent worker veru dedicated and focused on her tasks and always willing to help. We very happy to see her and say hello to her every time we stop there.

I have been coming to the food court for many years. Maria the Greek 🇬🇷 cleaning lady is so professional and polite, and cleans thoroughly. She also takes the time to say hello and ask how your day is.

This is a rarity these days!
She’s a keeper! 🪲

Concierge service is amazing - the staff always show such professional service and good manners .   We are always so well looked after and made to feel special after our day shopping .
BUT , this system of charging normal Carparking fees after a 3 hour stay is outrageous .  We come to go shopping , visit the banks and have some lunch and to then  be charged outrageous charges to park your car.   In this cost of living wouldn’t you want to encourage shoppers to your complex , rather than limit them by time and further charges ???
You used to offer free parking  depending on how much was spent in the shops - great idea 👍
A $15 fee on weekdays and $20 on weekends is already high enough -  the reason people choose valet is to feel special , by charging  soo much extra will discourage people from using valet and look for alternatives .  Cheaper to get a limo to drop you off .
Poor control and supervision of the parking lots, I had to wait more than an hour to be able to leave, and the owner never arrived, I left when the car in front moved, POOR SUPERVISION

Use to get stamps, send packages and buy commemorative stamps here. I didn't realize that this grand old dame had a big makover. Still pretty as ever with her quarterly hour and hourly notes still chiming.
Extremely beautiful building inside out. One of the biggest H&M outlet i have been too

The building is gorgeous, although the H&M signage and fake greenery does it no favours. Also the interior, when it was still the actual GPO, was beautifully painted in period colours and now it's all just white 😔. The best of the GPO building now is Postal Lane and it's restaurants. Great eating but also a lovely Lane to walk through to Little Bourke St, with lots of little historic details retained, including the scrapes on the walls from cars!
Loved it.

This is old iconic building in melbourne. Major renovation was done here and the feel of it is great. Real high ceiling with good fashionable clothes on display. I'm not a huge fan of H&M However they did a fantastic job. It's close to Myers and there are great cafes in the lane way next door.
Must visit if your in the city

The Strand Melbourne is a stylish shopping destination located in the heart of Melbourne’s central business district. Known for its sleek architecture and contemporary vibe, it offers a mix of high-end fashion, specialty stores, and local designer boutiques, making it a hotspot for fashion enthusiasts. Visitors can explore a curated selection of Australian and international brands, ranging from clothing and accessories to beauty products.

Apart from shopping, The Strand also houses various cafes and eateries, providing a cozy spot to relax and recharge after a day of browsing. The location, just a short walk from major landmarks like Bourke Street Mall, makes it a convenient stop for both locals and tourists.

With its modern design and blend of upscale and niche offerings, The Strand is a must-visit for anyone looking to experience Melbourne’s vibrant fashion and retail scene. Whether you’re after the latest trends or a unique local find, The Strand offers a sophisticated shopping experience in the city’s bustling heart.
Only a few shops in this little shopping area. The interior design of the shopping area are pretty unique. There’s also an alfresco cafe along the lane

ARCADE THAT CONNECTS TO EMPORIUM CENTRE
Most useful as an access point to Emporium Melbourne from Elizabeth Street, the Strand Arcade does contain some interesting shops.
Sushi Sushi is here and is quite popular as a lunch option (especially to takeaway).
Veronica Maine, Australia Post and ASICS among the tenants here in a fairly modern and well maintained arcade.
Modern, clean shopping lane next to Emporium with specialty stores ranging from coffee to clothes and everything in between.
Clean. Nice. Shopping.

This is a fancy food court nestled inside, between the high rise buildings. Lots of nice food options and good pastries. Glass rooftop is a nice touch.
A nice gateway to the Sofitel as well if you’re going up to the Atrium bar.

Plenty of seating.
Can get busy around lunchtimes but that’s the same as anywhere.

I had a very disturbing experience at Collins Place.
A security guard aggressively knocked on the door of the accessible/unisex bathroom on the second floor, shouted at me, asked invasive questions about what I was doing inside the bathroom, questioned whether I had a disability, and physically blocked my exit.

This behaviour was intimidating, inappropriate, and unacceptable.
Accessible bathrooms are not exclusive, and no one should be interrogated or blocked from leaving a bathroom.

I felt unsafe and distressed. This needs to be addressed

Collins Place is a sophisticated destination in the heart of Melbourne, offering a curated selection of high-end retailers that cater to those with discerning tastes. The elegant atmosphere of Collins Place is immediately noticeable, with its refined architecture and stylish ambience. The retailers here represent the pinnacle of luxury, featuring a variety of premium brands in fashion, accessories, and more.

Shopping at Collins Place is a truly indulgent experience, whether you're browsing the latest collections from iconic designers or seeking unique, high-quality items. The service is impeccable, with attentive staff who provide a personalized shopping experience. Beyond shopping, the location also offers excellent dining options, making it a perfect spot to enjoy a leisurely meal or coffee after a day of exploring.

For anyone seeking luxury and exclusivity in Melbourne, Collins Place is an exceptional choice. It’s more than just a shopping destination—it's a symbol of sophistication in the city.

Disability access is terrible. I would recommend everyone with mobility problems to avoid. It is just possible to move up and down levels. But this often requires huge detours horizontally and the signage is poor and inconsistent.

I had to pick up my passport from the passport office which is inside Collins Place, I found street parking out the front of Collins Place and was back to my car in less than 15 minutes, fantastic experience.

I really enjoyed visiting the Queen Victoria Market mainly because the food alone made it worth the trip. The quality and variety are the real highlights here. Fresh and interesting, excellent meat and seafood, great deli options, and no shortage of ready-to-eat food if you just want to grab lunch and wander. It’s very easy to arrive with good intentions and leave having eaten far more than planned. The produce looks genuinely fresh, and many of the vendors clearly know their craft and are happy to have a quick chat if you ask a question.

Once you look past the food, the history of the place adds another layer. Knowing the market has been operating since the 1800s gives it a sense of continuity that you don’t get in more polished or modern food precincts. Some parts still feel very old-school in a good way, and that contrast between history and everyday trade is part of its charm.

It’s not perfect, though. It can be very busy and a little overwhelming at peak times, and some areas feel more functional than atmospheric. But that’s also part of what keeps it feeling real rather than staged.

Overall, it’s a solid 4/5 for me. The food is easily the best part, and the history makes it more than just a place to shop. Definitely worth a visit, whether you’re local or just in Melbourne for a few days.

Spent the evening at the Queen Victoria Summer Night Market and it was absolutely buzzing!

From the moment we arrived, the whole place felt like a mini festival — lights everywhere, live music playing, and the smell of incredible street food in the air.

We wandered through rows of food stalls offering everything from dumplings and grilled meats to sweet desserts and refreshing drinks. It was hard to choose because everything looked so good!

There were also local artisan stalls selling handmade jewellery, art, and unique gifts — perfect for browsing between bites.

The live performances really added to the vibe. It felt relaxed but energetic at the same time — such a great way to spend a Wednesday night in Melbourne.

Definitely worth visiting if you love food, music, and a lively atmosphere.

Queen Victoria Market was one of the highlights of our visit to Melbourne. We had lunch there and also purchased some excellent products — everything was fresh, high quality, and beautifully displayed.

The atmosphere is vibrant but very well organized. The infrastructure makes it easy to walk around and explore comfortably. Even during the busy lunchtime period, it felt relaxed and enjoyable rather than overwhelming.

We were there with our young daughters, and it was completely comfortable to move around with them. They truly enjoyed the experience as much as we did.

A fantastic place to eat, shop, and experience the local culture. Highly recommended!

What a market to visit. It was one of the best fruit market I’ve visited. It had so many fresh fruits, food vendors, and a whole side of just accessories to clothing to toys. Prices for things were well priced and not too expensive. We ended up buying a whole box of mangoes for $5 that’s $40 at least here in nz. Would definitely recommend stopping by here if you’re in Melbourne city. Parking was paid parking and right outside the market.

I Really like this place and hopefully I can travel to Melbourne more than five times per year!
There are many nice shops inside, from fruit to seafood to dessert!
And is one of the best market I have ever been in Australia! I’m glad this time I do the video for them in my YouTube channel!
So, go for look and you will like this place like I do ！
And here is pretty and clean!

I recently visited Jhona Hair Stylish Studio, located inside Prime Nail Salon in Vic Gardens SC, for a haircut and hair treatment — and I’m so happy with the results! My hair feels so soft and looks amazing.
The service was also top-notch. Jhona is super friendly, skilled, and really takes her time to make sure you get the look you want.
If you’re in Vic Gardens and need anything done to your hair, don’t forget to drop by and look for Jhona. Highly recommend! 💇‍♀️✨

Great centre with all the majors … ikea, k mart, adairs, chemist warehouse, dusk, freedom, sportsgirl, coles, Ugg, and lots more to choose from.
Parking is underground and there’s plenty of parking.
Tran runs right out the front so if you’re in the city and want more shopping choices then this is a good option….

Victoria Gardens Shopping Centre is a must-visit for anyone looking for a great shopping experience. The food court is a standout, offering a wide range of delicious dining options to suit all tastes, whether you’re in the mood for something quick or prefer a sit-down meal. The centre is home to a great mix of shops, including many well-known and trusted brands, so you’ll find everything you need in one place.

Parking is a breeze, thanks to the spacious, well-organized car park with plenty of spaces, making it convenient for visitors. A major highlight is the presence of IKEA, which is perfect for home essentials and unique furniture pieces. Plus, the Hoyts cinema allows you to enjoy the latest movies right after your shopping spree. The mall is always clean, well-maintained, and lively, making it a great spot for families, friends, or solo shoppers. Whether you’re looking for fashion, food, entertainment, or home goods, Victoria Gardens has it all and more.
The Coles supermarket opens daily from 6am to 11pm. Kudoes to the whole team to provide convenience for the residents nearby!

Good parking availability and access to and exit from the parking place was decent. Shopping centre itself is good plus IKEA store is available so if you buy something worth $7 and more (excluding consumables) then you get up to 4 hours parking free which is a plus.

name: DFO Essendon
location: 100 Bulla Rd, Essendon Fields VIC 3041, Australia

reviews.text: DFO Essendon is a great shopping destination with a fantastic range of brands and bargains. There’s something for everyone — from fashion and footwear to homewares and accessories — all at great outlet prices. The layout is easy to navigate, and the centre is always clean and well maintained. There are plenty of food options for a quick bite or coffee break, and parking is usually convenient. The staff in most stores are friendly and helpful, which makes shopping here a pleasant experience. Perfect for a day out if you love finding a good deal!
Good outlet shopping doesn’t has Nike. A bit like Manawa Bay in Auckland. Worth going, the other one in cbd has more attraction maybe

DFO Essendon offers lux brands at sale prices every day. Situated between Melbourne Airport and the CBD it offers both tourists and locals accessible outlet shopping with parking right by the door. No supermarkets, but there are multiple food courts and toilets.
Great dfo … Hugh selection of lux and bargain stores…. East to move around as it’s one level… good sized car park, not undercover but you can’t have it all … worth a visit.

*Unpaid Reviewer*

DFO Essendon – Great Deals, Festive Crowds & Positive Customer Experience

Visited DFO Essendon over the Easter weekend and, as expected, it was quite crowded — but for good reason. Most of the stores were offering fantastic deals, making it a worthwhile trip for bargain hunters and brand lovers alike.

A highlight was the customer service, especially at FILA, where the staff remained friendly and efficient despite the long queues at billing. Another great experience was at the Superdry store — a Japanese-inspired brand from the UK — where the quality of products and the value for money stood out. The staff were attentive and the store layout made shopping smooth, even with the crowd.

Overall, it was a bustling yet rewarding experience with plenty of good buys and positive service, especially at key brand outlets.

It is the largest predominantly single-level shopping centre in Melbourn,It is anchored by Myer, Kmart, Target, ALDI, Coles, Woolworths and HOYTS Cinemas and includes international retailers H&M, UNIQLO and other famous brands, recently had a bad stigma like Stabbings, car drove through the centre, a fire, punch ons, car theft so bit scary to visit this center, overall had a great time and like the vibe, plenty of parking spaces and neat toilets.

Great shopping centre, has everything you'll need in the one spot. Coles, aldi, Woolworths all next to each other. Beautiful butchers that have friendly customer service. Fast food is decent with china bar and a few others.

Good sized shopping centre. Not too big but it’s got everything you may desire and more. As another reviewer noted too - it’s quite comfortable. The decor is pleasant with several seating areas dispersed throughout the building.
The cleanliness is usually satisfactory too.
Although, I was just sitting here eating some sushi when a wad of dust and debris descended on me from the heavens above. Luckily I caught it just in the nick of time, before I landed on my sushi!
Anyhow, a good shopping centre nonetheless. Just keep your eyes peeled.

I’m very grateful to have a full service shopping centre only 3km from home but there’s a lot that can be improved. The main change rooms in Myer’s women’s wear dept on the first floor has been in disrepair forever. I’m starting to wonder if shoplifting is to blame after seeing a security guard standing near the other change room today but the whole customer experience is fairly poor having to carry an armful of clothes a long way to try them on, just to redress, walk all the way back to get a different size. It’s so annoying. Also with Northland, the increase in crime and knife events is of huge concern. I am very grateful to the staff for enduring what they are currently dealing with. Wish our very lax government would do something about it.

It's a great shopping centre where you can find everything you need, including Aldi (yes I might be biased 😉). Great attraction for me is the food court with all the different cuisines. Car park is big enough too.
Fantastic informative and interactive virtual reality tour on the Titanic...above and below the water. So glad we added this to our quick visit to Melbourne.

Ok. This was the best.

Walking not seated the whole time, submerging in a glass sub, drinking champagne in 1st class, walking down the grand staircase, meeting the architect and captain, stoking the boilers, stearing the boat, experiencing the collision from the bridge, the panic of the passengers, the bravery of the crew, escaping on a lifeboat.
Highly recommend, would do again..

The entire experience was incredible from the moment we walked in. The staff were welcoming, friendly, and incredibly attentive. The VR experience was top-notch and truly immersive. It exceeded all expectations, offering a unique and thrilling adventure. We would highly recommend this to anyone looking for an unforgettable experience. In fact, we’re already considering a second visit! It’s definitely worth it for anyone seeking a fun and cutting-edge activity.

The Titanic exhibition was fabulous from start to finish. We had a wonderful time and to feel you have walked over a ship and had avatars talking to you, standing in front of you…. It was an amazing experience and a lot of fun….loved it from start to finish..⭐️⭐️
Discovered by accident but now my absolute favourite Thai fusion, the Thai food and Thai beers are so authentic, food and beverage, the service and restaurant layout is an amazing experience.

name: Westfield Airport West
location: 29/35 Louis St, Airport West VIC 3042, Australia

reviews.text: Had a wonderful time there, big and only one story so very easy to access. 👍

I lost my wallet during a shopping trip at Westfield Airport West. After searching everywhere for it on the day that I lost it I couldn’t find it and started stressing out about re- ordering all my cards and ID’s. The next morning to my surprise I received a phone call from the ANZ branch at the shopping center with news that they my wallet had been found and it was waiting for me at the concierge desk. I want to acknowledge Concierge staff for using initiative by handing my bank card to ANZ staff so that I could be contacted. Good initiative and professional service. Thank you so much.

Was quite surprised and happy with the state of the centre, looked extremely clean and well presented especially the bathrooms and baby change areas. Most shopping centres are disgusting in the bathrooms but was actually taken aback with how clean the facilities were. Also an honourable mention to the food court which was very well presented. I have now found my new go to shopping centre when I need to bring the kids.

Had a wonderful experience at the centre today. While lining up to take our Christmas photos we were greeted not only by the love staff taking the pictures but also by Ebru from centre management who was so kind and welcoming. Small attention to detail by the staff and the kindness shown really seperates Westfield airport west from other centres. Definitely will be going back. Great service and customer service skills

Thanks to the two  young men who were working in the car park outside the CBA on Monday who did a very honourable thing in returning a substantial amount of cash and all my cards that I had left in a trolley. Thank you very much 🙏🙏

name: RetroStar Vintage Clothing
location: First Floor, Nicholas building, 37 Swanston St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Fantastic store and friendly staff. Picked up some Levis jackets that were worn in and in amazing condition. Half the price of new and feel so much better to wear. Rest of the clothes selection is pretty dope as well.

i was really keen to check this place out but i left incredibly disappointed. despite being described as “vintage”, most items weren’t actually that at all, the racks were filled with mass-produced tees w/ old bands, cartoons and film posters printed on cheap blanks.

even a section of older sweatshirts with very obviously new screen-printed artist logos. you can feel the fresh print sitting on top of the worn garment which doesn’t match the condition of the fabric. it’s especially frustrating for people who can’t tell and end up paying over $40 for this!!

it really undermines the whole idea of shopping sustainably or finding real second hand treasures. not worth the stop unfortunately 😕

Wife took a trip with our son to Melbourne and brought back two T-shirts (we live in NZ) that she intentionally bought a size larger and yet both were still too small (more like medium than size XL) which was really annoying to say the least. Gutted. I'd rather pay more for correct sizing than less for something that I ultimately can't wear. This was never a problem with T-shirts until recent years where the market has been flooded with cheap, poorly sized knock offs. Not happy.

So much to choose from!! So many retro clothes that you can't be quick here. If this is something you're into, you can't not come and check it out. Some things look a little pricey, but overall the quality of these old outfits are great.
Well-curated selection, some fun current pop stuff, and a friendly staff make this store a winner. If only they could do something about that vintage “aroma.”

name: Sacred Heart Mission Op Shop - South Melbourne
location: 365 Clarendon St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia

reviews.text:
I’m an Op Shop aficionado. So are my adult kids. We all enjoy exploring second hand, reused, recycled stores as well as Op Shops. We have underlying values around not buying new if there is something preloved available.
I’ve been to Sacred Heart Mission Op
Shop many times but rarely buy anything.
Why?
I always say that I’ll eat average food but not tolerate average service. Same with stores, I might find something nice but unpleasant staff turn me away. Staff here are often grumpy which seems to be a theme in other reviews. I consider myself to be very friendly, affable and gracious but the staff were just unpleasant.
The store is well laid into sections eg books, men’s clothes, women’s clothes, jewellery, books etc, there isn’t a lot of kitchenware and mainly focussed on fashion. It’s always been that way - alas!
The stock is reasonable but some of it is a bit ‘scrappy’ and not worth considering, so choose carefully.

Notes: Location is great. Good parking out the front. Disability friendly. Kid friendly. Plastic bags available in store to take away your goodies.

A very lovely nice sized op shop with great bargains & cheap prices, kind service to,
If you’re looking for cheap DVDS, jewellery or little knickknacks this is the place to come, the DVDS are only 1 dollar each lots of different genres of movies, cds, records, music & more! definitely come check this place out if you’re near by. 📀🎞️❤️

Good items but expensive, prices are not fairly adjusted for condition of items, saw $25 for shirts that were quite worn and fading in multiple areas. As a result I have only ever browsed and never bought in this op shop
A great little op shop where you can find immense value in quality goods, they go through alot of stock so you should visit frequently

This Sacred Heart Mission Op Shop is one of the better op shops with newstock continually coming in.  It's quite large and is well laid out and has a good ambiance and feel to it.  Being on Clarendon Street, parking can be difficult but it's just a short walk from Park Street Stop 129 on St Kilda Tram Route 12 and Clarendon Street Stop 24 on South Melbourne Beach Tram Route 1.

If i could rate this place a 0 star i would. The amount of arrogance the blonde lady at the checkout has is absurd. When I entered and smiled, I was met with an eye roll and a cold stare. Why work in customer service if you cannot even have the decency to smile at customers as they enter your store? Customer service requires basic courtesy, and that was completely lacking here. DO NOT GO HERE, YOU'RE BETTER OFF GOING TO VINNIES OR SOMEWHERE ELSE!!!!!!!!!

Every time I buy an accessory it breaks. Just two days ago I bought a necklace. Broke on the first wear. Disappointing. On reinspection, all the jump rings are open and threatening to fall out. Same thing with a belt. Peeling on the surface, etc. I know it’s pleather but cracking on the first wear? Disgustingly low quality.

Best place to come thrifting for teenagers!
I’ve had the best experience here they have a perfect range of many different styles of clothing including vintage & unisex outfits, perfect for females & males with the most coolest outfits you have yet to discover, definitely recommend coming here if you love thrifting & styling outfits especially if you’re a teen with a sense of fashion.💕

Genuinely the best clothing store in Melbourne.

Everything looks incredibly, the selection is huge, the prices are UNBELIEVABLY cheap, 10/10 place
Amazing staff! Amazing clothing! Best prices in the CBD! Come n you wont be disappointed
The shop has lots of great stuff- many items are still brand new, and the prices really good
Quirky op shop with loads of quality items at about 1/3 retail. There's a $5 rack out front and the cool displays are worth venturing inside.

Used to shop here all the time and it was so good. Not sure what’s happened 😞 Came in this Saturday and the guys working weren’t very friendly and everything was Shein or Kmart. Shame because I used to find heaps of cool stuff here. Probably wouldn’t recommend if you’re after good quality at good prices.

One of the best thrift stores in Melbourne as a visitor here. Bought 1 Nike sweatpants looked like brand new for its price only $25 latest design, one sweatpant ellese for $5 and a really nice blouse. You should definitely check them out because they have almost new condition in some items that are worth over $60 like the nike sweatpants. I have a similar one and i bought for 65€ brand new. Very very much worth and i love streetwear. Very kind and friendly 😊 will definitely visit again to buy more stuff thanks 🤗
Great selection, quirky fun layout, super friendly guys in store. Thanks Storehouse!

If you love retro, secondhand designer and good quality vintage selections, this is the store for you. They have a bit of everything, and it’s massive. The staff also played some sick beats. We really enjoyed our time here.

High-class pre-owned products sold here. Mainly clothes and some shoes, posters, CDs and glassware but just a heads up, they are not cheap.

Not on ground level, so if you are having a problem walking, do note you need to climb at least 2 flights of stairs.

Personally, you can give it a miss.

The place is very interesting with lots of second hand items and clothes from multiple collectors.

One very disappointing thing was that one of the staff members ripped the price tag off the blouse I bought at cashier.
It was a chiffon blouse and I was so afraid that it might get damaged. He didn't even fold it properly before putting it into a paper bag.
I wish they would treat their clothes with more care, especially in front of customers.

Everything is really expensive so it didn’t feel all that welcoming to shop there.. many items I wondered if the original prices were maybe cheaper. Not entirely worth the stop in my opinion! You won’t be missing out on any good finds at a reasonable price
Idk why the stuff is so overpriced. It's all second-hand or even third or fourth hand. Some cool things tho but I wouldn't spend that much on them.

OMG. I love this place!  You need hours to go browsing through every little nook and cranny and it absolutely blows your mind. It’s like walking into a Time Machine and being taken off to different times in your childhood. There are modern things there too. Anything from memorabilia (incl. war) to jewellery, clothing, Lego, beautiful glass and kitchenware, Asian art, art of all kinds actually, to name just a few things. If you are into vintage and old stuff, this is your place. And even if you’re not, this will be your place anyway 😆

The staff are so knowledgeable and so very friendly, nothing is too much trouble. If you see something, you note it down and they come with the key and open up the cabinet. They chat with you and clearly enjoy the whole experience with their customers. It’s a very well organised and professional setup, and whenever I go to Melbourne, I head straight there for a day of wonder and anticipation (what might I discover this time!) Chapel Street Bazaar is the sort of place that puts a smile on your face every time you walk in, your shoulders drop from the stress of the city, and you are left to wander through a world of fascination. Give yourself a good half day because you will need it, you have to look at every tiny little corner otherwise you’ll miss out.  I never walk away without a treasure.

Been coming here for a good forage for over twenty years and it’s maybe not quite as jam-packed with amazing finds as it once was, but we have never left without a few interesting treasures. Whether your jam is a weird taxidermy curio, groovy midcentury homewares or Bakelite bangles in every colour, you’ll find it here, and if not, it’s still an interesting way to while away a couple of hours, checking out all the nostalgic ephemera. Prices vary from stall to stall, so it’s within everyone’s budget.

Walk through memory lane in this store. If you are a collector, you probably can spend a few hours inside,  as they even have a Playboy collection (sealed of course)!

It is maybe a hoarder's paradise!

Incredible store filled with treasures. You could spend many hours scouring through the endless maze of trinkets, clothing, collectables, kitchenware, furniture and the giant fake rhinoceros priced at a cool $3k. A definite must if you love the old school goodness 😍 Don’t go there expecting a bargain, but worth it to have an incredible curated collection of vintage pieces.

Packed to the rim with antiques & collectibles this place has 1 million things to look at, segregated into individual cubicals there is an assortment of special items here.
Follow people like me as we make the effort to showcase amazing places around the world for you to evaluate

name: IN WANG VINTAGE
location: level2/75-77 Hardware Ln, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
I absolutely loved it💕! The vibes were so cozy and authentically vintage, and the shop’s setup was just perfect.
✨Echo was incredibly kind and lovely, she helped me try on necklaces and guided me in choosing a color that suited me best.
I finally picked one that’s absolutely adorable!
I’ll definitely be coming back next time🫶🏻

Strolling down #hardwarelane in Melbourne, I spied a sign “Wang Vintage”. I just had to explore. The journey up the flights of stairs is somewhat a teaser. The walls covered with cute murals and artwork are worthy of photographing.
Once you walk in, you’ll be welcomed by the multitude of vintage and retro homewares, clothes, accessories and of course - the handbags.
The owner has taken the time to style her shop with care, placing mirrors with candelabras alongside dried flower arrangements next to an old piano sprinkled with nostalgia and more.
The handbags are all of high quality and price tags to match.
Cher, the sales woman, was delightful and helpful in giving me some insight into the history of how  #inwangvintage came to be.
If you’re into vintage then I highly recommend a visit to @inwangvintage

Notes: not wheelchair friendly as there are 3 flights of stairs. Trams and trains make this place accessible from all around Melbourne. Kid friendly.

#tripadvisor #tripadvisorreview #tripadvisorreviewer #googlelocalguide #googlelicalguides #letsguide
#thewanderingreviewer #vintagehandbags #melbournevintage #vintagefashion #melbournevintaheshopping #australianvintage #vintagefashion #hardwarelanemelbourne
Absolutely love this vintage shop! The staff here are super friendly, and they’ve got such a great range of styles to choose from. Always something unique to find. Definitely worth a visit!

I had a wonderful experience at this vintage bag store! The staff was friendly and knowledgeable, making the shopping experience enjoyable. Highly recommend for anyone looking for unique and authentic bags!
Went there last week and it's such a nostalgic vibe inside love the deco&music there. Most of the vintage bags there are in very good condition. Friendly staffs highly recommended
Lovely range of clothes. Friendly staff who helped me look for my mislaid brolly. This gorgeous Pure Hype dress will be perfect for throwing over swimwear,  was $7 but on sale 50% off!

great assistant.
held bags behind counter, weight of my sholders.
sold a large rug, valued ech day.

to the company,  transfer the lease to a building with bathroom, at least for your wonderful staff.
Small store, all little cramped for more than one person on each aisle but I scored a vintage 1970s Oxford heel for $8. Loved it !

Although the sign in the door says they close at 5:30 and so does their google maps opening hours, I arrived to a locked door at 5:15. The people inside closing up the till refused to let me in and told me that they actually close 15 minutes earlier than advertised anywhere. If you actually close at 5:15, then say so, so that people don’t trek over to your store after work for no reason.

I am not happy with that  gay male staff,  i was telling the lady manager  about i got heaps of brand new men apparelle to donate, he keep interupting me when i was speaking  with the lady manager. he was telling me no used undies, socks and etc. he was very rude. no point of donating to this city salvo store. they are more on money making business. better donate it directly to those needy recipients.

name: Hunter Gatherer City
location: Royal Arcade, Ground Level/335 Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
Such an amazing hidden little gem!
this thrift shop has lots of vintage unique clothing items such as, bags, jewellery, objects, they also sell real leather items for such great prices to good quality leather, it’s quite normally expensive these days for real high end quality in jackets & bags, the sales lady was so kind I bought a stunning red leather jacket from here & ever since i’ve gotten lots of compliments about it, truly such an astonishing thrift shop. 👗👒

Beware that it can still get pretty pricey these days for op shops including this one!
Awful, all the prices are way over blown. Cheap crap being sold more than retail prices. $80 for an old opshop jacket is ridiculous.
Was ridiculously over priced  for what is being sold not great quality a few good things but I won't be visiting again.

Some good stuff, but expect the prices to be a tad higher than other reviewers have said. Bought a skirt for $20. 🫣
Seemed excessive. For those wanting to save money and do the right thing it's drawing people away.
Most items are $10+25

Very hard to find and when located the staff members rude and told me the store was closing, even though I arrived 16:30. I wanted to volunteer and donate but never got chance to see what the charity was about. Probably won't go back :(

Very big and so much to offer! Was very surprised walking in. Such a lovely bigger gentleman asking us what we thought of the store in which my friend was gushing how much she loved it and everything they had set up. I loved down the back they had furniture and Manchester which had pieces that for sure would be sort after from huge paintings, beautiful couches and almost chandelier lights. Lovely staff and will be back to have a proper look. Prices weren't too bad either.

Great, big, colourful shop which is fun to browse. Clearly marked sections with plenty of options to browse. More expensive items are in their own section. Other items are reasonably priced.
Big book, cd and DVD section as well as clothes and accessories, homewares.
Great Op Shop. Such a lot of variety. The volunteers do a good job too. I was served by  one named Gordan who helped me move a few items I purchased to my car and with a cheery smile

Staff are friendly, it's an op-shop dedicated to helping those who can't afford more expensive stuff. Open to taking in good stuff, reasonable prices. HUGE space, for clothes, furniture, bedding, books, all household things.  They were really welcoming and chatty and open.

New location for the big and browsable Bro of St Laurence op shop in Brunswick, into the old Mirabella Lights/Travel building on Lygon St. Friendly greeting and easy to browse racks with a room of more designerish clothes and cheap racks of clean undamaged clothes. Found a great book as well.  Dress your kid for very cheap. Fun visit in a few light-filled rooms. Jewellery is still well above the usual standard and many choices at this store.

name: Salvos Stores South Melbourne
location: 183 Clarendon St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia

reviews.text:
The people working here are unhelpful.

I don’t recommend this thrift shop at all.

You’ll have to ask where everything is & then the people still won’t tell you where anything is & you might get lucky & buy something cool.

The last time I came here the woman looked at me with a dirty look like I was doing something wrong when I came in here decked out in my own fashion: Y Dank X Legend.

That’s my own brand.

When I wanted to buy something I was made to feel like I couldn’t afford $3

What?!

I bought cool pink shorts tho.

So I guess that’s worth 1 star.

Shop anywhere else please; until the staff here changes.

If you’re queer expect them to look at the floor instead of looking at you when you ask questions about the clothing.

This place is discriminatory & disrespectful.

Please be careful fellow queer people & maybe choose another thrift shop.

A small but still very pleasant salvos, the staff here are very friendly always greeting you when you come in, it has a small selection of clothing & there’s a corner of books & DVDs also ones that come in sets you may even find some vintage items! but the staff here are very lovely & might even compliment what you buy, though the prices at the heart op shop are much cheaper, there DVDs cost 1$ while here at the salvos it costs 3$, they also have a bigger range of DVDs & books, but for a small op shop it’s still pretty good for thrifting unique items.
❤️📀🧸

Friendly staff. Store is pretty small so it’s ideal to have one person at one rack at a time. I am so happy I got a pants that was priced at $13 but because it was a blue tag clearance day, I got it for $2. Would love to come back again.
Excellent op shop, very well kept, kitchenware organisation by colour, prices competitive, would recommend

I love this Salvos Op Shop and have donated quite a lot of my things to it. Compared to many of the others it's quite small and being on Clarendon Street, parking can be an issue but it's only a short walk from York Street Stop 127 on St Kilda Tram Route 12. The staff are really friendly there and you'll often come across a good find. Definitely worth a visit.

name: Brotherhood of St. Laurence City
location: Royal Arcade Basement, 335 Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
The staff were very friendly here and from what I say they had a good selection of everything. Definitely over priced for an OP shop but there were some hidden gems i was able to find that were underpriced considering how rare they are.

Is a bit cramp though but still very worth a visit if you're in the area.

Easy to locate in the underground of Royal Arcade. There are orange bins available outside at the entrance for charity donations, the cashier is friendly greeting every customer walking in. While the space is small with limited range, it is organised into its own categories. I didn’t end up buying anything but would return again for interesting finds. Toilet is not available inside the store and may not be disability wheelchair accessible as it requires staircase to get down.

Awfully overpriced

Clothing items comparable to new items

Single CDs at $5

Would be surprised if much stock actually moves

Used to be worth a look but not anymore

this used to be a reasonable op shop with the occasional great find at good price, but they've recently rebranded as "hunters and gatherers basement" (hunters and gatherers is their ground floor smaller shop with mostly overpriced "label" and "vintage" things which are rarely, if ever worth the price).  they've jacked up the prices by 50  to 400% compared to what they used to price things at before, and the quality control is practically nonexistent.  damaged clothes, with holes or various other serious issues priced as if they are pristine.  books, cd's and records also now too overpriced to make them worth getting.  the staff that were there when I last went were utterly clueless and obviously had no training in retail nor had any idea about the stock they had in the shop.  not worth going to anymore.  a sad loss to the few op shopping options in the city.  the "opportunity" part of "op shop" has been erased at this location.  what were you thinking, brotherhood of st. laurence?  one word:  avoid.

Unbelievably overpriced.
A sheet of acetate mix ins (for resin projects) from Anko was being sold for $3… exact same product currently 3 sheets for $2 at Kmart.
Multiple fast fashion items not in best condition being sold for $25-35.

name: Salvos Stores Port Melbourne
location: 243-247 Bay St, Port Melbourne VIC 3207, Australia

reviews.text: Your local op shop with good variety of  clothing, accessories, well lit floor space, nice atmosphere to find appreciated items
Lovely staff and a great selection of items. There are always fantastic bargains and the prices are very reasonable. One of my favourite op shops to visit!
I always like to pop into Op Shops that I'm literally walking past and this was a pretty good one. Didn't buy anything, though their Xmas stuff was cute.

This salvos has a wonderful collection of different items & clothing, even has a lovely little range of vintage tea sets with the cutest cups & the kindest customer service! thrifted a cute pair of boots from here for 13$ that’s just perfect for styling! great prices to very cheap.❤️

However remember to check clothing for any damages before buying!
One of my favourite places to buy second hand clothes!
good variety of clothes and prices. prices are a bit on the upper side but manageable overall

Nice little selection of 2nd hand clothing. Got a lovely bohemian style sweater that I've been wearing so often nowadays. 14 aud from topshop! Total bargain. That district sure has a lot of 2nd hand vintage shops so totally worth hanging around if you're looking for vintage.

Probably the best vintage second hand store in Melbourne. Went there today and loved the vibe, prices are a fair bit cheaper than similar stores, plus the guy working there had THE coolest style - like a blue and red sweater with jeans. But anyway. Really friendly cool place.

I hate to say it, but this store is getting a little greedy with it's pricing these days. I realise it is good for the Brotherhood of St Laurence's coffers but I have noticed that the stock is not moving as fast as it used to... I guess they know what they are doing but feel they have forgotten that it is a charity shop and the goods were donated in good faith. Maybe people should redirect their donations to other charity shops instead?

There's always a surprise waiting in Hunter Gatherer. It's one of  'the' destination Op Shops in Melbourne, on Brunswick St. The window dressing is standout on the street. Staff are friendly,& handle the many shoppers with humour & grace. Prices are above the suburban average,so too are the 'on trend' funky vintage outfits. Ever changing- a must see!

name: Sacred Heart Mission Op Shop - Fitzroy
location: Alexandra Pde end, 433 Brunswick St, Fitzroy VIC 3065, Australia

reviews.text: What a cool op shop! It’s a good size, it has character, an interesting mix of stuff, there are bargains to be had and friendly volunteers. Street parking is metered and a little limited, but there are usually spaces available.

Such a quirky well set up op shop!
Well sorted and not heaps of cheap brands. Prices were a little higher but they did have a massive rummage area with $2 items.
Worth checking out!
Lovely staff, well curated, great selection of quality and affordable items! And the van parked in the middle of the shop definitely gives it edge 😁
What a lovely, cozy store!! I got stuck in the book section for a good 30mins. Will visit again for sure!

I've been to tons of op shops in Melbourne, but this one's my new fave! 🤩 Huge selection, amazing prices, and the quality is incredible – most items look brand new!  The staff are super friendly too! 😊 #Melbourne #OpShop #Thrifting #Secondhand #Shopping #Bargains #Finds

OMG!!! I’m so in love with this store.
The vibe
The smell
The stock
The staff
I came away with two grandpa vests and a cardi.

I wish I wasn’t just visiting Melbourne.

Totally recommend!

Bought a German leather jacket from her in the late 90s still own it it came with a European train ticket in one of the pockets the owner waited for me after closing time to collect awesome shop and awesome people. Highly recommend.

The staff here was the most friendly and had the biggest smiles and acknowledgement when I was there.  It is refreshing to see when the customers can feel zombie like as they are probably taking in the wonderland of the place😊
im obsessed with this place. I went with my friend who wanted nothing to do with the place but I dragged her along and its such a nice store!!

Friendly staff and good range. A bit pricey, even compared to the other vintage stores in the area. My only issue is the volume of the music, every time I go to the store. It’s as loud as a nightclub. Even with earplugs in, I’m sometimes unable to shop here.

Op-shop pricing. With the obvious exception of large screen televisions which can apparently be purchased for the price of a panini, op-shop prices have increased pretty sharply over the past 10 years. Yeah yeah, there's the odd obscure church based one that has 2015 prices, but hey, ya know what, the stuff is very, very rarely any good. It's all like Anko and Rivers and tea cosies and low resolution plastic framed Margaret Keane prints.

Anyway, I stand by my opinion that Abbotsford Salvo's is THE best op-shop in Melbourne, bar none. Firstly, those $2 and discount racks up front. Yeah, there's good stuff in those racks. Legitimately good stuff. Abbotsford just has better quality and better variety of stock than any other op-shop, and the prices, while higher than years past, are still some of the best in Melbourne.

Staff are nice, it's well organised, the furniture is great and actually fairly aggressively priced for what you can get, and again, there's just fantastic variety. I feel like I'm never surprised by other op-shops, but I am constantly surprised by what I find in Abbotsford. It's busy, and it's a central collection point, so there's a pretty constant turnover of stock.

All Salvos stores are daylight robbery now and this one is no exception. However they do have a bunch of racks at the front of the store with $2 items, otherwise I would have given 1 star.

Pictured is the regular jeans rack, prices range $25-$35 a pair.

Prices are out of control now. Used to be my favourite op shop, it’s so sad but I bypass it now due to the greed. I found an old port bottle for $20, decided to buy it then found the same one at an independent op shop the next week for $5.
See photos below for a: $15 dirty top, $25 for a polyester Target coat, $48 for a beaten up pair of boots and $55 for a jug. Cheaper to go to Kmart, which is terribly unsupportive for our community and landfill.

I’ve picked up some lovely things for home here, but something is happening to the pricing approach at this location - it’s wild. Example, some bubble glass bud vases priced more than they would be in a curated vintage store. See photos. Another example, (which I don’t have photos for) a Bendigo Pottery vase which I picked up elsewhere for $19 recently (and I thought THAT was steep!) is going for $29 here. Woah.

This is one of the best thrifts in Melbourne. Easily the best in Melbourne if you're looking for furniture.

Unfortunately the kids clothes and toys are extremely overpriced - prices starting from $5 just for things like t-shirts. The kids racks are so overstocked (probably due to the pricing) that you can't even really look at them - not that they are affordable enough to purchase.

However, I decided to speak to the store manager and she was extremely receptive to the feed back, she asked a lot of questions and even passed it onto another team member while I was there. It sounded as if they are not aware of general pricing for second hand kids clothes - OR that kids clothes are (allot) cheaper than adult clothes RRP.

Hopefully the prices improve and they can clear some more stock - and also help people with children who are at the thrift to save money/reduce waste (since kids grow out of their clothes). It's weird in comparison to the adult clothing that is well organised and well priced (considering the quality).

My visits to Brunswick Savers over the past 3-4 months have all been incredibly disappointing. Very bare shelves, damaged items. Their focus seems to mainly be on keeping the clothing stocked, leaving homewares and entertainment extremely underwhelming.

If you need assistance with viewing an item from the cabinets, purchasing furniture, or paying cash (self serve is card only), they need to call a staff member over the loudspeaker off the floor to assist you, as there is usually only one person manning all of the checkouts.

It feels like Savers is cutting staffing levels to the absolute bare minimum across all their stores, but Brunswick seems to be the worst affected, and it's a damn shame.

Years ago this place was thriving, now there's so many Anko products and cheaply made items you might as well just go directly to Kmart. Prices are high and haphazard not sure anyone knows how they come up with them. Items can be found marked higher than their original sales price still seen on items. Other savers locations are definitely better stocked and less of a rip off.

Coming from New Zealand and knowing what the New Zealand opshop prices are like, this place is amazing for their pricing. Huge selection of quality items to choose from. The store is kept tidy which is good to see. Plenty of young and happy staff working here.

I went to this store at around 7 pm on a Friday, and it wasn’t too busy. Large, clean, bright space full of bargains! I was pleasantly surprised with the customer service and the selection of clothes. Some items seem to be a bit marked up just because of the brand, but overall affordable. Would recommend spending at least 2-3 hours in the store.

Half-price clothes! I snagged some Blue Illusion and Regatta blouses, almost-new shoes, and a summer hat. Next time, I'm bringing my trolley. Ellie, a staff member, did an amazing job handling the fitting rooms.

name: Salvos Stores North Melbourne
location: 55 Errol St, North Melbourne VIC 3051, Australia

reviews.text:
My family and i are locals, the workers are actually super sweet! Now and again, new hires come along to learn the ropes. Bee and Pan are my favourites. They are lovely, and Pan likes to chat to me about kpop and SEVENTEEN. Gorgeous.

10/10! Thrifting is always good here too!

Some things almost feel free with how many discount days they have. Plus, students can show their student ID card and get 20% off on Wednesdays.

Very accommodative!

Salvos Stores North Melbourne on Errol Street is an absolute treasure trove.

I dropped in expecting the usual op-shop finds and was pleasantly surprised by how much great stuff they have. The shop is clean, well laid out and spacious enough to browse comfortably, and I was especially impressed by the large book section tucked away at the back with a solid mix of fiction, non-fiction, classics and quirky reads. There were plenty of gems to flip through, which made the hunt for a good read fun and rewarding.

Beyond books, the store has a wide variety of pre-loved clothing, homewares, toys, CDs and records, so whether you’re after a bargain find or something unique, there’s always something new to discover. The pricing feels fair, and everything I picked up felt well worth it.
The best op shop I’ve ever been to, I always come here as it’s so organised and tidy! The staff are so lovely, Pan gets an honourable mention for always going above and beyond!
Great selection, many brand name items in good condition
2024: Salvos is back in North Melbourne.

Oh my goodness. I bought two pairs of worn, soft Lululemon tights for five dollars each. I also bought a very cute jumpsuit of corduroy for nine dollars. More importantly, I’ve been here twice and the sales representatives are awesome. There’s this female rep with purple hair who has incredible style and is fun to chitchat with. Today, I had a longer conversation with the gentleman wearing a knot fest hat about upcoming metal shows. He was so cool. Where do they find these people? Such gems!
Lots selection of great things

Made the journey there according to reviews but not really anything to write home about… I guess depends what you’re looking for. Great range of sizes and found a few ok items but weren’t anything I really needed or wanted to take home. Guess that’s the way the cookie crumbled on my visit 😅
Awesome place to shop. Great quality stuff and amazing people. Shout-out to Hermie for his exceptional service.
Big salvos store, plenty of stuff, washing machines and electricals available, several book shelves, parking available at the back,lots of shoes

Luke was an amazing DJ! From the start he was easy to communicate with, professional, and really understood the vibe we were going for. On the night everything ran perfectly and the music was spot on. The dance floor was full most of the night.

He did a great job reading the crowd and mixing different songs so everyone had a great time. A lot of our guests even commented on how good the DJ was.

If you’re looking for a DJ who is reliable, friendly and knows how to create a great atmosphere, I highly recommend Luke!

Booked my wedding around Nathan's availability as he is the best in the business!! Nathan is absolute rockstar, he was the DJ & Sax at our wedding and was phenomenal. He also did my brothers wedding and he just gets better every time. Nathan knows how to read the room and matches their level. We had absolutely everyone on the dance floor before entrée & again before main course. His energy elevated the whole night and everyone raved about him and continue to after the event! We are so grateful for Nathan and would highly recommend him to elevate any event!

We booked DJ Kim for our wedding reception, and we couldn’t be happier with our choice! I was quite nervous about finding the right DJ since we were having a cocktail-style wedding, and it was important to me that the music kept everyone on the dance floor all night. Kim absolutely nailed it — the music, the energy, and the vibe. His open communication and professionalism made the whole process so easy and stress-free. Thank you so much for being such a big part of our special night, Kim!

Patrick did an acoustic performance for a garden wedding in Fitzroy Gardens. It was so amazing, so many guests thought he was family as they found he was above and beyond anything they had experienced for entertainment.
We even had a special singing performance for the signing song by the Groom's cousin, with Patrick playing guitar. This was so special to all of us and after the formalities continuing playing while we had ice cream served.
Everyone stayed to listen to Patrick and it was something the Groom had said he loved more than anything else!

We booked La’Fell and DJ Tom for our wedding ceremony and reception.
La’Fell was exactly the vibe we were looking for and his magical voice was the best way to walk down the aisle.
We had DJ Tom was a HIT! We barely left the dance floor!
He was DJ, on the sax and MC and I cant thank him enough for how awesome he was!! The experience booking through MEC was so easy and everything went seamlessly and to plan.
Tom knew the schedule exactly but still checked in with us to ensure that we were ready for everything or if we wanted to move anything around.
Would highly recommend.

name: Crown Melbourne
location: Crown Melbourne, 8 Whiteman St, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text:
I love Crown Casino — the restaurants are beautiful and the location is perfect. I’ve been going for years and it’s always a great experience.
It can be tricky with kids in the casino area 😉, but the restaurants and walk outside are definitely family-friendly. The hourly fire show is a highlight, and parking is always so easy and convenient.

AMAZING!! So much going on here, Firballs every hour after 9pm were spectacular to watch. Very clean and very beautiful. Luna show was awesome to watch, highly recommend visiting crown at night. Atmosphere os incredible.

Crown Melbourne is definitely 2000% better than other Casino in Australia( such as Bris….)
The staff are so friendly, warm, helpful and professional !
Especially two staff I met GAJ(I’m not sure my memory is right or not) and Mary Shen!
They are lovely people and not only introduce us about the environment of casino(as we are visitors) but also being so friendly to us!
For this reason, I will always want to be back to Crown every time when I visit Melbourne!
And really appreciate to meet GAJ and Mary Shen every time 🙏
BTW, the concert here is also fantastic, go when you free, it’s worth definitely!!

We drop by Crown Towers to have a look around and it was lovely.
They had all the Christmas decorations and themes still up, so we had the opportunity to have a walk around and take some photos.
It’s Beautiful

Went there for a special High Tea, it was absolutely wonderful. Food was delicious and staff were wonderful.
We then went for a walk all around the Casino. So so big but wonderful.
Enjoy my morning there. Would go back again and again. Parking was good only a short walk to Casino. I thought at the time it was a bit expensive but worth every penny.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We had Elsa and Rapunzel from Entertainment Emporium for my daughter Bella’s 6th birthday, and they were absolutely incredible! ❄️👑✨
From the moment they arrived, they brought so much magic, energy, and kindness. The girls were completely captivated — Bella and her friends had the most magical day. Their singing, dancing, games, and interactions were perfect, and they stayed completely in character the whole time.
Both princesses were so patient, gentle, and amazing with the kids. They truly made the party unforgettable, and Bella hasn’t stopped talking about them!
I highly recommend Entertainment Emporium — thank you for making Bella’s birthday a real-life fairytale. 💙💜✨

We had Elsa attebd our 4year old daughter birthday party. Elsa (Isabelle) was absolutely amazing! The kids loved her and she was able to keep the groupd entertained the whole time. Was great she also encouraged breaks for food and availability to be present during the cake and for photos.
Highly recommend, will definately be booking another character party through entertainment emporium for my son. Customer service was amazing and patient with my enquiries and excellent communication. Super easy to deal with!

Nathan as Sonic the hedgehog was fantastic. Most of the kids with the age group from 4 to 10 were funny engaged. The birthday boy was so happy and loved his Sonic that he even asked can I have Sonic birthday again and the feedback from his friends was it was the best day ever. Highly recommended. Thank you Sonic for making his day memorable

Princess Aurora stayed in character the entire time and was absolutely wonderful! She was so engaging with all the children at my three-year-old daughter’s birthday party, and she even sang beautifully when we sang Happy Birthday. I couldn’t recommend her more highly!

We hired two entertainers from Entertainment Emporium for our child’s birthday and they were amazing. The professionalism, energy and the extra attention they gave to the birthday child right at the start of the party were incredible. The activities and face painting were wonderful and all the kids had so much fun (and the parents were all able to relax). I received a call ahead of the party from the entertainer directly to confirm details and the whole booking/confirmation process was so smooth and easy. The pricing was competitive and the service such a high quality. We will definitely be booking again. Thank you so much Caitlin and Hannah for adding some Barbie magic to our child’s birthday party!

name: Ocean 12 Entertainment
location: Shop 51, Level 3/8 Whiteman St, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text:
We celebrated a friend’s birthday here and it honestly exceeded all of our expectations. I have actually been there before for karaoke and always had such a fun time, but I had never tried the food. I am so glad I finally did because it was absolutely incredible.

From the moment we arrived, the atmosphere was amazing. The view is spectacular, especially around sunset and night time.  The music really sets the perfect vibe for a fun/upbeat relaxed birthday dinner. It feels lively and special without being overwhelming.

The food was packed with flavour and beautifully presented. Every dish we ordered was delicious. You can really tell there is so much thought and care put into both presentation and quality.

What truly made the night stand out was the service. The staff were so attentive, friendly, and genuinely accommodating. They happily took photos for us, made sure we got great shots, checked in regularly, and went above and beyond to make sure everything ran smoothly. One of my friends also snapped her heel strap and they helped us with it too.

As someone who does not drink, I especially appreciated how thoughtful they were with the drinks. They offered to make me a mocktail and only asked what my favourite fruit was and whether I preferred sweet or sour. They then surprised me with something they created on the spot and it was absolutely delicious. That level of care and personalization really meant a lot and made me feel included in the experience.

It is honestly so worth it. From karaoke nights to birthday dinners, they really do go above and beyond. 10 out of 10 experience and I will definitely be back!

Absolutely loved our party at Ocean KTV! This place is fantastic, the VIP room was luxurious and stylish. Huge props to the massive TV screen, great sound system that makes everyone sound good, and an extensive song list. The private balcony with river views, delicious food including amazing ice cream! and a cool bar with photogenic cocktails were all highlights. Plus, it was incredibly clean. A perfect, energising night out with friends. Highly recommend!

I had my birthday at Ocean 12 last year for a karaoke night and all my guests absolutely loved it. The atmosphere was so fun and the team made the whole night really special.

Recently my friend Shae invited me back to celebrate her birthday with a small group of six friends for Pisces season and once again they absolutely nailed it. They placed us at a beautiful table outside near the fire which made the setting feel so cozy and perfect for the evening.

We were welcomed with arrival drinks served by Dorothy, our lovely waitress, who made us feel extremely welcome from the moment we arrived. Her service was warm and attentive all night.

The food was absolutely beautiful. My favourite was the kingfish which was incredibly fresh and perfectly prepared. Of course we also had cocktails and I tried one called Pretty in Pink which was delicious.

Such a lovely experience again and I definitely cannot wait to come back for another milestone celebration.

Very pretty venue. It was hard to find but the food and drinks made up for it. I celebrated my birthday here with my partner, sat at the outdoor rooftop area and enjoyed watching the Crown flames go off every hour and the view was incredible. Service was great!

Feel into this place by accident as it is quite hard to find.  You have to take the escalator up after coming into the Crown main section.  But what a great surprise.  Great ambience, very nice staff,  and we had cocktails and dessert while watching the fire show. Perfect. Will definitely come back.

name: Rutherford Entertainment
location: Level/5/111 Cecil St, South Melbourne VIC 3205, Australia

reviews.text: To Adam, Richard and the Rutherford Entertainment team. You did an exceptional job with the music and set the tone for the entire reception. It was an amazing vibe and all of our guests have been extremely grateful and complimentary of your work. We all had an amazing time and certainly for Shez and I, it means so much.
