It was absolutely perfect. You took the worry and stress away from us and created what was an amazing atmosphere. We would absolutely recommend you all to anyone who is hosting a function/wedding. Perfect 10/10 from us!

We had nothing but a positive experience with Rutherford Entertainement. Ruby, who worked with us to find the best DJ for our wedding, was friendly and responsive from the very beginning. She was super thorough, but also incredible kind and personable. Together, we chose DJ Nic, who made our wedding so special! All I wanted was to dance all night, and he nailed it. Thank you to both of you for being such a big part of making our Wedding a wonderful day, well into the night!

Rutherford Entertainment were the perfect pick for our wedding!
From the beginning the process was so easy and we hadso many options to choose from.  Our coordinator Ruby was super helpful in guiding us to what was best for our day including music/vibe assistance right up to the day or two before.

It was such an amazing day/night and Hector on Sax and DJ Eddie were amazing!!! We got so many compliments throughout the night and after on how great the music!

We had such a fantastic wedding! Rutherford Entertainment were fantastic. All our guests were raving about how great Kane Duo was - they were exactly what we were after and did such a great job during the ceremony learning our songs as well as the cocktail hour!
The DJ was amazing too! He played the key songs we were after but read the mood of the room throughout the whole night and really nailed the feel of the night. Again had such great feedback from everyone saying how fun the dancefloor was!
Could not be happier with the service you guys provided us! Thank you again for making it so easy on our end and briefing them so well and nailing the right person for the look and feel we were after!

Our special night was incredibly beautiful and DJ Leon Duncan was exceptional! Everyone loved the music and energy in the room and spoke of it for days following the event.

Leon was also an amazing MC and kept checking in with us throughout the night.

Thank you for your support and making this event so wonderful and memorable.

name: Bar 20 Sports Bar & Strip Club Melbourne

reviews.text: Good vibes and women are really sweet too. Was made to feel relaxed once we came in despite being scared before going in.

Absolutely loved my night here with a few buddies! Good drinks, great bar snacks, and the absolute best service. In fact, we all woke up thinking to ourselves 'wow how could that service have been better?'. Wouldn't you know it, at exact that moment we all realised we had humongous rashes on our balls! A great (albeit itchy) reminder of a wonderful night. Will be back.
Fun vibe. Girls are stunners and know how to make conversations . Keep up the top quality. Insta content is hot too.

I came here with a few friends on Friday night. The place was amazing and we had a great time. The girls were hot and put on a nice show. Staff were very polite and accommodating to our
group. The bar girl would come up to us and offer us more drinks to save lining up at the bar, it was bery convenient. Highly recommend this place!

First time in Melbourne and showgirls didn’t disappoint! The girls were stunning and so lovely, super intimate club, me and my partner got private  dances they were amazing and so well priced. Cant recommend enough 10/10 xx

First, the online booking was not clear that VIP table tickets were for 2 people only, saying 2-6 people. So we had to pay extra $85 at the door, no problem. Total $250 for 3 people. Watch this: VIP table is 3 foldable chairs and a bench for a table. I don’t know who wrote all the good reviews, I am not sure they are all real. Or maybe people have never seen any shows.. When I walked in, I thought it’s a joke. The place is low quality night club, stage is tiny and barely fits 4 people dancing on it. As a former dancer I would be embarrassed to perform there. Our “VIP table” was between two puddles of water dripping from the ceiling, floor was sticky. Their “Strict Dress Code” is non-existent cause I saw people in hoodies and sweats. Uncoordinated dancing, costumes with holes in them, stench of sweat. One guy always looked lost and tried to catch up with choreography. There were total 3 guys and a girl dancing.
The show was okay, felt amateur and cheesy. I got pulled on stage, two dancers were pretty rough with me, they put their boots on my knees and light was shining into my eyes. I couldn’t catch up with everything happening so fast, it was a terrifying experience. I am sure I looked that way too.

Pros: some drinks were included in the tickets, which smoothed the experience. And Christian is an absolute angel that made the show, but he belongs in a more upscale establishment IMO.

I have been to Men Exclusive now, 6 times now over the span 4 months. The first time I went was the beginning of May this year. one of the guys said to me "you'll be back for more." And he was right. I loved it so much the first time. What I love about the show, is it's different almost each time. Some of the routines will change slightly. It's a unique show with choreographed routines. There is so much audience interaction.They don't always completely strip down. The MC, and the creator Sidney , is really fun, the way he interacts with the crowd, jokes around. They are all a bit cheeky but with big hearts. Afterwards they make sure they chat to most of the audience, and have a drink with people. I have been to a few strip shows. If you've been to Magic Men or another full strip show, this is a different show to those. Men Exclusive, are the most inclusive show adult show, I've been too. The venue is wheel chair friendly with accessible toilets.
We had a great night, entertainment was fantastic, food was delicious, service was spot on, I wouldn't hesitate recommending this show to anyone & I would definitely go again.

I absolutely love going to this show everytime. These guys never disappoint. The guys always dance their hardest and bring the best every week. I'm so happy to be a regular who goes to almost every show!! Bring on May 3rd 🥳

Saw this show in Townsville,  best night out in forever.  The choreography and dancing was fantastic 😍 and they are very easy on the 👀  as well. I highly recommend you see this show if you get the opportunity.

What a great fun place to visit. Loads of arcade games, pool, and all the fun activities, darts, shuffle board, lawn bowls and so much more.
Staff are super friendly and helpful.

It was an awesome time! 😍 Totally vibing, the staff were fantastic and really attentive. It's so good to go somewhere that isn't just a rigged arcade, they really feel like they want you to have the best time. Particularly impressed with Robyn and Jen! Girlies deserve a raise. 😋

Great service at Kingpin. The staff are very friendly, and the place is fun and enjoyable. There are many different games to enjoy, and the customer service is excellent. Special thanks to Acham for his outstanding service.

Kingpin Melbourne is the most aesthetically-pleasing Venue I have visited across Victoria.

Their iced strawberry matcha, donuts, and flavoured popcorn were delicious.

I found the staff to be approachable, energetic, and friendly; their dedicated managers led by example. The service provided to me throughout my multiple visits were consistently exceptional.

I would describe the atmosphere as warm-luxe, with an emphasis on street art which decorate many of the Venue's walls.

I was able to escape ordinary while in-Venue, and I look forward to returning for their Bar & Grill followed by a couple of rounds of pool.
Great for the little ones to have a lot of fun in the city. Lots of choice of activities and games. Good food and drink selection too. Will def be back.

Decided to visit this place 2 nights ago out of pure curiosity while on vacation in Melbourne. Didn't think much of it at first, but I totally did not expect to have such an amazingly wonderful time there.

The girls were all very gorgeous, friendly & assertive, and had no trouble making me feel welcomed and comfortable. The bouncers are also nice and ensure your safety as long as you behave appropriately (Don't be a Douchebag).

I'm not qualified to judge the reasonability of the prices charged but as a reference for anyone who wants it, here's what I paid (I went on a Thursday):
Entry Fee: $25
Lap Dances: $50 per Dance for the Duration of 2 Songs
Bottled Beer: About ~$13.50 per Bottle
"Tipping Money" - 1 for 1 Exchange Rate, 1 Stack = $20 Minimum

Lastly, I'd like to give Special Thanks to 3 Girls: Lucy, Bambi & Jay for making my night an unforgettable one

Had a great time, treated great by staff, the doormen are respectful gentlemen and the tall German lady that looked after me for about an hour was fantastic, cheers Thumbs up.
I'm the guy the walking stick.
P.S. the guy with the shaved head and beard was looking out for me the whole time, respect man I feel bad I didn't grab your name. Grazie goombah
The club has a cosy boutique feel rather than a massive, impersonal vibe, which makes the atmosphere relaxed and welcoming from the moment you walk in
We were there last night 10 of us VIP’s. Amazing service from Hollie, she was hardworking, kept professional and very friendly! She’s a credit to your team. Will be back soon. Thanks Hollie.

Dreams gentleman’s club is hands down the best venue in Melbourne. If you’re looking for a luxury experience look no further this is the place for you! From the moment I waked in I was enveloped in an atmosphere of elegance and excitement. The venues chic decor and ambient lighting set the perfect mood for an evening of top teir entertainment.

And the girls… WOW what captivating performances. Not only are they drop dead gorgeous, charismatic and extremely talented but they are such a pleasure to be around they made my evening totally unforgettable.

The staff were all exceptionally welcoming and attentive alongside making great cocktails they ensure every guest feels valued and comfortable.

What truly sets Dreams apart is its commitment to excellence in every detail from its impeccable service to the outstanding entertainment. It’s evident that the management takes great pride in providing a first-class experience.

I wholeheartedly recommend dreams to anyone seeking a sophisticated and memorable night out. It’s a shining example of how adult entertainment can be both classy and thrilling.

the workers are nice but the disorganisation and inconsistency when you're in the queue is not good. If you're getting on a guest-list be sure to be there extra extra early (like 9:30 pm or something) cause even if you go there 30 mins before 10:30 theres a chance you’ll not make it in and pay $35 😀. I organised and planned frees for 9 people and even got there extra early but because the queue was so long and no one was checking for frees, 3 people had to pay because by the time we got to the front it was way past 10:30 pm. Last time we had someone checking for guest-lists and stamping down the line so that this issue wouldn’t occur, maybe because last night there was a lot of people they didn’t want to do the same but this inconsistency and inconvenience made the rest of the night unenjoyable. I hope they improve on this in the future and keep consistent.

This was a very traumatic experience. Our table was double-booked, and when I raised the issue a staff member became aggressive and began yelling at me. Rather than acknowledging the mistake, he insisted he didn’t know who booked it and tried to shift the blame onto someone else. I calmly pointed out that he worked there and that there should be some accountability, but he kept shouting and trying to make it seem like it was our fault. I felt incredibly unsafe. Instead of calming the situation, I had to repeatedly ask him to lower his voice, but he continued while I was only trying to defuse things. No customer should ever be treated this way. I especially caution women to be careful here, as this staff member showed no respect for boundaries or basic courtesy. After the confrontation, he just walked away without apologising.

Friday only at CQ bar (do not  be confused with the old photos, its not at south yarra anymore), with outdoor terrace for smoking, primarily asian crowd that starts with Rnb hip hop into hardstyle/tech edm from 12am, highly recommend

I got kicked out because of 2 guys next to me smoking weed.
Guard came over, couldn't find the joint, picked up a random normal cigarette butt off the ground and said I was going out.
Those guys gave me a puff of their joint, no way I was gonna snitch.
Very overpriced door fee, charged $15 extra for coat check, 30 sec djs, would not go again.

name: Blue Planet Entertainment
location: Level 10/440 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: We have used Blue Planet Entertainment for the last 3 yrs for our company Christmas parties and both entertainers have been a complete success! Graham Owens is very professional and has a great relationship with all entertainers to know which one would suit each individual event. Second year on we had the amazing Paul Jamieson and we could not speak highly enough of his ability to interact with the children and the adults amazing performer, we have already booked him again for Next Christmas. We highly recommend Blue Planet Entertainment!!!

Graham arranged for the Champagne Serenade girls to perform at my party and they were just perfect.  It was great that they mingled with the guests handing around champagne and it really helped the evening take off.  Their singing is wonderful and it was a pleasure to have them as part of the night.  Highly recommended for a glamorous and gorgeous party!

Used blue planet for our wedding. Graham was very helpful in helping us plan. When the duo we had arranged weren't available at last minute, Graham found a suitable alternative who could play the music we wanted and showed up on the day to make sure everything ran smoothly and was alright. He was great to deal with. Highly recommend him.

Graham was an absolute dream to deal with!  Matched the soloist for our event perfectly - Tommy's voice and presence was brilliant and his acoustic covers suited everyone!  Graham - we look forward to working with you and your talent again REAL soon!

Sincere thanks to Graham for providing a stunning New Orleans Jazz band for my Dads funeral . What a wonderful joyous way to accompany Dad out of the church and along College Crescent . Had everyone smiling . And all arranged in three hours when I had given up THANKS Graham

name: Strike Bowling Melbourne Central
location: Shop 305, Level 3 Melbourne Central/211 La Trobe St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text:
An Afternoon of Fun at Strike Bowling, Melbourne Central

Spending an afternoon at Strike Bowling, Melbourne Central was an absolute delight. From the moment we arrived, the lively atmosphere and upbeat energy set the tone for a fantastic time with friends.

The venue was modern, well-designed, and full of character; the perfect spot for a relaxed yet vibrant social experience.

The staff were welcoming and friendly, adding to the fun with their great service and positive attitude. Every detail, from the smooth setup of the lanes to the overall vibe, made the experience enjoyable and effortless. Laughter, friendly competition, and good company turned a simple bowling game into an afternoon to remember.

Some places don’t just offer entertainment; they create moments of connection and joy. Strike Bowling truly delivered that, making it a go-to destination for anyone looking to unwind, have fun, and make lasting memories.

Regan is the best host ever. THE CERTIFIED GOAT 🐐. He was so good to our party the whole night and brought amazing energy. This location has amazing staff all around and is so friendly. Can’t thank their hospitality enough. Everyone come here for functions it’s fairly priced and amazing vibes. Thanks to everyone for making my friends birthday something we won’t forget!

I recently spent an evening at Strike Bowling Melbourne Central and it turned out to be a great choice for a fun night out in the city.

The venue has a lively atmosphere without feeling overwhelming. Music was upbeat, the lighting added to the vibe, and the whole place felt energetic but still comfortable enough to actually enjoy your game. The lanes were in good condition and everything ran smoothly without any technical issues.

Staff were friendly and efficient, especially during busy periods. Ordering food and drinks was easy, and service was quicker than expected. It’s a solid mix of bowling, arcade games, and a bar setup, so there’s plenty to keep everyone entertained even between games.

Overall, it’s a reliable spot for birthdays, group catch ups, or just a casual night out. Fun environment, good service, and right in the heart of the city which makes it super convenient.

I had an amazing night at strike last night celebrating my 23rd birthday. Our party host Regan was not only amazing in his hospitality but went above and beyond with his customer service. He threw in a few nice surprises for the party which we all loved and appreciated. Money well well well spent. Thank you Regan!

I’ve been bowling here a few times now and always recommend it to my friends, so I was back again today with one of them, but this was my best experience yet. We were helped by MJ, who was honestly the highlight of my visit. He was so kind, patient, and genuinely helpful the entire time. He gave us great advice on how to make the most of our hour and was more than happy to help us out even though it was super busy with online bookings. He made everything feel easy and fun, and it was really nice knowing I was in good hands. I really hope this gets passed on to him because he was an absolute star and made the experience way more enjoyable!

Thank you so much, MJ 🩷

name: Royal Botanic Gardens Victoria - Melbourne Gardens

reviews.text:
A true green oasis in the middle of the city, this place offers a peaceful escape from the busy urban atmosphere. There are many different birds to see, which makes it especially enjoyable for nature lovers. The wide grassy areas are perfect for sitting down, relaxing, and having a cozy picnic.
Very good maps and easy accessibility.
It is definitely worth a visit and can be easily combined with a trip to the Shrine of Remembrance nearby. Overall, it’s a lovely spot to unwind, enjoy nature, and take a break from sightseeing.

We recently visited Royal Botanic Gardens Melbourne and it was such a beautiful, relaxing family outing — and completely free.

The lotus flowers are currently in bloom and the Oriental Lake looks stunning, filled with soft pinks, whites and lush greenery. It’s the perfect spot for a slow wander, taking in the scenery and enjoying some downtime together as a family.

We spent time strolling along the paths, relaxing on the grass, grabbing a coffee from the café and letting the kids explore the children’s garden, which is easily one of the best in Melbourne. It’s thoughtfully designed, engaging and a great space for kids to move, play and discover.

The lotus blooms only last for a short time, so it’s a lovely excuse for a picnic or gentle walk. You can also take a punt on the lake to see the flowers up close from the water, which adds something special to the visit.

A peaceful, beautiful space that’s perfect for families, couples or anyone needing a calm break in the city. Highly recommend visiting while the lotus flowers are in bloom.

Lovely gardens. Very peaceful. One thing that made me impression were the many dogs and their owners- something usually not allowed in other countries around the world as the dogs might make a mess around the plants.
The cactuses on the East part of the garden were impressive. The Children garden was a real oasis for peace. There is even a hidden bamboo garden. The Observatory is also here.

I strongly recommend to visit the Royal Botanic Garden Victoria at Melbourne. It’s truly one of the best Botanic Gardens I have visited. Weather is awesome and cooling during summer with warming sunshine. People just laz around the park, sit down on the grass/mat for a picnic.

There are so many kinds of interesting plants, flowers and trees and it’s fascinating. We enjoyed ourselves a lot but the park is really huge and you would probably need at least 2.5-3.0 hrs to cover all the area by casual walk.

Next time when I’m back at Melbourne, I will surely go back there again.

The Royal Botanic Gardens is a place I truly love. The garden prioritizes natural ecosystems, creating a calm and refreshing environment rather than an overly artificial one. Its location near the Shrine of Remembrance makes it feel seamlessly connected, as if history and nature blend into one space. Overall, this is a wonderful tourism ecosystem, peaceful, meaningful, and a place I always enjoy visiting.

name: Basement Comedy Club
location: Downstairs, 120 Exhibition St, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: Was there in Dec, best $20 I spent in my time over in Melbourne plus saw England win at the MCG too ! Strangely enough had eaten upstairs in Morris House a few days earlier and the food there was good too

We had a brilliant night out at the Basement Comedy Club. Our surprise line-up included Arj Barker, Mike Goldstein, Dave Hughes and more. All were hilarious. A tiny gripe is the close seating. I lost part of my chair as I was sitting next to a larger person. Very squishy but it didn't spoil the fun.

So $20 for Arj Barker, Dave Hughes and newbie Adrian Portelli - bargain!  Great venue downstairs at Morris House, the basement comedy club, and with obligatory fake brick wall, fantastic.  Get along to this place, see who's performing, well worth checking out.

My boyfriend and i booked last minute for a date night and we had a great time! The comedians were genuinely hilarious, delivering punchlines that had the entire crowd laughing out loud. The venue itself is top-notch, offering a comfortable and welcoming atmosphere that really adds to the experience. My only minor gripe was the length and frequency of the intermissions—while it's nice to have a break, shorter or fewer intermissions would keep the energy flowing better. Overall, it's a fantastic place for a night of laughs, and I’d definitely return!
Great venue.....had a super fun Friday night. Surpise special guest was the icing on the cake. Quality laughs 😃 😀 😄 😁

name: Chasers Nightclub
location: 386 Chapel St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia

reviews.text: Went to see Shanti People perform here last night. Good venue for a concert. Drinks were fine and bartender was service focused.

Went to see Chicane play. Turned up early as they stated he would start at 11pm sharp. He came on nearly 30 minutes late.
There was time wasted getting him 'set up' with staff and the previous DJ trying to connect cables.

Once Chicane set eventually started, it was disorganised. He seemed like he had no idea what he was doing, kept stopping tracks mid-way to film the crowd.
The actual mix was really badly done.

We left early and feeling very disappointed.
The venue should do more to ensure whomever is playing starts on time and can deliver.

As others mentioned,  I'm sure the (overpriced) drinks are watered down and definately agree with people saying the bar staff are rude and inattentive.

Now I know it isn’t the actual clubs fault as security is hired from a separate company, but the security dude at the door was really rude. I came in for Rampage with a group of mates, at around 10pm or so. The guy at the door was fine with them, however he became really rude and demanding to me. I was getting my ID out of my bag and he kept yelling “ID” multiple times as I was literally grabbing it. He then went through my bag, started pulling out items while YELLING at me “what is this!!” whilst rolling his eyes, shaking his head etc It was a contour stick and perfume 💀 He told me to put my water in the bin which was fair enough, as I went to the bin directly next to him he again started yelling at me “put it in the bin” multiple times. I get security have to be a certain way, but he was a lot nicer to my mates who had more items in their bags than I did (he didn’t pull anything out let alone yell at them at all).

However, the actual venue was great and we all had a great night, apart from entry.

Not sure what's going on with the drinks here but the last couple of years they taste terrible. Alcoholic or non Alcoholic both just as bad, they taste metallic watered down and undrinkable. Only thing that tastes normal is a bottle of water. Staff that clear drinks are ruthless as well. You can take one sip of your drink and have it right next to you on a table and if you look away for a second they just clear it 3/4 full. Unbelievably frustrating.

I have been clubbing a long time. I also have been here plenty of times. This last time I found security to be the most rudest and arrogant people I have ever dealt with. The toilets need a revamp and to be fixed. We asked for an ice pack for a hurt ankle and was giving ice cubes in a towel which was fine but staff clearly don’t know where first aid kits are if any in the establishment. For a gay club I wouldn’t class it as very friendly at all. I have never felt so disrespected by staff in my life. I understand you can get some clowns out but honestly if you don’t like your job get a new one. Security acting like police officers and swearing and rude over nothing. Quite disgusted I think I will stick to the other gay venues around Melbourne.

name: The Palms at Crown
location: Level 3, Crown Melbourne, 8 Whiteman St, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text:
First time at the Palms to see the legends and have to say the chairs in front of the booths are terrible.
Two don’t face the stage and the other two do, but one of the seats view is blocked by the people in front, with the balcony seating because you are on the same level.
Not sure how they can sell these seats at same  pricing as the booth seating which is elevated and a far superior view.
Also have to add that it’s is dangerous for patrons navigating past these seats.
I would have seen at least a dozen people stumble and bump into the legs of the seats poking out as they walked past them.
I really don’t know how this public safety issue has gone unattended for so long.
The sound and lighting are pretty good however for this performance but there was some distortion in the sound when it was too loud.
The Legend performers were great and the show is worth going to however I would recommend balcony seating first, then booths but not the seating in front of booths.
Also note the Palms is going to undergo an upgrade this year to increase capacity.
Hopefully the seating issues will be addressed.

5 Stars! ⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
A Top-Notch Experience at Palm Crown Casino! We were there to see Andrew DeSilva’s dedication to Prince. 🎸

I was thoroughly impressed by the entire experience. From the moment I walked in, the atmosphere and vibe had a luxury feel a perfect mix of elegance and excitement.

The performance was great, Andrew is a class act performer and did justice to Prince!  He sang all the classics and his guitar playing was next level. 🎸

The venue is a stunning place to see a show, classy! 💫 we had balcony seating with a table that allowed for us to 🍷🍷

Highly recommend seeing a show here, you won’t be disappointed!

The Palms was a lovely venue to visit for the first time. I absolutely loved the way it was set out.  It was like a semi-circle set up. I ordered drinks from the table we were sitting at. It seemed to take a bit of time to get them, but it was nice to be able to watch the show whilst we waited. Parking in the multi-storey car park was good value as well. It was $10, where we parked. We left after the show. Access in and out was fairly easy as well.
A spectacular place for day/night out! Especially at Christmastime & all their beautiful decorations.

Will never return to this substandard venue. Aside of looking tired and old and very grotty, it is NOT an accessibility friendly venue. Enquired at the time of booking that the necessary parking and lifts and access would be all ok. Also rang a few hours before the event to double check all of that. The disabled parking area we were told to park in had an escalator entry, NOT a lift! We had to carry the wheelchair up the escalator! Then it took two different lifts, which were impossible to find, to make it to the Box Office we were told to pick up our tickets from. Box Office closed!!! Took two more lifts to make it to the theatre only to be seated in the wrong seats by the usher, even though we queried the seat numbers. Seats on the floor are so tiny and  so close together that everyone around us was pushed half over to the next chair, so by the end of the row you would be lucky to have one bum cheek on a seat. But wait, when it came to going to the bathroom we discovered that there is NO dedicated disabled toilet. There is an accessible toilet INside the ladies and INside the gents. My male carer queued with me inside the ladies, much to everyones discomfort. The wait in the queue took so long that we went and used the one INside the gents. AGAIN, much to everyones discomfort, including the gents walking out. A totally broken system within Crown and The Palms from start to finish. Will never return there.
